steps.m: A-L-M-S (special letters at the start of this chapter)
steps.n: These are special letters that appear at the start of some chapters. They are read as individual letters: 'alif, laam, meem, saad'. While their exact meaning is known only to Allah, they show the miraculous nature of the Quran - even though it's made of regular Arabic letters like these, no one can make anything like it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun by changing the first vowel to kasra and adding alif after the second letter
The alif is written with a special mark (alif khanjariyah) above the letter

steps.m: a book (subject of أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: Adding tanween dammah shows this is the subject of the verb أُنزِلَ (was sent down) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the Book is what was sent down. Added hamza with damma at start and changed middle vowel to kasra to show passive voice.

steps.m: to/towards (preposition showing direction of أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it shows the direction that the book was sent down.
The ى changes to ي with sukun (ْ) to prepare for attaching the next part

steps.m: to you (male) (showing recipient of أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows this is talking to one male person (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this case)
This particle connects what follows to the previous statement about the Book being sent down, showing a result or sequence

steps.m: so let there not be (connecting to يَكُن)
steps.n: لَا is added to make this negative. Together with the following word يَكُن, it forms a prohibition or negative command meaning 'let there not be'
Changed the root letters into a simple noun by adding vowel marks (damma and sukun)
Adds ي at start to show 'he' does the action, with vowels to show it's happening now/future
The word is shortened by dropping the و when it comes after special words like لَمْ or إِنْ that need this shorter form
This is a preposition that shows something is contained within or inside something else. In this ayah, it shows that something is inside 'your chest'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to the root letters to make the word for chest

steps.m: your chest (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The كَ shows this belongs to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who is being addressed in this verse. This word appears in the phrase فِى صَدْرِكَ (in your chest).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word from a verb (action) to a noun (thing) by adding tanween damma. This word is the subject of يَكُن (be) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing where something comes from or the source of something
Added emphasis on the م with a shadda for phonetic assimilation since it comes after another م sound in the previous word

steps.m: from it/him (referring to كِتٰبٌ - the book)
steps.n: Added هُ to show this refers back to the book (كِتٰبٌ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. Added sukun before هُ to help pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he warned' to 'you warn' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: so that you warn (purpose of أُنزِلَ - it was sent down)
steps.n: The لِ shows this is the purpose of the book being sent down (أُنزِلَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a preposition showing how the book was sent down. It connects to the verb أُنزِلَ (was sent down) in this ayah.
The هِ refers back to the كِتٰبٌ (book) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark showing how to say the هِ clearly.
This pattern makes the verb into a noun meaning 'the act or result of remembering'. This word serves as a reminder specifically for the believers mentioned after.

steps.m: and a reminder (for المُؤْمِنِين - the believers)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to show the person who does the action
Makes the word plural for multiple believers
Added لِ (for/to) and الْ (the) at the beginning, and َ at the end to show this word is connected to ذِكْرَىٰ (reminder) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he followed' to 'follow!' by adding alif at start and putting sukoon marks - makes it a command to one person
The letter ت merged with ب to make a stronger sound, showing you should follow very carefully

steps.m: all of you follow closely! (plural command)
steps.n: Added plural command ending to address multiple people

steps.m: all of you follow closely! (plural command)
steps.n: Added ٱ at start to show this is a command
This is a connecting word that links 'follow' (ٱتَّبِعُوا۟) with 'was sent down' (أُنزِلَ). It means 'follow what was sent down' in this context.
Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation
Added alif with madda (آ) to show long vowel sound. This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes someone cause the action - instead of something coming down by itself, someone is making it come down

steps.m: was sent down (to إِلَيْكُم/to you all)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it. This connects to إِلَيْكُم which shows it was sent down 'to you all'
This is a basic direction word that shows movement or connection towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching to the next part
Adding كُم shows this is directed to multiple people. This word shows who received what was sent down (أُنزِلَ) in this verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it comes from
Added shadda (doubled م) because it comes after a vowel at the end of the previous word. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery
Added kasra and shadda to show 'my Lord' (when speaking to God)

steps.m: your Lord (referring to the Lord of all those being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is your (plural) Lord
Starting with the two separate parts: و meaning 'and' + لا meaning 'not'

steps.m: and not (connects to تَتَّبِعُوا۟ - do not follow)
steps.n: The و means 'and', the لا means 'not' - together they make 'and not'. The little marks (َ) above the letters show how to say it correctly. It connects to the word 'follow' to tell us 'do not follow'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at start to make it 'you' (present tense) instead of 'he' (past tense)
Added shadda on ت and changed middle vowel to kasra to show intensive following

steps.m: you (all) follow closely/intensively (as a prohibition with لا)
steps.n: Added وا to show this is addressing multiple people. This form is used with لا to make a prohibition (don't follow)
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it shows the Quran was sent down from your Lord.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/lesser'
Added kasra (ِ) to connect it with the next word
Added هِۦٓ to show this means 'besides Him' referring to Allah

steps.m: to be close to and protect

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it from root to past tense verb
Forms broken plural pattern أَفْعِلَاء to show multiple protectors

steps.m: protectors/guardians (as object of لَا تَتَّبِعُوا - do not follow)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb لَا تَتَّبِعُوا (do not follow)

steps.m: to be small in amount or number

steps.n: Adding vowels to show this describes a quality of being small/few
Added ي to make it an adjective meaning 'little/few'

steps.m: a little (describing how much remembering happens)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how much the remembering happens in تَذَكَّرُونَ (you remember)
Basic particle that connects parts of a sentence, showing what is being referred to

steps.m: that which (refers to أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: The shadda (double letter mark) connects this word smoothly to what comes after it. It points to the Quran that Allah sent down.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The ت at start and doubled ك shows doing something thoroughly to oneself
The ون ending changes it from 'he' to 'you all' and shows multiple people are being addressed
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: And how many (connects to أَهْلَكْنٰهَا - We destroyed them)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it. This word introduces a rhetorical question about the many towns that were destroyed.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects to قَرْيَةٍ (town/city) to show 'from/of a town'.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word connects to the next word. It makes the 'م' sound stronger when reading but doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters

steps.m: a town/village (a place where people settle)
steps.n: The ة changes the general concept into a specific place where the action happens
The ٍ (kasratin) at the end shows this is the object - the town that something is happening to
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start and changing the vowels makes the verb causative - showing someone is causing the destruction
Adding نَا shows 'we' did the action (referring to Allah in this context)

steps.m: we destroyed it (referring to قَرْيَةٍ - town)
steps.n: The هَا refers back to قَرْيَةٍ (town) mentioned earlier in the verse. The alif with bar (ٰ) shows elongation of the previous sound.
Connected the letters with vowels and the middle letter ي changed to ا. This shows something that happened in the past.

steps.m: and then it came suddenly (after أَهْلَكْنٰهَا/We destroyed it)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened right after We destroyed it (أَهْلَكْنٰهَا) in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: and then it came suddenly to it (referring to قَرْيَةٍ/township)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the township (قَرْيَةٍ) mentioned earlier. The آ is just a longer way of writing ا.
Added vowel marks to turn the concept of being mighty into a noun meaning might or power. This word is what came (جَآءَ) to the people in this verse.
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of coming (جَآءَ)

steps.m: our might (as the doer of جَآءَ)
steps.n: Added نَا to show this might belongs to 'us'. The whole word بَأْسُنَا is what came (جَآءَ) to the people.
The ي is written as ٰ (alif with overbar) due to writing convention
The tanween (ًا) changes this into an adverb telling when something happened. This word describes when Allah's punishment came to the people.

This is a conjunction that connects two possible times when punishment came: either at night (بَيٰتًا) or while they were resting at midday (قَآئِلُونَ). It shows these are alternative possibilities, not both happening at once.

steps.m: they (the people of the town being destroyed)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers to a group of people (all male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it refers back to the people of the town (قَرْيَةٍ) that was being destroyed.
Changed from basic root 'to say' into 'it was said' by adding vowels
Changed middle letter to alif and added hamza to show who does the action. This is related to هُمْ (they) in the ayah.
Changed alif to madd (long alif) to show it's a strong doer of the action
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
Added َ (fatha) at end to show this is in the nominative case
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from something before. This word connects to كَانَ (was) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
The weak letter و changes to ا in the middle of the word

steps.m: to call out, to make a claim

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'claim' or 'plea'. The و changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter. This word is what was being made (the object of كَانَ - was) in this ayah.
Adds هُمْ to show possession by them

steps.m: when (connects to جَآءَهُم - came to them)
steps.n: This is a particle that points to a specific time in the past. In this ayah, it connects the time when punishment came to them.
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks
Added هُم at the end to show who received the action. This word connects to بَأْسُنَآ (our punishment) which is what came to them.

steps.m: to be mighty, severe, or cause harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'might' or 'punishment'. This word is what 'came' (جَآءَ) in this ayah.
Added نا at the end to show this might/punishment belongs to 'us'
Extended the alif at the end for emphasis when stopping on this word
Added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the meaning of exception
Added long alif (آ) at the end to add more emphasis to the exception. This is common in the Quran to show importance.

steps.m: that (introduces what they said in قَالُوٓا۟)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects what comes before with what follows. Here it introduces the content of what they said (قَالُوٓا۟).
Added fatha vowels to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas - this is a common change in words with و in the middle.

steps.m: they said (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show a group of people did the action. The ُ before و shows it connects to the word.
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed', making it emphasize what comes after it
Adding نا at the end shows this is about 'we/us', connecting it to the next word كُنَّا (we were)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he was' to command form 'be!' by changing vowels. This is a direct order.
Added emphasis by doubling the last letter
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did this action

steps.m: to be unfair or wrong to someone

steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from a past-tense verb to a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people who are unfair

steps.m: unfair people (predicate of كُنَّا - we were)
steps.n: Changed the alif to an alif with overbar (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to ask or question someone about something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from past to present tense and changed from 'he' to 'we' by adding ن at the start
The ل adds emphasis and certainty to the action
The فَ shows this action happens as a result of something before it, like saying 'so' or 'then'

steps.m: and we will most certainly ask
steps.n: The نّ at the end adds strong emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely' in English. It's like underlining or saying something twice to show you really mean it.
This is a special demonstrative word that refers to multiple people or things. It doesn't come from a root.

Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific, with a shadda on the ل. The ي gets a kasra and we add َ at the end. This word connects to the verb أُرْسِلَ (was sent) in this ayah - it refers to the people who received the message.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes the sending more purposeful or directed
Changed the vowels to show that someone or something received the action instead of doing it
The ى changes to يْ when we add something to the end of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added هِمْ to show we're talking about a specific group - in this case, the people who were sent to (mentioned in أُرْسِلَ)

steps.m: to ask or question someone about something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a complete word
Changed from past to present tense and changed subject from 'he' to 'we'
Added ل for emphasis and to show future tense
The و connects this action to what came before it

steps.m: and we will most certainly ask
steps.n: The نّ at the end adds extra emphasis to show this is absolutely certain to happen

steps.m: to send or deliver a message

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'sent' to 'messenger' by adding مُ at start to show the person receiving the sending
Added the plural ending to show multiple people

steps.m: the messengers (those who were sent with a message)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and refer to the known messengers mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to tell stories or follow tracks

steps.n: The doubled ص shows this telling was done with extra emphasis
Added نَ at start to show 'we' and changed vowel to show present tense
Added لَ to show emphasis and future tense
Added فَ to connect this to what came before

steps.m: and surely we will definitely tell
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end for extra emphasis

steps.m: and surely we will definitely tell
steps.n: The نّ at the end adds even more emphasis to the promise
This is a basic preposition showing something is above or over something else
The ى changes to ي when adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change

steps.m: upon them (relates to فَلَنَقُصَّنَّ - so we will surely relate)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group of males or mixed group). This connects to the verb فَلَنَقُصَّنَّ (so we will surely relate) to show what will be related.
Root meaning 'know' becomes the noun 'knowledge' by adding i-sound (kasra) and stopping sound (sukun)

steps.m: with knowledge (describing how فَلَنَقُصَّنَّ/we will narrate is done)
steps.n: Added ب at beginning to mean 'with' and tanwin at end to show it's indefinite. This explains how the narration will be done - with knowledge.
This is a special word that makes things negative, like saying 'not' or 'never'. It's connected to كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah to say 'we were not'.
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense
The weak letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to damma to compensate
Added shadda (double letter) to make it 'they (feminine) were' instead of 'he was'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
Changed from verb (to hide) to noun (one who hides)
Added ين to make it plural (more than one). The آ is just a spelling variation of ا. This word describes the state of 'we' in كُنَّا (we were).
Adding damma makes this a noun in the nominative case
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: and the weighing/weight (subject of يَوْمَئِذٍ ٱلْحَقُّ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect with previous statement. This word is the subject of the phrase 'is true on that day' in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word answers 'when' something happened

steps.m: on that day (when the weighing of deeds happens)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify this refers to a particular day mentioned in context - the Day of Judgment when deeds will be weighed
Adding the basic vowel mark to help pronounce the word correctly
Added ال (the) and doubled the middle letter to change from verb 'to be true' to noun 'the truth'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. Also changed the alif to wasl form since it's the standard writing.
This is a question word asking about people. In this ayah, it's asking about who will have heavy scales on the Day of Judgment.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what was mentioned before (the weighing being true on that Day)
The vowels َُ show that something gained the quality of being heavy

steps.m: they became heavy (referring to مَوٰزِينُهُۥ - his scales)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show this refers to مَوٰزِينُهُۥ (his scales), which is treated as a feminine plural in Arabic
Added مِ at start to show it's a tool, and added ي and vowels to make the pattern for tools/instruments
Changed the word to show many scales by adding وَا after first letter and changing vowels
Added ُ to show this is doing the action (being heavy) in ثَقُلَتْ (became heavy)
Added هُۥ to show these are his scales. This word is the subject doing the action in ثَقُلَتْ (became heavy)
This is a pointing word used to indicate multiple people or things that are far away (like saying 'those people over there')

steps.m: so those (referring to the successful ones mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers to the people whose good deeds are heavy.
Basic form of the pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed group
Added damma (ُ) on ه to show this is a subject pronoun
Added damma (ُ) on م because it connects to the next word ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (the successful ones)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ (mu) at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'someone who succeeds'
Made plural to show many successful people
The word gets 'the' (ال) added at the start to make it 'the successful ones' - it describes who 'they' (هُمُ) are
This is a question word that can mean 'who' or 'whoever'. Here it's the subject of خَفَّتْ (became light).
The و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it, showing this is another part of a list or sequence
Connected the root letters with fatha and added shadda to show emphasis. This word describes what happened to مَوٰزِينُهُۥ (his scales) in this ayah.

steps.m: they became very light (subject: مَوٰزِينُهُۥ - his scales)
steps.n: Added ت to show this is talking about مَوٰزِينُهُۥ (his scales) which is feminine plural in Arabic
Added مِ at start to make it mean 'tool for weighing' and arranged vowels to show it's a noun for an instrument
Changed pattern to make it plural, showing there are multiple scales
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in خَفَّتْ (became light)
Added هُ meaning 'his' and combined وَا into ٰ. This word is the subject doing the action in خَفَّتْ (became light)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people. Think of it like pointing and saying 'those ones there'
Adding ء at the end makes it specifically for pointing to multiple things or people
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this is a result of what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about whose scales are light
The word gets ئِكَ added at the end to show we're pointing to or addressing specific people, like saying 'those specific ones there'
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make a relative pronoun
Adding ن makes it refer to many people or things
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'ones who'. This word is the subject (doer) of خَسِرُوٓا۟ (they lost) in this ayah.
Adding the proper pronunciation marks and changing ال to ٱلّ with shadda for correct Quranic spelling

steps.m: to lose or suffer a loss

steps.n: Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing someone experienced loss
Added وا to show multiple people did this action
Added optional pronunciation marks (alif of prolongation and small high waw) that don't change the meaning
Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'. This word is what's being lost in the phrase 'they lost themselves'.
Changes from single 'self' to plural 'selves' by adding 'a' at start and changing middle vowels
Added هُم at the end to show these souls belong to them (the people who lost their souls)
This is a connecting word that links to the verb يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong/do injustice), showing what they are doing wrong about

steps.m: because of what (they did to يَظْلِمُونَ)
steps.n: The بِ shows the reason or cause - it explains that they lost themselves because of/due to their wrongdoing with Allah's signs

steps.m: to be or exist in a certain state

steps.n: Basic past tense form - added vowel marks to show 'he' did the action
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong/oppress) in this ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: Root meaning 'show/be clear' became a noun for 'one sign' by adding ة at the end
Adding long alif at start makes the word stronger/more emphatic
Added ات to make it plural
Added نَا meaning 'our' at the end

steps.m: with our signs (object of يَظْلِمُونَ - they wrong)
steps.n: Added بِ meaning 'with' at the beginning. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong/do injustice to)

steps.m: to treat unfairly, to harm, to wrong

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يَ at start, 2) putting sukun on first root letter, 3) putting kasra on middle root letter, and 4) putting damma at end

steps.m: they wrong/treat unfairly (referring to those who lost themselves in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Added ل at the beginning to add emphasis to the certainty
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows the action is done with extra strength or emphasis
Added نا to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we established you firmly (in الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who received the action. This connects to 'in the earth' (في الأرض) later in the verse.
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it connects with الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where Allah established mankind.
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word - fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter makes it a basic noun
Added 'al' at the beginning to make it specific and kasra at the end because it comes after the word 'in' (فِي) in the ayah

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set up

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb. This shows someone did this action in the past.

steps.m: we made/established (connected to مَكَّنَّاكُمْ - we established you)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. This connects to the previous verb مَكَّنَّاكُمْ (we established you) to show these are things Allah did for people.
Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous statement about establishing people on earth, showing this is an additional divine favor
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word is connected to جَعَلْنَا (We made) showing what was made for them.
Added kasra (ِ) to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: in it (feminine, referring to الْأَرْضِ/the earth)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what the preposition applies to. Here it refers back to الْأَرْضِ (the earth) which was mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: to live life, make a living

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show what we use to live, and ة at the end to show one specific way of living
Changed to plural form by replacing the ة with ٰ and adding ِ. This shows we're talking about many different ways of making a living that Allah has provided on earth.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Forms an adjective describing the quality of being small in amount

steps.m: little (describing how much تَشْكُرُونَ you thank)
steps.n: Shows how much the action of thanking happens. Connected to تَشْكُرُونَ (you thank) in this ayah.
Basic particle that connects to other words to show 'what' or 'that which' is being talked about

steps.m: what/that which (connects to تَشْكُرُونَ - you give thanks)
steps.n: Added shadda for emphasis. In this ayah, it connects to the word تَشْكُرُونَ to mean 'how little you give thanks'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The تَ prefix changes it from 'he' to 'you' and shows it's happening now
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the base word that means certainty
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: to create from nothing, to make something new
steps.n: This is about bringing something into existence that wasn't there before
Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added sukun to prepare for adding suffixes
Added نا to show 'we' did the creating

steps.m: we created you all (followed by ثُمَّ صَوَّرْنٰكُمْ - then we formed you)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this was done to 'all of you'. This action is followed by 'then we formed you' showing the sequence of creation.

steps.m: then (connects to صَوَّرْنٰكُمْ - We fashioned you)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time or space between them. Here it connects 'We created you' with 'We fashioned you', showing these were separate steps.

steps.m: to give shape or form to something
steps.n: This is about giving something its form or shape
Added the shadda (double mark) on و to show this was done with extra care and effort, like carefully sculpting something
Added نا to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: we shaped you all (with extra effort/care)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this was done to 'all of you'. The alif (ٰ) above ن is written this way when followed by كُمْ.
This is a sequential particle that shows something happened after something else, with a gap in between
Added fathas to make it into a past tense verb meaning 'he said'
The middle weak letter و changes to make the word easier to say. The damma and sukun pattern is common in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
Added نَا at the end to show that 'we' are doing the action. This is Allah speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'.
Changes from a verb about power into a special being (angel) who has divine power
Changes the word to show many angels instead of just one
Adds ة to complete the plural form
Adds ال to show we're talking about specific angels
Adds لِ at beginning to show direction and ِ at end to connect with following words

steps.m: to bow down with your forehead touching the ground

steps.n: Root letters س-ج-د got vowels (َ) to make the past tense verb 'he prostrated'
Changed from 'he prostrated' to a command meaning 'prostrate!' by adding ٱ at the start and changing the middle vowel to 'u'
Added وا at the end to make it a command to multiple people

steps.m: to be brown/dark-skinned (root meaning that gave rise to the name Adam)

steps.n: Forms proper name from root meaning 'brown/dark-skinned'
Added fatha at end to show Adam is receiving the action

steps.m: to Adam (object of قُلْنَا - we said)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show the command was given to Adam. This word is connected to قُلْنَا (we said) in this ayah.

steps.m: to bow down with your forehead touching the ground
they bowed down with their foreheads touching the ground
steps.n: Added vowels and وا to show many people did this action together

steps.m: so they bowed down with their foreheads touching the ground (in response to قُلْنَا - We said)
steps.n: Added فَ at the start to show this happened because of Allah's command mentioned in قُلْنَا (We said)
The وا ending shows multiple people (they) did the action
Added shadda to emphasize the exclusion. This word introduces Iblis as being different from the angels who prostrated.
Added the long alif at the end to show this is a complete thought that connects to the next word. This is like a pause before introducing who didn't prostrate.
This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root. It may be derived from Greek 'diabolos' meaning 'slanderer' or from Arabic 'ablasa' meaning 'to despair'.

steps.m: Iblis/Satan (as the exception to those who prostrated)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is the object of إِلَّا (except). In the ayah, it means 'except Iblis' - he was the one who didn't prostrate when Allah commanded the angels to prostrate to Adam.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَكُن) from present to past tense and makes it negative. It's like adding 'did not' before a verb in English.
Changed root letters into a noun by adding damma (u) and sukoon (silent mark) vowels
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now and who is doing it

steps.m: he exists/is (after لَمْ meaning 'he was not')
steps.n: The و is dropped because لَمْ makes the verb negative and changes it to past tense - like saying 'was not' instead of 'is'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added shadda to emphasize the م because it follows another word ending in a similar sound
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word السَّاجِدِينَ (those who prostrate)

steps.m: one who bows down in worship
steps.n: Added ا after first letter and ِ on middle letter to show who does the action. This word comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: those who bow down in worship
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people

steps.m: the ones who bow down in worship
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific/definite - now talking about particular worshippers
Basic root ق-و-ل became past tense 'he said' by adding the vowel marks. This verb introduces Allah's question to Iblis.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
This is an interrogative word used to ask about what prevented or stopped someone. In this ayah, Allah is asking what prevented Iblis from prostrating when commanded.

steps.m: he prevented (addressing Allah's question to Iblis)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This is part of Allah questioning Iblis about his disobedience.

steps.m: he prevented you (Allah asking Iblis what prevented him)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who was prevented. This is Allah directly questioning Iblis about what stopped him from prostrating.
Starting with the first part أَنْ which means 'that' and introduces what follows
Adding لَا (not) after أَنْ to create the negative meaning
The نْ combines with ل creating a shadda (ّ). This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he prostrated' to command form 'prostrate!' by putting a stop mark on first letter and u-sound on middle letter
Added تَ at beginning to show second person (you). This word refers to Allah commanding Iblis to prostrate.
This word connects two events in time, showing that one happened when the other happened. Here it connects Allah's command with what happened before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for suffix attachment
Added تُ to show that 'I' did the action
Added كَ to show who was commanded
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word introduces what was said: 'I am better'
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ because و changes to ا in hollow verbs
This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'I'. In this ayah, Iblis (Satan) is using it to refer to himself when explaining why he refused to prostrate, showing arrogance by emphasizing himself.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create comparative form
Added the -un ending (ٌ) to show this word describes 'I am better' - like saying 'I am good' vs just the word 'good' alone
This is a basic particle that shows comparison or origin. In this ayah, it's used for comparison since Iblis is comparing himself to Adam.
Added sukun to help pronunciation when connecting words
Added هُ to show who is being compared to. This connects to Adam who was mentioned earlier in the conversation.
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in ن, causing the ن sound to merge into م

steps.m: to create, make, or form something

steps.n: Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make it a past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' (one male) did this in the past. Also changed the last vowel to sukun (ْ) before adding تَ
Added نِى at the end to show that 'me' is the one being created. This word appears in a conversation where Iblis (Satan) is speaking to Allah, saying 'You created me from fire'
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of. Here it shows that Iblis (Satan) is claiming to be better because he was created from fire.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا when making this noun form
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning

steps.m: of fire (in مِن نَّارٍ - from fire)
steps.n: The tanween kasra shows this word follows مِن (from) in the phrase 'from fire'
Root letters got fatha marks to make 'he created' in past tense
Changed to show you (masculine singular) did the action in the past
Adding هُ shows the object (him) of the verb. This is a complete verb phrase showing who did the action (you) and who received it (him).

steps.m: and you created him (object of comparison with خَيْرٌ/better)
steps.n: Added و meaning 'and' to connect this to the previous statement. This word is being used to compare how Allah created both Iblis and Adam.

steps.m: from (showing what something is made from)
steps.n: This is one of the most common Arabic prepositions. It's a particle word that doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: from (showing نَّارٍ/fire is the material Iblis was created from)
steps.n: In this ayah, مِن connects to نَّارٍ (fire) to show what Iblis was created from when he's comparing himself to Adam.
Adding the kasra vowel mark makes it a proper noun meaning clay

steps.m: clay (used in مِن طِينٍ meaning 'from clay')
steps.n: Added tanween kasratain (ٍ) to show this is indefinite and follows the preposition مِن (from)
Added fathas to make it into a past tense verb meaning 'he said'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word is connected to the next word فَٱهْبِطْ (so descend) as Allah is speaking to Iblis.
Changed the word to a command form by adding sukun at the end and changing vowels

steps.m: so go down! (command to Satan after his refusal to bow)
steps.n: Added فَ at the start to show this command is a result of Satan's disobedience mentioned before. The alif at the start changed to a different form (ٱ) because it came after فَ.
This is a preposition meaning 'from'. The sukun (ْ) is added to show where the word stops.
Added ها which shows this is happening to something feminine (like Paradise/الجنة). This connects to the previous ayah where Allah tells Iblis to get down from Paradise.
This is the base word that means 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's followed by يَكُونُ (is/will be).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. Here it connects to Allah's command to 'go down' (فَٱهْبِطْ) in the previous phrase.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ marks. The و stays because it's part of the root. This word connects to لَكَ (for you) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation

steps.m: for you (masculine, being addressed by Allah)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed to a single male person being spoken to. In this ayah, Allah is addressing Iblis.

This is a particle that introduces what someone should not do or what is not allowed to happen. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَتَكَبَّرَ (be arrogant), showing what Allah forbade Iblis from doing.
The ُ (damma) in the middle shows this is about having a natural quality
Adding shadda (ّ) makes the meaning more intense - from 'being great' to 'making something great'
Adding تَ shows doing the action to oneself - making oneself great = acting proudly

steps.m: that you act proudly (in فِيهَا - in it/there)
steps.n: Adding another تَ changes it to 'you' form. This connects to فِيهَا (in it/there) in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it' referring to something feminine (in this case, referring to Paradise/Heaven which was mentioned before)
Changes to command form by adding vowel marks
Added 'u' sound at start to make the command easier to say - this is how Arabic commands normally begin

steps.m: so get out! (consequence of Allah's statement)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this command is a result of what came before (Allah telling Iblis to go down)
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word

steps.m: indeed you (addressing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end makes this word address someone directly. In this ayah, Shaytan is speaking to Allah.
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or belongs to a group
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الصَّاغِرِينَ (the disgraced ones). This shows that someone is part of that group.
The root letters got vowels added to make a past tense verb meaning 'he became small'. Like adding -ed in English to show something already happened.
Changes from a verb to a noun describing someone with this quality
Changes from one person to many people who are humbled by adding ين at the end
Adding ٱل makes it specific. The shadda (ّ) appears because of merging sounds when ٱل is added.
Added vowel marks to make the past tense form. This word is connected to the next word أَنظِرْنِىٓ (give me time) as it introduces what was said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to an imperative (command) verb by adding أَ at start and changing middle vowel
Added sukun to mark end of command form
Added نِى to show the command is being done to 'me'
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to emphasize the command
This is a preposition that shows direction or endpoint
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this is how it's written in the Quran. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
The ِ at the end shows this word follows the word إِلَىٰ (to/until) in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice (being raised up instead of raising up) and present tense

steps.m: they are raised up (referring to all people on the Day of Resurrection)
steps.n: Added ون to show this happens to many people. This word appears in a verse about the Day of Resurrection when all people will be raised up.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is connected to what comes next (إِنَّكَ - 'indeed you') as Iblis responds to Allah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
This is a special word that makes what comes after it more certain or emphasized, like adding 'definitely' in English
Added كَ at the end to show this is directed at 'you' (one male person). In this ayah, Allah is speaking to Iblis.
This is a helper word that shows something belongs to or comes from something else. Here it shows being part of a group (the respited ones).
The َ is added when this word comes before ال (the)
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to show someone receiving the action of being looked at/waited for
Added ين to show multiple people receiving the action

steps.m: the ones who are given time (those who are granted respite)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at end to show its role in the sentence
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form. This shows Shaytan speaking to Allah after being asked about his disobedience.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Starting with the basic interrogative particle that means 'what'
Adding بِ changes 'what' to 'because of what'

steps.m: so because of what (connects to أَغْوَيْتَنِى)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this is a result of what came before. Here it connects to the next word أَغْوَيْتَنِى (you misled me)
The alif is written with madda (آ) to show it's a long vowel. This is just a spelling variation with no meaning change

steps.m: to go astray, deviate from right path

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to cause someone to go astray'
The تَ shows that 'you' did the action in the past
The نِى at the end shows that 'me' is the one being led astray
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense first person (I) form
Adds لَ at start to emphasize 'definitely/surely'

steps.m: I will most certainly sit/remain (threatening Iblis's words to Allah)
steps.n: Added نَّ for extra emphasis, showing Iblis's defiant oath
This is a particle showing possession or purpose

steps.m: for them (object of لَأَقْعُدَنَّ - I will surely sit)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which shows this is happening to multiple people. This word connects to لَأَقْعُدَنَّ (I will surely sit) in the ayah, showing who is being affected by the sitting.
Added the basic vowel pattern to connect the letters
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb لَأَقْعُدَنَّ (I will surely sit/lurk). The alif is written as a long vowel mark (ٰ) in this word.
Added كَ at the end to show this path belongs to the one being addressed (you). This path is what Shaytan is saying he will lurk upon to mislead people.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُسْتَ at the start to show something is trying to achieve or become this quality
Changed و to ي because it's between two vowels - this is a common Arabic rule
Added ٱلْ at the start to make it specific
Added َ at the end to show this word describes صِرٰطَكَ (your path)
Basic form of this connecting word that shows sequence

steps.m: then (connects to لَءَاتِيَنَّهُم/I will surely come to them)
steps.n: Added dots above and below to make 'then' - shows one thing happens after another, like steps in a plan
Root 'come' changed to 'I come' by adding first person prefix
Added fatha at end to show subjunctive mood
Added لَ (for emphasis) at start and نَّ at end to show very strong promise - 'I definitely will'

steps.m: I will most certainly come to them
steps.n: Added هُم to show who is being come to
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added kasra under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda on م to show it's pronounced strongly because it comes after another م in the ayah (لَءَاتِيَنَّهُم مِّنۢ)
Added a small ن mark (called nun qutni) to show clear pronunciation before ب in بَيْنِ
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make a noun meaning the space that separates things
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by 'their hands' (أَيْدِيهِمْ), forming the phrase 'between their hands' which means 'in front of them'

steps.m: to extend or reach out with the hand

steps.n: Root letters form a special short noun meaning 'hand' - one of the few two-letter nouns in Arabic
Changed from 'hand' to 'hands' by adding hamza at start and making middle letter silent
Added ي at the end to make it feminine plural, showing multiple hands
Added هِمْ to show possession by a group
Basic preposition showing direction or source. Here it works with أَيْدِيهِمْ (their hands) to show direction.

steps.m: and from (direction in relation to أَيْدِيهِمْ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase to the previous direction mentioned in the ayah (مِّنۢ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ - from before them)
Root meaning 'follow' becomes a direction word meaning 'behind' by adding vowel marks
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it
Added هِمْ which means 'them' or 'their' when referring to a group
Basic word showing movement or direction away from something
Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous directions mentioned (from before them and from behind them)

steps.m: to be on the right side, to be blessed

steps.n: Root letters connected with fathas (a-vowels) to make the word for 'right side'
Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'more right' or 'right side', like how we add 'more' in English
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show it's being used after a preposition or as a possessive
Added هِمْ to show possession by a group
Basic preposition showing movement or direction away from something. In this ayah, it's used with أَيْمٰنِهِمْ (their right sides) to show direction.

steps.m: and from (the direction of أَيْمٰنِهِمْ - their right sides)
steps.n: وَ connects this to the previous directions mentioned (from before them and from behind them)

steps.m: to gather or include from all sides

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'gathered' to noun meaning 'left side/direction'
The word for 'left' becomes plural by changing the middle vowel and adding ائل at the end
Added possessive pronoun suffix meaning 'their'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: and not (connects to previous statement and negates تَجِدُ/you will find)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase to what came before it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding ُ at end. The و drops because it's a weak letter.
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning
The vowel pattern َُ shows something naturally grew or increased by itself
Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative, and changed the vowels to show comparison
Added هُمْ at the end to show it refers to 'them', with َ connecting it to the word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to describe someone who does the action (like changing 'thank' to 'thankful person')

steps.m: grateful people (in grammar position of object)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) shows this word is being acted upon by the verb 'you will find'
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is connected to the next word ٱخْرُجْ (get out), showing who gave this command.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a command form (sukun-damma-sukun pattern)

steps.m: Get out! (command from Allah to Shaytan)
steps.n: Added ٱ at the beginning which is required for command forms starting with two consonants. This is Allah commanding Shaytan to leave paradise.
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from, like saying 'The water comes from the sky'
The ها at the end refers back to Paradise (which is feminine in Arabic). In this ayah, Allah is commanding Iblis (Satan) to get out from Paradise.

steps.m: one who is blamed/disgraced (object of قَالَ ٱخْرُجْ - He said 'Get out')
steps.n: Added مَ at start and made passive by adding ء and و to show Shaytan is receiving blame/disgrace when Allah expels him

steps.m: as one blamed and disgraced (describing how Shaytan is expelled)
steps.n: Added ًا (an) at the end to make it describe the state/condition of how Shaytan was expelled - as a disgraced one
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to show who received the action instead of who did it

steps.m: forcefully driven away (describing how اخْرُجْ/get out happens)
steps.n: The shadda on م shows it connects to the word before it. The ًا at the end shows this describes how the action of 'getting out' happens - like saying 'get out being driven away'
This is a combination of لَ (for emphasis) and مَن (meaning who/whoever)
The shadda shows this word is connected to what came before it

steps.m: to follow behind or go after something/someone

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he followed' by adding vowels

steps.m: he followed you (object of لَأَمْلَأَنَّ - I will surely fill)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows who was followed. This word connects to لَأَمْلَأَنَّ (I will surely fill) in the ayah, showing who Allah will fill Hell with.
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere

steps.m: from them (referring to those who follow Shaytan)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being sent away. In this ayah, it refers to those who follow Shaytan's path.
This is the basic concept of filling something up
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes it from 'to fill' to 'to make fill'
Adding لَ makes it into an oath/promise
لَ at start makes it a strong statement
This is the basic form of the word, borrowed from Hebrew/Aramaic, that names the place of punishment in the afterlife. The shadda on ن shows emphasis.

steps.m: Hell (as the object being filled in لَأَمْلَأَنَّ - I will surely fill)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of being filled. It connects to لَأَمْلَأَنَّ (I will surely fill) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is happening to 'you all'. This word is connected to لَأَمْلَأَنَّ (I will surely fill) in this ayah, showing what will be filled from.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to pattern أَفْعَل to mean 'most/all' instead of just 'gather'

steps.m: all together (describing the people who will fill Hell)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this describes multiple people. This word is describing those who will fill Hell (مِنكُمْ) in the phrase 'I will surely fill Hell with all of you together'
Root letters combine to make Adam's name, which comes from word for brown/dark earth
Adds يا (ya) at the start to call out to Adam, like saying 'Hey Adam!'

steps.m: And O Adam! (being called to dwell in Paradise)
steps.n: Adds 'and' (و) at the start to connect this call to Adam with what follows
Changed from root word to a command by adding special marks - like telling someone 'Live here!'
Added hamzat al-wasl (ٱ) at the beginning to help pronunciation since Arabic words can't start with a sukun
This is a basic pronoun used to talk directly to one male person. It's one of the fundamental building blocks of Arabic that doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: you (when talking to one male, subject of اسْكُنْ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word. This word is being addressed by the command اسْكُنْ (dwell/live) in this ayah.
Changes from verb 'to pair' into noun meaning 'a pair'
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action with أنت (you)
Added كَ at the end to show this is your spouse. This word works with أنت (you) as the subjects of اسكن (dwell/live)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to cover or hide from sight

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show it's a past action
The ة changes it from 'covering' to mean a covered/protected place like a garden
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific garden - Paradise itself
Added َ at the end to show this is what Adam and his wife are being told to dwell in
Command form made by dropping the first letter and adding dots above and below

steps.m: both of you eat! (commanding Adam and his wife)
steps.n: The ا at the end shows this command is for exactly two people (Adam and his wife in this ayah)

steps.m: so both of you eat! (following Allah telling them to live in Paradise)
steps.n: The فَ shows this command comes as a result of the previous statement (Allah telling them to live in Paradise)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'wherever' (حَيْثُ) with 'you both wish' (شِئْتُمَا) to mean 'from wherever you both wish'
This word is used to talk about a place or location

steps.m: wherever (in relation to شِئْتُمَا - you both wish)
steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make this word mean 'wherever'. This word works with شِئْتُمَا (you both wish) to say 'wherever you both wish' in the ayah.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to make it mean 'you' (masculine)
Changed تَ to تُمَا to make it mean 'you two'
Added ما to show that exactly two people did the action
Added fatha to make the basic negative command particle. This tells Adam and his wife not to do something.

steps.m: and do not (command to Adam and his wife)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one (فَكُلَا - 'and eat'). This shows these are connected instructions to Adam and his wife.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added ت to make it 'you' instead of 'he'
The ا at the end shows it's talking about two people doing the action
The base word ها is used to point to things. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above the ه to show it's a demonstrative pronoun.
Adding ذِ shows we're pointing to something nearby (like 'this' vs 'that')
Adding هِ at the end makes it feminine, showing we're pointing to something feminine like الشَّجَرَةَ (the tree) in this ayah
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The ة makes it refer to one specific tree
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific tree that is known
The final َ shows this tree is being acted upon - it's what Adam and Eve were told not to approach
Added damma to make basic command form
Added تَ to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ا to show the action is done by two people (Adam and his wife in this case)

steps.m: and then you both become (referring to becoming wrongdoers)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a result of something else (eating from the tree)
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it's made of
When مِنْ comes before a word starting with ال, the ن gets a fatha (َ) instead of sukun (ْ) to make it easier to pronounce

steps.m: to do wrong, to be unfair

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense verb meaning 'he did wrong'
Changes from verb (he did wrong) to noun meaning 'wrongdoer'
Makes the word plural - more than one wrongdoer
Makes the word definite - specifying these particular wrongdoers
Shows this word is the object in the sentence

steps.m: to whisper repeatedly with evil intent
he whispered evil suggestions (subject of لِيُبْدِىَ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to form past tense. This is a special four-letter root that repeats sounds to show repetitive action. This verb connects to لِيُبْدِىَ (in order to show) in the ayah.

steps.m: then he whispered evil suggestions (subject of لِيُبْدِىَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action follows after something else in the story.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added هُ (him) to show who the action is directed towards

steps.m: to/for both of them (referring to Adam and his wife in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ما to change it from singular (him) to dual (both of them). This word connects to وَسْوَسَ (whispered) showing who the whispering was directed to.
Root letters got extra letters to make the word mean 'one who is very rebellious/distant from good'
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the whispering in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he showed) to present tense (he shows) by adding يُ at the start. The middle letter changes from َ (fatha) to ِ (kasra)

steps.m: so that he shows/reveals (purpose of وَسْوَسَ - whispered)
steps.n: The لِ prefix shows purpose - it tells us why something is happening
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for both of them (object of وَسْوَسَ)
steps.n: Added the dual pronoun هُمَا which refers to both Adam and Eve in this context. This word is receiving the action of وَسْوَسَ (whispered).

This is an interrogative particle used here by Satan to question Adam and Eve about why Allah prohibited them from the tree. It connects directly to the verb نَهَىٰكُمَا (prohibited you two) in this ayah.
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it 'he hid' in the past tense

steps.m: it was hidden (from سَوْءٰتِهِمَا - their private parts)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it
Word stays the same since it's already in passive form showing something was hidden
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'

steps.m: from both of them (referring to Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Added the dual pronoun هُمَا which means 'both of them' to show who is being moved away from. In this ayah, it refers to Adam and Eve.
A simple preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects to سَوْءٰتِهِمَا (their private parts) to show what was being hidden from them.
Root letters combined with vowels to make a noun meaning 'something shameful'
Added ة to show one specific instance
Made plural by adding ات
Changed alif to alif maqsurah for writing style
Added هِمَا to show possession by two people
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he said' by adding vowel marks. The middle letter و becomes ا, which is common in hollow verbs.
Adding the conjunction و (and) to connect this clause with the previous one

steps.m: what (referring to the thing that was hidden from them)
steps.n: This is an interrogative particle used here to refer to something specific - the thing that was hidden. In this ayah, it's referring to what was concealed from Adam and Eve.

steps.m: to prevent or stop from doing something

steps.n: Connected the letters with fathas and changed final ي to ى. This word shows what the subject of قَالَ (he said) did.

steps.m: he prevented/stopped you both (referring to Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Added كُمَا at the end to show the action was done to both Adam and Eve together

steps.m: to care for, develop, bring to maturity
one who nurtures and develops to completion
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows this is someone who does this action completely and perfectly

steps.m: the one who nurtures (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the one doing the action of قَالَ (saying)

steps.m: your (both of you) nurturer (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added كُمَا which means 'your' when talking to exactly two people (in this case, Adam and Eve)
This is a word that shows something being separated or hidden from something else. In this ayah, it shows their private parts were hidden away from them.
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ذا to specify something nearby
Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) for pronunciation
Changed from هٰذا (for masculine) to هٰذِهِ (for feminine) by adding هِ at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic noun
Added ة to show this is one specific tree
Added ٱل to specify this particular tree and doubled the ش because it comes after ٱل
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word عَنْ (from) in the ayah
This is the basic form of the exceptive particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before.

The final alif is lengthened to add emphasis to the exception being made. This emphasizes the contrast between what was mentioned before (being forbidden from the tree) and what comes after (the reason given).
This particle introduces a new clause explaining what was prohibited. It connects to the word إِلَّآ (except) to show the reason or purpose.
Changed the root letters to make a present tense verb by adding vowels
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you two exist/become (addressing Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show this is addressing two people (Adam and Eve in this verse)
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowels 'a' to mean 'angel' - a special messenger of God
Added ين to show there are exactly two angels being talked about

steps.m: or (connecting تَكُونَا مَلَكَيْنِ with تَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلْخٰلِدِينَ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects 'becoming angels' with 'becoming immortal' as two possibilities that the devil tempted them with.
This root represents the basic concept of being or existing
Added ت at beginning with fatha to show 'you' are doing the action, and added damma (u) in middle to show it's happening now
Added alif with fatha at the end to show this refers to exactly two people
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The kasra (ِ) under the م shows it's a preposition.
The fatha (َ) is added to connect smoothly with the next word الْخٰلِدِينَ (the eternal ones). This combination means 'from the eternal ones'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he lasted) to a doer (one who lasts) by adding an alif after kha and putting a kasra under lam
Added ين to make it plural - meaning multiple people/things that last forever

steps.m: the ones who last forever (among مِنَ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it follows مِنَ

steps.m: to take an oath or swear

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show mutual action between two parties
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous statement

steps.m: and he swore to both of them
steps.n: Added هُمَا suffix to show this action was directed at two people (Adam and Eve)

steps.m: and he swore to both of them
steps.n: Changed final alif to long alif for writing style, no meaning change
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. The shadda shows this strength.

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to لَكُمَا - you two)
steps.n: The ي at the end means 'I' or 'me', making it a first-person pronoun. This word is connected to the next word لَكُمَا (to you two) in the ayah.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for both of you (addressing Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Added كُمَا which shows this is talking to exactly two people (in this case, Adam and Eve)
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added مِ (from مِن) which means 'from'. The ن will be added in the next step.
Added the final ن with fatha to complete the word مِن (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added ين to make it plural. This word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Added ال (the) at the beginning to make it 'the advisers' instead of just 'advisers'

steps.m: to guide or lower something down

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action was done with extra force or multiple times
Adds 'so/then' to connect this action with what happened before
Changes the word from an action (tricking) into a powerful concept (deception)

steps.m: by/through deception (describing how Satan فَدَلَّىٰهُمَا/brought them down)
steps.n: Adds بِ at the start to show how something was done. Here it shows how Satan brought Adam and Eve down - through deception.
Adds the tanween mark ٍ because it comes after the word بِ. This is like adding 'a/an' in English while also showing it follows بِ.
This is a time particle that shows something happened right after something else, like saying 'as soon as' or 'when' in English

steps.m: and when (connects to ذَاقَا - they both tasted)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this to what happened before. It shows this happened right after they were deceived (mentioned in the previous ayah)

steps.m: to taste or experience something directly

steps.n: Added vowels and changed و to ا because it's in the middle of a past tense verb

steps.m: they both tasted (referring to Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end shows that two people (Adam and Eve) did the action together
Added vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'it grew'
Adding ة at the end turns a word about growing into the name of the thing that grows (a tree)
When 'the' (ال) is added before a word starting with ش, the ش gets doubled with a shadda
Added the definite article ال (written as ٱل) to specify this particular tree

steps.m: the tree (as object of ذَاقَا - they both tasted)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being tasted in the phrase
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters. The weak letter و changes to ا at the end of past tense verbs.
Added ت at the end to show the subject is feminine. In this ayah, this refers to سَوْءٰتُهُمَا (their private parts) which became visible to them.
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to show it connects to what follows
Added هُ to show this is happening to 'him'
Added ما to change it from singular (him) to dual (both of them)
Root letters س-و-أ turned into the noun سَوْء (saw') by adding vowel marks. Changes a general idea of evil into a specific evil thing or act.
The ة makes it refer to one specific shameful thing (in this case, private parts)
The ات makes it plural, and the alif is written as ٰ
The ُ shows this is in the nominative case
The هُ means 'his', showing possession
Changes from 'his' to 'their (both)', showing it belongs to two people
Added vowels to make it past tense. This word is connected to يَخْصِفَانِ (they both cover) showing what they began doing.
Added alif at the end to show Adam and Eve did this action together
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what happened before in the story
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they both cover (referring to Adam and Eve covering themselves with leaves)
steps.n: Added انِ to show two people doing the action together. In this ayah, it refers specifically to Adam and Eve covering themselves with leaves from Paradise.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when adding pronouns after it
Added هِمَا which means 'them both' - used when referring to exactly two people
This is a basic preposition showing that they were covering themselves with/from the leaves of Paradise. It connects the word وَرَقِ (leaves) to the verb يَخْصِفَانِ (they both began to cover).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'leaves'
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used after a preposition
Added basic past tense form with doubled middle letter to show intensity of covering/protecting
Forms a noun for a place that is enclosed and protected, which came to mean 'garden'
Shows this word is connected to another word that comes after it
Makes it refer to a specific garden - in this case Paradise, which Adam and Eve were leaving
Basic past tense verb ending with ى
The small alif (ٰ) helps show correct pronunciation
Added و at start to mean 'and'
Added هُمَا at the end to show the action was done to both of them

steps.m: to nurture, care for, or be master of

steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does this action completely
Adding ُ shows this is the doer in the sentence
Adding هُمَآ shows this refers to both of them (Adam and Eve). This word is calling out to them in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to negative past tense

steps.m: did I not? (addressing رَبُّهُمَآ - their Lord)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a rhetorical question, showing Allah reminding Adam and Eve of His previous warning
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to 'I' form by adding أ at start and dropping the last ى

steps.m: I prevented you both (referring to Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Added كُمَا at the end to show the action was done to both of them (Adam and Eve)

steps.m: away from (connects to أَنْهَكُمَا - forbid you both)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing distance or separation. Here it connects with the verb أَنْهَكُمَا (forbid you both) to show what Allah forbade them from - the tree.
This is the basic feminine demonstrative pronoun used to point to something far away
Added كُمَا at the end to show this is addressing exactly two people. In this ayah, it's referring to Adam and Hawwa (Eve).
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific tree
Added ال to point to a specific tree mentioned in the story. The ش gets doubled (shadda) because ل and ش merge together in pronunciation

steps.m: the tree (object of عَن/about in this ayah)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after عَن (about/concerning) in this ayah
Added أَ prefix to show first person and shortened the word for jussive mood

steps.m: and I say (connected to أَنْهَكُمَا - 'did I not forbid you')
steps.n: Added و to connect this phrase to the previous statement. This word completes the question that started with 'did I not forbid you'
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Adding emphasis with shadda to show importance or certainty
Adding the dual pronoun كما to show this applies to two people (Adam and Eve in this context)
The alif is elongated (آ instead of ا) for emphasis in pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Satan is their clear enemy. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' at the start of a sentence in English.

steps.m: to be rebellious or act defiantly

steps.n: Added ي after ش and vowels to make the word mean 'one who rebels' (like turning 'rebel' into 'rebel-er')
Added ا to make the word stronger - like turning 'rebel' into 'super-rebel'
Added ٱل to point to a specific devil (Satan) rather than any devil
Added َ at the end because this word is affected by إِنَّ which came before it
This is the basic preposition ل that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for you both (addressed by رَبُّهُمَآ - their Lord)
steps.n: Added كُمَا which shows this is addressing exactly two people. In this ayah, Allah is addressing Adam and Eve.
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun pattern for someone who does the action
Added double damma and shadda to show this is a definite, intense form. This word is describing Satan (الشَّيْطٰنَ) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show something that does the action of making things clear
The doubled م (with shadda) emphasizes that something is very clear/obvious
The tanween shows this is describing the enemy (عَدُوٌّ) mentioned in the ayah
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter. This word is connected to رَبَّنَا (our Lord) which comes after it in the ayah.
Adding ا at the end shows that two people (Adam and Eve) are speaking together to their Lord

steps.m: Lord (the one who takes care of everything)
steps.n: The doubled letter ب shows this is someone who does a lot of caring and nurturing

steps.m: Our Lord! (speaking to Allah in the ayah)
steps.n: Adding نَا at the end shows we're talking about our Lord. The ! shows this is a call or plea to Allah, since this word comes in قَالَا (they both said) before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past

steps.m: we wronged (subject of قَالَا - they both said)
steps.n: Added نَآ at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is part of what Adam and Eve said together.

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, or relate to the self/soul

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
This is a broken plural form that changes the internal structure of the word to show multiple
Added نَا to show it belongs to us. This word is the object (thing being acted upon) of ظَلَمْنَا (we wronged) in this ayah.

steps.m: and (connects this condition to the previous statement)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins this condition to their previous statement about wronging themselves

steps.m: and (connects this condition to the previous statement)
steps.n: Added fatha to show how the و is pronounced

steps.m: and if (introducing the condition تَغْفِرْ - You forgive)
steps.n: Added إِن which makes this a conditional statement - it shows that what follows (Allah's forgiveness) is something they're hoping for but isn't certain yet
This is the basic negative particle that changes verbs to past tense. In this ayah, it negates تَغْفِرْ (forgive).
Adding shadda (ّ) on ل adds emphasis to the negation. This emphasizes that the forgiveness has definitely not happened yet.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make a past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to make it a command (like 'you forgive!')

steps.m: you forgive (in the context of 'if you do not forgive' from لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as the doer. The word appears after لَّمْ which makes it mean 'if you do not forgive'
This is a particle that shows who receives or benefits from something. Here it connects to the verb تَغْفِرْ (forgive) to show who is being forgiven.
Added نَا which means 'us' when attached to the end of a word. In this ayah, Adam and Eve are asking Allah to forgive them, so they say 'forgive us'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make the basic past tense verb
Changes to present tense for 'you' by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Adds first person plural object pronoun suffix نا meaning 'us/to us'

steps.m: and you show mercy to us
steps.n: Adds و at beginning to mean 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The ن prefix shows that 'we' are doing the action
Added emphatic ل prefix to indicate strong intention or certainty
Added emphatic نّ suffix to further strengthen the certainty of the action
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what group it belongs to
