Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْخٰسِرِينَ (the losers). This combination means 'from the losers'
Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Adding an alif after the first letter makes this describe someone who does the action
ين makes it plural (more than two). The word comes after مِنَ (from/among) in this ayah
ٱلْ makes it definite (specific losers). The long alif (ٰ) is just a writing variation
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is connected to اهْبِطُوا (go down) as it introduces what was said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he went down' to 'go down!' by adding 'i' at start and changing end - makes it a command
Adding plural masculine 'you' suffix to make command apply to multiple male addressees
Adding a helper alif to make it easier to pronounce at the start of speech
This turns the idea of separating into a word for one of the separated parts
The ُ shows this is the main thing we're talking about in this part of the sentence

steps.m: some of you (as enemies to others)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' or 'your' when talking to a group. In this ayah, Allah is telling some people they will be enemies to others.

steps.m: to separate or divide into parts/portions

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning a portion or part
Added the prefix لِ which shows direction or purpose

steps.m: to/for some/part (referring to others in the phrase 'some of you to others')
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is part of the phrase 'some of you to others' in the ayah

steps.m: to be hostile, to transgress limits

steps.n: Added vowels to make a noun describing someone who does the action
Adding shadda makes the quality stronger and more permanent
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (any enemy). This word is part of what Allah is saying when He commands them to go down.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where Allah has placed mankind.
The root letters أ-ر-ض become the common noun 'earth' when you add the fatha mark
The kasra ending shows this word follows في (in/on) in the ayah

steps.m: the earth (location where people will stay)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to THE earth specifically. The whole phrase في الأرض means 'on the earth' where Allah tells Adam, Eve and Satan to go down and stay.
Added fathas to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
The doubled ر at the end shows this is a place where something happens
The double dammah (ٌ) at the end makes it indefinite, meaning 'a' settlement rather than 'the' settlement
Basic root letters connected with short 'a' vowels (fatha) to form the noun meaning 'provision'
Adding long 'aa' sound makes it a noun meaning something that provides benefit
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it an indefinite noun - meaning 'a provision' rather than 'the provision'
Added و to connect this word to what came before it in the verse
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or endpoint. In this ayah, it connects to the word حِينٍ to mean 'to/until a time'
Added an alif that looks like a small stick (ىٰ) at the end to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Forms a noun meaning 'time' from the root letters
The tanween at the end shows this is indefinite (any time/period). The word follows إِلَىٰ (until/to) in the ayah, showing how long they will stay on earth.

steps.m: to say something with words (not just think it)
he said (introducing what comes next: فِيهَا تَحْيَوْنَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word introduces what Allah said next.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is very common in hollow verbs
This is a preposition that shows something is inside something else
Added the kasra vowel mark to show how the word is pronounced

steps.m: in/within it (referring to الأَرْضِ 'the earth' mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to refer back to 'the earth' which was mentioned before. This connects this word to what's being talked about.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed pattern to show present tense action
Added ون to show multiple people and changed ي to و due to weak letter rules
Adds a connection word to join this phrase to what came before it
Adds the meaning of being inside or within something

steps.m: and in it (referring back to Earth/paradise mentioned earlier)
steps.n: The ها refers back to a feminine noun (like Earth or paradise) mentioned earlier in the conversation
Connected the root letters with basic vowel markings to make it pronounceable
Changed the noun 'death' into a verb 'you die' by adding تَ at start and changing vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people. This verb describes what happens 'in it/there' (فِيهَا) referring to the earth mentioned earlier
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added preposition مِنْ which shows something coming from somewhere

steps.m: and from it/her (referring to the earth mentioned earlier in the verse)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the earth (الأرض) which is feminine in Arabic. This connects to the full meaning of the verse which talks about coming out from the earth.
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he exited'
Changed vowels to make it passive, showing the action is being done to someone
Added تُ at start to show 'you' as subject and present tense, middle letter got quieter (sukun) to match present tense pattern

steps.m: you (all) are made to exit (from it)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show this applies to a group of people

steps.m: to build or create something from parts

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added يٰ (yaa) at the start to turn it from 'children' into 'O children!' - like when you call out to someone
Added the long vowel mark (ى) at the end to show proper pronunciation
Forms the proper name Adam with its distinctive pattern. This name is historically connected to the root meaning since Adam was created from earth.
This is just the name Adam - no change needed
This is a particle that adds emphasis or certainty to the past tense verb أَنزَلْنَا (We sent down) that follows it. It's like saying 'We have definitely sent down' or 'We have indeed sent down'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at the start makes the action causative - instead of something coming down by itself, someone is making it come down. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
Adding نا suffix changes the verb from 'he sent down' to 'we sent down'. This is a common conjugation in the Quran when Allah speaks in the royal 'We'.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ when we add something after it
Added كُمْ at the end to show this affects multiple people being spoken to
Changes from verb 'to cover' into noun 'clothing'
The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of being sent down (We sent down clothing)
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense and who is doing the action. This verb describes how the clothing (لِبَاسًا) covers private parts (سَوْءٰتِكُمْ).

steps.m: to be bad, evil, or harmful

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'badness' by adding vowels
Added ة to specify one instance of something shameful
Changed from singular to plural by adding ات at the end, with the alif written as a small mark above

steps.m: your private parts (object of يُوٰرِى - covers)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show possession by multiple people. This word is what is being covered in the phrase 'covers your private parts'

steps.m: to provide with feathers, adorn, or furnish

steps.n: Adding the kasra vowel helps turn the root into a noun
Added 'and' to connect this with لِبَاسًا (clothing) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: and clothing (object of أَنزَلْنَا - We sent down)
steps.n: The ًا (tanween) ending shows this is a direct object - something that was sent down
Changed the root into a noun meaning the thing used for the action (clothing for wearing)
Added damma to show this is the subject of the phrase 'that is better'
Added و at beginning to connect this with the previous statement about clothing

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels (fatha) - means 'he protected'
Changed from verb to noun by making middle letter silent (sukun)
Added ال (the) at the start to mean 'the'. The word changes to start with ٱلتَّ when ال is added, with a strong T sound (doubled letter).
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show distance
Added لِكَ to specify this is masculine singular and make it more formal. This word points to خَيْرٌ (good) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form comparative adjective pattern
Added double dammah to make the word indefinite and show it's the subject
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for pointing to something nearby. It's one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
The alif is written with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show it's extended when reading
Adding لِ changes the word from pointing to something near ('this') to pointing to something far away ('that')

steps.m: that (points to something far away) (describes لِبَاسُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word. In this ayah, ذٰلِكَ is pointing to لِبَاسُ (the clothing) that was mentioned before.

steps.m: from (connecting ءَايٰتِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'signs' (ءَايٰتِ) with what was mentioned before. It's one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Root letters were changed to make a noun meaning 'sign' - hamza with fatha at start, long alif in middle, and yaa with fatha at end
Added ات to make it plural, showing multiple signs
The word 'signs' got a special mark (ٰ) and an 'i' sound at the end to show it belongs to Allah
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, showing this is a special divine name
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else. In this ayah, it shows these are the signs/verses of Allah.
This is a special word that shows hope or possibility. It's one of the basic words that doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: perhaps they (expressing hope that they will يَذَّكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this hope applies to 'them'. This word connects to يَذَّكَّرُونَ (they remember/take heed) in the ayah, expressing hope that they will remember.
Added vowels to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more active
Changed from 'reminded others' to 'remembers himself' by adding يَ at start and changing ending
The ذ gets stronger (doubled) to show he is remembering within himself
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to construct or build (especially family relationships)

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a proper past tense verb

steps.m: O sons! (addressing the sons of آدَمَ)
steps.n: Added يا at the beginning to make it 'O sons!' (a way to call out to someone). This is calling out to all humans since we are all children of Adam.

steps.m: to be brown/dark-skinned, or to mix/combine with earth

steps.n: The root letters أ-د-م become the name Adam (ءَادَم) by adding vowels and stretching the first sound with a long alif

steps.m: Adam (as the object of يٰبَنِىٓ - O children of)
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end because Adam is part of the phrase 'O children of Adam' - Adam is being talked about, not directly spoken to
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَرَوْنَهُمْ (you see them) into its opposite meaning 'you do not see them'. It's one of the most common ways to make something negative in Arabic.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adds emphasis to the verb meaning 'surely/definitely'
Adds second person masculine plural object pronoun suffix

steps.m: to be rebellious or distant from good

steps.n: Added letters to show this describes someone who has this quality very strongly

steps.m: the one who is very rebellious
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ش because it comes after ال

steps.m: the one who is very rebellious (subject of يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the doer of the action in يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ (let him not tempt you)
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity between things
Adding ما makes the comparison more specific and connects it to the following action. Here it connects to how Satan expelled your parents.
The alif is lengthened to show a pause in reading, but doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he made (someone) exit (referring to الشَّيْطٰنُ making أَبَوَيْكُم exit)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show Satan caused your parents to exit, rather than them exiting on their own
Connected the root letters أ-ب-و with the vowels َ (fatha) to make the word 'father'
Added dual form marker to show exactly two parents
Added suffix to show the parents belong to multiple people being addressed
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word الْجَنَّةِ (the garden/paradise)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show it's a past-tense verb

steps.m: garden (a place with lots of cover)
steps.n: The ة at the end makes it a noun meaning a place with this quality - in this case, a place with lots of cover (like a garden with many trees)
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the garden' instead of just any garden
Added ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense. In this ayah, Satan (الشَّيْطٰنُ) is removing the clothing.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'from'
Added هُ to show this applies to one male person (referring to Adam or Eve's clothing in this ayah)

steps.m: away from both of them (referring to Adam and Eve's clothing)
steps.n: Changed 'him' to 'both of them' by adding ما at the end - this shows we're talking about exactly 2 things
Changes from action to thing by adding ِ at start and ا in middle. This word is what's being removed in the phrase 'removes their clothing'.
Adds َ at the end to show this clothing is being acted upon (being removed)
Adds هُمَا at the end to show the clothing belongs to two people
Changed root letters into past tense 'he saw' by adding the vowels fat-ha (َ)
Changed to present tense causative form - making someone see something means showing it to them
Added لِ to show purpose - this connects to the previous part of the verse showing why something was done
Added َ to show this is in the subjunctive mood since it follows لِ (to/in order to)
Root letters connected with vowels to make a noun meaning 'badness'
Added ة to show one specific instance
Changed from one private part to many by adding ات at the end
Added هِمَآ to show it belongs to both of them (referring to Adam and Eve in this verse)
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Satan sees you.
The هُۥ at the end means 'he' and refers back to Satan (الشيطان) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to see/perceive with eyes or mind
steps.n: This root is about seeing or perceiving things
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start. The أ in the middle drops out because it's between two vowels.
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ى to show it's a long vowel
Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is happening to 'you all'
This is a pronoun that means 'he'. In this ayah, it refers back to Satan (الشَّيْطٰنُ) who was mentioned earlier.
This root relates to facing or accepting something
Adding ي makes it into a noun meaning a group or category
The word gets a 'u' ending to show it's doing the action (nominative case)

steps.m: his tribe (subject with هُوَ of يَرَىٰكُمْ)
steps.n: The هُۥ shows possession - 'his tribe'. This word joins with هُوَ (he) as subjects of the verb يَرَىٰكُمْ (sees you)
The وَ at the start means 'and'

steps.m: from (shows where they see them from)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it shows the place or direction from which they can't see the devils.
This word is used to talk about a place or location

steps.m: from where (location in relation to يَرَىٰكُمْ - he sees you)
steps.n: The root letters ح-ي-ث became the word حَيْثُ by adding vowel marks. It means 'from where' and shows the location/place where something happens. In this ayah, it tells us the place where Shaytan sees humans from.
This is a negative particle that makes something negative. Here it negates the verb يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ (let him tempt you), changing the meaning to 'do not let him tempt you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with ت prefix. The weak letters cause the word to shorten.
Added و and ن to show multiple people are doing the seeing
Added هُمْ to show who is being seen (them)
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added نا as a suffix to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the verb جَعَلْنَا (we made) in the next part of the ayah.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added sukun to prepare for adding 'we' at the end

steps.m: we made/we put (doer of the action)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is the main verb telling what happened.
Forms the plural noun 'devils/satans'
Root meaning 'rebel' becomes a noun meaning 'one who rebels' (like Satan)
Changes to plural form to show many devils/satans
Makes it specific by adding 'the'
Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلْنَا (We made/appointed)
This root carries the basic meaning of being close and supporting/protecting
Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to be close to/protect'
Changes the word to plural form using the pattern أفعلاء which is used for important roles or qualities
Adds the accusative case ending to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is the basic relative pronoun that points to someone or something specific
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people (masculine plural)

steps.m: for those who (who do not believe)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this word is connected to something else, showing purpose or direction. In this ayah, it connects to 'do not believe' (لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَرَوْنَهُمْ (you see them) into 'you do not see them'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.
This root carries the basic meaning of trust, belief, and security
Adding the vowels makes it a complete past tense verb about feeling secure
Added an alif to change from 'feeling secure' to 'actively having faith/believing in something'
Added يُ at the start to show 'he' doing the action in present time. The first letter changed shape (from ا to ؤ) to make it easier to say
Added ونَ to show that multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: when (introduces the condition that is followed by فَعَلُوا۟ - they did)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition. In this ayah, it sets up the condition 'when they commit' that is followed by their response

steps.m: and when (connects this condition to previous statements)
steps.n: The و connects this conditional statement to what came before it, showing this is another example being given
Changed the last vowel to damma to prepare for adding the plural ending
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The small circle (۟) shows this is connected to what comes next in قَالُوا۟ (they said).

steps.m: to go beyond proper limits, be immoral

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show who/what does the action
Added ة to make it refer to a specific instance or act of immorality

steps.m: an immoral act/deed (object of فَعَلُوا - they did)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this particular word.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Common change in hollow verbs where و becomes ا
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action
Adding vowel marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from singular 'found' to plural 'we found'. This word is connected to قَالُوا (they said) - when they did something wrong, we found our fathers doing it.
This is a preposition that comes from the root meaning of being high or elevated
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting to the next part
Added هَآ at the end to show this is happening upon/to something feminine (referring back to the shameful deed mentioned earlier)
Changed from a root meaning 'to father/nurture' into the noun 'father'
Changed to plural form by lengthening first vowel and adding اء ending
Changed alif to alif madd for proper pronunciation
Added نَا at the end to show possession. This word is the object (thing found) of وَجَدْنَا (we found) in this ayah.
This is the unique name of God in Islam. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is a proper noun.
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل
Changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects with previous word
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in أَمَرَنَا (He commanded us)

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of أَمَرَنَا)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before

steps.m: to command or direct someone to do something
he commanded (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fathas to show this happened in the past. This word is part of what they said in the ayah.

steps.m: he commanded us (object of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show who was commanded (us). The word shows what they claimed in their statement.
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected

steps.m: with it/her (referring to فٰحِشَةً - shameful deed)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the shameful deed (فٰحِشَةً) mentioned earlier in the verse
The root letters change to make a command - the middle و drops out and we add a dot on top (ُ) and a circle (ْ) to make it mean 'say!'
This is a basic word that adds emphasis and connects to what comes next. It's like saying 'Listen up! This next part is important!'
Added the vowel mark ِ (kasra) under إ and doubled the ن with ّ (shadda) to show how it's pronounced
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: The God, Allah (the one true deity)
steps.n: Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال (the). The two ل letters combine into one stronger ل with a shadda.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being spoken about)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being spoken about in this sentence. Also changed the initial alif to a wasl form since it connects to the previous word.
This is a negative particle with no root. It negates what comes after it.
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation. This particle negates the verb يَأْمُرُ (commands/orders) that comes after it, making it mean 'does not command'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to ُ, and adding ُ at end. This verb is negated by لا in the ayah, showing 'he does not command'

steps.m: to act or speak in a shamefully immoral way

steps.n: Changes from verb (to act shamefully) to noun (a shameful act)
Added اء at the end to make it into an abstract concept
Added ال to make it specific
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'with' and ِ at the end because it follows the word يَأْمُرُ (commands)
Connected the root letters and added damma to show basic verb form. The middle weak letter و stays because it's between two different vowels.
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' are doing the action in present time
Added ُونَ suffix to show multiple people are doing the action
Added أَ prefix to turn the statement into a question. This word appears in an ayah asking if people say things about Allah that they don't know.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱللَّهِ (Allah) to show people are saying things about Allah.
Changed to the proper name for Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name.
Added ِ at the end to show this describes what you're saying about (connected to the word عَلَى meaning 'about/concerning'). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to hamzat wasl.
This is an interrogative pronoun (ism istifham) that has no root since it is a particle/tool word

steps.m: what (asking about what they do not know)
steps.n: Added the fatha mark to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it's asking about what they don't know regarding Allah's commands.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَأْمُرُ (he commands) negative, changing it to 'he does not command'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding damma at end shows this is a normal present tense verb

steps.m: you (all) know (addressing multiple people in أَتَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: This word is connected to أَتَقُولُونَ (do you say?) in the ayah, showing that Allah is addressing multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, and 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.

steps.m: he commanded (subject of قُلْ - he said)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word comes after قُلْ (he said) in the ayah, showing what was said.

steps.m: to nurture, sustain, develop, care for
lord, master (one who nurtures and sustains)
steps.n: The doubled letter shows intensity, making it mean 'one who greatly nurtures/sustains'

steps.m: lord (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word will be connected to something that follows it

steps.m: my Lord (the one speaking to Allah)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord. In this ayah, this is being said by the Prophet ﷺ to Allah.
Root meaning 'being fair' becomes a noun meaning the idea of fairness/justice
Adds 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' justice instead of just justice

Added بِ at the beginning to show how the command was given, and kasra at the end because it follows بِ. This word explains how رَبِّى (my Lord) gave the command in the phrase أَمَرَ رَبِّى (my Lord commanded).

steps.m: to stand upright or establish something

steps.n: Basic root letters connected with vowels to make past tense 'he' form
Added أ at beginning to show causing something to happen. The middle و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
Changed from 'he established' to 'you establish!' by changing vowels. The middle alif (ا) becomes a yaa (ي) in this form.
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Added وا at end to make it plural - addressing multiple people
Changed from action to the thing itself - the face that can be turned
Changes from singular 'face' to plural 'faces' by adding و and changing vowels to make وُجُوه
Added كُمْ to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed. This word is the object (thing being affected) by the command أَقِيمُوا (establish/set up) in this ayah.
Adding kasra changes this from an action into a word showing location
Adding fatha at the end shows this word connects to مَسْجِدٍ (mosque) in the ayah
The doubled ل with damma makes it mean 'all' or 'every', showing complete inclusion
The kasra shows this word is connected to the word مَسْجِدٍ (mosque) that comes after it, making it mean 'every mosque'

steps.m: to bow down with forehead touching ground

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens, like how 'play' becomes 'playground'
Added ٍ at the end to show we're talking about any mosque in general, because it comes after the word كُلِّ (every)
Command form telling one person to do something. The last letter و is dropped to make it shorter and easier to say.
Adding ٱ at start helps pronounce the word since it starts with a silent letter
Adding و and ا at the end makes it plural command
Added هُ meaning 'him' as the object being called upon
Added وَ (and) to connect this command with previous text
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Shows who does the action of making something pure/sincere

steps.m: ones who make [it] pure/sincere (describing وُجُوهَكُمْ - your faces)
steps.n: The ين ending changes this from 'one who makes pure' to 'ones who make pure' (plural). It's describing how they should make their worship sincere, connecting to the command 'establish' in the verse.
Added هُ (him) to لَ (for/to) to show who the action is directed towards. This word connects to مُخْلِصِينَ (being sincere) to show being sincere to Him.

steps.m: to follow a path/way, to submit to judgment

steps.n: Adding the kasra changes it from a verb (to follow) into a noun (religion/way)
Adding definite article to make the noun definite
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of مُخْلِصِينَ (making sincere) in the ayah
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity
Adding مَا makes the comparison more general and connects it to the following verb تَعُودُونَ (you will return). The full meaning is 'just as He originated you, so will you return.'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb meaning 'he began'

steps.m: he began you all (object of بَدَأَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who was begun/created. This word is connected to تَعُودُونَ (you return) in the next part of the ayah, showing how Allah began your creation and you will return to Him.
Added basic past tense vowels. The middle و changes to ا in past tense because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter).
The ت at beginning shows 'you' and present tense. The middle vowel changes to ُ in present tense.

steps.m: you (all) will return (as He began you)
steps.n: ونَ shows plural 'you all'. This word relates to كَمَا بَدَأَكُمْ (as He began you) in the ayah, showing that people will return to Allah just as He first created them.

steps.m: to split or divide into distinct parts
a distinct group (that has been split off from others)
steps.n: When you add ي between the second and third letters, it turns the word from 'to split' into 'a split-off group'

steps.m: a distinct group (object of هَدَىٰ - He guided)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this group is being acted upon by the verb هَدَىٰ (guided) in this ayah

steps.m: to guide to the right path

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to show past tense. This word relates to حَقَّ (became due/necessary) in the ayah.
Changed the final ي to ى because this is a verb ending with a weak letter
The small alif (ٰ) is added above the ى to show it should be pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Forms a noun meaning 'a distinct group' - those who are separated or distinguished from others. This word is being acted upon by هَدَىٰ (guided) in this ayah.
The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of guidance (هَدَىٰ guided). It's like saying 'He guided a group.'
Adds 'and' to connect this with what came before

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters together and make it into a past-tense verb
Combined the repeated ق into one letter with shadda, making the meaning stronger. This word connects to عَلَيْهِمُ (upon them) to show something became necessary for them.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is affecting or placed on top of something else. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting to pronouns. This is a common pattern with Arabic prepositions.

steps.m: upon them (the group who deserved misguidance)
steps.n: Adds 'them' (هِمُ) to show who is being affected. In this ayah, it refers to the group that deserved to go astray, connecting to الضَّلٰلَةُ (misguidance) that comes right after this word.

steps.m: he wandered off the right path
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Forms abstract noun meaning 'the act/state of going astray'

steps.m: a specific instance of going astray
steps.n: Adds ة at the end to show one specific case of being lost/misguided (like saying 'a misguidance' instead of general 'misguidance')

steps.m: the specific instance of going astray
steps.n: Adds 'the' (ال) at the start, which doubles the first letter ض because it's a special letter that combines with ال
Adding damma shows this word is doing the action in حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ (became deserved upon them)
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it
Adds attached pronoun 'they/them' as a suffix. The ُ (damma) is added because this word comes before another word in the ayah (ٱتَّخَذُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The ت combines with first letter to show doing something with effort
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action

steps.m: to rebel, to be distant from good
one who rebels/is distant from good
steps.n: Added ي (ya) after first letter to make it mean 'one who rebels' instead of just 'to rebel'
Added ا after second root letter to intensify meaning
Changed the middle of the word and added ين at the end to make it mean 'many devils' instead of just one

steps.m: the devils, the satans (plural, object of اتَّخَذُوا)
steps.n: Added ال with shadda on following letter to make the word definite. This word is what is being taken as protectors in the phrase 'they took the devils as protectors.'

steps.m: to be close to and protect/support
one who is close and protects/supports
steps.n: Changed from a basic verb (to protect) into a noun meaning 'protector/guardian' by adding vowels and doubling the last letter
Changed from singular to plural by adding أ at start and اء at end, like changing 'friend' to 'friends'
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of 'they took' (اتَّخَذُوا) in the verse. Also lengthened the alif for pronunciation.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, مِن connects 'the devils' (ٱلشَّيٰطِينَ) with 'besides' (دُونِ) to mean 'from besides'
Adding damma (ُ) changes it to a preposition meaning 'besides' or 'without'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, showing possession
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One True God
Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection. This word is connected to دُونِ (besides/other than) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening in the present/future
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added وَ at start to connect this with the previous statement
This is a particle that connects ideas and emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: that they (subject of يَحْسَبُونَ - they think)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who is being talked about. This connects to the verb يَحْسَبُونَ (they think) in the ayah, showing who is doing the thinking.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb. The ى replaces ي at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added اِ at start and ت in middle to show someone following guidance themselves
Changed from a past action (he was guided) to describing someone who is guided - like changing from 'ran' to 'runner'
Changed to plural by adding ون at the end. The shadda (double م) is just because it comes after ال, not for emphasis
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
These are two different words - 'banaa' means 'he built' while 'bani' means 'sons/children'. They just look similar but aren't related.

steps.m: O sons/children! (addressing the children of آدَمَ)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to make it a call or address. This word is followed by آدَمَ to mean 'O children of Adam!'
Changed hamza to ء and added long alif to make it a proper name. This is a special formation for this important proper name.
Added fatha at the end because Adam is the object being referred to (like saying 'of Adam')
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to basic command form by dropping first letter and changing vowels
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added kasra under first letter to make it a noun
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of decoration
Changed the ending to connect it with the next word (like saying 'decoration of' instead of just 'decoration')

steps.m: your (all of you) decoration (object of خُذُوا - take)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show it belongs to multiple people being spoken to. This word is what is being 'taken' in the command 'take' (خُذُوا) in this verse.
Connected the root letters to form the base word
Added vowel marks to change it from a concept into a preposition showing location or presence. In this ayah, it shows the location where people should take their adornment - at/near the masjid.
The doubled ل with damma makes it mean 'every' or 'all'. This word describes مَسْجِدٍ (mosque) in this ayah, meaning 'every mosque'.
The kasra ending shows this word is connected to مَسْجِدٍ (mosque), making it mean 'of every mosque' or 'at every mosque'

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Adding مَ at the beginning and ِ in the middle turns this into a noun meaning 'the place where people prostrate'
Adding tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is 'a' mosque (not 'the' mosque) and shows it belongs to the word كُلِّ (every/all) that comes before it
Changed from basic verb 'to eat' into a command by dropping first letter and adding vowel marks - now means 'eat!'
Added وا to show we're talking to multiple people (like saying 'you all')

steps.m: and eat! (command to all of you) (connected to وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ - and drink)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the next command to drink. This shows these actions go together.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding alif at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show we're talking to multiple people

steps.m: and all of you drink! (connected to وَكُلُوا۟ - and all of you eat)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the previous command to eat
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb تُسْرِفُوٓا۟ (be excessive) into its opposite meaning - 'do not be excessive'
The وَ at the start connects this command with the previous ones (eat and drink), making it part of the same set of instructions

steps.m: to go beyond proper bounds or waste

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'you' instead of 'he' in present tense

steps.m: you (all) go beyond bounds (as a prohibition with لا)
steps.n: Added plural ending وا to show multiple people are being addressed. This word comes after لا (do not) to form a prohibition.
This is an emphasis particle that adds certainty to what comes after it

steps.m: indeed He (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added هُ which refers to Allah in this context, showing who the statement is about
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تُسْرِفُوٓا۟ (be excessive/wasteful) in this ayah. It tells us not to do something.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter is doubled with shadda to show intensity
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at the start and changing the middle vowel to kasra. This word is negated by لا in the ayah.

steps.m: to go beyond proper bounds, waste
one who wastes/goes beyond bounds (object of يُحِبُّ)
those who waste/go beyond bounds (plural, object of يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: Makes it refer to multiple people who do this action. Still the object of what Allah does not love (لا يُحِبُّ).

steps.m: the ones who waste/go beyond bounds (plural, object of يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it refer to these specific people as a known group, not just any wasteful people. This is what Allah says He does not love in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.

steps.m: who (asking about who forbade in حَرَّمَ)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people. In this ayah, it's asking about who did the forbidding mentioned in the next word حَرَّمَ (forbade).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he made completely forbidden (object: زِينَةَ - adornment)
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more absolute. In this ayah, Allah is asking who has forbidden the adornments (زِينَةَ) that He created.
Added the kasra (ِ) under the first letter to change from an action to a thing
Adds ة at the end to make a noun that means 'a specific beautiful thing' instead of just 'beauty' in general
The word 'beautiful things' is receiving the action of being forbidden (by حَرَّمَ). The small 'a' sound at the end (ةَ) shows this.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on lam for emphasis. The alif at the beginning changed to a special connecting form (hamzat wasl)
This is the basic form of the feminine relative pronoun, used to connect descriptions to feminine nouns (like saying 'the book which/that' in English)
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ت with shadda to make it definite and stronger. This helps connect it clearly to what comes before it.

steps.m: the one which/that (feminine) (describes زِينَةَ ٱللَّهِ - the adornment of Allah)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and sukun on ر to show making something else do the action
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'one who serves/worships'
Changes the singular 'servant' into a plural form meaning 'many servants'
Added لِ at beginning to show these servants are receiving something, and ِ at end to show connection to next word

steps.m: for His servants/worshippers (referring to Allah's servants)
steps.n: Added هِۦ at the end to show these are specifically Allah's servants. The ۦ mark helps show proper pronunciation.
This root carries the basic meaning of goodness and purity
Added shadda to make the quality stronger, like saying 'very good'
Added ات to make it plural feminine, meaning multiple good things

steps.m: the good things, the pure things
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific - these particular good things

steps.m: and the good things (object of حَرَّمَ - forbidden)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. This word is what is being forbidden in the question 'Who has forbidden...?'
This is the basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلرِّزْقِ (the provision). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when speaking.
Changed from action to thing by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: the provision (something given by Allah to live on)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at start to make it specific, and changed the ending mark to match 'from' (مِنَ) before it
Changed root letters into command form by adding damma (u) and sukun (silent mark) - tells someone to say/speak
The weak letter و is dropped in command form. This word appears at the start of many verses where Allah commands Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say something.
This is a pronoun used for feminine things or people. In this ayah, it refers back to the blessings/good things (الطَّيِّبٰتِ) mentioned before and connects to خَالِصَةً (exclusively) after it.
This is the basic relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people (masculine or mixed gender)
Adding لِ at the beginning shows direction or purpose. The ل combines with ال, making the ل sound stronger (shown by ّ)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added alif after hamza to make the meaning stronger and more specific to religious faith
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to the word الْحَيَوٰةِ (life) to mean 'in life'.
Root letters ح-ي-ي changed to حَيَوَ by adding و in the middle to make the word mean 'life' instead of 'to live'
Changed the root word to mean 'a life' by adding ة at the end and putting ٰ (long alif) over the و
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it definite. Note the special form of alif (ٱ) used
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Root meaning 'near' became a noun meaning 'worldly life' - the life that is closer to us now (compared to afterlife)
Added 'the' to make it specific. The ل doubles (becomes دّ) because د is a sun letter that combines with ل
Changes from verb 'to be pure' to noun meaning 'one who is pure'
Added ة to make it feminine since it describes something feminine in the ayah
Added tanween fatha to show this describes how something is done
Connected the letters with fatha-sukoon pattern to make the basic noun 'day'
Added fatha at the end because this word is connected to the next word (القيامة), making it 'Day of Resurrection'
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'proper state'
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of rising - in this case, the final resurrection on Judgment Day
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific, well-known resurrection on Judgment Day
The kasra at the end shows this word follows 'Day' (يَوْمَ) in the ayah, meaning 'Day of the Resurrection'
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this case.
Adding لِ changes the word from 'this' to 'that', making it point to something farther away
Adding كَ makes it mean 'that specific thing' instead of just 'that'

Adding كَ at the beginning changes the meaning to 'like that' or 'similarly'. This word connects to نُفَصِّلُ (We explain in detail) in the next part of the ayah, showing how the explanation is being done.

steps.m: to separate or divide into parts
to explain in detail, to elaborate
steps.n: Adding the shadda (ّ) on the middle letter shows we're doing something thoroughly and repeatedly - like taking something and dividing it into many clear parts

steps.m: we explain in detail (subject of this part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added نُ at the start to show 'we' are doing the action. The ُ at the end shows this is happening now or will happen. This word is the main verb that tells what 'we' are doing with الْءَايٰتِ (the signs/verses).

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: Adding ة turns this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple signs/verses

steps.m: the signs, the verses (object of نُفَصِّلُ)
steps.n: Added definite article to specify these particular signs. This word is what is being explained in detail by the verb نُفَصِّلُ (we explain in detail).
The word 'signs/verses' gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's receiving the action of the verb 'we explain'

steps.m: people who stand together (as a group)
steps.n: The root meaning of standing firmly evolved to mean a group that stands together as one unit
Added لِ to show 'for/to' and tanween to show indefiniteness. This word connects to يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in the ayah, describing which people are being referred to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

This is a command to one person to speak. The middle weak letter و is dropped, and the vowels change to make it a command form. This word starts verse 7:33 where Allah commands Prophet Muhammad to speak.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more certain or definite
Adding مَا changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, meaning 'only' or 'just'. This word introduces a list of things that Allah has specifically forbidden.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding a shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic. This word is what Allah commanded the Prophet to say (قُلْ means 'say!')
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this action perfectly and completely
Added ا after first letter to show something characterized by this quality. This forms the base for describing shameful acts.
Added ة to make the word feminine and specify a particular instance of shameful behavior
Changed to plural form by adding و after first letter, showing multiple shameful acts. This is a broken plural form common for words describing negative behaviors.

steps.m: the shameful acts (object of حَرَّمَ - what Allah has forbidden)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify these particular shameful acts and َ at the end because it's the object of حَرَّمَ (has forbidden). The long alif (ٰ) is just a writing variation.
In this ayah, مَا is used to show what Allah has forbidden, connecting to the verb حَرَّمَ (has forbidden). It's like saying 'Say: My Lord has forbidden what...'
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what happened to مَا (what/that which) in this ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' with sukun added for pronunciation

steps.m: from it/her (referring to الْفَوٰحِشَ - the shameful deeds)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to الْفَوٰحِشَ (the shameful deeds) mentioned earlier in the ayah, showing what the 'from' applies to
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb ظَهَرَ (appeared) in this ayah, showing what appeared
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: it was hidden (contrasts with ظَهَرَ - appeared/was visible)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word contrasts with ظَهَرَ (was visible) in this ayah to show things that are hidden versus things that are visible.

steps.m: to do something harmful or wrong

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'doing wrong' into a noun meaning 'a sin' by adding إِ at the start
Added ٱلْ to point to specific sins that were mentioned before

steps.m: and the sin, and the wrongdoing
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this with the previous bad things Allah has forbidden

steps.m: and the sin (object of حَرَّمَ - He forbade)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) to show this word is the object/thing being forbidden
Changed from action to noun form. This word is something being forbidden by حَرَّمَ (he forbade).
Added 'and' at start and made it specific with 'the'
Added fatHa to show this is something being acted upon (forbidden) by حَرَّمَ (he forbade)

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'other than'. This word modifies الحَقِّ (the truth) in this ayah.
Added the preposition بِ to show how something is done
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by the word الحَقِّ (the truth)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter turns the verb into a noun meaning 'truth'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific truth rather than truth in general
The kasra ending shows this word belongs to or is connected to غَيْرِ (without) in the phrase بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ (without the truth)
Compound particle made of و (and) + أن (that)

steps.m: to make partners or equals with something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تُ prefix
The damma at the end shows this is a normal statement
Added وا to show multiple people are being addressed. This verb follows أَن (that) in the ayah, which is why it ends in وا instead of ون.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity or god
Added shadda on ل to emphasize this is the proper name for The One True God, not just any deity
Added fatha on the shadda to help with pronunciation
Added بِ (bi) at the beginning to mean 'by/with', and a kasra ( ِ ) at the end to connect it to the sentence. The ٱ is just a helper letter that comes with ال.

steps.m: what (refers to things that were not sent down with authority from Allah)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it refers to the things that Allah did not send down authority for. It helps connect this concept to the rest of the verse.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يُنَزِّلْ) from 'send down' to 'did not send down'. It also changes the verb's tense from present to past.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repeated
Adding يُ at the start and changing vowels makes it mean 'he sends down' (present) instead of 'he sent down' (past)
Adding sukoon at the end because it comes after لَمْ (not) in the ayah
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or what is used

steps.m: with it (referring to authority/permission mentioned after this word as سُلْطٰنًا)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'it' to show what the preposition connects to. In this ayah, it refers forward to the word سُلْطٰنًا (authority/permission) that comes right after.

steps.m: to have authority or power over something

steps.n: Changed the word from an action into a noun showing strong authority. The long alif (ٰ) makes the authority seem more complete or absolute.

steps.m: any authority/proof (that Allah did not send down)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يُنَزِّلْ (send down) in the ayah. The full meaning is that Allah did not send down this authority.
Basic particle that introduces a new clause. Here it connects to the verb تُشْرِكُوا (you associate partners) to show what was forbidden.
Adds و at the beginning to connect this with the previous things that were forbidden in the verse
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Changed the root 'say' into a command by adding a damma (u sound) on the qaf
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Primary preposition - not derived from a root. In this ayah, it shows what the saying is against/about, connecting تَقُولُوا۟ (you say) with اللَّهِ (Allah).
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, transforming it from 'a deity' to the proper name 'Allah'
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the preposition بِ (with/by). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: what (referring to what follows in لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ)
steps.n: This is a particle that means 'what' here because it's introducing something Allah has not sent down authority for
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) negative, changing it to 'you do not know'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The ت at the start shows this is happening now and the person doing it is 'you'
Adding ون at the end makes it plural - now talking to many people instead of one
The doubled ل shows this means 'every single one' with no exceptions. This word is describing أُمَّةٍ (nation/community) in this ayah.
The لِ at the beginning shows this means 'for' or 'belonging to'
Adding conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous statement
Added damma and shadda to create the base noun meaning 'mother' or 'source', which is the foundation for 'community'
Adding ة changes the word from 'mother/source' to mean a whole community or nation of people

steps.m: a community (belonging to وَلِكُلِّ - and for every)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this community belongs to the phrase 'and for every' (وَلِكُلِّ)
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun meaning the result of setting a time limit
Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject doing the action in جَآءَ (came)

steps.m: when (introduces the time condition for جَآءَ)
steps.n: This is a time word that sets up a condition - it connects with جَآءَ (came) to show 'when it came'

steps.m: then when (introduces the time condition for جَآءَ)
steps.n: The فَ shows this introduces a result or consequence - it connects what happens after 'when' with what came before
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb form
Adding long alif to emphasize the action of coming
Added damma at the end to show this is the main subject being talked about
Added هُمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This word is the subject of جَآءَ (came) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ (they delay) into 'they do not delay'. It's one of the basic negative words in Arabic that tells us something doesn't happen.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced with an 'a' sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show someone is trying to do something

steps.m: they seek to delay (related to أَجَلُهُمْ/their appointed time)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This word relates to their appointed time (أَجَلُهُمْ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels and turning و into ا in the middle
Adding ة makes it into a noun meaning one instance or unit of time

steps.m: an hour/moment (object of يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ - they delay)
steps.n: The tanween (ً) shows this is indefinite and is the object of the verb 'they delay' - meaning they cannot delay even for a moment
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (they advance) in this ayah
The وَ connects this negative particle to the previous negative phrase لَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ (they do not delay), showing that neither delaying nor advancing is possible
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or trying to do the action

steps.m: they seek to go forward/ahead (negated by لا)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word is negated by لا in the verse, meaning they cannot go ahead of their appointed time.
Added vowel marks to make the word mean 'children' - this form is often used for groups that come from something
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we're calling out to the children. This word is connected to ءَادَمَ (Adam) in this ayah, making it mean 'O children of Adam'
The root letters were arranged into the name Adam (the first human) by adding a long 'aa' sound
Added fatha at the end to show Adam is being addressed in 'O Children of Adam'
Combined the conditional particle إِنْ (if) with the generalizing particle مَا (what/ever) to create a more general conditional meaning
The ن from إِنْ combines with the م from مَا, creating a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark). This is a common change in Arabic when ن meets certain letters.
Added يَ at start to show 'he' in present tense, changed middle letter to sukun, and last letter to kasra
Added نَّ to add emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'without doubt'

steps.m: he will definitely come to you all (referring to رُسُلٌ - messengers)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being addressed - 'you all'. This word connects to رُسُلٌ (messengers) in the ayah.

steps.m: to send or be sent as a messenger

steps.n: The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Changes the vowel pattern to show multiple messengers instead of just one
Adds double damma to show this is the subject doing the action of telling/narrating (يَقُصُّونَ) in this ayah
This is a combination of مِن (from) and كُمْ (you all)

steps.m: to tell in detail, narrate a story step by step

steps.n: Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it 'he told' in past tense
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to ُ, and doubled the middle letter to show detailed telling

steps.m: they tell in detail (about ءَايٰتِى/My signs)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people (3 or more males) are doing the action. In this ayah, they are telling about Allah's signs (ءَايٰتِى)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to a pronoun
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to show, make clear, be apparent

steps.n: Root letters are rearranged and given vowels to make the noun 'sign', with ة added at the end
Changes the word to plural form
Adds kasra at end to show the word is being possessed or follows certain prepositions
Adds first person possessive pronoun to show ownership
The basic word مَن means 'whoever'. Adding the kasra (ِ) at the end connects it to what comes after it.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this comes as a result of what was mentioned before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb ٱتَّقَىٰ (fears/guards against evil) in this ayah.
Root letters joined together to make past tense 'he protected'. The last letter ي becomes ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added Form VIII pattern (افتعل) which shows doing action to/for oneself
The ت and ق merge with a shadda, making the word easier to say
Changed hamza-kasra (اِ) to alif-wasl (ٱ) since it's at the start. Added small alif (ىٰ) to show the final long vowel.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start changes the meaning from 'being good' to 'making something good' - like changing 'fix' to 'fixed something'
The وَ connects this action to what came before it in the ayah
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'

steps.m: then not (connects to وَأَصْلَحَ - and reforms)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. Here it connects to the previous phrase 'and reforms' to complete the thought.

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'fear' by adding the vowels َ (fatha) and ْ (sukun)
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of فَلَا (there is no). In the ayah, it means 'there is no fear upon them.'
The ى at the end changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the negation particle

steps.m: and not (connects to فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ - then no fear upon them)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects to the previous phrase 'then no fear upon them' to complete the meaning.
This is a pronoun that means 'they' (for a group of people). In this ayah, it's the subject doing the action of يَحْزَنُونَ (they grieve).
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
The ي at the start shows it's happening now or in the future
The ون at the end shows multiple people are doing the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the start to point to specific people we're talking about
Added و at the start to connect this with what came before, like saying 'and'

steps.m: to deny or declare something false

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows intensity and deliberate action. This word relates to بِـَٔايٰتِنَا (our signs) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Added وا to show multiple people did this action, and changed the last vowel to match. The small circle (۟) is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'a sign'
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
Adds نَا to show these are Allah's signs that are being denied
Adds بِ to show these signs were the means by which the denial happened
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he acted arrogantly (sought to make himself greater)
steps.n: The است prefix changes the meaning to seeking/requesting the root meaning for oneself
The وا shows many people did this action. This word is connected to كَذَّبُوا (they denied) in the ayah, referring to the same group of people.

steps.m: and they acted arrogantly (subject of كَذَّبُوا)
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect to previous phrase
This is a small word that shows something is moving away or turning from something else

steps.m: away from it/her (referring to آيَات/signs mentioned before)
steps.n: Added ها with an elongated alif to refer back to the signs (آيَات) mentioned earlier in the verse. The آ at the end makes the word pause nicely at the end of its phrase.
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (subject of أَصْحٰبُ)
steps.n: Adding ئِكَ makes it specifically point to things that are far away. This word is the subject of أَصْحٰبُ (companions) in this ayah.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Changed to broken plural form to show many companions
Added damma at end to show this is the subject. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style. This word is the subject that هُمْ (they) refers to in this ayah.
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the root letters to make it a basic verb meaning 'to shine'
The middle و becomes ا, making a noun for something that gives light/heat
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show it belongs to or follows something

steps.m: they/them (masculine) (refers to الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا - those who denied)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to 'those who denied' (الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا) in this ayah. It shows we're talking about that specific group of men.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it's showing what's inside the Fire (النَّارِ) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: in it (referring to النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: The ها refers back to النَّارِ (the Fire) mentioned earlier in the ayah. It's feminine because النَّارِ is a feminine word in Arabic.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make past tense verb meaning 'he stayed forever'. This word tells us what 'they' (هم) did in the sentence.
Changed from verb (he stayed) to noun (one who stays) by adding alif after first letter
Added damma to show it's in the nominative case
Added ون to make it plural (more than one)
Added sukun at the end to show the word stops here. This is the basic question word that asks about people.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. This word is asking about who could be more unjust (أَظْلَمُ) in the following phrase.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: more/most unjust (subject of who in فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'more/most'. This word is asking 'who is more unjust?' in the ayah.
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَن (who). This word asks about who did something.
The ن from مِن combines with the م from مَن to make a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark)
Added ِ at the end to connect it with the next word افْتَرَىٰ (invented). Together they ask 'who is more unjust than the one who invented...'

steps.m: to make up or invent falsely

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added اِفْتَ to show doing something deliberately or making extra effort to do the action
Changed the initial alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ) and added an alif maksura (ى) at the end. This word relates to اللَّهِ in the ayah through عَلَى, showing he fabricated lies against Allah.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or against something else. In this ayah, it connects with كَذِبًا (a lie) to show someone making up lies against Allah.

steps.m: The One Who is worshipped, Allah
steps.n: The root letters are joined with ال at the beginning and the middle letter is doubled with a shadda to show emphasis. This creates the unique name for God in Arabic.
The ِ at the end shows this is being acted upon by the word دُونِ (besides) in the phrase 'besides Allah'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and turn the action into a noun
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being invented/fabricated against Allah in the phrase 'invents a lie against Allah'
This is a basic connecting word that gives choices between options. It's not built from a root - it's a complete word by itself.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و. In this ayah, it connects two things Allah's enemies do: making up lies about Him OR denying His signs.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ذ makes the meaning stronger and changes it from general lying to specifically rejecting or denying something strongly. In this ayah, it refers to rejecting Allah's signs.

steps.m: to show, make clear, be apparent

steps.n: Root letters changed to make a noun meaning 'one sign/miracle' by adding alif and taa marboota
Changed singular to plural by adding ات
Added بِ to show these signs are being used as tools or means

steps.m: with/by signs, miracles, or verses (connected to next word)
steps.n: Added kasra to connect to the next word (هِ)
Added هِ to show these are Allah's signs
Starting with the base demonstrative form and adding damma to show it points to something
Adding the alif maksura extends the sound to emphasize distance
Adding ئِ shows we're pointing to something far away
Adding كَ at the end completes the demonstrative meaning, showing we're pointing to something specific but far away
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added هُمْ to show who is being reached. This word connects to نَصِيبُهُم (their portion) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'assigned' to noun meaning 'portion/share'
Adds هُم (their) at the end + ُ (u sound) to show it's doing the action
This is a preposition showing source or origin. The kasra is added to show it's a preposition.
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word (الْكِتٰبِ meaning 'the book')
The ن merged with م to make a stronger م sound (shown by the shadda)
Added vowels to make a complete past tense verb
Changes verb into a noun meaning the result of writing
Added 'the' at beginning and kasra at end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)
Starting with the basic particle that shows a limit or endpoint
Adding shadda strengthens the meaning to show reaching a limit
Adding the elongated alif (ىٰ) and small alif (ٓ) completes the particle. This word connects to إِذَا (when) in the next part of the ayah to show 'until when'
This is the basic form of the word that indicates a point in time

steps.m: when (referring to جَآءَتْهُمْ - came to them)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future or conditional time. Here it connects to the verb 'came to them' that follows it.
The middle letter ي changes to ا with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long sound
Added feminine marker ت with a sukun to show it's the end of the word

steps.m: she came to them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who she came to. This refers back to the disbelievers being discussed in the ayah.
Added و between second and third letters to show someone who does the action of sending/delivering
Changed the vowels to make it plural (more than one messenger)

steps.m: our messengers (subject of came to them)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show these are our messengers. This word is doing the action of coming (جَآءَتْهُمْ) in this verse.
The doubled middle letter shows intensity and completeness of the taking action. In Quranic usage, this often refers specifically to angels taking souls.
Adds يَ at the start to show it's happening now (present tense) and means 'he takes'
Plural marker changes subject from 'he' to 'they'

steps.m: they (angels) take them (their souls) completely (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Adds object pronoun 'them' referring back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier. In this ayah, this describes angels taking the souls of disbelievers at death.

steps.m: he said (subject of يَتَوَفَّوْنَهُمْ - they take their souls)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to make past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels - this is a common change in words with و in the middle.

steps.m: they said (subject of يَتَوَفَّوْنَهُمْ - they take their souls)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This is what the angels say when they come to take souls.

steps.m: where (asking about the false deities they used to call upon)
steps.n: This is a question word asking about location. In this ayah, it's being used to ask about the location of the false deities they used to worship besides Allah.

This is a relative pronoun here meaning 'what/that which' because it's asking about what they used to worship besides Allah. It connects the question word 'where' (أَيْنَ) with the verb 'call upon/worship' (تَدْعُونَ).
This is the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Changed the root letters to past tense by adding fathas and an alif
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command meaning 'be!' by dropping the last letter and changing vowels
Added تُمْ to show this happened in the past to multiple people being spoken to
Added vowels to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
The ت at the beginning shows it's 'you' doing the action
The ون suffix indicates masculine plural - 'you all' rather than just 'you'
This is the basic form of the preposition without any vowel marks

steps.m: from/besides/other than (referring to دُونِ - instead of)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how it connects with the next word. Here it introduces what is being worshipped instead of Allah.

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/lesser'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this connects to the word Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it
Changed إِلَه to اللَه by adding ال and merging letters, making it a proper name for The One True God
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of عَلَى (against). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: they said (referring to the disbelievers when the angels come to take their souls)
steps.n: The middle letter و changed to ا, and وا was added at the end to change 'he said' to 'they said'. This shows a group of people speaking instead of one person.

steps.m: to lose the way or go in the wrong direction

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Combined the repeated ل letters with a shadda (ّ) to show emphasis
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
This is the basic preposition that shows something moving away from something else
Added نا (us) at the end. The ن of عن combines with the ن of نا, shown by the shadda mark (ّ)

steps.m: to witness or testify about what one has seen or knows

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Added ُوا at the end to show many people did it together
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action to what happened before. In this ayah, they are testifying against themselves about their disbelief.
This is a preposition that shows something being on top of or against something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is a spelling variation showing the long 'aa' sound at the end
Added a small alif (ٓ) to show connection with the next word أَنفُسِهِمْ (themselves)
Root meaning 'breathe' becomes the word for 'soul' by adding vowels - like building blocks!
Changes the singular word into a plural by adding أ at the start and changing the middle vowel to ُ
Adds هِمْ to show the souls/selves belong to them

steps.m: that/indeed (connecting to كَانُوا۟ كٰفِرِينَ - they were disbelievers)
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that connects two parts of a sentence and makes the second part stronger or more certain
Adds هُمْ (them/they) to show who the statement is about. This connects to the next phrase saying they were disbelievers.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people (they) and ۟ to mark the end of the word

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Shows someone who does the action of rejecting truth. The alif (ا) after the first letter makes it about the person who does the action.

steps.m: those who reject truth (acted upon by كَانُوا)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this is plural (more than two) and is being acted upon by the verb كَانُوا (they were) in this ayah.
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command
