Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier

steps.m: enter! (command to all of you) (after قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added وا to make it a command to many people. This comes after قَالَ (he said) showing someone giving this command to a group.
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something. Here it shows the communities these people will enter into.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to gather, to lead, to be a mother/source/origin

steps.n: Changes the root into a plural noun by adding vowel marks to make 'umam', meaning multiple nations or communities
Adds tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word follows فِي (in/among) in the ayah
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. Here it emphasizes that these nations definitely passed away before.

steps.m: to pass away or go before

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. In this ayah, it's talking about nations that passed away or went before.

steps.m: she passed away (before) (referring to أُمَمٍ - nations)
steps.n: Added ت to show this is about nations (which are feminine in Arabic). The و at the end drops when we add ت.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects 'before you' with 'nations' to show which nations are being talked about.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by what comes after it

steps.m: before you all (مِن قَبْلِكُم = from before you all)
steps.n: Added كُم to show this refers to multiple people being talked to. This word is part of the phrase مِن قَبْلِكُم (from before you all) in this ayah.
Basic preposition that shows something comes from or belongs to something else
The n sound blends into the next letter, shown by the shadda mark

steps.m: from/of (emphasized, connecting to next word الْجِنِّ)
steps.n: The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word الْجِنِّ (the jinn), showing they are linked together in meaning

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight

steps.n: Adding the kasra vowel turns this into a noun meaning a type of being
The shadda makes the meaning stronger and more specific
The kasra at the end shows this is possessed by something

steps.m: the jinn (beings hidden from human sight)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to all jinn as a group
Forms a noun meaning 'mankind' from the root meaning 'to be sociable'
Shows this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Adds the definite article to make the noun definite
Added 'and' at the beginning
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Here it connects the command 'enter' (ٱدْخُلُوا۟) with 'nations' (أُمَمٍ), showing where to enter.
Changes the root into a noun meaning 'fire'
Added ٱل at the beginning to mean 'the' and doubled the ن with a shadda to show it's a specific fire being talked about
Added ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) to make it mean 'all/every', and َ (fatha) at the end shows something is being done to it
Adding مَا changes the meaning to 'whenever' or 'every time that'. This word connects to دَخَلَتْ (entered) in the ayah to mean 'whenever [a nation] entered'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past

steps.m: she entered (subject of لَّعَنَتْ - she cursed)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female (she) did the action. This word is the subject of لَّعَنَتْ (she cursed) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be in front or lead others

steps.n: Changed root letters into a simple noun meaning 'mother' by adding a damma mark and doubling the middle letter

steps.m: community/group (those who follow the same path)
steps.n: Added ة to show a specific group or community that shares a common path or leadership
Added tanween dammah to show this is referring to one of many communities mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to curse or reject from mercy

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Added ت at the end to show a female did the action
Added shadda on first letter to show emphasis. This word refers to how each nation (أُمَّةٌ) cursed the other nations that came before them.
Changes the word to mean a female family member
Adds the fatha mark to show this word is the object of a verb
Adds 'her' to show possession/ownership

steps.m: her sister (object being cursed by أُمَّةٌ)
steps.n: Added هَا meaning 'her'. In this ayah, each nation (أُمَّةٌ) curses its sister nation that came before it.
Basic particle showing end point or limit
Adding ى (alif maqsurah) at the end is the normal way to write this word - it's not about emphasis, it's just the complete spelling
Adding alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى is just for pronunciation, it doesn't change the meaning
Adding the small alif (ٓ) is just for pronunciation, it doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: when (connects to ٱدَّارَكُوا۟ - they overtake one another)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects the time when all the people gather together in the Fire. It's like saying 'at the time when' they all catch up with each other there.

steps.m: to catch up with or reach something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: they caught up with each other
steps.n: Added تَ at start and ا after first root letter to show mutual action between multiple people

steps.m: they all caught up with each other
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action together
Basic preposition indicating location or containment. This word has no root since it's a particle.

steps.m: in it (referring to النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added feminine pronoun ها to refer back to النَّارِ (the Fire) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Adding ي (yaa) between the middle and last letter makes this mean 'all together' or 'completely gathered'

steps.m: all together (describing how they entered the Fire)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end makes this word describe how the action happened - they entered as one complete group
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from قَوَلَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا, a common change with hollow verbs
Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female is speaking

steps.m: to come after or behind something

steps.n: Changes to feminine form meaning 'another' or 'other'
The alif above ى (ىٰ) is just a writing style - both words are the same feminine form meaning 'other/another'

steps.m: their last ones (referring to the later groups entering the Fire)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. In this ayah, it refers to the later groups of people entering the Fire speaking about those who came before them.
Added damma (ُ) to create the base form meaning 'first'

steps.m: first ones (referring to the earlier groups mentioned)
steps.n: Added ى to make it feminine and refer to a group (like 'first ones' instead of just 'first')
Added لِ with kasra to mean 'to/for'

steps.m: to their first ones (referring to the earlier nations entering the Fire)
steps.n: Added هُمْ meaning 'their' and the alif extension mark (ٰ)
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the nurturing quality
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us (our Lord). The word is being used to call out to Allah directly in this ayah, shown by the final alif.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to multiple things or people that are near. This word doesn't come from a root.
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) above ه to help with pronunciation
Added vowel marks and elongation signs to show exact pronunciation. This word is the subject of the verb أَضَلُّونَا (they misled us) in this ayah.
Form IV makes it causative - causing someone to go astray
Changes from singular 'he' to plural 'they'

steps.m: they led us astray (said by the followers about their leaders)
steps.n: The suffix 'نا' shows who was led astray (us). In this ayah, this is being said by followers complaining about their misleading leaders.
Root letters joined together with alif madd (آ) at start to make 'I am' form
Changed to command form by dropping final ي
Added فَ to connect this command with previous statement

steps.m: so bring them! (referring to punishment mentioned after)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show who receives the action - refers to the punishment being brought to them
Changed the action into a noun by adding an alif between the last two root letters and vowel marks

steps.m: a punishment (as the object being given)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being given in the sentence

steps.m: to make something double or multiply

steps.n: Added kasra under first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it mean 'double portion'

steps.m: a double portion (object of فَـَٔاتِهِمْ - give them)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha at the end to show this is the object receiving the action of 'give them' (فَـَٔاتِهِمْ) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of
The 'n' sound at the end of the previous word combines with this word's 'm', making it stronger (shown by the shadda)
Added fatha at the end to connect this word to the next word الْجِنِّ (the jinn)
Changed the middle letter و to ا and added vowels to make the noun for 'fire' - something that gives light
Added ال ('the') to make it 'the fire' instead of just any fire. The ِ (kasra) at the end comes after the word في (in). The dot on top of ن doubled to make a stronger 'n' sound.
Added fathas to make basic past tense form. This shows a completed action.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
The shadda (ّ) doubles the letter to show strength, while the damma (ُ) changes it from a verb to a noun meaning 'every' or 'all'
ل means 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for each one (related to ضِعْفٌ/double portion)
steps.n: The tanween (ٍ) shows this word is connected to ضِعْفٌ (double portion) that comes after it in the ayah, forming the meaning 'for each one is a double portion'

steps.m: to make something double or multiple times more

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This word describes the double portion of punishment being requested in the ayah.
Added tanween damma (double damma) at the end to make it a complete indefinite noun
This is a basic particle used to show contrast or exception. It introduces a statement that contrasts with or makes an exception to what came before.
The و connects this contrasting statement to the previous statement قَالَ (He said). It shows that this new thought follows from what was just said.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) that follows it
The shadda makes the negation stronger, emphasizing that they absolutely do not know
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start
Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed
Added vowels to connect the root letters together. This is the basic past tense form before the weak letter change.
The middle letter و becomes ا because it's a weak letter. This is the final common form you see in the Quran.
Adding ت shows a female did the speaking
Adding و connects this to what happened before
Added damma to make it into a noun meaning 'first'
Added ى with alif above (ىٰ) to make it feminine singular form

steps.m: their first ones (referring to the disbelievers speaking to each other)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. In this ayah, it refers to the first group of disbelievers speaking to another group.

steps.m: to come after, to be behind

steps.n: Changed the root letters to make a feminine word meaning 'other/another' by adding ى with alif (ىٰ) at the end

steps.m: their last ones (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in أُولَىٰهُمْ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show it belongs to them, connecting to the earlier word أُولَىٰهُمْ (their first ones)

steps.m: to their last ones (speaking to أُولَىٰهُمْ)
steps.n: Added لِ (to) at the beginning to show they are speaking to their later companions
This is a question word that means 'what'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from and is connected to what came before. This word connects to كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'so what was...'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he' form

steps.m: he was/existed/happened (describing the state of فَضْلٍ)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter) in the past tense
This is a pronoun meaning 'you' when talking to multiple people
Adding the preposition لَ meaning 'for' or 'to'. This word shows who benefits from or receives something in the phrase 'there was not for you' (فَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ)
This is a preposition showing something being above or having authority over something else
Added ي with sukun to prepare for attachment of pronoun
Added نا at the end to show this applies to 'us'. This word connects to كَانَ (was) in this ayah, showing what was upon us.
This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that can't be broken down into smaller parts. It shows where something starts or comes from.
Added the kasra (ِ) under the م to show how it connects with the next word فَضْلٍ (favor). Together they mean 'from favor' or 'of favor'.

steps.m: to give extra or be better than others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that means the result of giving extra. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: any extra gift or special favor (after مِن)
steps.n: Added the ٍ ending to show this word comes after مِن (from) and means 'any' instead of 'the'
Changed the root into a command by adding a damma (u sound) at the start - telling someone to taste/experience something
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people

steps.m: so taste! (all of you) (connected to عَذَابَ - punishment)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. In this ayah, it's commanding them to taste the punishment as a result of their actions.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together. This makes it a past tense verb.
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is the direct object (what is being tasted) of the command 'taste' (ذوقوا).
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Gets a fatha mark at the end because it receives the action of 'taste'
This is a word that means 'what' or 'that which'. It's often used to refer to actions or situations.

steps.m: because of what (connects to تَكْسِبُونَ - you earn)
steps.n: The بِ (bi) at the beginning shows why something happens. Here it connects to the verb تَكْسِبُونَ (you earn) to explain that they are being punished because of what they earned through their actions.
Added damma and sukun to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added ت at the end to mean 'I/you' and changed the middle و to make it past tense ('was/were')
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to show we're talking to multiple people
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed vowels and added ت to show it's happening now and you're doing it

steps.m: you all earn (referring to the disbelievers being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes the statement 'ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟' that follows)
steps.n: This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that those who deny the signs will definitely not enter paradise.
This is the basic form of the word that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one)

steps.m: those who (subject of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ to make it specific. This word introduces the people who denied Allah's signs in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning intensive/causative
Changes from 'he denied' to 'they denied' by adding وا at the end

steps.m: to show evidence or make clear

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
Changed from single sign to multiple signs by adding ات at the end - like changing 'sign' to 'signs' in English
Added بِ to show how these signs were used, and ِ to connect it to what comes after
Added نَا to show these are Allah's signs. This word is being denied/rejected by the disbelievers mentioned in كَذَّبُوا (they denied).
The ُ (damma) in the middle shows this is a natural quality
The prefix 'ٱسْتَ' shows someone is trying to make themselves something - here trying to make themselves seem greater than they are
The وا shows multiple people did this action in the past

steps.m: and they tried to act big (arrogantly)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this with the previous phrase about those who denied Our signs

steps.m: away from it/her (referring to آيَات/signs that were rejected)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the signs (آيَات) that were mentioned earlier in the ayah. This word is connected to the verb استكبروا (they were arrogant), showing what they were arrogant about.
This is a basic negative word that makes the verb after it (يَدْخُلُونَ - they enter) into its opposite meaning (they do not enter)
Added fatha mark on first letter to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter shows the action happens multiple times or with intensity

steps.m: they (the doors) are opened repeatedly (passive present tense)
steps.n: Added تُ at start and ُ at end to show this happens to the doors (passive) in the present/future. This word relates to أَبْوٰبُ (doors) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition
Added هُ which means 'him/his' to show who something is for
Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them', making it refer to multiple people
Adding alif makes it a common noun pattern for objects/things
Changes from single door (باب) to many doors by adding أ at start and putting وا in middle
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb تُفَتَّحُ (are opened). The alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added ء at the end and kept the long ا to make it a noun meaning the place that is high up
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by أَبْوٰبُ (doors), forming the phrase 'doors of the sky'
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen

steps.m: and not (connects to لَا تُفَتَّحُ - are not opened)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement. This connects to لَا تُفَتَّحُ (are not opened) to show multiple things that won't happen to the disbelievers.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present/future tense

steps.m: they enter (referring to those who denied Our signs)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to 'those who denied Our signs' mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to cover or conceal (like how a garden's trees cover the ground)

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) and doubled the middle letter to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it covered'

steps.m: a garden (a place with covering trees)
steps.n: Added ة to make it a noun for a place where covering happens
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific garden
Added َ at the end to show this is the object that something is happening to
This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence, showing that the first part continues until the second part happens
The shadda emphasizes the meaning, showing a definite endpoint
The ى with alif above (ىٰ) is added at the end to show this is a complete particle

steps.m: to enter deeply or penetrate into something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start. The middle vowel changes to kasra. This word comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) which requires this form.

steps.m: to gather, collect, or make beautiful

steps.n: Root letters got fatha vowels to make the word for 'camel'
Adding the definite article ال makes it refer to a specific camel
The damma ending shows this is the subject doing the action of entering (يَلِجَ) in this ayah
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows the needle's eye that the camel must pass through.

steps.m: to make a hole or opening

steps.n: The doubled م shows intensity or emphasis
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to 'the needle' that comes after it, forming the phrase 'eye of the needle'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed verb 'to sew' into noun meaning 'sewing tool'
Added the definite article to specify this particular needle
Added kasra to show this word belongs to سَمِّ (eye), forming 'eye of the needle'
The base word ذا (this) is changed to ذٰلِكَ (that) by adding لِ and كَ. This makes it point to something far away instead of something close.
Adding كَ at the beginning changes 'that' to 'like that' or 'similarly'
Adding وَ connects this word to the next verb نَجْزِى (we recompense), showing how something will be done
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form. This word shows what 'we' (Allah) do to reward or punish المجرمين (the criminals) mentioned right after.

steps.m: to commit wrong or cross boundaries

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added ين to show this refers to many people who do this action
Added ال to specify these particular wrongdoers and َ to show this is the object receiving the action of نَجْزِى (we recompense)
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُم (them) to show who the preposition is referring to. This word connects to مِهَادٌ (bed/resting place) later in the ayah, showing what they will have.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The shadda is added because this word comes after a pause in reading. In this ayah, it connects the word جَهَنَّمَ (Hell) with مِهَادٌ (bed/resting place), showing that the bed is from Hell.
This is a proper noun that doesn't come from an Arabic root. It's a name for the place of punishment in the afterlife. The word may have originated from Hebrew 'Gehinnom'.
The fatha at the end shows that Hell is being described in this ayah - it's being given a 'bed' (مِهَادٌ)

steps.m: to spread out or prepare something
a resting place (that is spread out)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun describing a place. In this ayah, it refers to the bed or resting place in Hell.
Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject of the sentence 'they have'. The word connects to لَهُم (for them) to mean 'they have a resting place'.
Combines the conjunction و (and) with the preposition من (from)
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'above'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word that follows it
Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'them' or 'their'

steps.m: to cover or wrap something completely

steps.n: Basic past tense verb showing someone covered or overwhelmed something
Changes from a verb (to cover) to a noun meaning 'one who covers'
Changed from single 'one who covers' to plural 'coverings', like changing 'blanket' to 'blankets' but in Arabic style
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that shows similarity
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an alif khanjariya, which is just a writing variation
Adding ك at the end makes it point to something specific, like saying 'that' instead of just 'like'

steps.m: and likewise (connects to نَجْزِى - we recompense)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, showing these things happen together

steps.m: to repay or give what is due

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added نَ at start to show 'we' are doing it, changed vowels to show present tense. This word shows how Allah and His angels will repay the wrongdoers mentioned right after this word.
Changed the final ي to ى which is easier to read at the end of words but doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from a verb (to wrong) to a noun meaning 'wrongdoer'
Regular masculine plural ending. This word is the direct object (accusative case) of the verb نَجْزِى (we recompense), showing who receives the action of the verb.

steps.m: the wrongdoers (acted upon by نَجْزِى/we recompense)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ظ with shadda to show it's a specific group of wrongdoers being talked about
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people we're talking about
Added و at the beginning to connect it to what came before. This word is connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) which tells us what these people did.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Adding alif after hamza changes the basic word 'trust' into 'believe/have faith'. This is a special case that became the standard way to say 'believe'. When you see this word with 'good deeds', it means 'those who believe'.
Added ending to show many people did this action. This word is connected to وَعَمِلُوا۟ (and they did) in the phrase 'those who believed and did good deeds'
Added vowels to make past tense verb. This action is done by the same people who believed (ءَامَنُوا۟) in the ayah.
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. These are the same people who believed (ءَامَنُوا۟).
Added وَ (and) to connect this action to the previous one (believed). The small mark ۟ above the final و is just to help with pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into an active participle that describes something as having this quality
Made feminine plural to show multiple good actions. This is the object of عَمِلُوا (they did/performed).
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which made the first letter stronger (doubled)
Added kasra (i) sound at the end to show these good deeds are being done by someone
This is a negative particle that makes the verb نُكَلِّفُ (we burden) into 'we do not burden'. It's one of the most common words used to say 'no' or make something negative in Arabic.
Added fatha mark on ل to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: to give someone a task or responsibility
to give someone an important responsibility
steps.n: The doubled ل shows this is about giving important or serious responsibilities
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This verb is giving responsibility to نَفْسًا (a soul/person) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'soul' or 'self'. This word receives the action of نُكَلِّفُ (we burden/task).
Added ًا at the end to show this word receives the action of the verb نُكَلِّفُ (we burden/task). Like saying 'We don't burden a soul.'
This is the basic form of the word that means 'except'. It's made up of hamza with kasra (إِ) and lam (ل)
Adding shadda on the lam makes the word stronger and more emphatic
Adding alif at the end completes the word. This word is used to make an exception to وُسْعَهَآ (its capacity) in this ayah, meaning Allah doesn't burden any soul except within its capacity.

steps.m: to be wide or have capacity

steps.n: Changed from verb meaning 'to be wide' into noun meaning 'capacity' by adding vowel marks (damma and sukun)
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: its capacity (object of نُكَلِّفُ - we burden)
steps.n: Added هَآ to show this is 'its capacity'. This word is what's being acted upon by نُكَلِّفُ (we burden/task) in the ayah
This is the basic form of the word used to point to multiple people or things
Adding ا makes the word stronger and more emphatic
Adding ئِ shows that what we're pointing to is far away

steps.m: those (pointing to something far) (subject of أَصْحٰبُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end completes the word. In this ayah, this word is the subject of أَصْحٰبُ (companions/people), pointing to the believers mentioned in the previous ayah.

steps.m: to be close to someone or something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a past-tense verb
Changed to show someone who does the action
Changed to plural form to show many people

Added a small u-sound (ُ) at the end to show these are the doers of the action. The middle alif is written slightly differently but pronounced the same. This word pairs with 'Paradise' to mean the people who get to live there
Added ة to make a noun referring to a place that is covered/concealed, which came to mean garden or paradise
Added ٱلْ to specify this particular paradise/garden

steps.m: of the garden/paradise (possessed by أَصْحٰبُ - companions/people)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this belongs to or is connected to the word أَصْحٰبُ (companions/people) in the ayah

steps.m: they (the companions of Paradise mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to أَصْحٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ (the companions of Paradise) mentioned in the previous part of the ayah. It shows who will be in Paradise.
This is a word that shows something is inside or within something else
Added هَا at the end to show we're talking about Paradise (which was mentioned just before this word). Since الْجَنَّةِ (Paradise) is feminine in Arabic, we use the feminine 'it' (هَا).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he lasted) to a describing word meaning 'lasting/eternal' - like changing 'ran' to 'running'
Added two dots on top at the end to make it a complete standalone word meaning 'eternal one'
Added ون to show this describes multiple people. This word describes هم (they) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action in the past
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with what came before
This is a negative particle that makes the verb كُنَّا (we were) into 'we were not'. In this ayah, it's part of saying 'we would not have been guided'

steps.m: in/within (connects صُدُور 'chests' to what is inside them)
steps.n: A preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects the word صُدُور (chests) with what is inside them.
Changed the word to show multiple chests by adding ُ marks and و
Added ِهِم at the end to show these chests belong to them (referring to the people of Paradise mentioned in the context)
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added shadda and sukun. The shadda is added because this word comes after a vowel sound in the ayah. The sukun shows where the word stops.

steps.m: to hate deeply or hold grudges

steps.n: The doubled ل (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger, changing it from just 'hate' to 'intense hatred'
The ٍ shows this word comes after مِنْ (from), like saying 'free from any hatred'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to feminine form by replacing يَ with تَ. This refers to الْأَنْهٰرُ (the rivers) which is feminine in Arabic.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to the word غِلٍّ (hatred/rancor) to show what is being removed from their hearts.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِم to show this belongs to them or is connected to them
Added a 'u' sound at the end to show this is doing the flowing action
Root meaning 'flow' becomes 'river' by adding vowels - like turning 'flow' into a thing that flows
Changed to plural form to show many rivers
Added ال to show these are specific rivers being talked about
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the flowing (تَجْرِى) in the verse
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Adding وا makes it plural masculine - means more than two people said it
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to the previous statement
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun meaning the act of praising
Added 'the' at beginning to make it specific and ُ at end to show this is what's being talked about. This word is what's being said in قَالُوا (they said).
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added لِ at beginning to show the praise belongs to or is for Allah. This word connects to الْحَمْدُ (the praise) in the ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative form that points to something
Adding ٱلّ changes this from pointing at something to connecting parts of a sentence. Here it connects to هَدَىٰنَا (guided us) to tell us who did the guiding.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to show it's pronounced long

steps.m: he guided us (object of the verb)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show who was guided (us). This word is being acted upon by Allah (اللَّهُ) in this ayah.
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. The alif at the end makes it masculine.

steps.m: for this/because of this (explaining why they praised Allah)
steps.n: The لِ prefix adds the meaning of 'for' or 'because of'. In this ayah, it explains the reason for their praise of Allah mentioned in the previous words.
This is a particle that means 'not' in this context since it's followed by كُنَّا (we were) to negate the past action
The وَ connects this negation to the previous statement where they praised Allah, showing contrast: 'they praised Allah... and (they said) we would not...'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out, and the vowels change to match the pattern for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the ن sound.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added reflexive pattern to show the action is done to oneself
Changed from 'he' past tense to 'we' present tense - both about finding guidance

steps.m: so that we may find guidance
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose and َ at the end to show subjunctive mood (possibility)
This is the basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Adding لا changes the meaning to express something that would have happened if not for something else

steps.m: if not for/had it not been for (connected to هَدَىٰنَا)
steps.n: The alif is elongated to add emphasis. In this ayah, it introduces what would not have happened (their guidance) if not for Allah's help.

steps.m: that (introduces what was called out/announced in نُودُوٓا۟)
steps.n: This is a connecting particle that introduces what was announced or called out. In this ayah, it connects to the word نُودُوٓا۟ (they were called/announced to) to show what was announced to them.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added fathas and the ى replaces ي at the end because it's a weak letter. This shows the action happened in the past.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel sound
Added نَا at the end to show we are the ones being guided. In this ayah, Allah is being praised for guiding the believers.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and emphasizing the ل with a shadda. The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain or definite
The لَ prefix adds even stronger emphasis. This word emphasizes that the messengers definitely came with the truth.
Basic past tense verb with fatha marks
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine. This word is describing what رُسُلُ (messengers) did in this ayah.
Changed the word to mean the person who does the action of sending messages
Changed the word to plural form. The ُ ending shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence. This word is connected to رَبِّنَا (our Lord) in this ayah.
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun showing someone who does this action completely
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is owned by or connected to something
Added نَا at the end to show this belongs to 'us'. This word is possessed by رُسُلُ (messengers) in the phrase 'messengers of our Lord'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
The middle letter is doubled to add emphasis and make it a noun
Adding 'ال' makes it specific - not just any truth, but THE truth

steps.m: with the truth (describing how رُسُلُ رَبِّنَا/the messengers of our Lord came)
steps.n: Added بِ (with/by) at the start and ِ at the end because it follows the word جَآءَتْ (came). The alif in ال changes to ٱ after بِ. This shows how the messengers came - they came with/by the truth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding damma at start and kasra in middle. The weak letter ي changes to و due to this pattern.
Added وا to show multiple people were called out

steps.m: and they were called out (to hear about الْجَنَّةُ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this with previous statement. The long vowel mark and small و are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects to هَدَىٰنَا (He guided us) to show that something happened.
This is the basic form of the feminine demonstrative pronoun used to point to something far away
Changed the ending vowel to prepare for adding the plural 'you' ending
Added مُ at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This word is pointing to Paradise (الْجَنَّةُ) which is mentioned right after it in the ayah.
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show complete covering
The ة changes it from 'covering' to mean the place that is covered with trees
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
The ُ shows this is the subject being described in this part of the verse

steps.m: to receive something from someone who has passed away

steps.n: Adding أُ at the beginning and ِ in the middle shows this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it themselves

steps.m: you all were made to inherit
steps.n: Adding تم shows this happened to multiple people (you all)

steps.m: you all were made to inherit
steps.n: Adding ُ shows this is in the nominative case

steps.m: you all were made to inherit it
steps.n: Adding و at the end makes it refer to something being inherited

steps.m: you all were made to inherit it (Paradise)
steps.n: Adding ها refers to Paradise (الجنة) which is mentioned in the previous part of the verse
This is a basic preposition that shows how or why something happens

steps.m: because of what (you used to do)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what' to ask about or refer to something. In this ayah, it refers to the good deeds that people did, connecting their past actions to their reward.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Command form ('Be!') - made shorter by dropping middle letter
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding مْ at the end to show we're talking to multiple people
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter to emphasize the action
Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before it
Added ى at the end to show this is a past tense verb ending with a weak letter
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple companions
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the calling (نَادَىٰ). Also changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) which is just a writing variation.
Added vowels and connected the letters together. The doubled middle letter shows intensity of the action.
Adding ة changes it from an action (covering) to a place that provides cover/shade (garden)
The small i-sound (kasra) at the end shows this means 'of the garden' instead of just 'garden'
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: to accompany or be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'one who accompanies'
Changed from single companion to plural companions by adding أ at the start and changing the middle vowels - this is a common plural pattern
Changed spelling slightly and added mark at end to show these companions were being called out to
Changed from a root meaning 'light/heat' to the noun 'fire' by adding an alif between first and last letters
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and shadda on ن because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because this word is the object of أَصْحٰبَ (companions/people of)
This is a connecting word that introduces what was announced - in this case, it introduces the curse of Allah. It's like using quotation marks in English to show what someone said or announced.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the first letter to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'anna'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the verb وَجَدْنَا (we found) that comes after it. It shows that the finding has definitely happened.

steps.m: to find/discover something after searching or effort

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past

steps.m: we found (speaking about what we discovered in the past)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' are the ones who did the finding. This word is part of a conversation between the people of Paradise and the people of Hell, where they are discussing whether they found what Allah promised to be true.
This is a connecting word that links to وَعَدَ (promised). In this ayah, it's asking about 'what' Allah promised them.
Added vowel marks to make past tense 'he promised' - like adding -ed in English
Added نَا at the end to show that 'us' is the object being promised to

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or sustain completely

steps.n: Root letters combined with fatha and doubled middle letter to make the word for 'Lord' - someone who takes care of others
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action of promising (وَعَدَ)

steps.m: our Lord (as the doer of وَعَدَ)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this Lord belongs to us or is our Lord. This word is the subject who did the promising (وَعَدَ) mentioned in the verse.
Root letters combined with shadda (doubled middle letter) to make the simple word meaning 'truth'
Added tanween alif to make it describe how the action happened. This word describes how they found what their Lord promised - they found it truly/in truth.
This is a question word that asks about whether something happened or not. Here it's asking about whether they found what was promised.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from what was said before. It connects this question to the previous statement.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he found' to 'I found' (not 'you found')
Changed from 'I found' to 'you all found' by adding م and putting a shadda on ت
Basic interrogative/relative particle used to ask about things or refer to 'that which'

steps.m: that which (referring to what Allah promised)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. In this ayah, it refers to what Allah promised, connecting to وَعَدَ (promised) which comes after it.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks. This word refers to what 'your Lord' (رَبُّكُمْ) promised in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop and sustain

steps.n: The root letters ر-ب-ب become رَبُّ (Lord) with the damma ending to show it's doing the action of promising
Adding كُمْ shows this Lord belongs to multiple people being addressed
This root carries the basic idea of truth and reality
Adding fatha and combining the doubled ق with shadda creates the noun meaning 'truth'

steps.m: truly/really (describing how they found what Allah promised)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes this into an adverb describing how they found Allah's promise
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter

steps.m: they said (the people of the Fire responding to the people of Paradise)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word shows the response of the people of the Fire to the question from the people of Paradise about whether they found what their Lord promised to be true.

steps.m: yes (in response to the question from أَصْحٰبَ ٱلنَّارِ)
steps.n: This is a basic word used to strongly agree or confirm something. Here it's used by the people of the Fire to confirm that they found what their Lord promised them to be true. It's stronger than just saying 'yes' - it's like saying 'yes, indeed' or 'yes, absolutely'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes the announcement stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: and then he announced loudly (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this announcement follows what happened before in the verse

steps.m: to announce, permit, or call out
steps.n: These letters together represent the basic concept of announcing or calling out
Adding fathas connects the letters and makes it a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: one who calls out/announces (as their role)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and changing the middle vowel to kasra makes it refer to the person who does this action
Adding tanween makes it indefinite (like saying 'a' instead of 'the')
Adding vowels changes this from a root into a common preposition
Adds هُمْ at the end to show who or what is being separated/between. In this ayah, it refers to the people of Paradise and the people of the Fire who are being announced about.
This is a connecting word (particle) that introduces what the people of Paradise said. It's like using quotation marks in English to show someone's words.

steps.m: to reject or push away with anger

steps.n: Changes verb into a noun meaning the act of cursing
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of cursing
Adding ُ shows this word is the subject or in the nominative case
The ُ shows this word is the subject that connects to عَلَى (upon) in this ayah
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
The kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else

This is a basic preposition that shows something (in this case, Allah's curse) is directed against or upon something else (the wrongdoers). In this ayah, it connects لَّعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ (Allah's curse) with ٱلظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers).
This root carries the basic idea of doing something wrong or unfair to others
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from describing the action to describing the person who does it, like changing 'wrong' to 'wrongdoer'
Adding ال makes it talk about specific wrongdoers that we know about
Makes it plural and shows it comes after the word عَلَى (upon)
Special demonstrative word combining ال (definite article) + ذي (who/which) + ين (plural suffix). The shadda on ل is due to sun letter assimilation.
Adding vowel marks makes it a proper past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this action is done with extra force
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels to ُ shows this happens in the present

steps.m: they block/prevent (referring to ٱلَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Adding ونَ shows multiple people are doing this action. This refers back to ٱلَّذِينَ (those who)
This is a preposition that shows something is moving away from or being prevented from something else. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَصُدُّونَ (they prevent/turn away) with سَبِيلِ (path).

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Basic noun pattern showing a path or way
Adding ي makes it the common word for 'path' that appears often in Quran
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to 'Allah' - like saying 'path of Allah'
Changed from 'a deity' to specifically mean 'Allah' - the One True God. The ال here is special and permanent, making this a unique proper name.
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to/describes something else (the path/way). Also added shadda to emphasize the L sound and changed alif to wasl since it comes after another word.
Added present tense prefix يَ to show ongoing action
Added ْ (sukun) on ب to make it a basic present tense verb
Added ون suffix to show multiple people doing the action
Added conjunction و to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: crookedness (as something they seek in Allah's path)
steps.n: Added tanween (ًا) to show this is the object of يَبْغُونَهَا (they seek it), referring to their attempt to make Allah's path crooked
Added damma to show this is a separate pronoun meaning 'they'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This pronoun 'they' refers to the same people who are described as كٰفِرُونَ (disbelievers) later in the ayah.

steps.m: to be behind or after something

steps.n: Adding آ at start makes it mean 'more/most after' = 'last'

steps.m: and on/upon (connects to الْأَعْرَافِ - the heights)

Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is the subject - these men are doing the action

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added damma and shadda to make it mean 'all' or 'every'
Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَعْرِفُونَ (they recognize)

steps.m: to mark or label something distinctively

steps.n: Changed the و to ي and added ى at the end to make it into a noun meaning 'mark'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'by means of' or 'with' the mark
Added هُمْ at the end to show the mark belongs to them

steps.m: to call out or announce to someone

steps.m: and they called out (to أصحاب الجنة)

Adding the double damma makes it 'a peace' instead of 'the peace'
Basic preposition showing something being above or upon something else
Changed the ending to prepare for connecting to pronouns
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being talked to

Added vowels to make it past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و to make it plural - means multiple people are doing the action
Added هَا to show what is being entered (referring to الجَنَّة - Paradise)
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' (masculine). It's the subject of the verb يَطْمَعُونَ (they desire/hope) in the next part of the ayah.
The و prefix connects this word to what came before it, adding the meaning 'and'
Added يَ at the beginning to show someone is doing this action right now, and added vowel marks to connect the letters
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing this action together

steps.m: was turned (subject is أَبْصٰرُهُمْ - their eyes)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice using damma-kasra pattern to show the action happened to something rather than something doing the action. In this ayah, it's specifically their eyes that were turned.

steps.m: were turned (subject is أَبْصٰرُهُمْ - their eyes)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت because أَبْصٰرُهُمْ (their eyes) is feminine plural
The regular alif (ا) is written as a long alif (ٰ) here. This is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added هُمْ at the end with ُ before it to show these eyes belong to them. This word appears when their eyes are turned towards (تِلْقَآءَ) the companions of the fire.
Changed the verb into a noun that means the act of meeting
Adding ت at start changes the meaning to indicate direction or facing something

steps.m: towards, facing (أَصْحٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ - the companions of the Fire)
steps.n: Lengthened middle vowel and added ending mark to show direction/movement towards something

steps.m: to accompany, be close to, or associate with

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make a noun meaning 'companion' from the verb meaning 'to accompany'
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and ا after second root letter
Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection to 'the Fire' (النَّارِ) that comes after it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels
The middle و changes to ا to make a noun meaning 'fire' (related to light/heat)
Added ال ('the') and doubled the ن sound to show it's a specific, intense fire
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows something else in the sentence
The root letters ق-و-ل became قَالَ (he said) by changing the middle letter و to ا in the past tense
Changes 'he said' to 'they said' by adding the plural masculine ending وا
Adds the small circle diacritic (۟) above the plural ending وا to show it's a group of people
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The doubled letter shows completeness of the nurturing quality
Added نَا meaning 'our' and fatha before it because this is a call for help (vocative). This word appears after قَالُوا (they said) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. In this ayah, it negates the verb تَجْعَلْنَا (make us), creating the meaning 'do not make us'.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
تَ at the start shows 'you' are doing the action
نا suffix indicates 'us' as the object of the verb
This is a basic preposition that shows being together with something. In this ayah, it connects to 'the people' (ٱلْقَوْمِ) to show who you shouldn't be together with.

steps.m: to stand upright or rise up

steps.n: Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Adding ال makes it specific/definite (like 'the' in English)
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: to do wrong or act unjustly

steps.n: This pattern shows someone who does the action - like adding '-er' in English (teach → teacher)
ين at the end makes it plural (more than two)

steps.m: the wrongdoers (the unjust people being referred to)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the beginning to make it specific. The ل combines with ظ to make it doubled (ظّ)
Basic past tense verb ending with ى
Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before
Added ى at the end to show this is a past tense verb ending with a weak letter
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
The word changed from 'he stayed close' to mean 'companions' by rearranging the letters and adding 'a' at the start to make it plural - like changing 'friend' to 'friends' but in a special Arabic way
This word is the subject (doer) of the verb نَادَىٰ (called out) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: elevated places (where people can recognize/observe others)
steps.n: Forms a plural noun describing high places from which one can observe/recognize others
Adding ال makes it refer to specific elevated places mentioned in the Quran

steps.m: of the elevated places (where people of أَصْحٰبُ observe others)
steps.n: Adding kasra shows this word belongs to أَصْحٰبُ (companions/people) mentioned before it in the ayah

steps.m: man, foot (basic concept of walking/standing on foot)

steps.n: The vowels make it into a noun meaning 'man'
Changes to broken plural form to show multiple men

steps.m: men (as the objects being called out to by أَصْحٰبُ ٱلْأَعْرَافِ)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is the object being called out to by the people of Al-A'raf
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now
Adding ون shows many people are doing the action

steps.m: they recognize them (referring to the men mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: The هُم refers back to the رِجَالًا (men) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to mark or brand something with a distinguishing sign

steps.n: Root س-و-م became سِيمَى by changing و to ي and adding ى at the end, turning the verb 'to mark' into a noun meaning 'a mark or sign'

steps.m: by their distinguishing marks (referring to how the people of A'raf recognize others)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these marks belong to them
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people did the action
This is a special word that negates the verb كُنتُمْ (you were) in this ayah. Looking at the context, it's being used to say 'was not' rather than as a question word.
Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation
Added the elongation mark (آ) to show that the 'aa' sound is stretched when reading
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to make something/someone free from need' instead of just 'being free from need'
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a past tense verb. This word is being negated by ما in the ayah, so it means 'did not avail'.

steps.m: from you all (being addressed in قَالُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is talking to multiple people. This word is connected to قَالُوا۟ (they said) in the ayah, showing who is being spoken to.

steps.m: to gather or collect things together

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'gathering' by adding vowels
The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence
Added كُمْ to show this gathering belongs to multiple people being spoken to
Added fatha to help with pronunciation and connection to next word

steps.m: and what (questioning the value of their gathering)
steps.n: Added ما with fatha to create a question word. In this ayah, it's used to question what benefit their gathering had.
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense
Changed from past tense 'was' to a command meaning 'be!' by changing the vowels
Added تُمْ at the end to show 'you all' in the past tense
Added vowels (fatha, damma, fatha) to make the root into a past tense verb meaning 'he became big'
Added تَسْتَ to show doing something to yourself (being proud/arrogant), and changed to present tense 'you' form
Added ونَ at the end to show that many people are doing this action
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to multiple people or things that are near. It's like saying 'these people here' while pointing to them.

steps.m: are these ones? (referring to الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question. It's like changing 'these ones' into 'are these ones?' The speaker is asking about specific people (الَّذِينَ - those who) mentioned in the conversation.
