steps.m: those who/the ones who (pointing to people mentioned in أَهٰٓؤُلَآءِ - 'Are these the ones')
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to connect them. This makes it specific and points back to particular people being discussed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made someone swear/take an oath
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Adding ت at the end shows 'I' did the action instead of 'he'
Changed from 'I swore' to 'you all swore' by adding م at the end
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. Here it's saying that Allah's mercy will 'not' reach them.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Adds pronoun 'them' as object of the verb
Adds damma (ُ) to show this is in the nominative case
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into abstract noun meaning 'mercy'
Adds preposition بِ meaning 'with'
Adds two dots under the last letter to show it's receiving the action 'will not grant' - like saying 'with any mercy at all'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he entered' to 'enter!' by adding u- at start and changing ending - makes it a command to one person
Changed to address multiple people by adding وا at the end and changing the alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ)

steps.m: to cover or conceal (like how a garden's trees cover the ground)

steps.n: The doubled ن shows the covering is complete/intense

steps.m: garden/paradise (a place with lots of covering)
steps.n: Added ة to make it refer to a specific place that has lots of covering (like a garden's shade)
Added ال to show we're talking about THE Paradise, not just any garden
Added fatha at the end because Paradise is the object being entered in this ayah (ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْجَنَّةَ means 'enter Paradise')
This is a negative particle that makes يَنَالُهُمُ (reaches them) negative, so the meaning becomes 'does not reach them'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks َ (fatha) on first letter and ْ (sukun) on middle letter

steps.m: fear (as the subject of لَا...عَلَيْكُمْ - 'there is no fear upon you')
steps.n: Added ٌ (double damma) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. In this ayah, it's saying 'there is no fear upon you.'
This is a common preposition that comes from the idea of being above or elevated. The root letters combine with vowels to make the basic preposition.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
The كُمْ at the end shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to (you all).
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Extended the alif to add emphasis to the negation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before it
This is the basic singular form of the pronoun meaning 'you' when talking to one person
Adding مْ with ُ changes this from addressing one person to addressing many people
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Adds تَ prefix to show it's happening now or in the future and refers to 'you'

steps.m: you (all) feel sad (addressing the people of Paradise)
steps.n: Adds plural masculine ending to show it's addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing the people who will enter Paradise, telling them they won't feel sad.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he called out'
Added alif after first root letter to strengthen the meaning
Added alif maqsurah to elongate the final sound
Added conjunction و at beginning to connect to previous statement

steps.m: to be a close friend or companion

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make the root into 'he became a companion' in the past tense
Changed from 'he became a companion' to 'companions' by adding أ at start and making it plural. This group of companions are the ones doing the calling out.
Changed و to ا and added fatha to make the noun 'fire'. This pattern often creates nouns from light/heat related roots.
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and shadda on ن because it comes after ٱل
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by أَصْحٰبُ (people/companions) in the phrase 'people of the fire'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb

Adds long alif after first root letter to create active participle meaning 'one who accompanies'. This word is in a possession relationship with النار (the Fire), where أصحاب (companions) is possessed by النار, making it the companions/people of the Fire.
Forms the broken plural of صاحب, indicating a group or collection of companions
The word got a fatha mark at the end because it's receiving the action of 'called out to'
Added vowel marks and doubled the middle letter to show intensity
Transforms from root meaning 'to cover/conceal' to concrete noun meaning 'garden'
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: of the garden, of Paradise (possessed by أَصْحٰبَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or is connected to something else. In this ayah, it shows the people/companions (أَصْحٰبَ) of Paradise.
This is a connecting word that joins أَفِيضُوا (pour forth) to what came before it, similar to how we use 'that' to connect parts of sentences in English

steps.m: to pour out or flow abundantly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show causing action. The middle letter changed from ي to ا because it's a hollow verb.
Changed vowels to present tense pattern. The middle ا changes back to ي in present tense.

steps.m: Pour forth! (plural command to a group)
steps.n: Added وا to make it a command to a group (in this ayah, the people of Hellfire are calling out to the people of Paradise asking them to pour forth water)
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of basic preposition
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is a spelling change that happens when adding endings to على.
Added نا at the end to show who the action is upon (us)
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Added kasra vowel mark for pronunciation.
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word الْمَآءِ (the water). This shows the preposition is linked to what comes after it.
The root letters transform into the common noun 'water'
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific water rather than just any water
Adding kasra shows this word follows مِنَ (from/of). The alif is written with a special mark (آ) because it's a long vowel at the end of the word.

steps.m: or (connecting two options in أَفِيضُوا۟ عَلَيْنَا مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ)
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to form the basic conjunction meaning 'or'. In this ayah, it connects two things that the people of Hell are asking from the people of Paradise: water or other provisions.
Combined the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what)

steps.m: from what (of رَزَقَكُمُ - what He has provided you)
steps.n: The ن changes into م and gets a shadda because it's easier to say. This is like how 'in memory' becomes 'immemory' in fast English speech.

steps.m: to provide with what is needed

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word is part of what was said (قَالُوا - they said) in this ayah.
Added كُمُ at the end to show who received the provision - 'you all'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added pronunciation marks that don't change the meaning
Basic emphatic particle that introduces a statement to make it stronger
Adding shadda makes the emphasis stronger. This particle introduces the statement that 'Allah has forbidden them' in this ayah.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Changed first letter to ٱ and added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to prevent access or make something not allowed

steps.n: The doubled letter ر makes the prevention stronger and more complete
هُمَا means 'them both' - shows who was prevented
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْكٰفِرِينَ (the disbelievers) to show something is upon/against them.

steps.m: to reject, cover up, hide truth

steps.n: Added the vowels 'fatha' (َ) to make it mean 'he disbelieved' in the past
Changes verb into noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Changes from singular (one person) to plural (many people) by adding ين at the end
Makes the word specific and adds final vowel showing its relationship to على
This is the base demonstrative word used to point to or describe someone or something
Added ٱلْ at the beginning with a shadda on the following letter to make it specific
Added ينَ at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word describes the people who took their religion as amusement.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The ت combines with the first root letter to make a shadda, showing more intensity in the action

steps.m: they took for themselves (subject of يَجْحَدُونَ)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is connected to يَجْحَدُونَ (they deny) in the ayah, showing the same group doing both actions.

steps.m: to follow a path or way

steps.n: Added kasra and connected the letters to make a noun meaning 'religion'. This is what is being 'taken' (object) in the phrase 'they took their religion'.
Added fatha to show this is the object being acted upon
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is their religion

steps.m: to divert attention, to amuse oneself

steps.n: Changed the action into a noun showing the result of being diverted or amused
Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb اتَّخَذُوا (they took/adopted)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun instead of a verb
Added و to connect with previous word لَهْوًا (diversion)
Added ًا because this word is being acted upon by the verb اتَّخَذُوا (they took) along with لَهْوًا

steps.m: to deceive or mislead by making something seem better than it is
deceived strongly (by making something seem better)
steps.n: Doubling the ر shows this deception was done thoroughly and effectively

steps.m: it/she deceived (referring to الْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: The ت shows this is feminine because it's talking about worldly life (الْحَيَوٰةُ) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: it deceived them (referring to those who took their religion as amusement)
steps.n: Added هُمُ (them) to show who was deceived - refers back to 'those who' from earlier
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'life' by adding و and ة
Added ٱلْ to make it definite ('the life') and ُ to show it's the subject of غَرَّتْهُمُ (deceived them)

Changed from the root meaning 'near' to make the word 'worldly life'. The و changed to ي to make a special noun form. This is not actually a feminine comparative (closer/nearer) - it's the noun meaning 'this world/worldly life'.
Added ال to make it specific. When we add ال, the د gets doubled (shown by the ّ mark). In this ayah, it's describing 'life' (الحَيَوٰةُ) which appears right before it.
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'a day'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - like saying 'the day' instead of just any day
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what happened before - like saying 'so' or 'then'

steps.m: so today (being acted upon by نَنسَىٰ - we will forget)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this day is being acted upon by the verb 'we will forget'. Also, اَلْ is written as ٱلْ when it comes after فَ.

steps.m: to forget or abandon something deliberately

steps.n: Connected root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'we' form and made present tense by adding ن at start. The ى with a small alif on top is added because it's a word ending in ي
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being forgotten. This word relates to نَسُوا (they forgot) later in the ayah, showing a reciprocal forgetting.
This is a comparison particle that shows something is similar to something else
Adding مَا makes the comparison more general, like saying 'like what' instead of just 'like'
Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'
Changed the word to show many people did the action instead of just one person. The ي changed to و because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: to meet or come face to face with someone/something

steps.n: Changed from action to the name of the action itself
Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb نَسُوا (they forgot) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
The kasra ending shows this word will be connected to something that comes after it

steps.m: their day (referring to the day they meet their Lord)
steps.n: The suffix هِمْ shows this day belongs to them. In context, it refers to their meeting with Allah that they denied.
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many pointing words
The alif is written above the ه as a small mark (ٰ) to show it's for nearby things
The ذا part makes it specifically mean 'this' for pointing to something nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing to 'their meeting of this day' (لِقَآءَ يَوْمِهِمْ) that they forgot.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the question word ما

steps.m: and what (referring to the signs they denied)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word asks about the signs (آيات) that were denied.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: they were (referring to those who took their religion as amusement)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people (they) instead of just one person (he). This refers back to those mentioned earlier in the verse who took their religion as amusement.
Forms the basic singular noun by combining the root letters and lengthening the first vowel
Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end
Adds بِ at the beginning to show these signs are being used or dealt with

steps.m: with our signs (object of يَجْحَدُونَ - they deny)
steps.n: Adds نَا to show these are our (Allah's) signs. This word is what is being denied/rejected in the verse.

steps.m: he denied (something known to be true)
steps.n: Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha

steps.m: he denies (something known to be true)

steps.n: The word relates to بِـَٔايٰتِنَا (our signs) which is what is being denied. The ون ending shows this is being done by a group.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain
Adding لَ makes the certainty even stronger, like saying 'most certainly'
Adding وَ connects this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and'
The middle letter ي changes to hamza (ئ) with kasra (ِ) under first letter and sukun (ْ) on hamza
Added نٰ with alif above it to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: we came to them (object of جِئْنٰ)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who we came to. This word is followed by بِكِتٰبٍ (with a book) which explains what we came with.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding alif
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is any book (not a specific one) and that it's being affected by the word before it

steps.m: with a book (connected to جِئْنٰهُم - we brought them)
steps.n: The بِ means 'with' and connects this word to جِئْنٰهُم (we brought them), showing we brought them with a book
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Added نا to show 'we' did the action. The sukun (ْ) is added before نا according to Arabic grammar rules

steps.m: we explained it thoroughly in detail (object of جِئْنٰهُم)
steps.n: Added هُ meaning 'it' as the object being explained. The alif in نا becomes ٰ when connected to هُ. This word is the object of the verb جِئْنٰهُم (we brought to them)
Basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
Added a small alif (ىٰ) at the end to stretch the word, showing it flows into the next word about guidance. Like saying 'upon...guidance'

steps.m: to know deeply or be certain of something

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on second letter. This word follows the preposition عَلَىٰ (upon/with) in the ayah.
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is an indefinite noun following the preposition عَلَىٰ (upon/with).
Changed from basic root meaning 'guide' to a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding marks above and in middle

steps.m: guidance (object of جِئْنٰهُم - we brought to them)
steps.n: Added tanwin and changed final ي to ى to show this is something being given. This word is what was brought (object of جِئْنٰهُم - we brought to them) in this ayah.
Root letters connected with vowels to make the basic noun 'mercy'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of mercy

steps.m: a mercy (object of جِئْنٰهُم/we brought them)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being brought

steps.m: and a mercy (object of جِئْنٰهُم/we brought them)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word to what came before it
Forms the basic noun meaning 'people' from the concept of standing/establishing
Added لِ at beginning to show this is for/to someone, and tanwin at end to show it's indefinite (any people)
Added emphasis to لِ with shadda to connect it with previous word. This people are described in the next word as those who believe.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to change from 'feeling secure' to 'actively putting trust/faith in something'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: do/does/did? (asking يَنظُرُونَ if they are looking)
steps.n: This is a simple word that turns a statement into a yes/no question, like adding a question mark in English. In this ayah, it's asking if they are looking (يَنظُرُونَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed middle vowel to ُ to show present tense

steps.m: they look/watch (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people doing the action
This is a special word that limits what comes before it by excluding what comes after it. Like saying "I like all fruits except apples"

steps.m: to return to the original state/meaning

steps.n: Added vowels (َ) and doubled the middle letter (وّ) to make it mean 'he explained/interpreted'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'interpretation' by adding ت at start and ي after second root letter

steps.m: its interpretation (object of يَنظُرُونَ - they await)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show possession (its) and َ to show this is the object being awaited
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Added يَ at the beginning to show who is doing the action (he) and when (now/future). Added vowel marks to connect the letters.
Added ى at the end to mark this as a present tense verb

steps.m: interpretation (act of returning something to its original meaning)
steps.n: Adding تَ at start and ي in middle creates a noun meaning the act of interpreting - taking something back to its original meaning
The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence
The هُ means 'its'. This word is what 'comes' in the verse (it is doing the action of يَأْتِى - coming/arriving)
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in this pattern
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, ُ after first letter, and ُ at end
Added ال with shadda to make it definite. This turns a general 'who' into 'the one who'

steps.m: those who (subject of نَسُوهُ - they forgot it)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word connects to نَسُوهُ (they forgot it) in the ayah, describing the people who did the forgetting.

steps.m: to forget or leave something behind

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'
Changed vowels and added و to show multiple people doing the action. The ي drops because it's a weak letter meeting و.
Added هُ to show what was forgotten. This word connects to the previous parts of the ayah, referring to the truth/warning they were given.
This is a preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it combines with قَبْلُ (before) to mean 'from before' or 'previously'
Basic noun form meaning 'before' or 'front'
Adding damma at the end shows this word is in the nominative case
This is a particle that emphasizes the certainty of the past action that follows it (جَآءَتْ - came). It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' before a past action in English.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. Added vowel marks to show past tense.
The alif is made longer to emphasize the action of coming
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine. This word is describing رُسُلُ (messengers) in this ayah.
Changed the word from an action to a person who does that action
Changed from singular 'messenger' to plural 'messengers' by changing the vowels inside the word. This word is the subject of جَآءَتْ (came) in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who really does this action a lot - like a caretaker or guardian

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow (possessed)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) to show this word is owned by or connected to something else
Added نَا at the end to show this is our guardian. This word is connected to رُسُلُ (messengers) in the phrase 'messengers of our Lord'
Added vowels to make it 'he did' in the past tense
Doubled middle letter to make a noun meaning the concept itself
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific truth rather than truth in general

steps.m: with/by the truth (describing how رُسُلُ رَبِّنَا came)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how the messengers came - with/bringing the truth. The ِ at the end shows this word follows بِ.
This is a question word that asks about the existence of something. Here it's asking about potential intercessors (شُفَعَآءَ) who could help.

steps.m: so is there? (asking about شُفَعَآءَ - intercessors)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the start shows this question follows from what was mentioned before - they're asking about intercessors as a consequence of realizing the truth of what the messengers brought.
Combined the preposition ل (for/to) with نا (us) to show something is being done for us or belongs to us

steps.m: surely for us (emphasizing their request for intercession)
steps.n: The shadda (doubled ل) adds emphasis to show how desperately they want intercession on the Day of Judgment
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with قَبْلُ to mean 'from before'

steps.m: one who helps others by speaking for them
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show who does the action

steps.m: those who help others by speaking for them
steps.n: Changed the word pattern to show many people who do this action

steps.m: those who help others by speaking for them (object of لَنَا)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon. In this verse, it's connected to لَنَا (for us) - they are the ones who would intercede for us.
Added basic past tense pattern by connecting letters with fatha
Present tense verb pattern with يَ prefix for 3rd person singular
Plural masculine suffix وا added to indicate multiple male doers
The فَ here shows this is what they wish would happen as a result of having intercessors. The context shows they're asking if they can have intercessors who would then mediate for them.
This is the basic preposition that shows something belongs to someone or is for someone
Added نا which means 'us' to show who the preposition is referring to
The alif is lengthened to show emphasis in the question. This word is part of the phrase فَهَل لَّنَا مِن شُفَعَآءَ (Do we have any intercessors?)

This is a basic coordinating conjunction that connects two possible things the disbelievers are asking for: either to have intercessors who will intercede for them, or to be sent back to do good deeds. The word أَوْ shows these are alternative options they're desperately requesting.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone/something rather than someone doing it
When two same letters come together, we combine them with a shadda
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' and changed vowels to show present tense
The root letters represent the basic idea of doing work or acting
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' as the doers, and changed vowels to show possibility
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before

steps.m: to be different or to change

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else

steps.m: other than (object of نَعْمَلَ - we do)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb نَعْمَلَ (we do). It shows what different actions they wish they could do.
This is a relative pronoun used to connect clauses, like 'that' or 'which' in English. The ل is assimilated with shadda.
The root letters show the basic concept of being or existing
Added fatha vowels to make the past tense form. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did this action in the past
The root letters represent the basic idea of doing work or acting
Adding the vowel marks connects the letters and makes it into a past tense verb
Adding نَ at the beginning shows 'we' are doing the action, and changing the vowels makes it present tense
This is a particle that emphasizes the verb that comes after it (جَآءَتْ). In this ayah, it emphasizes that the messengers definitely came with the truth.

steps.m: to lose or suffer a loss

steps.n: Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Word is complete with plural 'they' ending
This root relates to the essence or core of something, like a person's self or soul
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the singular noun
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding أ at start and changing vowels to make it mean 'many souls'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon

steps.m: their selves (object of خَسِرُوا - they lost)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their selves. This word is what was lost in خَسِرُوا (they lost)
This is the basic meaning showing when someone cannot find the right path
Added fatha and combined the two ل letters with shadda to show something that happened in the past

steps.m: and he lost the way (from them)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this idea with what came before. The 'from them' refers to the false gods they used to worship.
Basic preposition showing separation or distance. In this ayah, it shows what has gone away or been lost from them.

steps.m: away from them (referring to what they used to make up/invent)
steps.n: Adds 'them' to show who is being separated from something. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods or ideas they invented being lost from them.
Basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to things rather than people

steps.m: what/that which (referring to their false claims)
steps.n: The shadda adds emphasis and connects this word to يَفْتَرُونَ (they used to invent), showing that it refers to their false claims

steps.m: to be or exist in a certain state

steps.n: Adding the fatha mark makes this a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا with damma to show multiple people (they) did the action
Adding اِ and ت changes the meaning to doing something deliberately to create something new, in this case making up false things

steps.m: he makes up something false (happening now)
steps.n: Changed to show it's happening in the present time

steps.m: they make up false things (referring to الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Changed from one person to many people. This refers back to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes رَبَّكُمُ ٱللَّهُ - your Lord is Allah)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It's like adding 'really' or 'truly' before an important statement. Here it emphasizes that Allah is truly your Lord.

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture to completion

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb pattern

steps.m: your (all of you) master who completely nurtures and cares for (subject of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The root رَبَبَ (nurture/raise) becomes رَبّ (lord/master) with shadda, then كُمُ (your) is added to show it belongs to all of you
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'. This word connects Allah (اللَّهُ) with what He did (خَلَقَ - created).

steps.m: to create or make something from nothing
he created (subject of the verb that describes what Allah did)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. In this ayah, this word describes what Allah did when He created the heavens and earth.
Adding ا makes it into a noun showing something that rises a lot
Forms the feminine plural by adding ات, showing multiple heavens/skies
Adding ال to make the noun definite
Adding kasra at the end shows this is the object of a preposition
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being created in the verse
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific earth we live on rather than any earth/ground
Adding و connects this word to السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it, showing Allah created both
This is a basic preposition that shows when something happened. Here it connects 'created' with 'six days' to show when the creation happened.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding the kasra vowel helps make this a proper number noun
Added ة to make it feminine form of 'six' (used when counting feminine nouns)
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the word 'days' that follows it
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make a singular noun
Adding أ at the start is the first step to make it plural
Adding shadda (ّ) makes the middle letter stronger for plural

steps.m: days (plural, in a state of being acted upon)
steps.n: Added the -in sound at the end to show these days are being described or counted

steps.m: then (connects ٱسْتَوَىٰ with what came before)
steps.n: This word connects two events in sequence, showing that Allah's rising over the throne happened after He created the heavens and earth. The shadda makes the meaning stronger.

steps.m: to be level, straight, or complete

steps.n: Added vowels (a) to make it past tense 'he became'
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or achieving a state
Changed the ending to ى (alif maqsurah) which is common for past tense verbs
This is a preposition that shows Allah's establishment over His throne in a way that befits His majesty. In Arabic, عَلَى shows something is higher than or above something else.

steps.m: to raise up, elevate, build high

steps.n: The root letters form the word 'throne', and the kasra ending shows it belongs to or is connected to something else (like saying 'throne of...')
The ال makes it refer to a specific throne - in this case, Allah's throne mentioned in the Quran
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا in past tense.
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The middle letter gets sukun, and the last vowel changes to kasra. This shows Allah actively covering the night with the day.
Added vowel marks to make it a basic noun
Added ال with shadda to make it definite. The alif gets the waṣla mark (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object being acted upon by the verb يُغْشِى (covers/enshrouds)
Added vowel marks (fat-ha) between the root letters to make it a noun meaning 'river/stream'
Added alif after second root letter to create a noun meaning the time when light flows
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda
Added fatha at the end to show this is what is being covered by the night in the verse
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This verb describes an ongoing action related to يُغْشِى (he covers).
Adds هُۥ at the end to show what is being sought. The ۥ mark shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show something that has the quality of being quick or intense in pursuit

steps.m: in hot pursuit (describing يَطْلُبُهُ/it pursues it)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the pursuit happens in the phrase 'the night pursues the day'

steps.m: to shine brightly or give off light/heat

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic noun for the bright celestial body
Added ال (the) at the beginning. When ال comes before a word starting with ش, the ل disappears and the ش gets doubled (shown by ّ)
Coordinating conjunction و adds 'and' meaning
Added َ at the end to show this is the object being covered by the night in the phrase 'يُغْشِى ٱلَّيْلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ وَٱلشَّمْسَ' (He covers the day and the sun)

steps.m: to shine with reflected light (like the moon)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic noun for the celestial body
Adding 'al' (ال) at the start makes it mean 'the moon' instead of just any moon

steps.m: and the moon (connected to الشَّمْسَ - the sun)
steps.n: Adding و to connect this word with الشَّمْسَ (the sun) mentioned just before it in the ayah

steps.m: and the moon (object being controlled, along with الشَّمْسَ)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is an object being acted upon in the sentence, parallel to الشَّمْسَ (the sun) which is also being controlled
Root letters ن-ج-م became the noun نَجْم (najm) meaning 'star' by adding the vowels fatHa and sukoon
Changed from single star to plural stars by adding dammas and و
Adds ال (the) at the start and puts a shadda on the ن to mean 'the stars'
Adds و with fatha to connect to previous word
Adds fatha at end to show this is an object of the verb يُغْشِى (covers/envelops)

steps.m: to control or make something serve a purpose

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or to many things

steps.m: being made to serve (describing the sun, moon, and stars)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start changes it from 'making something serve' to 'being made to serve' - like changing from active to passive
Adding ات shows we're talking about multiple things (here: the sun, moon, and stars)

steps.m: things being made to serve (describing the sun, moon, and stars mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: The tanwin (double dots) at the end shows this word is describing multiple things (sun, moon, stars) mentioned earlier
Changed from basic root meaning 'command' into the noun 'a command' by putting a stop mark (sukun) on the middle letter
Added بِ at start to mean 'by/with' and kasra at end to connect with next word
Added هِ at end to show it belongs to Allah (mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added fatha to clarify pronunciation
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative particle to an attention-getting word that emphasizes what follows, like saying 'Listen!' or 'Behold!'
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose. In this ayah, it's connected to أَلَا (indeed/truly) to emphasize what belongs to Allah.
Adding هُ makes it mean 'for Him', referring back to Allah who is mentioned earlier in the ayah as the one who created the heavens and earth.

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something from nothing
creation (the act or process of creating)
steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun that means the process itself, like changing 'create' to 'creation'

steps.m: the creation (specifically referring to Allah's act of creating everything)
steps.n: Adds ال to show we're talking about Allah's specific act of creation mentioned in this ayah

steps.m: the creation (as the subject along with الْأَمْرُ/the command)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is paired with الْأَمْرُ (the command) as subjects in this ayah, meaning both creation and command belong to Allah
Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added the definite article to make it specific
Added و at beginning to connect this word to previous items in the list

steps.m: to bless, make something grow and prosper

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The added alif makes the blessing more emphatic/intensive

steps.m: He is supremely blessed (referring to Allah)
steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to make it extra special - like saying Allah is the most blessed and highest above all. It's like adding 'super' or 'supreme' to the meaning
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific

steps.m: to nurture and develop something to its full potential
one who nurtures and develops completely
steps.n: The doubling of ب shows this is someone who nurtures perfectly and completely
Adding ُ shows this word is doing the action in تَبَارَكَ (being blessed)

steps.m: to know, to mark, to distinguish

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known/distinguished' - referring to a complete realm or world
Forms regular masculine plural to show multiple worlds/realms
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and stretches the 'aa' sound

steps.m: to call out to someone or invite them

steps.n: The root letters د-ع-و were connected with fatha vowels to make the past tense 'he' form. The و at the end turned into ا because it's at the end of the word.
Changed from 'he called' to 'you call!' by adding u- at start. The final -aa sound drops in commands.

steps.m: call out! (command to all of you) (to رَبَّكُمْ)
steps.n: Added وا to make it plural command. The hamza changed to wasl form (ٱ) because it's at the start of a command. This word is connected to رَبَّكُمْ (your Lord) which is what they are commanded to call upon.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and sustains completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery and lordship in nurturing and sustaining
Adds كُمْ to show the Lord belongs to or relates to multiple people being addressed. This word is what is being called upon in the command ٱدْعُوا۟ (call upon)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding تَ and doubling ر shows doing the action to oneself with extra intensity
Changed the verb into a noun showing the act itself

steps.m: in intense humility (describing how to call upon رَبَّكُمْ)
steps.n: Added tanween to make it describe how the action ٱدْعُوا۟ (call upon) is done - with complete humility
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and added ة to make it a noun showing the state of being hidden
Added tanween to make it describe how the action is done (adverb)

steps.m: and secretly (describing how to call upon رَبَّكُمْ)
steps.n: Added و to connect with previous word تَضَرُّعًا (humbly), showing two ways to call upon your Lord
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it
The ه at the end means 'he' or 'it'. This word إنّه (indeed he/it) is a complete phrase that introduces the following statement, but is not directly related to other words through case.
Added the missing vowel marks to show exactly how it's pronounced
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper showing that the هُ is pronounced with a long 'u' sound
Basic negative word that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it negates يُحِبُّ (loves) to mean 'does not love'
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
The middle letter gets doubled (shown by shadda) to show stronger meaning
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show present tense. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'he does not love'

steps.m: to go beyond limits, to transgress

steps.n: Connected root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Form VIII pattern shows doing action intensively or for oneself
Active participle form shows the doer of the action
The ين ending makes it plural
Added ال for definition and final fatha because it's the object of the verb يُحِبُّ (loves) in لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (He does not love the transgressors)
This is the basic negative particle that stops something from happening. Here it's telling people not to do the action تُفْسِدُوا (spread corruption).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding ت prefix and changing vowels
Changed to plural 'you' by adding وا at the end
This is a basic preposition that shows location. Here it connects the verb 'corrupt/spread corruption' (تُفْسِدُوا) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ), showing where the action happens.
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to make it a proper word
Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in)
Added vowel marks to create the word meaning 'after'. This word connects to إِصْلٰحِهَا (its reformation/fixing) to show something happens after something else.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he fixed' into 'the act of fixing' by adding إِ at start and ا after second letter

steps.m: its fixing/improvement (referring to الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: The word comes after بَعْدَ (after) which puts it in the genitive case (with ِ ). The هَا refers back to الْأَرْضِ (the earth) which was mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to call upon, invite, or pray to

steps.n: Added ٱ at start and damma (ُ) on middle letter to make it a command. The sukun (ْ) on first letter helps pronunciation.
Added هُ meaning 'Him' to show who is being called upon (Allah in this context)

steps.m: and call upon Him! (connected to not spreading corruption)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the previous command about not spreading corruption (لَا تُفْسِدُوا)

steps.m: in fear (object of ادْعُوهُ - call upon Him)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how to call upon Him - with fear
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he eagerly desired', not a noun
Adds conjunction meaning 'and' to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and in hope (describing how to call upon اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adding tanwin alif (ًا) makes this describe how the action is done. This word pairs with خَوْفًا to show we should call upon Allah with both fear and hope.
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to help with pronunciation
Added shadda (ّ) to make the meaning stronger. This word emphasizes that Allah's mercy is truly close to those who do good.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning mercy
Added ة to make it a specific instance of mercy
Changed the ending to show this word is connected to 'Allah' in the phrase 'mercy of Allah'
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The Deity' - the one and only God. The ال makes it specific and the word became a proper noun.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to رَحْمَتَ (mercy). The shadda emphasizes the ل. The hamza at the start became a joining hamza (ٱ) because it connects with the word before it.
Changed from root letters to a simple word meaning 'near' by adding the vowels a-i
Added ي to make it a stronger descriptive word

steps.m: near (as a quality of Allah's mercy)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this describes Allah's mercy (رَحْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ) mentioned in the ayah
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
The shadda is added because this word comes after a word ending in a similar sound, making it easier to pronounce
The fatha is added at the end to connect this word to the next word المحسنين (the good-doers), showing that it's describing who or what something is from
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is something someone does
The مُ prefix turns the verb 'to do good' into 'one who does good' (like changing 'help' to 'helper')
Makes the word definite, referring to a specific good-doer
Changes the word to refer to multiple people who do good deeds
The َ at the end shows this word follows مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb يُرْسِلُ (sends/releases).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a special word (demonstrative) that points to something or someone specific. It's one of the basic building blocks in Arabic that helps connect ideas together.

Adding ٱلَّ (which is a special form of ال) makes it more specific. The doubled letter (ّ) makes the sound stronger. This word connects to يُرْسِلُ (sends) in this ayah, pointing to Allah who sends the winds.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

Changes to present tense by adding يُ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, kasra on second root letter, and damma at end. This word is connected to الرِّيٰحَ (the winds) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Basic concept of wind or moving air
The و changes to ي and takes kasra to make it a noun meaning one wind
Added ا after ي to make it plural, like how 'book' becomes 'books'
Added ٱل at beginning to mean 'the' and doubled the ر with shadda
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being sent. The alif is written as ٰ when it comes after ي.

steps.m: to give good news or glad tidings

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter to make it mean the thing itself rather than the action
Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being sent - Allah sends the winds as good news before His mercy
Added vowel marks to form the word meaning 'between'. This word is possessed by يَدَىْ (hands/before) to form the phrase بَيْنَ يَدَىْ which means 'before/ahead of' literally 'between the hands of'.
Basic root meaning a hand or power/ability
Connected the letters and added fatha to show basic noun form
Added ى with sukun at the end to show this means exactly two hands. This word comes before 'His mercy' in the ayah, showing these are the two hands of His mercy.
Changed from a root meaning 'show kindness' into a simple noun meaning 'kindness' by adding the vowel marks (fatHa and sukun)
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific instance of mercy
Added ِ at the end to connect it to the next word, showing 'mercy of' something
Added هِ to show this is Allah's mercy (the ۦ is just a pronunciation mark)
This is the basic form of the word that shows a limit or endpoint
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger and more definite
The ى with alif above it (ىٰ) and the small alif (ٓ) at the end show this word connects to what comes after it (إِذَآ meaning 'when'). Together they mean 'until when'
This is the basic form meaning a point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future events instead of past ones
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense form with shadda on middle letter for emphasis

steps.m: it caused to lift/carry (subject: الرِّيٰحَ - the winds)
steps.n: Added 'أ' at start makes it mean 'caused to lift' instead of just 'lifted' - like the winds making the clouds rise up

steps.m: it (feminine) lifted/carried (subject: الرِّيٰحَ - the winds, object: سَحَابًا - clouds)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت because الرِّيٰحَ (winds) is feminine in Arabic. The winds are lifting the clouds (سَحَابًا).
Added vowels to connect the root letters together, making it a past tense verb

steps.m: clouds (something that is dragged across sky)
steps.n: Changes verb into noun for the thing being dragged (clouds across sky)
Shows this word is being acted upon by أَقَلَّتْ (carried/lifted) in the verse
Changed the word to show multiple heavy things by adding specific vowels and an alif
Added an -an ending to show this word is describing the clouds (makes it match with clouds/سَحَابًا)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels. The middle و becomes ا in this pattern.
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by adding نٰ at the end and changing the middle vowel

steps.m: we drove it (referring to the heavy clouds mentioned earlier)
steps.n: The هُ means 'it' referring back to the heavy clouds (سَحَابًا ثِقَالًا) mentioned earlier
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a noun meaning a specific place of settlement

steps.m: to a town/city (destination of سُقْنٰهُ - we drive it)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show direction/destination. Added tanwin (ٍ) at end to show it's indefinite (any town). This word shows where Allah drives the clouds.
Root م-و-ت (death) changed to مَّيِّت (dead) by adding shadda marks and vowels to make it mean 'something that is dead'
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word is describing something in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding hamza at the start makes the action causative - instead of something coming down by itself, someone is making it come down. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
نا at the end shows that 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.

steps.m: and then we made (something) move down (after gathering the clouds in the previous part of the ayah)
steps.n: The فَ shows this action happened as a result of something before it
Basic preposition indicating how something is done

steps.m: with it (referring to water in this ayah)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (it) to show what is being used. In this ayah, it refers to the water (الْمَآءَ) that Allah sends down.
The root letters change to make the common word for 'water' - the middle و becomes ا and the last letter becomes ء
Added the definite article ال to specify 'the water'. The alif is lengthened with a مد (stretch mark) because it's followed by hamza

steps.m: the water (object of أَنزَلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object that receives the action of 'sending down' in the phrase 'we sent down the water'
Adding vowels to create basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause someone/something to exit'
Adding نا at end shows 'we' did the action
Added kasra (ِ) to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to the rain cloud)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (it) to show what is being acted upon. Here it refers back to the rain cloud (سَحَابًا) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to the rain cloud)
steps.n: Added a small ي (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
This is a helper word that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with 'fruits' (ٱلثَّمَرٰتِ) to show we're bringing forth FROM all kinds of fruits.
The doubled ل with damma makes it mean 'all' or 'every'
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to 'fruits' that comes after it
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific fruit
Makes the word plural to show multiple fruits. This word comes after مِن (from) in the ayah.
Adding ال makes it refer to specific fruits. The shadda (ّ) appears on the ث because it comes after ال.
The ِ at the end shows this word is owned by or connected to something else. The alif becomes long (ٰ) in the final form.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word. The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) in this form
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ at the end completes the demonstrative word 'that'

Adding كَ at the beginning makes it mean 'like that'. This word connects to نُخْرِجُ (We bring out) in the next part of the ayah, showing that just as Allah brings forth fruits from dead land, similarly He brings forth the dead (on the Day of Resurrection)
Connected letters with fatha vowels to make past tense
Added alif at beginning to make the action causative - showing someone making something else do the action
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and ending with damma
Changed the root into a plural noun describing those who have died. This word is the object (what is being brought out) of نُخْرِجُ (we bring out) in this ayah.
The definite article ٱلْ makes the word specific
The dagger alif (ٰ) is added to show that the ى at the end is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound rather than an 'ee' sound
Adding shadda (ّ) on ل makes the word stronger, showing it's an important word that connects what comes before with what comes after

steps.m: perhaps you all/so that you all might (connected to تَذَكَّرُونَ)

Adding تَ at start shows someone is doing this to themselves
The ُ at the end shows this is happening now
The ون ending changes it from 'he' to 'you all' and shows multiple people are doing the remembering/reflecting

steps.m: to settle, live in a place

steps.n: Root letters turned into a simple noun meaning 'land' by adding fathas
Shows this is the doer of the action 'comes forth' (يَخْرُجُ) in this verse
Makes the noun definite
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what comes before it
Added vowels (َ and ِ) to the root letters to make an adjective meaning 'good/pure'
Adding the double mark (ّ) makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: the good, the pleasant, the pure
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning to point to something specific
The ُ shows this word is doing the action in يَخْرُجُ (comes forth)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ on middle and end. This describes how the vegetation (نَبَاتُهُۥ) emerges from the good land.

steps.m: to sprout, grow from the ground

steps.n: Root letters n-b-t became a noun 'vegetation' by adding vowels. The damma ending shows it's doing the growing.
Adds possessive pronoun suffix to show ownership. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَخْرُجُ (comes forth/grows).
Changed from a verb meaning 'to allow' into a noun meaning 'permission' by adding إِ at the start and making the middle letter silent
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'by/with' and ِ at end to connect it to the next word (رَبِّهِۦ)
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
Adding kasra shows this lord belongs to or is connected to something
Adding هِۦ shows this Lord belongs to him. This word comes after 'permission' (إِذْنِ) in the ayah, forming the phrase 'by permission of his Lord'
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to things
Adding ال (the) turns 'who/which' into 'the one who/which', making it point to something specific

steps.m: and the one which (connects to خَبُثَ)
steps.n: The وَ connects this to what came before, and ى is an alternate spelling of ي at the end of words. This word connects to خَبُثَ (became bad) in the ayah.
The vowels َُ show something became bad or corrupt
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَخْرُجُ (it comes out) into 'it does not come out'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and damma on second root letter. This word describes what نَبَاتُهُۥ (its vegetation) does.

steps.m: except (connects to لَا يَخْرُجُ - does not come out)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows an exception - it connects what comes before it with what comes after it, excluding something from a general rule. Here it connects to لَا يَخْرُجُ (does not come out) to show that this is the only way something comes out.
Added vowels to make it describe a state or quality

steps.m: scarcely, with difficulty (describes how يَخْرُجُ happens)
steps.n: Added tanween (double marks) to make it describe 'how' something happens - like adding 'ly' in English
Basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ل with kasra to change 'this' to 'that', showing something farther away
Added ك at the end to complete the demonstrative word
Added كَ at beginning to mean 'like/similar to'. This word connects to نُصَرِّفُ (We explain in detail) to show how Allah explains His signs.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repetitive
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, changed middle vowel to kasra and added damma at end for present tense. This word connects to آيَات (signs) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة changes it from a verb root into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Made the word plural to show multiple signs

steps.m: the signs, verses (object of نُصَرِّفُ - we explain/detail)
steps.n: Added 'the' and the kasra ending to show this is being acted upon by the verb نُصَرِّفُ (we explain/detail)
Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added لِ at the beginning to show this word is being used to express 'for' or 'to'
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this means 'a people' rather than 'the people'. This word is connected to يَشْكُرُونَ (they give thanks) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic particle that shows certainty or completion of an action

steps.m: Indeed certainly (followed by أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis. This combination is often used at the start of important statements in the Quran.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza and sukun to show causing someone else to do the action. This verb is acting on نوحاً (Noah) who is being sent to his people.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The verb is acting on نوحاً (Noah) who appears after this word in the ayah.
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show standard pronunciation of the name

steps.m: Noah (as the object of أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this is the object of the verb 'we sent' (أَرْسَلْنَا)
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It doesn't come from a root.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word. In this ayah, it connects 'Noah' to 'his people', showing who Noah was sent to.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long 'aa' sound at the end.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' or 'nation' - those who stand/are established together
Adding kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Adding هِ ('his') at the end shows who the people belong to. The word comes after إِلَىٰ (to) making it 'to his people'
The ۦ after هِ makes the 'his' sound longer when reading
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). The fatha vowels cause this change.

steps.m: and then he said (connected to أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: The فَ shows this statement follows after the previous action of sending Noah. It connects these two events in sequence.

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands/gathers together'
The vocative particle يٰ is added at the start to address someone directly, equivalent to 'O!' in English

steps.m: O my people! (being addressed by نُوحًا)
steps.n: The kasra suffix shows possession ('my people'). This is Nuh (Noah) addressing his people in the ayah.
Changed from basic root to a command form by adding 'u' at start, telling someone to worship
Added وا at the end to make the command apply to a group instead of one person
Added emphasis to middle letter to show this is the one true God
Changed first letter to special alif and added fatha at end to show Allah is the one being worshipped in this sentence

steps.m: not (negates the existence of any god besides اللَّهَ)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that works with the words after it to say there is no other god. In this ayah, it connects with لَكُم مِّنْ إِلٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ to mean 'there is no god for you except Him.'
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose
Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This phrase tells who should do the worship mentioned in اعْبُدُوا (worship!)
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something. The kasra (ِ) is added under the م to help pronounce it.
The م sound is doubled because it comes right after a word ending in ـه, making it easier to pronounce
The sukun (ْ) is added on the ن to show where to stop the sound. This word connects to إِلٰهٍ (god) to say "from/of any god".

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'a god' by adding the vowel marks. Like changing 'worship' into 'the one who is worshipped'
The small alif (ٰ) makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any god in general, not a specific one. This word follows مِنْ (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added damma to show this is the subject form, describing إله (deity)
Added هُ to mean 'Him', referring back to Allah
Added lengthening mark ۥٓ after هُ to emphasize the pronoun
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds certainty to what follows
Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me/I'. This word connects to أَخَافُ (I fear) in the next part of the ayah.
Changed the final ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it. This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to feel fear or worry about something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from past tense 'he feared' to present tense 'I fear'. This is part of Prophet Nuh's speech to his people where he's expressing his current worry for them.
This is a common preposition in Arabic meaning 'upon' or 'on'. Unlike most words, it doesn't come from a three-letter root.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding endings
The suffix كُمْ shows this is addressing multiple people (you all)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is the object (what is feared) of the verb أخاف (I fear) in this ayah.
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being feared

steps.m: to pass time or spend time during daylight

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'day'
Added two dots under the last letter to show it's 'a day' that connects to describe what kind of punishment

steps.m: to be great/mighty in size or status

steps.n: Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing something becoming great

steps.m: great/mighty (describing عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ - punishment of a day)
steps.n: Changed the verb into an adjective by adding ي between second and third root letters. This describes the punishment mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: great/mighty (describing عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ - punishment of a day)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to match the word it describes (عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ)

steps.m: he said (subject of إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ - indeed we see you)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is what introduces what the chiefs said to their prophet.
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ because of weak letter و - this is a common change in hollow verbs
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Added 'the' (ال) to change it from a verb (to gather) into a noun meaning a specific group of important leaders

steps.m: the noble assembly/council (subject of قَالَ - said)
steps.n: Added damma to show this group is doing the action of saying (قَالَ) in this verse
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'the chiefs' (ٱلْمَلَأُ) to 'his people' (قَوْمِهِ), showing the chiefs were from among his people.

steps.m: to stand firm, to rise up

steps.n: Changed root meaning into a noun for a group that stands together
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is possessed by something
Added ه to show these are his people
Added ۦ after ه to show proper pronunciation of 'his'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger
Adding ا shows this is about "we/us" - this is a shortened form of نا

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Root letters changed to make past tense 'he saw' - last letter becomes ى
Changed from 'he saw' to 'we see' by adding نَ at start to show 'we' are doing the action
Added لَ at start to show emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly'
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at end to show this is from a root ending in ي
Added كَ at end to show who is being seen (you)
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else

steps.m: in (connects ضَلٰلٍ 'error' to نَرَىٰكَ 'we see you')
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it connects 'we see you' to 'error', showing where they claim to see the person - in error.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
Changed verb into a noun showing the state or action of going astray. This word follows the preposition فِى (in) in this ayah.
The word gets two dots under it (ٍ) because it comes after في, making it mean 'a straying' instead of just 'straying'

steps.m: to make clear or separate things

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he made clear' in the past tense
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show something that does the action of making clear. In this ayah, it's describing the misguidance (ضَلٰلٍ) as being clear/obvious.

steps.m: one who/that makes clear (in genitive case)
steps.n: Added ٍ at end to show this word is indefinite and in genitive case because it's describing ضَلٰلٍ which is also genitive

steps.m: one who/that makes very clear (in genitive case)
steps.n: Adding shadda on م makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: he said (subject of يٰقَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِى ضَلٰلَةٌ...)
steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form. This word is the main verb introducing what he said to his people.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي as middle letter)
Changes from verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Adds kasra at end to show this word is being possessed or follows certain prepositions

steps.m: O my people! (being called by نُوحٌ)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show Nuh is calling out to address his people directly
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.
This is a special negation word that works like a verb but only comes in this form. In this ayah, it's negating the word ضَلٰلَةٌ (misguidance), saying 'there is no misguidance in me'.
This is a basic connecting word (preposition) that shows how something is connected to something else

steps.m: with me (object of لَيْسَ - there is not)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this applies to 'me'. This word is being affected by لَيْسَ (there is not) in the sentence, which is why it has the special ِ mark.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a past-tense verb (he went astray) to a noun meaning the state of being lost, like changing 'strayed' to 'straying'

steps.m: a specific instance of being misguided
steps.n: Added ة to show this is one specific case of being misguided
Added tanween to show this is indefinite. In this ayah, it's part of a response to 'O my people' (يٰقَوْمِ), saying there is no misguidance in the speaker.
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast between ideas
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning
وَ (and) was added at the start to connect this contrasting statement with what came before
Adding ى shows I am speaking about myself. The vowel before ى changes to match it.
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added double damma to show this is indefinite, meaning 'a messenger' rather than 'the messenger'
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
The م gets doubled (shadda) to help connect smoothly with the word before it. Like adding a bridge between words when speaking.

steps.m: to nurture, sustain, develop, care for

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship
Adding kasra to show this word is possessed by or connected to what follows it (the worlds)
Added alif to create a noun meaning 'that which is known' - referring to the created world/universe
Added sound masculine plural ending to show multiple worlds

steps.m: the worlds (referring to all created realms)
steps.n: Added definite article ٱلْ to specify 'the worlds' and case ending َ because it follows رَّبِّ (Lord of) in the ayah
The doubled letter ل shows this is done with extra effort or importance
The أُ at the start means 'I' do the action

steps.m: I deliver/convey to you all (رِسٰلٰتِ رَبِّى)
steps.n: The كُمْ shows this is being delivered to multiple people. This word connects to رِسٰلٰتِ رَبِّى (messages of my Lord) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Added alif and taa marbouta to create a noun for one specific instance of sending (a message)
Changes from one message to many messages by adding ات

steps.m: to care for and develop something/someone to their full potential
one who completely cares for and develops others (master/lord)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does this caring perfectly and completely
Adding the 'i' sound at the end shows this word is connected to another word that owns/possesses it (like 'my lord' or 'lord of')

steps.m: my Lord (the one speaking in أُبَلِّغُكُمْ - I convey to you)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord. This word is being said by the same person who is speaking in أُبَلِّغُكُمْ (I convey to you).
Connected the root letters with fathas to make it a past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to make it mean 'more' or 'most' sincere

steps.m: and I am more sincere in advising (to لَكُمْ - to you all)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect it with the previous statement. This word is connected to لَكُمْ (to you all) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show this is connecting something to someone. Here it connects the advice (أَنصَحُ) to who receives it.

steps.m: to/for you all (object of أَنصَحُ - I advise)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who the advice is being given to - all the people being addressed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone know'
Adding ُ at end shows this is present tense 'I' form

steps.m: and I know (connected to أُبَلِّغُكُمْ - I convey)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this verb with the previous action in the verse
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one supreme deity. This makes it a proper name for the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word مِنَ (from) before it. The alif at the start changes to a wasl form (ٱ) because it connects to the previous word.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to the knowledge that Allah has given to Nuh (AS) that his people don't have. It's the object of the verb أَعْلَمُ (I know).
Basic negative particle that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) into 'you do not know'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The ت at the start shows this is happening now (present tense) and the person doing it is 'you'. The vowel pattern changes to show it's happening now.

steps.m: you (all) know (referring to the people being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: The suffix ونَ shows that many people are being addressed, not just one person
Added vowels to make past tense verb
Added تُمْ at the end to change from 'he' to 'you all' in the past tense
Added conjunction وَ to connect with previous statement

steps.m: are you (all) amazed? (connected to أَن جَآءَكُمْ)
steps.n: Added أَ to make it a question. This question is about the fact that a reminder came to them, as shown in the next part of the verse (أَن جَآءَكُمْ)

steps.m: that (connects to جَآءَكُمْ - came to you)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a new clause. Here it connects 'are you amazed' to 'that a reminder came to you.' The fatha mark helps with pronunciation.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. Added fatha after first and last letters to show past tense.
The alif is written with a small hamza above to show emphasis in coming
Added كُمْ at the end to show the action was directed at multiple people being spoken to
Changed into a noun meaning 'reminder' or 'message'. In this ayah, it refers specifically to the reminder/message from Allah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from.
Added a shadda (double letter mark) because this word connects to the word after it in pronunciation. It's connected to رَجُلٍ (a man) in this ayah.
Adding fatha and shadda creates an intensive noun showing complete mastery of the action
When مِن comes before رَبّ, the ر gets doubled (shadda) to show stronger emphasis
Adding kasra shows this word is possessed by what follows

steps.m: your Lord (referring to all those being addressed)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this Lord belongs to all those being spoken to
This is a basic word that shows something being physically or metaphorically above something else. Here it connects to رَجُلٍ (a man) to show the revelation came upon/to a man.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a complete word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
The vowels make it a singular noun meaning 'man'
Added two dots under the last letter to show this man is receiving an action (like 'to a man' or 'of a man')
Added shadda on م for pronunciation because it comes after ذِكْرٌ in ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ

steps.m: from you all (referring to the people being warned)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is happening to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to warn or alert of danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make a basic past-tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show it happens in present/future
Added لِ at start to show purpose - this warning is happening for a reason

steps.m: so that he may warn you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ at end to show who is being warned - it's all of you
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added ت and merged it with doubled letter to show reflexive meaning (doing action to oneself)
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose and تَ to show 'you' as subject
Added وا at the end to show plural 'you all'

steps.m: and so that you (all) protect yourselves
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect with previous phrase
This is a word that shows hope or possibility
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: and perhaps you all (connected to تُرْحَمُونَ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the start to connect this with what comes after it (تُرْحَمُونَ - you may receive mercy). The و here connects the hope of receiving mercy with the previous commands.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Changed from 'he showed mercy' to 'you receive mercy' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show someone else is doing the action
Added plural ending to show multiple people receive the mercy
Doubling the middle letter shows doing the action strongly or repeatedly
Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of something before it
Adding وا shows a group of people did the action
Adding هُ shows who they denied (referring to the messenger mentioned before)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added أ at beginning to show causing someone else to be saved
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who was saved
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before
This is a special word that points to someone or something. Think of it like saying 'the one who' in English.
Changed from talking about one person to many people by adding ين at the end
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous part, like saying 'and'
This is a preposition showing companionship

steps.m: with him (referring to نَجَيْنٰهُ - the one who was saved)
