steps.n: Added هُ (him) at the end to show who is being accompanied. The ۥ mark shows this is a long vowel. This refers back to the person who was saved (نَجَيْنٰهُ) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows Noah and his followers were inside the ship.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in the Quran's writing style. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: ship (from the idea of something that moves in circles/orbits)
steps.n: The word for ship comes from the idea of a vessel that moves in circular paths through water
Adding ال makes it specific (the ship), while the ِ at the end shows it follows the word فِى (in)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes 'he drowned' to 'he made someone/something drown'
Adding نا suffix indicates 'we' as subject

steps.m: and we caused to drown (the people who denied Our signs)
steps.n: This word connects to the previous verse showing the sequence of events: first saving the believers, then drowning those who denied
Added ال with shadda to make it definite. This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of sentences.

steps.m: those who (refers to the group who were with him)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the group who were with him in the ship.

steps.m: to deny or reject something as false
to strongly deny or reject (something as false)
steps.n: Adding a shadda (ّ) on ذ makes the denial stronger or more emphatic, like saying 'completely reject' instead of just 'reject'
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action. The small mark after وا (۟) is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: The root letters combine and change to make the word for 'a sign' - like how a sign shows or makes something clear
Changes the word to plural form to show multiple signs
Adds the preposition 'with/by' to show how they denied the message
Adds 'our' to show these were Allah's signs that were denied. The long alif at the end shows emphasis.
Adding shadda makes this word stronger - it changes from a word meaning 'if' to one that emphasizes that something is definitely true.

steps.m: indeed they (refers to those who denied Our signs)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being talked about - in this case, it refers to the people who denied Allah's signs mentioned in the previous part of the ayah.

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state or condition)

steps.n: Added fathas to show past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas.

steps.m: they were (referring to those who denied Our signs)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.

steps.m: to stand together, to rise up

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'

steps.m: a people, a nation (acted on by كَانُوا)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted on by كَانُوا (they were). The ًا also shows we're talking about any people/nation, not a specific one.

steps.m: to be spiritually blind/unable to see truth

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense verb showing state of being spiritually blind
Added ين ending to show this describes multiple people who rejected the signs. This word describes 'those who denied' (الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the word عَادٍ (the people of 'Aad) to show the message was sent to them.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end which is a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to return or go back repeatedly

steps.n: Adding ا makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he returned'

steps.m: Aad (as the object being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed to show the name of the ancient tribe of Aad when it appears after the word 'to' (إلى) in this ayah
Connected the letters and dropped the weak letter و to make the basic word for 'brother'
Added ا at the end because this word is the object of the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is their brother. This word is the object of the preposition إِلَىٰ (to)

steps.m: to return to the right path

steps.n: Adding damma changes it from action to the person who receives guidance

steps.m: Hud (as their brother who was sent to them)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show Hud is the direct object (the one being sent as their brother). Like saying 'sent Hud' instead of just 'Hud'.
Added fathas to make past tense form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is very common in hollow verbs
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The root meaning of standing together became associated with a group of people who stand as one.

steps.m: O my people! (being called by هُودًا)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show calling out to someone, and ِ at the end to show possession (my people). This is Hud calling out to his people.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worshipped' to 'worship!' by adding ٱ at start and changing middle vowel to 'u' - this makes it a command to one person
Added وا at the end to make it a command to multiple people
Added ال to make it specific and unique, then merged letters according to Arabic rules. This is a unique name that refers to the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of اعْبُدُوا - worship)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark (fatha) at the end because it's receiving the action of worship. Think of it like: '(You) worship Allah'

This is a negative particle that makes the statement 'you have a god' into 'you do not have a god'. In this ayah, it works with لَكُم (for you) and مِّنْ إِلٰهٍ (any god) to say there isn't any god except Him.
This is a basic particle meaning 'for' or 'to' that shows purpose or direction
Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added shadda on م and sukun at end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word إِلٰهٍ (god)

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as divine

steps.n: Added alif after second root letter to create a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (any god) and possessed by something mentioned after it

steps.m: to be different from or change

steps.n: Changed from basic root to a noun meaning 'other than' by adding vowel marks
Added pronoun هُ meaning 'him/his' to show who is being referred to
This particle turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or concern
The فَ shows this question follows from what was said before - in this case, asking about their response to the previous statement about Allah

steps.m: will you not then...? (connects to تَتَّقُونَ)
steps.n: Adding لا makes this a negative question, expressing concern about why they are not doing something. This connects to the next word تَتَّقُونَ (fear/protect), asking 'will you not then fear/protect yourselves?'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

The word gains two ت letters at the start (one combines with the other to make تّ with shadda) to mean 'you' instead of 'he'. The و is dropped and the ي at the end makes the word mean 'protect yourself'
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it's addressing the people being warned to protect themselves from Allah's punishment by following His commands.

steps.m: he said (subject of إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ - 'indeed we see you')
steps.n: Added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. The و changes to ا between first and second letters because it's a hollow verb. This word is connected to what comes after it - it introduces what the chiefs said.

steps.m: to gather, assemble, or be filled (especially with people)

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb

steps.m: the assembly, the chiefs (subject of قَالَ - said)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) to change from 'gathered' (verb) to 'the assembly/chiefs' (noun) - these are the people speaking

steps.m: the assembly, the chiefs (subject of قَالَ - said)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of saying (قَالَ)
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who' or 'which'. We start with this since it's a special word without a root.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people or things
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'ones who'
Added final vowel marks and changed spelling slightly. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) to tell us who did the disbelieving.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense singular form. This word describes what the chiefs (الْمَلَأُ) did.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (the chiefs) did this action together

steps.m: from (connecting قَوْمِهِۦٓ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a helper word that shows something comes from somewhere. In this ayah, it connects 'his people' (قَوْمِهِۦٓ) to what came before, showing where the disbelieving chiefs were from.
Changed the root into a noun meaning a group that stands together
Added kasra to show this word is owned by something and comes after the word مِن (from)
Added هِ to show these are his people. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
Added ۦٓ after هِ to show proper pronunciation of 'his'
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to a word that adds emphasis to what comes after it

steps.m: indeed we (subject of نَرَىٰكَ - we see you)
steps.n: Added نا at the end in the form of ا to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to نَرَىٰكَ (we see you) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ن (we) at start and changed to present tense 'see' instead of past tense 'saw'
Adds emphatic particle لَ to indicate certainty/emphasis

steps.m: surely we see you (object of seeing)
steps.n: Adds كَ to show who is being seen. This word appears in a statement from disbelievers to their prophet, saying they see him in foolishness.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to the word سَفَاهَةٍ (foolishness) to show the state they see him in.
Added vowel marks (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it mean 'he acted foolishly' in the past
Changed the verb into a noun describing the quality of being foolish

steps.m: in foolishness (describing how they see the person in فِى سَفَاهَةٍ)
steps.n: Added two dots under (tanween) to show 'in a state of foolishness' - like saying someone is 'in sadness' or 'in happiness'
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which shows certainty
When we add 'we' (نا) to إِنَّ, it becomes إِنَّا - the نا shortens to just ا after the double letter

steps.m: and indeed we (subject of لَنَظُنُّكَ - we surely think you)
steps.n: Adding و to connect this phrase to what came before. This word is the subject of the verb لَنَظُنُّكَ (we surely think you) that comes after it.

steps.m: to think/believe something without being certain

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels and doubled the final ن with shadda
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Added لَ at start to add emphasis - like saying 'surely' or 'certainly'
Added كَ at end to show who is being thought about (you)
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added َ (fatha) at the end because it connects to the word ٱلْكٰذِبِينَ (the liars) that comes right after it. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from a verb (to lie) to a noun meaning 'liar'
Adding ين makes it plural (more than one)

steps.m: the ones who tell untruths (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific. The word comes after مِنَ (from) in this ayah

steps.m: he said (subject of يٰقَوْمِ لَيْسَ... - what he said follows)
steps.n: Added fathas to make past tense verb. This shows who did the action (he) and when it happened (in the past)
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.

steps.m: to gather, come together, rise up

steps.n: Added vowels to make the root into a noun meaning 'people' - those who gather or stand together as a community
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something or comes after a preposition
Added يٰ at the beginning to make this a direct address - calling out to 'my people'
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. Here it's saying 'there is not foolishness in me' in the ayah.
This is a preposition that connects words

steps.m: with me (object of لَيْسَ - is not)
steps.n: Added ى which means 'me'. This word is the object of لَيْسَ (is not) in the phrase 'there is not with me foolishness'
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he became foolish/unwise' in the past tense
Changed from verb 'he lacked wisdom' to noun meaning 'the state of being foolish'
Added ة to make it into an abstract quality
Added tanween dammah to make it indefinite nominative case
This is a basic connecting word that shows contrast with what came before. In this ayah, it contrasts with the previous statement about foolishness.
وَ at the start means 'and'
The ى at the end means 'I' or 'me', requiring kasra before it
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role

steps.m: a messenger (who is speaking in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the main subject doing the speaking in this part of the ayah
This is a fundamental preposition particle that shows origin or source. It has no derivational steps as it's not derived from a root.
Added shadda to show this word connects with the next word. In this ayah, it connects 'messenger' (رَسُولٌ) with 'Lord' (رَّبِّ) to say 'messenger from the Lord'.

steps.m: to nurture, sustain, develop, care for

steps.n: The doubled letter shows this is a permanent, strong quality
The kasra shows this word is connected to العالمين (the worlds) that comes after it, showing He is Lord of the worlds
Forms a noun meaning 'world' or 'realm' - the thing that can be known or experienced
Makes the word plural and shows it's owned by رَبِّ (Lord)
Makes it specific by adding ٱلْ (the) at the start and adds َ at the end to show it follows رَبِّ

steps.m: to deliver or convey a message

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intensive or repetitive
Added أُ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action in present tense. This verb is connected to رِسٰلٰتِ (messages) which comes after it.

steps.m: I convey to you all (رِسٰلٰتِ messages)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show the message is being delivered to multiple people
Root meaning 'send' becomes a noun meaning 'sending' by adding vowels (ِ َ) and alif (ا)
Adding ة makes it mean one specific thing that is sent

steps.m: messages (object of أُبَلِّغُكُمْ - I convey to you)
steps.n: Changed to plural form and added ِ to show it's being acted upon by the verb 'I convey' (أُبَلِّغُ)

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow (connected to رِسٰلٰتِ)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) shows this word is connected to رِسٰلٰتِ (messages) in the ayah
Adding ى shows this is my caring lord. This word connects to رِسٰلٰتِ (messages) in the ayah to mean 'messages of my caring lord'
This is a pronoun meaning 'I' - it shows who is speaking and doesn't come from a root
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to the previous statement about delivering messages. The small mark (۠) at the end is just to help with pronunciation.
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb أُبَلِّغُ (I deliver) to show who receives the message.

steps.m: to/for you all (object of أُبَلِّغُ - I deliver)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' to show who is receiving the action. This word connects to أُبَلِّغُ (I deliver) to show who the message is being delivered to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'advised' to 'advisor' by adding alif after first letter

steps.m: a sincere counselor (describing the speaker in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is describing the speaker. In this ayah, the speaker is saying they are a trustworthy advisor to their people.
Transforms the root into an adjective meaning 'trustworthy'. This word is describing نَاصِحٌ (sincere advisor) in this ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is describing someone in a general way
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding تَ at the end
Changed تَ to تُمْ to make the verb plural 'you all'
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question

steps.m: and did you all become amazed?
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this question to what came before it

steps.m: that (connects to جَآءَكُمْ - came to you)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it connects 'Are you amazed' with 'a reminder came to you'. It's like saying 'Are you amazed that a reminder came to you?' in English.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) but pronunciation stays the same
Added كُمْ at the end to show the action was done to multiple people
Changed the action into a thing by adding ِ under first letter and ْ on middle letter
Adding two damma marks at the end shows this is 'a' remembrance (not 'the' remembrance)
Basic preposition showing origin or source. Here it connects 'a man' (رَجُلٍ) with 'you all' (كُمْ) to mean 'from among you'
Added shadda because the next word (كُمْ) starts with ك, which causes the ن to merge into it in pronunciation
Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship

steps.m: O my Lord! (calling upon one's Lord)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end makes it into a form of address/calling out to one's Lord
Added كُمْ to show this Lord belongs to all of you (plural). This word is connected to جَآءَكُمْ (came to you) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to رَجُلٍ (a man) to show the message came upon/to a man.
Added a small alif (ٰ) above the final ى. This is just a writing style to show the correct pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
The vowels make it a singular noun meaning 'man'

steps.m: a man (receiving the action of جَآءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن - a reminder came to you from)
steps.n: Added two dots under (tanwin) to show it's 'from a man' - like saying 'from any man'
Added a shadda to emphasize the ن because it comes before another similar sound
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being talked to
The root letters represent the basic concept of warning
The َ (fatha) at the end shows it's in a special grammar form
Adds 'li' at start to mean 'so that' or 'in order to' - changes from passive to active voice

steps.m: so that he may warn you all

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from 'he remembered' to telling one person to remember (command form)
Added وا to make it a command for many people
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and', and changed اُ to ٱ after و

steps.m: when (connecting to جَعَلَكُمْ - He made you)
steps.n: This is a word that connects two events in time, showing that one happened after another. Here it connects to the next word جَعَلَكُمْ (He made you), showing when Allah made them successors.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added كُمْ to show who the action was done to - in this case, all of you
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed middle vowel to 'i' and added ي to make it mean 'the one who succeeds/follows'

steps.m: successors (after قَوْمِ نُوحٍ - the people of Noah)
steps.n: Changed vowels and added آء at the end to make it plural. This word refers to those who came after Noah's people.

This is a preposition that connects two words, showing that something comes after or from something else. Here it connects 'successors' (خُلَفَآءَ) with 'the people' (قَوْمِ) to show who came after whom.
The root meaning of distance developed into a word meaning 'after' in time or sequence
Adding these vowel marks creates a common noun pattern meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to 'the people of Noah' that comes after it
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning a group that stands together
Added kasra at the end to show these are Noah's people, mentioned in نُوحٍ right after this word in the ayah
This is the basic form of the proper name Noah
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is describing whose people are being talked about in the phrase قَوْمِ نُوحٍ (people of Noah)

steps.m: to increase, add, or make more
steps.n: The middle letter is a weak letter (ي)
Adding vowel marks changes it from a root to a complete verb
The base word زَيَدَ changed to زَادَ (middle letter changed), then كُمْ was added at the end to show 'you all' as the receivers of the action
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the verse

This is a preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects how Allah increased them 'in' their physical form, linking زَادَكُمْ (He increased you) with الْخَلْقِ (the creation/physical form).

steps.m: creation (the act or result of creating)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to turn the verb into a noun meaning the act or result of creating

steps.m: the creation (in فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ - in the creation)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific ('the creation') and ِ because it comes after the word فِى (in/regarding)
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun
Changed from general 'spreading' to one specific instance of being vast/large by adding ة, with ص making the word sound stronger
Added tanween (ً) to show this word is receiving the action of زَادَكُمْ (He increased you) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding alif with damma at start and sukun on first root letter
Added plural ending to make command apply to a group
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from previous statement, and long vowel marker on final و
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (giving) to a plural noun (blessings) that is the object being remembered. The plural form shows multiple blessings.
Changed to the specific name for the One God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on ل, and changed initial alif. This word shows who the blessings (ءَالَآءَ) belong to.
This is the basic form of the particle that expresses hope or expectation for something to happen

steps.m: perhaps you all (expressing hope that you will تُفْلِحُونَ)
steps.n: Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُمْ to show who the hope is directed towards. This connects to the next word تُفْلِحُونَ (succeed/prosper) to complete the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The ون ending shows this is addressing multiple people (you all)
Connected the letters and changed و to ا (alif) because it's between two fathas. This shows the action happened in the past.
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action together
Added small alif (ٓ) and small circle (۟) above as pronunciation helpers - these don't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with basic past tense pattern. The middle letter ي changes to ا due to being a weak letter.
Changed vowels and added past tense 'you' ending. The middle ا returns to ي with sukun due to the addition of تَ.
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
Added نَا ('us') at the end to show they are asking 'did you come to us?'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The نَ at the start shows 'we' are doing the action
The لِ shows this is about purpose - they're explaining why they want to do something (in response to قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا - 'they said: Have you come to us')
Added shadda to show emphasis and changed alif to wasl-alif for pronunciation

steps.m: Allah (as the object of نَعْبُدَ - we worship)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being worshipped in the phrase 'to worship Allah alone'

steps.m: to be alone, to be unique

steps.n: Adds vowel marks to turn root into a usable noun

steps.m: Him alone (object of نَعْبُدَ - we worship)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show this refers to Him (Allah). This whole phrase is being acted on by the verb نَعْبُدَ (we worship) in the ayah.
The small waw after هُ helps show correct pronunciation of the pronoun

steps.m: to leave something or abandon it

steps.n: Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he left'

steps.m: and we left/abandoned (connected to نَعْبُدَ - we worship)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start and changed to نَ (we) form to match نَعْبُدَ (we worship) before it
This is a question word asking about what their fathers used to worship. It's the object being acted upon by the verb يَعْبُدُ (worships) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he was (describing what their forefathers يَعْبُدُ worshipped)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic singular noun
Changes from singular 'father' to plural 'fathers' using a special plural pattern
Changed alif to long alif (آ) for proper pronunciation
Added damma and changed hamza to waaw-hamza for nominative case. This shows it's the subject doing the action.
Added نَا at the end to show possession (our). This word is the subject of يَعْبُدُ (worships) in this ayah.
Changed from basic verb 'to come' into command form by dropping letters and adding kasra
The suffix نا indicates first person plural object (us). This is a command (imperative) verb, so it doesn't have a case ending.

steps.m: so bring us! (responding to their challenge)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this is their response to the prophet's warning - they're challenging him to bring the punishment he warned about
This is a relative pronoun referring to the promise/threat being discussed in the conversation

steps.m: with what (with that which you promise)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows we're asking about the thing being promised. This connects to تَعِدُنَآ (you promise us) in the next part of the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The ت at the start shows 'you' are doing the action. The و is dropped because it's a weak letter in present tense.
Adding نَآ shows that 'us' is receiving the promise. The long alif (آ) at the end shows a pause in reading.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects to the verb كُنتَ (you were) to say 'if you were'. The whole phrase means 'if you were among the truthful ones.'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. The ُ shows it's a basic noun form.
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action in the past. The و is dropped because it's a weak letter between two vowels.
Basic form of the word that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added َ (fatha) at the end because it connects to the next word الصَّادِقِينَ (the truthful ones). Together they mean 'from the truthful ones'
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple truthful people

steps.m: the truthful ones (referring to those who are genuinely truthful in their faith)
steps.n: Adds definiteness and doubles the ص letter due to merging with ال. In this ayah, it refers specifically to those who are truly faithful in their claims.
Added vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'he said'
Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا
This is a special word that adds emphasis to the verb that follows it (وَقَعَ meaning 'has befallen'). It's like saying 'truly' or 'definitely' before telling someone something important.

steps.m: it befell (subject of punishment in this ayah)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, it refers to punishment/anger falling upon the people being addressed.
Basic preposition showing something being above or upon something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun
Adds كُم to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added shadda to match the sound of the next word رَّبِّكُمْ (your Lord). This happens because the words are connected in pronunciation.
Doubling the ب makes the meaning stronger - like going from 'nurturer' to 'ultimate nurturer'
The kasra and doubled letter show this is 'my Lord' - someone's personal Lord

steps.m: your (all) Lord (the one who nurtures you completely)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this refers to your (plural) Lord
Changed from action to noun by adding kasra (ِ) and sukun (ْ). This word is what 'has fallen upon' (وَقَعَ) them in this ayah.
Added tanween damma (ٌ) to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Adding vowel marks creates the basic noun form
Adding tanween (double damma) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'an anger'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with رِجْسٌ (filth) mentioned before it in the ayah
Base root meaning relates to back-and-forth discussion or argument
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif to show the debate goes back and forth between people
Changed from past to present tense and added who is doing the action

steps.m: you all debate back and forth
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added ني at end to show the debate is with 'me'

steps.m: do you all debate with me?
steps.n: Added أَ at start to make it a question
This is a basic word that shows something is inside or about something else. Here it connects to the word أَسْمَآءٍ (names) to show we're talking about names.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing rule and doesn't change the meaning.
Root relates to height or elevation
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into 'he named' in past tense
Changed from 'to name' (verb) into 'a name' (noun) by adding alif at start and dropping last letter
Changed to broken plural form with hamza at beginning
Added dots under the word (tanwin kasra) to show these are specific names being talked about
Added shadda to emphasize the action of naming
Added ي because the root ends in و
Added تُم to show that many people did the action in the past
Added و to show who was being named (him)

steps.m: you all named it/her (referring to the false names/idols)
steps.n: Added هَآ to show what was named (referring back to the false names/idols mentioned in the verse)
This is the basic form of the second person pronoun meaning 'you'
Adding مْ at the end changes 'you' from singular to plural, like changing 'you' to 'you all' in English. The َ (fatha) and ُ (damma) help with pronunciation.

steps.m: to nurture/care for as a father
steps.n: Root relates to fatherhood
Root letters أ-ب-و became the simple noun 'father'
Makes word plural using special pattern for important family terms
Shows this word is the subject (doer) in the sentence
Added كُم to show possession by multiple people
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a negative particle that means 'not' or can be used as 'what' in questions. This is a standalone particle that has no root or derivation.
Added a strong mark (shadda) to connect smoothly with the word before it. This word points to the false gods/idols mentioned in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The doubling mark (shadda) on ز shows that the sending down happened multiple times or with emphasis

steps.m: a deity, something that is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'

Added ال (the) and merged it with the word, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence. This special word always refers to The One True God in Islam.
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning 'with'

steps.m: with it/her (referring to the names أَسْمَآءٍ mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end which refers back to the names (أَسْمَآءٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse that they had made up
Basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else. Here it shows that punishment and anger come from your Lord.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Added damma and sukun to create basic noun pattern
Adding ان at the end makes it mean someone/something with power or authority

steps.m: any authority/proof (that Allah has sent down)
steps.n: Changed to show 'any/some authority' by adding the -in sound at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Form VIII (with added ت) makes the waiting more personal and focused - like waiting specifically for something
Adding the damma (ُ) at the end makes it a present tense verb
Adding وا changes it from singular to plural command
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds certainty to what follows
Added ي at the end to show this refers to 'I/me', with a kasra (ِ) before it to connect the sounds
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word, but the pronunciation stays the same
This is a preposition that shows being together with something or someone. Since it's a preposition, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: with you all (being addressed by اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being talked to. In this ayah, Allah is addressing a group of people.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. This is a primitive particle (not derived from a root)
Added shadda to emphasize the م because it comes after another م in the previous word (رَبِّكُمْ)
Added fatha at the end to show connection to the next word (الْمُنتَظِرِينَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and ت between ن and ظ to make it mean 'someone who is waiting' (like saying 'a waiter')
Added ين to show multiple people waiting (comes after مِنَ meaning 'from/of')
Added ٱلْ to point to specific waiters mentioned in the context
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to make it mean 'cause someone to be saved'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ at the end to show who was saved
Added فَ at beginning to connect to previous statement
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to things or people
Adding ال at the start and ي at the end changes it from just pointing to something to describing who or what we're talking about
Adding ن at the end makes it talk about many people instead of just one
Adding وَ at the start connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'. The hamza on the ال becomes silent (ٱ) after و
This is a word that means 'with' or 'along with'
Added هُ at the end to show this means 'with him'. The ُ (damma) on ه shows it means 'him' rather than 'her' or 'it'
Added ۥ after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed from an action to a thing by adding ة at the end and putting a sukun on the middle letter
Added بِ at the beginning to show this kindness/mercy is being used to do something (connected to أَنجَيْنٰهُ - we saved him)

steps.m: with a special kindness/mercy (from مِّنَّا - from Us)
steps.n: Added the tanween (two dots under) to show this is 'a' mercy - making it specific and special, connecting to the next word that tells us it's from Allah
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Combined with نا (meaning 'us') and the ن merged with م to make a stronger م sound
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb

steps.m: we cut off completely (acted on دَابِرَ)
steps.n: Adding نَا changes 'he' to 'we' and shows we did the action. The word دَابِرَ is what gets cut off.

steps.m: and we cut off completely (acted on دَابِرَ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this action to the previous statement about saving the believers

steps.m: to come after, to follow, to be at the end

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to follow' to noun meaning 'the last/end part'

steps.m: last part/remnant (being cut off by قَطَعْنَا)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb قَطَعْنَا (We cut off)
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and turn 'who' into 'those who'
Added shadda on ل and fatha at the end for proper pronunciation, and changed the initial alif to a waṣla form
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to strongly deny, to reject as false
steps.n: Adding shadda intensifies the meaning to strong denial/rejection

steps.m: they strongly denied/rejected (the signs/verses mentioned in بِـَٔايٰتِنَا)
steps.n: Adding وا makes it plural masculine past tense. This word connects to بِـَٔايٰتِنَا (our signs/verses) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: The ة at the end shows this is one sign/miracle
Changes from one sign/miracle to many signs/miracles by adding -ات at the end
Adding نَا shows these signs belong to Allah (speaking as 'We')

steps.m: with/by our signs (object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows these signs were the means by which they denied. This connects to the verb كَذَّبُوا (they denied) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the following statement negative. Here it negates 'they were believers', making it mean 'they were not believers'
Adding وَ at the start connects this negative statement to what came before it in the verse
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word connects to مُؤْمِنِينَ (believers) in the ayah, showing they were not believers.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed the word to show who does the action instead of the action itself
Added ending to show we're talking about multiple male believers
This is a basic directional particle showing movement or connection towards something
Added alif maksura with a small alif above it (ىٰ) at the end to show proper spelling
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This word connects to ثَمُودَ (Thamud) showing 'and to Thamud'
This is the basic form of the name of an ancient Arabian tribe mentioned in the Quran
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the name correctly
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the word 'to' (إِلَىٰ) in 'and to Thamud'
Changed from the root letters for 'brotherhood' into the simple word 'brother' by adding the vowel mark fatha
Added alif at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added هُمْ to show it belongs to them. This word is the object of the preposition إِلَىٰ (to) in this ayah.

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to make it describe someone who has this quality
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an overbar.
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said' in the past

steps.m: he said (introducing what Salih said to his people)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the noun 'people' from the concept of standing together
Added يٰ to call out to the people (like saying 'hey' or 'O')
Added kasra at the end to show possession (meaning 'my people')
This form turns the verb into a noun meaning 'one who serves'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command to one person
Added alif at beginning to complete command form
Added damma at end to complete the word
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people. This word is directed at قَوْمِ (people) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam

steps.m: Allah (as the object of اعْبُدُوا - worship)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ), and added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the command 'worship' in the ayah
This is a negative particle that comes before the word being negated. Here it negates إِلٰهٍ (god) to say 'there is not for you any god except Him.'
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose
Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
This is a preposition showing where something starts from or comes from
The م gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes after a word ending in a certain way. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to worship, to be devoted to
steps.n: The word changes from an action of worship to mean the one who is worshipped

steps.m: a god, deity (one who is worshipped)
steps.n: Changed first letter to إِ and added ا after second letter to create noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The long alif is written as a small alif above (ٰ) instead of a full alif (ا) - this is just a writing variation with no meaning change

steps.m: any god (after مِنْ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) because it comes after مِنْ (from/of) in the phrase مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلٰهٍ (you have no god)
Forms a noun meaning 'other than' from the root meaning 'to change'
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'other'
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Adds هُۥ to show we're talking about 'other than Him', referring to Allah in the phrase 'you have no god other than Him'

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing جَآءَتْكُم - has come to you)
steps.n: This is a special word that adds certainty to the verb that follows it (جَآءَتْكُم). It's like adding 'definitely' or 'indeed' before saying something happened.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common pattern for hollow verbs (verbs with ي or و in the middle).
The alif is written with a small hamza above it to show it's a long vowel sound
Added ت with sukun to show feminine past tense
Added كُم to show the action was done to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he made clear) to an adjective (clear) by adding a shadda and changing the middle vowel
Added feminine ending to create noun meaning 'clear evidence'
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of the verb جَآءَتْكُم (it came to you) in this ayah
This is a basic word that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it's used with إِلٰهٍ to say 'any god from/besides (Allah)'
The shadda (ّ) appears on the م because it comes after a word ending in ن or م, making the م sound stronger. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and have authority over
lord (one who nurtures and has authority)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows intensity, making it mean the ultimate caretaker and authority
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something
Adding كُمْ shows this lord belongs to multiple people being addressed
The letter ر gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes after a word ending with a vowel sound
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this'. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in the Quran.
Added هِ at the end to make it feminine, and added ِ (kasra) before it to connect the sounds smoothly
Added a small ي (ۦ) after the word in the Quran to help with pronunciation
Forms the word for a female camel from the root meaning 'excellence'
Adds the nominative case ending to show this word is the subject
Added ُ to show this is the subject being described as a sign in the ayah
Changed the word to become the proper name of God by adding ال and merging it with the first letter

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession, and added shadda on ل. This word is possessed by 'earth' in the phrase 'in the earth of Allah'.
This is a preposition showing that something belongs to or is for someone. Here it connects to the word ناقة (she-camel) to show who it's for.
Added كُمْ to show this is addressing multiple people. The م has a sukun (ْ) to show it's the end of the word.
The root letters combine with the first letter becoming hamza and the double ي becoming alif
The ة shows this is one specific instance of a sign or miracle
The ً (tanween fatha) shows this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added vowels to connect letters. The initial و is dropped because it's a weak letter.
Changes 'he left' to 'you all leave!' by adding و at the end - makes it a command for multiple people
The فَ connects this command to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: so leave her! (plural command) (referring to نَاقَةُ - the she-camel)
steps.n: The هَا refers specifically to the she-camel (نَاقَةُ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic meaning of consuming food
Changed from root 'eat' to command 'eat!' by adding marks above and below letters
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' as subject, and sukun at end because it's in a command/prohibition context (after لا)
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَرْضِ (earth/land) to show where something is happening.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
The kasra ending shows this is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) and follows the word في (in)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added kasra (small diagonal line) at the end to show possession/ownership - like saying 'Allah's' instead of just 'Allah'
This is a negative particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to touch the camel mentioned in the previous ayah.
The و connects this command with the previous command (فَذَرُوهَا - so leave her) in the previous ayah, showing these are related instructions about the camel

steps.m: to touch, feel, or make contact with something

steps.n: Root م-س-س became past tense verb مَسَّ by doubling middle letter and adding vowels
The ت at the start shows this is happening now and the person doing it is 'you'
The doubling of the middle letter س indicates intensification of the action of touching
The و shows that multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: you (all) touch her/it (referring to نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ - Allah's she-camel)
steps.n: The هَا refers back to Allah's she-camel mentioned earlier in the verse
Root meaning 'harm' becomes a noun meaning 'evil' by adding the sound 'oo' (damma)
Added the preposition بِ to show how something is done
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show indefiniteness and the elongated و (وٓ) for emphasis. This word describes what kind of touching is forbidden in تَمَسُّوهَا (touch her).
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added pronoun suffix to show who is being taken

steps.m: and then he will take you all (consequence of previous actions)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'a' (َ)
Adding alif changes the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action

steps.m: a punishment (that will come from فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ - then it will seize you)
steps.n: The tanween shows this is an undefined noun that will be the subject of the verb 'seize' later in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This pattern makes an adjective that means 'full of' the root meaning
Adding the double damma makes it indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he remembered' to a command 'remember!' by adding U sound at start and putting sukoon and damma marks
Added وا to make it address multiple people
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and hamza wasl (ٱ) for proper pronunciation
This is a particle that helps tell when something happened in the past. Here it connects with the command 'remember' to say 'remember when...'

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'

steps.m: he made you all (into خُلَفَآءَ successors)
steps.n: Added كُمْ with sukun at end to show this was done to 'you all'. This connects to خُلَفَآءَ (successors) which comes right after in the ayah to complete the meaning.

steps.m: to come after or replace something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Changed first vowel from 'a' to 'u' sound to make it mean 'someone who replaces' instead of 'to replace'
Changed from singular 'successor' to plural 'successors' by adding آء at the end. This word describes what Allah made you into (connected to جَعَلَكُمْ).
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of جَعَلَكُمْ (made you)
This is a preposition that shows where or when something starts. In this ayah, it connects the word عَادٍ (Aad) to بَعْدِ (after), showing the sequence 'after Aad'.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic noun form meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to عَادٍ (the people of 'Aad) that comes after it
Base Form I past tense. The weak middle letter و changes to ا as per hollow verb rules
Adding alif after first letter makes it past tense Form I hollow verb
The word changes from the name of the tribe 'Ad to 'of Ad' by adding the -in ending, showing it comes after the word 'after' in the sentence
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Form II pattern makes the verb causative - changes meaning from 'to settle' to 'to cause to settle'
Adds the conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and He caused you all to settle (connected to جَعَلَكُمْ - made you)
steps.n: Added second person plural masculine pronoun suffix كُمْ as direct object. This word connects to جَعَلَكُمْ (made you) earlier in the verse, showing how Allah established the people.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the location where something happens.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fee'
Changed the regular ي to ى (alif maqsura) at the end because it's at the end of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a usable word
Added ال to make it specific (like saying 'the earth' instead of just any land)
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/on) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The ت merges with following letter to show strong/deliberate action
Changed from 'he took' to 'you take' by adding تَ at start - makes it present tense and changes who does the action
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb takes قُصُورًا (palaces) as its direct object.
Basic preposition showing where the successors came from - connects 'Aad' to 'successors' in this ayah
Root letters got vowels to make a simple noun meaning 'flat land'
Changed the middle and first vowels from 'a' to 'u' and added و to make it mean 'many plains' instead of just one plain

steps.m: its plains (referring to الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: Added هَا to show possession by the earth mentioned earlier, with ِ connecting them
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form singular noun
Changes from single palace to multiple palaces by changing middle vowels

steps.m: palaces/fortresses (as the object of تَتَّخِذُونَ - you take)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the object (thing being taken/built)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with prefix ت and vowel pattern changes
Added plural ending to show multiple people are doing the carving. This connects to الْجِبَالَ (the mountains) and بُيُوتًا (houses) in the ayah.

steps.m: and you (all) carve (the mountains into houses)
steps.n: Added conjunction to connect this action with previous statements in the ayah
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changed vowel marks and added alif to show many mountains
Adds the definite article to specify these particular mountains
Adds fatha at the end to show this is the object being carved in the verse
Changes the 3-letter root into a plural noun meaning 'houses' by adding vowel marks and و

steps.m: houses (as objects being carved/taken in the mountains)
steps.n: Shows these houses are what's being carved/taken (objects of تَنْحِتُونَ and تَتَّخِذُونَ in the ayah)
Added sukun and damma to make basic command form
Added helper alif with damma because Arabic words can't start with sukun
Added وا to make it plural - now it's telling multiple people to remember

steps.m: so all of you remember! (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect with previous statement, and hamza changes to wasl form after فَ
Root letters changed to make plural noun meaning 'blessings' - like turning 'gift' into 'gifts'
Added final vowel mark to show this is the object (thing receiving the action) of 'remember' (فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟). Also added the long alif mark to show extended pronunciation.
Changed first letter and added shadda to make it a proper noun referring specifically to Allah
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This word is possessed by ءَالَآءَ (favors/blessings) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen or exist
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative command with the previous positive command to remember Allah's favors
Added basic past tense vowels. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. The و returns in present tense.
The ا at the end shows this is for a group of people
This is a basic preposition showing where the action takes place. It connects with the word ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where they are taking/building things.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: in the earth (location for تَتَّخِذُونَ - you take)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this is the location where 'you take' happens
Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle shows who does the action - like adding '-er' to 'teach' to get 'teacher'
Adding ين makes it talk about many people, like adding 's' in English to make something plural

steps.m: people who cause damage (being warned against in this ayah)
steps.n: The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the warning in the ayah - Allah is telling people not to be like these corrupters
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. This word shows what the chiefs (ٱلْمَلَأُ) did.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to gather, assemble (especially of noble people)

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Adding ال (the) turns the verb 'gathered' into a noun meaning 'the assembly/group of leaders'

steps.m: the assembly (of nobles) (subject of قَالَ - said)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this assembly is doing the action of speaking (قَالَ) in this verse
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it specific, pointing to particular people. In this ayah, it refers to the arrogant people who were proud.
The word gets special marks: a curved alif at start, a double-letter mark on ل, and an 'a' sound mark at the end
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added است at start to show they deliberately made themselves act proud
Added وا to show multiple people did this action
This is a preposition that shows رَّبِّهِۦ (his Lord) is the source/origin of مُّرْسَلٌ (being sent). In this ayah, it's used to show that Salih was sent from his Lord.
The root meaning of standing together becomes a word for a group of people who stand as one. This is why قَوْم means 'people' or 'tribe' - they stand together as a group.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Added هِ to show these are his people. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark. This word comes after مِن (from) in the ayah.
This is the basic relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something. The ال makes it specific.
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Adding لِ at the beginning shows direction or purpose. When لِ combines with ال, the ل of ال merges into the لِ, shown by the ّ mark.
Basic meaning of weakness or feebleness

steps.m: to consider someone weak or oppress them
steps.n: Added 'است' at the beginning to show someone actively making others weak or treating them as weak

steps.m: to be considered weak/to be oppressed
steps.n: Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا to show multiple people were oppressed
Relative pronoun meaning 'who/whom/whoever'
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction/purpose. This word connects to آمَنَ (believed) in the ayah, showing who the belief came from.
Root meaning of security and trust in base form
Connected the root letters أ-م-ن with fat-ha vowels (َ) to make 'he' past tense
Changed the first letter to hamza and added alif, which makes the meaning stronger and more specific to religious belief
Preposition meaning 'from'
Added pronoun suffix for 'them'
Basic meaning of knowledge
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense and from 'he' to 'you' by adding تَ at start
Made it refer to multiple people by adding ون at the end
Made it into a question by adding أَ at the beginning. This word appears in a question to the disbelievers about whether they know Salih is truly a messenger.
This is a basic particle used to connect parts of sentences, like saying 'that' in English

steps.m: that/indeed (introduces what the disbelievers knew about صٰلِحًا)
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it introduces what the disbelievers knew about Salih.
Root meaning of goodness and righteousness

steps.m: one who is good/righteous (subject of مُّرْسَلٌ)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter and vowels to show who does the action. This word is describing who is being sent (مُّرْسَلٌ) from their Lord.
Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this is describing someone specific. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above it.
Root meaning of sending or dispatching
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added مُ prefix and changed vowels to show this is something/someone receiving the action of being sent
The ٌ at the end shows this is indefinite - meaning 'a messenger' rather than 'the messenger'

steps.m: one who is sent (as a subject)
steps.n: The letter م has a shaddah (doubled) mark added to it, showing it merged with a previous letter. The tanween was already there in both words.
Basic preposition showing something comes from or originates from something else
The 'n' sound is doubled because it comes after a connecting word - this is a common rule in Arabic
Root meaning of lordship and nurturing
Doubling the middle letter makes it a noun meaning 'one who does this a lot'

steps.m: lord (possessed by or connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this lord belongs to or is connected to something

steps.m: his Lord (referring to the messenger Salih)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this is 'his' Lord. The small ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.
Root meaning of speaking or saying
Added fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb
Added وا to show many people did the action
This is an emphatic particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the word مُؤْمِنُونَ (believers) later in the ayah, showing that 'we are the believers'
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to things or concepts. It connects two parts of a sentence, like saying 'what' or 'that which' in English.
Added بِ at the beginning to show how or why something is done. Here it connects to the word مُؤْمِنُونَ (believers) to show what they believe in.
Root meaning of sending
Added vowels (a-a) to make it mean 'he sent' in the past tense
Changed to passive form by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to show someone/something was sent rather than did the sending
Basic preposition showing how something is done or connected

steps.m: with it/him (referring to what was sent down)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ to show what/who the preposition connects to. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, believe in
steps.n: Root meaning of security and faith
The مُ at the start and ِ in the middle show who does the action of believing
Adding ُونَ shows we're talking about multiple male believers. In this ayah, they are specifically the ones who believe in Prophet Salih.
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is the main verb introducing what the arrogant people said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people. The plural form indicates it refers to multiple people.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together. This is the basic past tense form.
Added اِسْتَ at the beginning to show someone making themselves act in a big/proud way
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word describes what the people mentioned in قَالَ (he said) did.
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'
Adding ا at the end shows this applies to 'we' (first person plural)
The base word is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. The hamza is written as ٱ and ي as ى in Quranic text.
Added the preposition ب which shows how something is done or what it's connected to. In this ayah, it connects to the verb ءَامَنتُم (you believed), showing what they believed in.
Added the elongation mark (ٓ) which indicates a slight lengthening in recitation but doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the root letters into Form IV verb pattern by adding hamza and alif at start, meaning 'to cause/make someone believe'
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he believed' to 'I believed'
Changed تُ (I) to تُم (you all) to show it's talking to multiple people instead of one person
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: by/with him/it (referring to what they believed in)
steps.n: Added هِ (hi) at the end to show this refers back to what was believed in

steps.m: by/with him/it (referring to what they believed in)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to describe someone who does the action of rejecting truth
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Changed to plural form to show multiple people who reject truth
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb. This word is part of a sequence of actions with وَقَالُوا۟ (and they said).
Adding فَ shows this happened right after something else. The object being slaughtered is الناقة (the she-camel) which comes right after this word.
Changed to plural by adding وا, showing multiple people did this action together. They slaughtered الناقة (the she-camel).
Connected the root letters with vowels. The و becomes ا because it's between two vowels.
Added ة to make it specifically refer to a female camel
Added ٱل to specify this particular female camel. The ن gets doubled (shadda) because it combines with the ل from ٱل.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb فَعَقَرُوا۟ (they slaughtered)
Added fathas to make it past tense. The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous action (slaughtering the she-camel)

steps.m: and they rebelled arrogantly (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added وْا at the end to show that a group of people (the people of Thamud) did this action together. This connects to them speaking next in قَالُوا (they said).
A preposition that shows moving away, turning from, or going against something. In this ayah, it shows how they turned away from their Lord's command.
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to make it a noun. This word is owned by رَبّ (their Lord) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to رَبّ (their Lord), forming the phrase 'command of their Lord'

steps.m: lord/master (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: The doubled letter shows the complete and perfect nature of this lordship

steps.m: O my Lord! (calling upon one's Lord)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end makes it a call or plea to one's Lord

steps.m: their lord (object of عَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this is their lord. This word is part of the phrase 'they were insolent against the command of their lord'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: to make right, to be good

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who has this quality
Added يٰ at the beginning to show this is calling out to someone directly. The ُ at the end shows this is the main subject being addressed.
Command form of 'to come' - the أ changes to ا and ي drops to make it easier to say
Added نَا at the end to show who is being come to (us), and changed initial alif to ٱ
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' and is used to refer to the thing being promised in this ayah
Added the preposition بِ (with) to show how the action relates to what follows. In this ayah, it's connecting to تَعِدُنَآ (you promise us).
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed to present tense 'you' form. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start.

steps.m: you threaten us (object of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show who is being threatened. This word is what they said, coming after قَالُوا (they said).

steps.m: if (introduces condition for كُنتَ - you were)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects to the verb كُنتَ (you were) to say 'if you were...'

steps.m: to be or exist (like saying 'am', 'is', or 'are' in English)

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past
The و in the middle drops out and the first vowel changes to ُ because و is a special 'weak' letter that often disappears

Added تَ at the end and ْ in the middle to change from 'he' to 'you'. In this ayah, this is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ when the disbelievers are questioning if he was really one of Allah's messengers.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (the messengers). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when speaking.

steps.m: one who is sent as a messenger
steps.n: Added مُ at the beginning to show someone receiving the action (like a messenger being sent), and changed the middle vowels to make it passive
Added ين at the end to show there are many messengers

steps.m: the messengers (mentioned in this ayah about Prophet Salih)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at start to specify these particular messengers and َ at end because it's being acted upon in the sentence
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it past tense 'he took'
Added ت at the end to show a female (she) did the action instead of a male (he)
Added هُمُ to show who was taken

steps.m: and then she took them (subject of ٱلرَّجْفَةُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the start to show this happened after something else. In this ayah, ٱلرَّجْفَةُ (the earthquake) is doing the taking/seizing.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changes from 'shook' (verb) to 'one shake' (noun) by adding -ah ending
ال at the start makes it specific, like 'the' in English
The ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ (then it seized them)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense voweling
Added hamza at beginning to show entering into a state
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: then they became (after the earthquake struck them)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of the earthquake mentioned in the previous word (الرَّجْفَةُ)
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Not derived from a root.
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This word shows where they were crouching - in their homes.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to something that comes after it

steps.m: their home/dwelling (referring to the people of Thamud)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ at the end to show the home belongs to them (the people of Thamud mentioned earlier in the ayah)

steps.m: to crouch down, sit still on ground

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who is crouching (subject of أَصْبَحُوا)
steps.n: Changed to describe someone doing the action. This word tells us what they became after أَصْبَحُوا (they became).
Added ين to show this describes many people. The word is in a special form because it comes after أَصْبَحُوا (they became).

steps.m: ones who are crouching (in accusative case)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is affected by the verb أَصْبَحُوا that came before it

steps.m: to turn away or take charge

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he turned away'
Adding shadda makes the action more complete or intense
Adding تَ shows he did this action to himself
Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of something before it
Adding the alif at the end (ى) is because this is a weak verb ending in ي

steps.m: away from (related to فَتَوَلَّىٰ - he turned away)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows direction away from something. Here it's connected to the verb فَتَوَلَّىٰ (he turned away) to show who he turned away from.
Adding هُمْ means 'them' (for a group). This shows who he turned away from.

steps.m: to speak, tell, or say something
he said (connects to يٰقَوْمِ - O my people)
steps.n: Changed to past tense 'he said' by adding the vowels. The middle letter و became ا

steps.m: and he said (connects to يٰقَوْمِ - O my people)
steps.n: Adding و with fatha at the beginning connects this to the previous action (فَتَوَلَّىٰ - so he turned away)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The meaning shifted from the action of standing together to describe the group that does it.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed or follows a preposition

steps.m: O my people! (being called by Prophet Salih)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show Prophet Salih is calling out to his people
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This word emphasizes that something definitely happened in the past.

steps.m: certainly indeed (strongly emphasizes the verb أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to make the emphasis even stronger. This combination of لَ + قَدْ is very common in the Quran to show something definitely happened.
Simple past tense verb meaning 'he delivered'. The root letters ب-ل-غ got vowel marks (fathas) to show it happened in the past. رِسَالَةَ shows what was delivered.
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'made someone deliver' or 'caused to be delivered'
Adding تُ changes it from 'he delivered' to 'I delivered'
Adding كُمْ shows who received the message - 'you all'
Changed from basic root meaning 'send' into a noun meaning 'message/sending' by adding vowels and alif
Added ة to show this is one specific thing that was sent. This word is what was delivered in the phrase 'I delivered to you'
Added َ at the end to show this is the object being delivered
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who completely cares for and nurtures
steps.n: The root verb 'to care for' becomes 'master caretaker' by doubling the middle letter

steps.m: my Lord (the one speaking to قَوْمِ/his people)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show it belongs to me. This word is being spoken by a prophet to his people, as shown by him saying يٰقَوْمِ (O my people) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to sincerely advise or give honest counsel

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end. The sukun (ْ) helps pronunciation.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects with the verbs 'I conveyed' and 'I advised' to show who received these actions.

steps.m: for/to you all (object of أَبْلَغْتُ and نَصَحْتُ)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is directed at multiple people being spoken to. The ُ (damma) and ْ (sukun) help with pronunciation.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'but' or 'however'. It's used to introduce a contrast with what came before.

steps.m: and yet/but still (contrasting with نَصَحْتُ 'I advised')
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. In this ayah, it connects to how the speaker advised his people (نَصَحْتُ) but they still didn't listen.
Basic negative particle used to negate what comes after it
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added shadda to make the negation stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: to love, to feel affection for something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Combined the repeated ب into one letter with shadda to show intensity
Adding تُ at start changes 'he' to 'you', and changes vowels to show present tense
Adding ون changes 'you' (singular) to 'you all' (plural)
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changes from action to the person doing the action
Makes the word plural to show multiple advisors

steps.m: the sincere advisors (object of لَّا تُحِبُّونَ - you do not love)
steps.n: Adds 'the' and shows this is what's being acted upon in the phrase 'you do not love the sincere advisors'
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show proper pronunciation
The ًا shows Lut is receiving the action in this sentence

steps.m: And Lut (as object of the sentence)
steps.n: The conjunction وَ is added to connect this clause with the previous one
This is a time word that connects two past events. Here it introduces what happened when Lut spoke to his people.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone (he) did this action in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to gather, stand firm, or be straight

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand/gather' becomes a noun meaning 'people' - refers to the group Lut was speaking to
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِ at the end to show these are specifically Lut's people
Added ت at beginning to show 'she/it' and vowels to make it present tense - means something is happening now
Changed the ending to ون which shows we're talking to multiple men. The ُ vowel before ون is required.
Added أَ at start to make it a question. In this ayah, it's asking in disapproval about their bad actions.

steps.m: to do something shameful or excessive

steps.n: Changed vowels to make an adjective meaning 'shameful' from the root meaning 'to do something shameful'
Added ا to show this describes someone who does the action
Adding ة changes it from describing the person to describing the act
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific shameful act rather than any shameful act

1. Changed alif to waṣla form (ٱ) since it's at start of word
2. Changed long alif to alif with overbar (فٰ)
3. Added final fatha to show this is the object of the verb أَتَأْتُونَ (do you commit)
