This is a negative particle that negates the verb that follows it (سَبَقَكُم meaning 'preceded you'). In this context, it helps express that no one before them had done what they were doing.

steps.m: to go ahead or come before

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. This word is part of what Lut said to his people.

steps.m: he went ahead of you all (object of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who was gone ahead of - in this case, all the people being addressed
Basic preposition meaning 'with'
Added kasra vowel mark to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: with it/her (referring to الْفٰحِشَةَ - the immorality)
steps.n: Added ها which means 'it/her' - in this ayah it refers back to الْفٰحِشَةَ (the immorality) that was mentioned
This is a preposition that shows something comes from or is part of something else. Here it connects 'anyone' (أَحَدٍ) with 'the worlds' (ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ) to mean 'anyone from the worlds'
Connected the letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is affected by the word مِنْ (from) that comes before it in the ayah
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from or belongs to something else
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a vowel sound, making the م sound stronger
The fatha (َ) is added at the end because this word connects to the next word الْعٰلَمِينَ (the worlds), showing it means 'from the worlds'
Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known/distinguished' - referring to all of creation/existence
Adds sound masculine plural ending to show multiple worlds/realms
Adds definite article to specify these particular worlds/all worlds
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain

steps.m: indeed you all (emphasizes لَتَأْتُونَ - you approach)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This connects to the next word لَتَأْتُونَ (you approach) which it emphasizes.
Connected the root letters together
Added present tense prefix تَ and vowel marks
Changed from singular (she/you) to plural (you all) form

steps.m: surely you all come (present) (addressing the people of Lot)
steps.n: Added emphatic لَ to show certainty/emphasis
Emphatic prefix لَ (truly/surely) + تَأتُونَ (you all come) = you truly/surely come
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic singular noun
Changes the singular 'man' into plural 'men' using a broken plural pattern
Added definite article and doubled the ر with shadda
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb لَتَأْتُونَ (you approach)
Changes from verb 'to desire' into noun meaning 'a desire'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of the feeling

steps.m: out of lust (describing how they approach men)
steps.n: This word describes how they perform the action in the verb تَأْتُونَ (you approach). The tanween at the end shows it's working like an adverb here.
This is a primitive preposition (حرف جر) that shows origin, source or partitive meaning. It has no root or derivational steps.
Added shadda for emphasis and to show connection with the following word. This word connects to النِّسَآءِ (the women) to show 'from/instead of the women'.
Added damma to change the root into a preposition meaning 'besides' or 'instead of'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to النِّسَآءِ (the women) in the ayah, showing it means 'instead of the women'
Forms a special plural for 'women' by adding vowels and stretching the middle with alif
Adds 'the' at the beginning and doubles the ن to help pronunciation
Adds kasra at the end because it comes after the words مِّن دُونِ (instead of/other than)

steps.m: rather/but rather (contradicting the previous statement about شَهْوَةً)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a correction or contradiction to what was just said. Here it's contradicting their claim about their desires (شَهْوَةً) by stating what they really are - a transgressing people.
This is the basic form of the word meaning 'you' when talking to one male person
Adding م with a sukun (مْ) at the end and changing the last vowel to ُ makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: a group that stands together (describes أنتم)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'. This word describes أنتم (you all) in this ayah.

steps.m: a group that stands together (describes أنتم)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the subject of the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added emphasis to the م with shadda

steps.m: those who go beyond limits (men) (predicate of أَنتُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to show this describes multiple men. This word describes the subject 'you all' (أَنتُمْ) in the ayah.

steps.m: what/not (can either ask questions or make something negative)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that can either ask questions (like 'what?') or make something negative (like 'not/no')

steps.m: and what/not (connects to previous statement while asking 'what?' or saying 'not')
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like adding 'and' in English
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to hollow verb form with alif due to weak middle letter و
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif after the second root letter to make it a noun meaning 'the response itself'. This word is being acted upon by the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah.

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something else
Added هِ to show possession by 'him'. The ۦٓ is just a pronunciation mark for the هِ.
Added shadda to emphasize the 'l' sound. This comes from combining إِنْ (if) + لا (no) where the ن merged into the ل
Added a long 'aa' sound at the end to link with the next phrase 'that they said'. This word means 'except' and introduces what comes next.
This is a connecting word that introduces what the people said in response. It connects 'their answer was nothing except' to 'they said: expel them...'
The root letters ق-و-ل become قَالَ (he said) by changing و to ا - this is a common change in past tense verbs with و in the middle
Adding وا at the end makes it refer to a group (they) instead of just one person (he)
Added elongation mark (ـٓ) and small و (۟) as pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes the verb mean 'to cause someone to do the action'
The vowel pattern changes to show it's happening now (present tense)
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added هُم to show who is being expelled
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from or out of something else
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in a vowel (قَالُوٓا۟). This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters. The fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter pattern creates a basic noun form.
Adding ة makes this a specific place where people settle
Adds -i ending to show this town belongs to someone
Adds second person plural possessive pronoun to indicate ownership
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to an emphasis word meaning 'indeed'

steps.m: indeed they (are people who purify themselves)
steps.n: Added هُمْ (them/they) to show who is being emphasized. This connects to يَتَطَهَّرُونَ (purify themselves) in the next part of the ayah.
Added damma to create a basic noun from the concept

steps.m: people (who are close/friendly with each other)
steps.n: Changed from 'closeness' to 'people' by adding an alif and tanween - like changing 'friend' to 'friends' in English
Added تَ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself, and ّ (shadda) on ه to show doing it thoroughly
Changed يَ at start to show it's happening now or in the future

steps.m: they purify themselves (referring to the people mentioned in قَوْمِهِ)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to the people mentioned earlier in قَوْمِهِ (his people).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to make someone do the action' instead of just doing it yourself
Adding نا changes the subject to 'we' (first person plural)
The prefix فَ shows this action happened as a result of something before it
Adding هُ shows who was saved (him)
Changes from root letters to a noun meaning 'family'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أنجى (we saved)
Added pronoun هُ to show possession (his)

steps.m: and his family (as object of أنجى/we saved)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect with previous word (فَأَنجَيْنٰهُ/so we saved him). The ۥٓ are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.
This is a special word that shows something or someone is not included in what was mentioned before. Here it shows that everyone in his family was saved except his wife.
Root letters م-ر-أ got short 'a' vowels to make the basic word for 'woman'
Added 'i' sound at start to make the complete word for 'woman'
Added feminine marker ة to specify a single woman/wife
Added 'his' (هُ) to 'wife' and changed the first letter. The word means 'except his wife'
Adding the fatha mark makes this a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha completes the past tense verb pattern
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (referring to the wife of Lut in this ayah)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. It doesn't come from a root.
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْغٰبِرِينَ (those who remained behind). This makes it easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and changed vowels to make active participle (doer of action)
Added ين to make it plural. This word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah.

steps.m: the ones who remain behind (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. The word follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the verb causative - showing someone made the rain happen. The word مَّطَرًا (rain) later in the ayah shows what was made to fall.
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.

steps.m: and we caused (something) to rain (on مَّطَرًا)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix to connect with previous statement
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding هِم means 'them' (for a group). This word shows who received the rain mentioned in وَأَمْطَرْنَا (and We rained).
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into the noun 'rain'
Added ًا to show this noun is being acted upon in the sentence
The م (meem) became stronger/doubled to emphasize the destructive rain that was sent down upon them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changes from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding 'u' at the start and making it a command
Adds فَ at the beginning to connect this command with what came before it, showing it's a result or consequence
This is a basic question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something

steps.m: how (asking about the state of عٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on first letter (كَ), sukun on middle letter (يْ), and fatha on last letter (فَ). In this ayah, it's asking about how the end result was for the wrongdoers.

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make it past tense. This shows something that happened before.
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to make it a noun meaning 'result'. This word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Added U ending to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) but pronounced the same.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural (more than one criminal)

steps.m: the criminals (whose end/outcome is being described)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and show these are particular criminals being referred to in the story. This word comes after عاقبة (end/outcome) to show whose end it was.
The base word إِلَى is a preposition meaning 'towards'. The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the ayah.
This root relates to civilization, settling, and city-building
Basic root letters م-د-ن got vowel marks (َ ْ) to form a noun meaning a settled place
Added ي to make an adjective describing something that is settled or civilized
Changed from the root letters for 'brotherhood' into the simple word 'brother' by adding the vowel mark fatha
Changed the ending to ا because it's being used as the object (the person receiving the action)
Added هُمْ at the end to show this brother belongs to them. This word is the object (receiver) of the verb 'sent' in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the prophet's name Shuaib (peace be upon him)

steps.m: Shuaib (as the object being sent to Madyan)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween alif) at the end to show that Shuaib is the object being sent in this sentence. This connects to the earlier word أَخَاهُمْ (their brother) which describes him.

steps.m: he said (introduces what شُعَيْب says next)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word introduces what شُعَيْب says to his people in the next part.

steps.m: he said (introduces what شُعَيْب says next)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا - this is a common change in Arabic verbs that makes them easier to say
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'

steps.m: O people! (being called by Prophet Shuayb)
steps.n: Adding يٰ to call out to someone directly. This word comes after Shuayb قال (said)

steps.m: O my people! (being called by Prophet Shuayb)
steps.n: The ِ at the end shows possession - these are Shuayb's people he is addressing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he worshipped' to a command telling someone to worship by changing the vowel marks
Added alif at beginning to make it a proper command
Added plural marker to show this command is for a group
Changed to the specific name for the One God by replacing the first letter with ال and merging it

steps.m: Allah (as the object of اعْبُدُوا - worship)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark (fatha) at the end to show it's receiving the action of worship. The L sound is made stronger with a shadda mark.
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' true. In this ayah, it's used with لَكُم (for you) to say 'you do not have'.
Added fatha to show the correct pronunciation
Added كُم to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of. Like saying 'made from wood' or 'a piece of cake' in English.
Added a shadda (ّ) on م because it comes after a word ending with a sukun. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes the root into a noun meaning the one who is worshipped
Changes the word to the basic noun form meaning 'god'
Adds the tanween kasra ending because it comes after the word مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the main subject of the sentence
Added هُۥ at the end to show this means 'other than Him', specifically referring to Allah in this context

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing جَآءَتْكُم - has come to you)
steps.n: This is a particle that emphasizes the verb that comes after it (جَآءَتْكُم). It's like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' before a statement in English to make it stronger.

steps.m: to come/arrive (especially with news or information)

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added long alif to emphasize the action of coming

steps.m: it came (referring to بَيِّنَةٌ - clear evidence)
steps.n: Added ت to show the subject is feminine because we're talking about بَيِّنَةٌ (clear evidence) which is feminine

steps.m: it (clear evidence) came to you all
steps.n: Added كُم to show this clear evidence came to all the people being addressed
Added shadda to intensify the meaning
Added feminine ending to make it a noun meaning 'evidence/proof'
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of the verb جَآءَتْكُم (it has come to you)
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or that it's part of something. No root since it's a helper word.

Added a shadda (ّ) on م because it comes after a word ending in a similar sound. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to إِلٰهٍ (god) to show there is no god from/besides Allah.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense/complete
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
Adding كُمْ shows this Lord belongs to all the people being spoken to
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he fulfilled' in the past tense
Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'to fulfill completely/perfectly' - this form (أفعل) often adds emphasis or completeness to the action
Changed to command form for multiple people
Added فَ to show this command follows from previous statement

steps.m: to measure out (especially grains or liquids)

steps.n: Changes the action of measuring into the actual amount or quantity being measured. The ْ (sukun) shows this is a noun rather than a verb.

steps.m: the measure (object of أَوْفُوا - fulfill/give in full)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific ('the' measure), and َ shows it receives the action of the command 'give in full' (أَوْفُوا)
Forms an instrumental noun indicating the tool used for weighing
ٱلْ makes it specific ('the' scale)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (thing receiving the action) of the verb فَأَوْفُوا (so fulfill/give in full)
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. Here it negates the verb تَبْخَسُوا۟ (diminish/decrease), telling people not to decrease what belongs to others.
The وَ connects this negative command to the previous commands in the verse, making it part of a series of instructions.

steps.m: to decrease or shortchange someone's rights/dues

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed middle vowel to sukun and final vowel to damma for present tense
Added وا to make it plural - now means multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Root means being social, turned into the word for people since humans are social beings
Added ال to make it specific people rather than just any people
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of cheating/shortchanging (تَبْخَسُوا)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to form the singular noun
Changed the word shape to make it mean many things instead of one thing - this is a special plural form
Added a long alif to show this is a special plural form for 'things'
Added هُمْ suffix to show possession by them
This is the basic negation particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to do تَبْخَسُوا۟ (cheat/shortchange).
Added و at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one, showing these are related instructions.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to show causing something to happen
Changes to present tense with you (feminine) or he as the doer
Added plural marker to show many people are doing the action
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the corruption is happening inside the earth.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/on)

steps.m: to be distant or come after something

steps.n: Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make the word mean 'after'
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: to make right, to fix, to repair

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added إِ at start and ا after second letter to create noun meaning 'the act of fixing'
Added kasra at end to show this word is owned by something else
Added ها meaning 'its/her' referring to الأرض (the earth) which is feminine
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Adding لِ changes 'this' (ذا) to 'that' (ذٰلِ) - like pointing with your finger to something close versus far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) for you all
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is addressing multiple people (you all)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic comparative adjective
Added double-damma at the end to show it's indefinite. This word is the subject of the demonstrative pronoun ذٰلِكُمْ (that), forming the phrase 'that is better'.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which means 'for' or 'to'
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Adding كُمْ shows this is directed to multiple people being spoken to
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - something that needs to be true for something else to happen. Here it connects to كُنتُم (you were) to mean 'if you were'.
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Changes from the root 'to be' into a simple command 'be!' by adding damma and sukun marks. The middle letter drops out.
Added ت at the end to change from a command (be!) to past tense (was). The ت shows who did the action (I, you, or she)
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to show it's talking to a group
Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, like changing 'trust' to 'truster'
Added ين to make it plural (many believers), and doubled the م sound to emphasize their strong belief

steps.m: many who truly trust/believe (being described in this ayah)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show these believers are being described in the sentence
Added fatha to show this is a negative particle that tells us the following verb (sitting) should not happen
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before it in the verse
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
This root gives us words about completeness or totality
The doubled ل (with ّ on top) makes the meaning stronger to show total completeness. The ُ sound makes it into a noun.
The ِ shows this word belongs to or describes the path (صِرٰطٍ) that comes after it
The بِ at the start shows this is happening at or by every path mentioned in the ayah
Added vowels and alif to make it the noun 'path'. This word connects to what comes after it to show 'path of...'
Changed the long 'aa' sound to a shorter one and added two dots below to show it belongs to what comes next
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense and adds 'you/she' meaning

steps.m: you (all) threaten/promise (in this ayah: you all threaten people on every path)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show this refers to multiple people doing the action. This word relates to تَقْعُدُوا (sit) in the ayah, describing how they threaten people on every path.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong blocking
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed middle vowel to ُ for present tense
Added وَ at start to connect this action with previous actions in the verse

steps.m: and you all block strongly (referring to the disbelievers blocking others from Allah's path)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the blocking
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it shows people being turned away from Allah's path.
Added ي between second and third letters to make it a noun referring to a specific path or way
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word after it (Allah). This creates the meaning 'path of Allah'
Added shadda to show emphasis in pronunciation of middle letter
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, the path/way mentioned before it)
This is a basic word used to refer to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it's being used to talk about the people who are being threatened (تُوعِدُونَ).
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he believed (being prevented from Allah's path by those who صَدُّونَ/hinder)
steps.n: Adding the long alif changes the meaning from just feeling secure to actively putting trust in something/someone, which is what belief means. This word is being acted upon by those who hinder others from Allah's path.
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added the pronoun هِ to show who/what is being referred to. Here it refers back to 'the path of Allah' mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added the small ۦ mark after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Connects this action to the previous actions in the ayah (تَقْعُدُوا and تَصُدُّونَ)

steps.m: and you (all) seek it (the path) crookedly
steps.n: The هَا refers back to سَبِيلِ (path) mentioned earlier in the ayah, showing they seek to make Allah's path crooked
Changed the verb (being crooked) into a noun (crookedness) by adding the special vowel pattern
The ًا ending shows this is what they are seeking in the path. This connects to تَبْغُونَهَا (they seek it) in the verse, where they are trying to make Allah's path crooked.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he remembered' to 'remember!' by adding U-sound at start (command marker) and changing vowels
Added plural command ending وا with a long vowel mark
Removed 'and' (وَ) at start, command to many people to remember
This is a time word that connects two events in the past. Here it introduces 'when you were few' as a reminder of their past state.
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he was' in the past
Changed vowels to make command form. The weak letter ا is dropped following Arabic rules for hollow verbs.
Added تُمْ at the end to change from a command (be!) to past tense 'you all were'
The ي makes it into an adjective describing something that has the quality of being small in amount

steps.m: small in number (describing كُنتُمْ - you all were)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this word is describing how many people there were in the past. It's connected to كُنتُمْ (you all were) in this ayah.
Adding the vowels connects the letters and makes it a past tense verb. The shadda (double letter) shows someone is causing the increase to happen.
Adding كُمْ at the end shows who was affected by the action - in this case, all the people being addressed

steps.m: and so He made you all increase (after you were few)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of what came before. From the context of the ayah, this refers to Allah increasing their numbers after they were few.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding ٱ at start and changing vowels. The ٱ with sukun (ْ) helps start the word smoothly.
Added وا to make the command apply to a group instead of one person
Added وَ (and) to connect this command with previous commands in the verse
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to make the question word pronounceable
Adding the final vowel mark to complete the question word
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
Because و comes between two vowels (َ), it changes to ا. This is a common change in Arabic for middle-weak verbs.

steps.m: to follow or come after something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to make it mean 'result/outcome'. This word is what was (كان) something in the ayah.
Changed regular alif to alif with bar on top (آ) which is just a writing variation
Added damma at end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: people who damage things (in a possession relationship with عٰقِبَةُ/end)
steps.n: Sound masculine plural suffix added to indicate multiple corrupters. The word is in the genitive case because it comes after عاقبة (end/outcome) in a possession relationship, showing it is the end/outcome of the corrupters.

steps.m: the people who damage things (in a possession relationship with عٰقِبَةُ/end)
steps.n: Adding ال makes the word definite, changing it from 'corrupters' to 'the corrupters'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added vowel marks to show past tense
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: to go around or circle something

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowels - now means the action of going around
Changed from action to doer of action - someone who circles/patrols
Extended the middle vowel sound to make it stronger
Added ة to make it refer to a group rather than an individual
Added ٌ to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was)
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
The shadda (ّ) is added because of how it connects with the previous word in speech. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is talking about multiple people being addressed
Changed the word to Form IV which makes the meaning more active and specific to religious belief
Changed to plural form. This group is one of two parties mentioned in the verse, waiting for Allah's judgment (يَحْكُمَ).
Combines the preposition 'bi' with the relative pronoun 'alladhi'

steps.m: to send or dispatch (a message or messenger)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he sent' to 'was sent' by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel - this makes it passive (something done to someone)
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this in the past
Basic preposition meaning 'with'

steps.m: with it/him (referring to what was sent in أُرْسِلْتُ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'hi' to show what/who the preposition refers to. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark. This connects to the message mentioned in أُرْسِلْتُ (I was sent) earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning 'the act of going around'
Changed from action to the person doing the action

steps.m: a group (literally: those who go around together)
steps.n: Added ة at the end to change from 'one who circles' to 'a group that circles/moves together'
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Added ٌ to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was)
This is a negative particle that makes present tense verbs refer to the past. It's used with the verb يُؤْمِنُوا۟ (believe) to say 'did not believe'.
Added shadda (ّ) on ل because it comes after a word ending in ن. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and adding ؤ in middle
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes 'he was patient' into 'you be patient!' - a command to one person
Adding وا at the end changes this from 'be patient' for one person to 'be patient' for a group of people

steps.m: so be patient! (command to a group)
steps.n: The فَ shows this command is a result of or response to what was mentioned before in the ayah
Added vowel marks to the basic particle to make it readable. This word shows when something continues up to a certain point in time.
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning. This makes the word more emphatic, showing a definite endpoint.
Added alif maqsura (ىٰ) at the end. This connects it to the next word يَحْكُمَ (He judges), showing that something continues until God makes His judgment.

steps.m: to make decisions between right and wrong

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Changed from past to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changes to subjunctive mood, often used after 'that' or 'so that'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity
The ُ shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the preposition form
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb يَحْكُمَ (judge)
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'between us'. This whole phrase is being acted upon by the verb يَحْكُمَ (judge/decide)
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the pronoun
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the comparative/superlative form meaning 'best'
The ُ at the end shows this word is describing Allah as being the best of judges in this ayah

steps.m: to judge wisely or make decisions

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Makes the word plural to show multiple judges

steps.m: the judges (possessed by خَيْرُ - best of)
steps.n: Adds 'the' to make it specific and adds a mark to show it's possessed by the word 'best' (خَيْرُ)

steps.m: he said (subject of the sentence before ٱلْمَلَأُ)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show this is about something that already happened. This word introduces what the chiefs (ٱلْمَلَأُ) said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle

steps.m: to gather in a group or assembly

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to noun meaning 'group' by adding fatha marks
Added ال to show this is the specific assembly being talked about. This assembly is the subject doing the action of saying (قَالَ)
Added damma at the end to show this word is doing the action of saying
The definite article ال combines with the base ذي, with the ل receiving shadda due to sun letter assimilation. The ينَ suffix makes it plural masculine.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: to act arrogantly, to be proud
steps.n: Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show they deliberately made themselves proud/arrogant
The suffix وا changes the verb to third person masculine plural (they)

This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects 'our town' (قَرْيَتِنَآ) to the verb 'we will surely expel you' (نُخْرِجَنَّكَ), showing they will expel them from their town.

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Changes from action to mean a group of people who stand together as a community
Adds kasra to show this word is owned by something or follows certain words like مِن (from)
Adds هِ to show these people belong to him. The ۦ mark is just a pronunciation helper.
Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he left/exited'
Adding hamza prefix makes the verb causative/transitive
Changed to first person plural (we) and present tense form
The لَ prefix adds both future tense and emphasis to the verb

steps.m: we will most certainly make leave
steps.n: The نَّ suffix adds additional emphasis/certainty

steps.m: we will most certainly make you leave
steps.n: The كَ suffix indicates 'you' as the object of the verb
This is a proper name, likely of Hebrew origin meaning 'branches' or 'divided'. The name appears in the Quran as one of Allah's prophets.
Added damma at the end to show this is the main subject being addressed
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we are calling out to Shuaib. This is a shortened form of يا used for calling someone's attention.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال (the) at the start and doubled the ذ letter to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Changed to past tense 'he believed' by adding alif at start. Form IV pattern makes it active - someone doing the believing
Changed spelling of initial alif to hamza for proper writing

steps.m: they believed (subject of لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ - we will surely expel you)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word describes the people who believed with Shuayb who the disbelievers are threatening to expel.
This is a preposition meaning 'with'. Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation.

steps.m: with you (object of لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ - we will surely expel)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to mean 'you'. This word connects to لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ (we will surely expel) to show who will be expelled.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. In this ayah, it connects 'the chiefs' to 'his people', showing the chiefs came from among his people.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the root letters
The ة at the end makes it a feminine noun meaning 'place where people gather'
The word changed to show it belongs to/is owned by something, with -ti ending
Adding the possessive pronoun suffix نا to indicate ownership. The word is in the genitive case because it comes after the preposition مِن (from).

steps.m: or (connecting two options: returning to their religion or being expelled)
steps.n: This is a basic connecting word (particle) that gives choices between options. In this verse, it connects the choice between returning to their religion or being expelled.

steps.m: or (connecting two options: returning to their religion or being expelled)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و

steps.m: to return or go back repeatedly

steps.n: Adding ا makes it a past tense verb meaning 'to return'
Changed to present/future tense and second person (you) by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added لَ for emphasis and to show certainty about future action
Added نَّ for extra emphasis, showing this is a strong statement or oath
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects to مِلَّتِنَا (our religion/way) to show 'in our religion/way'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changes from verb (following) to noun (path/way) by adding ة and changing first vowel
Added 'our' to show possession. This word appears after في (in) in the phrase 'in our way'
Added fathas to make basic past tense form
Common change where و moves to make a long vowel
This is a question particle that turns a statement into a question, like adding 'is it that...?' in English

steps.m: is it that and...? (questioning with connection)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this question with the previous statement

steps.m: is it that and even if...? (questioning with connection and condition)
steps.n: Adds 'even if' to make this a conditional question. This combination of particles creates a complex meaning showing both questioning and a hypothetical situation.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make past tense 'he' form of 'to exist'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense
Changed from 'he was' to 'we were' by adding shadda and changing middle vowel to 'u' sound
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Adding vowels to make past tense verb showing feeling of dislike
Changed to show who does the action (like changing 'hate' to 'hater')
Added ين to show multiple people doing the action. This word describes the state they were in after كُنَّا (we were)
Added fatha at the end to show this describes their state (accusative case)

This is a particle that adds emphasis or certainty to the verb that follows it (ٱفْتَرَيْنَا meaning 'we would have invented'). It makes the statement stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: to make up or invent something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ٱ at start and ت in middle to show someone making up something false on purpose
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This verb follows قَدِ which emphasizes that something definitely happened.
This is a preposition that shows complete reliance and trust being placed upon Allah in this context. It's stronger than just saying 'in' or 'on' - it shows total dependence.
Added emphasis on the L sound and changed initial alif to wasl form
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word عَلَى (upon) before it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun instead of an action

steps.m: a lie (object of افْتَرَيْنَا/we have fabricated)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being fabricated in the phrase 'we have fabricated a lie'

steps.m: if (introduces condition for عُدْنَا - we returned)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical scenario. In this ayah, it connects the condition 'if we returned' with what would happen as a result.

steps.m: to return or go back repeatedly

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he returned' in the past tense
Adding نَا shows that 'we' did the action. This is part of a conditional statement starting with إِنْ (if), meaning 'if we returned'.
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something. Here it connects 'we returned' (عُدْنَا) with 'your religion' (مِلَّتِكُم) to show they returned into/to the religion.
Added kasra to start and shadda to make it a strong noun
Adding ة at the end turns the basic word for 'path' into the word for 'religion/way of life'
Adds كم suffix to show possession by multiple people. This word is the object of the preposition فِى (in) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be distant or separate from something

steps.n: Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make a word meaning 'after' - like saying 'there's space between things'

steps.m: after (connects to إِذْ 'when' to mean 'after when')
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it in the sentence
This is a particle that points to a specific time in the past. Here it refers to the time when Allah saved them.

steps.m: to rescue or deliver from danger

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he rescued' in the past tense
The doubled middle letter shows this was a strong or complete rescue
Added نَا at the end to show 'us' (we are the object being rescued), and changed from 'we rescued' to 'he rescued us'
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam
Allah has a shadda (double ل sound), starts with a special alif (ٱ), and ends with ُ to show it's doing the action of willing
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Added sukun at the end to show it connects to something.
Added ها which means 'it/her' to show what we're moving away from. Here it refers back to their religion (مِلَّتِكُم) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَكُونُ (to be) into 'is not'. In this context, it means 'it is not for us'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the idea that 'we would be inventing lies about Allah if we returned' with 'and it is not for us to return'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he/it is/becomes (subject of what is possible)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present/future tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action. In this ayah, it's describing what is possible to happen.
Starting with the preposition ل which shows purpose or direction
Added نا which means 'us' to show who the action is for
Added elongation mark (آ) to show the extended pronunciation at the end of the word when continuing to read

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what is being talked about - returning to their old ways. It works with the next word نَّعُودَ (we return) to show what isn't possible for them.

steps.m: to return or go back to a previous state

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: that we would return (in context: it is not for us that we would return)
steps.n: Added shadda on ن to show emphasis and connection to previous word أن (that)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it's used with مِلَّتِكُم (your religion/way) to show returning to that way.

steps.m: in it/her (referring to مِلَّتِكُم - your religion/way)
steps.n: Added feminine singular pronoun suffix ها to refer back to مِلَّتِكُم (your religion/way) which is feminine

steps.m: in it/her (referring to مِلَّتِكُم - your religion/way)
steps.n: Changed the final alif to a long alif (آ) to show emphasis when pausing in recitation
This is the basic exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. It's used here to say something can only happen with Allah's permission.
The alif is lengthened to آ to add emphasis and to help with the flow of reading, showing this is an important exception that depends on Allah's will.
This is a special word that connects two ideas. Here it connects 'it is not for us' with 'we return', showing what is not possible.
The word أَن makes the verb after it change its ending. It's like saying 'to' in English before a verb.
Root letters show basic concept of willing or wanting
Changed root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel marks
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending
Changed ending vowel to fatha to make it subjunctive mood
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the One True God who saved us)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and merged it with the word. The shadda shows emphasis. This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ُ shows this is the doer of نَجَّىٰنَا (saved us) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action وَسِعَ (encompassed)
Added نَا at the end to show possession (our). This word is doing the action of وَسِعَ (encompassed)

steps.m: to be vast or contain completely
steps.n: Root conveys the concept of vastness or encompassing capacity
Added vowels to show this is a past tense verb describing how our Lord (رَبُّنَا) completely encompasses everything with His knowledge

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture to completion

steps.n: The doubled ب shows this is someone who nurtures and cares for others perfectly and completely

steps.m: master who nurtures completely (doer of وَسِعَ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of وَسِعَ (encompassed/extended) in this ayah

steps.m: our master who nurtures completely (doer of وَسِعَ)
steps.n: The suffix نا indicates first person plural possession ('our'). This word is the subject (doer) of the verb وَسِعَ (encompassed/extended).
Adding the shadda (ّ) on ل shows this means 'completely' or 'totally' without any exceptions. The ُ (damma) on ك shows this is the base form of the word.
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is receiving the action of وَسِعَ (encompasses) in the phrase 'encompasses all things'
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukoon) to the root letters to make the common noun 'thing'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this thing is being possessed by كُلَّ (every/all) in the phrase 'every thing'
Changes from the action (to know) into the thing itself (knowledge)
The ًا at the end shows this word is receiving the action of وَسِعَ (encompassed). In this ayah, it's saying that Allah's knowledge encompasses everything.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or relying upon something else. Here it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show reliance upon Allah.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word عَلَى (upon/against). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at start and doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to actively put trust in' rather than just 'trust'

steps.m: we put our complete trust (in Allah)
steps.n: Added نَا to show that 'we' are doing the action. This word relates to عَلَى ٱللَّهِ (upon Allah) in the ayah to complete the meaning.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adding the double letter (shadda) changes this from a verb (to nurture) into a noun meaning 'lord' - someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely
Adding نا at the end means 'our'. The word is being used as a vocative - directly calling upon or addressing our Lord.

steps.m: to open, judge, or decide between

Changed to a command form. In this ayah, it's a prayer asking Allah to judge between the believers and their people with truth.
Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added نَا at the end to show who/what is being separated. The whole phrase means 'judge between us' in the ayah.

steps.m: to be separate or distinct from something

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'between' - describing the space that separates things. This word is being acted upon by the verb افْتَحْ (judge/decide) in this ayah.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous word. Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb افْتَحْ (judge).

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Changes the idea of standing together into a noun meaning the people who do this

steps.m: people (ready to be connected to something)

Adding the possessive pronoun نا to show ownership/possession by 'us'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
The middle letter is doubled to add emphasis and make it a noun
اَلْ means 'the' when added to the start of a word
بِ means 'with' or 'by' when added at the start
The kasra (ِ) shows this word follows the preposition بِ
This is a pronoun meaning 'you' (when speaking to one male). It's connected to the word خَيْرُ (best) that comes after it in the ayah.

steps.n: This form shows someone who makes judgments or decisions between people. In this ayah, it refers to Allah being asked to judge between the believers and their people.
The ين ending makes this plural to refer to multiple judges/decision-makers. Here it's used in the phrase 'best of judges' to praise Allah's perfect judgment.

steps.m: the judges/deciders (referring to Allah as the best of judges)
steps.n: ٱلْ (the) makes it specific. The word appears after خير (best of) in خير الفاتحين (best of judges), a phrase praising Allah's perfect judgment.
Added vowels to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'
Changed وَ to ا because it's easier to say in Arabic
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this word to what came before it. This word is what ٱلْمَلَأُ (the chiefs) did.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun referring to a group
Added ال to make it specific chiefs, and ُ to show it's doing the action of saying. This word is the subject of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things. The ال makes it definite, and ين makes it plural masculine.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense singular form
Changed the word to show many people did this action. This word is connected to قَالَ (said) in this ayah, showing who was speaking.

steps.m: from (connecting قَوْمِ with the previous phrase)
steps.n: Shows where something comes from or what group something belongs to. Here it connects 'people' (قَوْمِ) back to 'the chiefs who disbelieved' in the previous phrase.

steps.m: from (connecting قَوْمِ with the previous phrase)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is complete and ready to connect to the next word.
Changes from action to mean a group of people who stand together
Adds kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: to follow behind or along with something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added alif and doubled the ت to show choosing to do the action

The ًا (tanween fatha) at the end shows that Shuaib is the object of the verb 'follow' (اتَّبَعْتُمْ) in this ayah - meaning he's the one being followed.
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
This is the base form meaning a point in time

Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who loses
Makes it plural to show multiple losers
Adds emphasis to show they will definitely be losers
Adds extra emphasis with shadda and final vowel. This word describes what 'you all' are being called in the sentence.
Past tense feminine form of 'take' - the ت at the end shows a female (the earthquake) did the action
The word 'took' had 'them' (هم) added to the end to show who was taken
Adds conjunction prefix فَ meaning 'then/so'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

Added ال (the) to make it 'the specific shake' instead of 'a shake', and the ر got doubled because of this
Changed alif to wasl-alif since it's at start of word, and added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Added أ at beginning to show entering into a state
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: then they became (after الرَّجْفَةُ struck them)

This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects to the word دَارِهِمْ (their homes) to show where they were.
Adding the fatha vowel mark shows this is a past tense verb
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show the home belongs to them. This word comes after فِى (in) in the phrase 'they became in their homes'

steps.m: to sit or crouch down low

Changed to active participle form to describe someone in this state

steps.m: ones who are crouching (subject of أَصْبَحُوا)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people in this state. This describes how 'they became' (أَصْبَحُوا) in the ayah.

steps.m: ones who are crouching (in nominative case)

This is the basic form of the word used to describe or point to someone or something
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it specific
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word describes the people who are the subject of كَانُوا۟ (they were)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they'. This word is connected to كَانُوا۟ (they were) in this ayah.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the proper name Shuaib, who was a prophet mentioned in the Quran

steps.m: Shuaib (as the object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)

Starting with the basic particle أَنَّ (indeed/that) and lightening it to أَن (that)

steps.m: as if (connects to كَانُوا۟ - they were)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to show similarity or comparison. This creates the meaning 'as if' which connects to 'they were' (كَانُوا۟) later in the verse
This is the basic negative particle that makes verbs express past negation
When لَمْ comes after a word ending in ن (like ٱلَّذِينَ), the ل gets a shadda (ّ) to make the 'l' sound stronger. This is like when two similar sounds meet and blend together.
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he dwelled' in the past
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel from َ (fatha) to ْ (sukoon)
Added وْا to show multiple people (they) and changed ending to match. In this ayah, it's talking about the people who rejected Prophet Shuaib, as if they had never lived in their homes.
This is a basic word that shows something is inside or within something else

steps.m: in it (referring to their homes/dwellings mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to their homes/dwellings (feminine) mentioned earlier in the story
Added نَ at the end to change it from singular (the one who) to plural (those who). This word introduces the people who denied Shuaib, as shown by كَذَّبُوا۟ (they denied) that follows it.

steps.m: to deny, to reject as false

Adding وا changes it from one person (he) to many people (they) doing the action. The ُ before وا is needed to connect the letters properly.
This is the base form of the prophet's name mentioned in the Quran
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show Shuaib is the object of the verb كَذَّبُوا (they denied/rejected)
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it 'he was' in the past tense
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
This is the basic form of the separate pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' (for a group of males or mixed group)
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb كَانُوا (they were) in this ayah. This ُ helps connect it smoothly to the next word.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changes from 'he lost' to 'loser/one who loses' by using the active participle pattern. Like changing 'write' to 'writer'.
Adding ين to the end makes it mean 'many losers' instead of just 'one loser'. The ين shows it's talking about more than one male person.

steps.m: the losers (predicate of كَانُوا - they were)

Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong action
Added تَ at beginning to show doing action to oneself

steps.m: so he turned away (from عَنْهُمْ - from them)

steps.m: away from them (the people being turned away from in فَتَوَلَّىٰ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being moved away from - a group of males. This connects back to the people mentioned in فَتَوَلَّىٰ (he turned away)

Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing weak letter و with ا

steps.m: and he said (connecting to فَتَوَلَّىٰ - 'then he turned away')
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one. In the ayah, this shows that after turning away, he then said something.

steps.m: to stand together, rise up, establish

steps.n: The root ق-و-م becomes 'people/folk', then adding ِ (kasra) at the end makes it 'my people' - like saying 'O my people!'
Adding يٰ at the start makes it a call to someone, and the ِ at the end shows possession (my people)
This is a basic particle that shows something is certain or has already happened
Added لَ at the beginning to make the meaning stronger. This combination is used to strongly emphasize the verb that follows it (أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ - I conveyed)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to cause to reach, to deliver/convey (acted on)
steps.n: Adding hamza prefix with fatha and sukun on first root letter transforms this into causative form, meaning 'to make something reach/be delivered'
Adding past tense first person singular suffix تُ to indicate 'I'

steps.m: I delivered to you all (the messages of رِسٰلٰتِ)
steps.n: Adding second person masculine plural object pronoun كُمْ to show who received the delivery. This word connects to رِسٰلٰتِ (messages) which is what was delivered.

Adds an alif to make it a singular noun meaning 'message'
Changed alif to long alif and added ات at the end to make it plural (more than one message)
Added kasra at the end because this word belongs to رَبِّى (my Lord) in the ayah

steps.m: to care for and develop something/someone to their full potential
Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares in a complete way
The ى at the end shows this means 'my Lord' - it makes it personal, like saying 'my book' instead of just 'book'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding تُ changes 'he' to 'I' and shows I did this in the past. The ْ (sukun) is added before تُ to help pronunciation.

steps.m: and I sincerely advised (connected to أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ - I conveyed to you)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement 'I conveyed to you' (أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ), showing these actions happened together
Added fatha to the basic particle ل to show it means 'for' or 'to'
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being spoken to
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding these vowel marks creates the standard question word 'how'

steps.m: so how (connects to فَتَوَلَّىٰ - then he turned away)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question to what happened before - after the people rejected the message, the prophet asks 'so how could I grieve for such people?'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding آ at the start makes it mean 'to cause' the original meaning

steps.n: Root letters connected with a fat-ha (a) and sukoon (stop mark) to make 'settling'
Adding ة makes it refer to a specific place where people settle
Changes from single to plural by changing the vowels inside the word and ending with ى instead of ة

steps.m: the settlements (villages/towns) (object of نَقُصُّ - we narrate)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific, and the alif at the end is written with a small mark above. This word is what is being narrated in the verse.
Changed to basic command form with shadda (double letter) to show emphasis

steps.m: we narrate/tell (the stories of these towns)
steps.n: Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. The shadda shows we are telling the story with detail and emphasis. This word connects to تِلْكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ (those towns) whose stories are being told.
This is a preposition that comes from the idea of being high or elevated, which became used to mean 'upon' or 'on top of'
The ى changes to يْ when we add something to the end of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'you'. This is like adding 'you' to 'upon' to make 'upon you'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَنۢبَآئِهَا (its news/stories) to show the stories are coming from these towns.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun for one piece of news
Changed to plural form by adding أ at start and اء at end. This special pattern is used for important or significant things.
Changed the ending to show this word is owned by something else

steps.m: its news items (referring to the towns/cities)
steps.n: Added ها to show these news items belong to the towns (القرى) mentioned before
This is a particle of emphasis that strengthens the meaning of what follows
Adding لَ makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
و connects this statement with what came before it, adding the meaning of 'and'
Connected letters and added vowels. The ي changed to ا because it's between two fathas.
Added ت at the end to show a feminine subject (their messengers) did the action of coming
Adding هم suffix to indicate 'to them' as the object
The و after س makes it mean 'one who does this action a lot'
The ُ marks change it from singular to plural messenger
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in جَآءَتْ (they came)
Added هُم at the end to show these are the messengers belonging to them (the people of those towns mentioned earlier)
Added a shadda (double letter) to make it mean 'very clear/evident' instead of 'to make clear'
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple clear signs
Changed ending to show these signs belong to something else (like 'signs of Allah')
Added بِ (with/by) and ٱلْ (the) at beginning

steps.m: with the clear proofs/signs (fem pl owned)
steps.n: Added definite article 'the' after the preposition
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This word connects to كَانُوا۟ (they were) in the ayah.
Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of a past tense verb

steps.m: they were (referring to the people of those towns)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change 'he was' to 'they were'. This refers back to the people of the towns (القُرَىٰ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed the first letter to make it mean actively choosing to believe rather than just feeling trust
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added لِ to show this is the purpose or reason for something
This is a relative pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before. Here it refers to their previous denial of truth.
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how the action was done. This connects to كَذَّبُوا (they denied) to explain what they denied with.

steps.m: to deny, to reject as false

steps.n: Adding shadda (ّ) on middle letter makes the denial stronger and more emphatic. This word connects to كَانُوا (they were) in the ayah.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (for a group of men or mixed group). This shows that many people were doing the denying.
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or partial amount. Here it shows we're talking about some of the news/stories, not all of them.

steps.m: to be in front of something

steps.n: Changes from verb root to a noun meaning 'before'
Adds damma to show this word is in the nominative case
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا (this) and extending the alif to show distance
Added لِ to make it point to something far away, changing 'this' to 'that'
Added كَ at the end to complete the demonstrative word

steps.m: like that, similarly (describes how يَطْبَعُ/seals happens)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to mean 'like/similar to'. This word describes how Allah seals the hearts in the next part of the ayah (يَطْبَعُ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and adding ُ at the end. This word shows what Allah does to the hearts of the disbelievers.
Changed to the proper name for Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This makes it specific to the One True God rather than any deity.

steps.m: Allah (the subject of يَطْبَعُ - He seals)
steps.n: Added the shadda to emphasize the ل and added ُ to show Allah is doing the action in this ayah. The word is the subject (doer) of يَطْبَعُ (He seals).
Basic preposition showing position or authority over something. In this ayah, it connects with قُلُوبِ (hearts) to show where Allah places the seal.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is the end of the word. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to make the noun meaning 'heart'
Changed vowels and added و to make plural form
Added kasra at end to show this word connects to 'the disbelievers' (الكٰفِرِينَ) that comes after it

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changes verb into noun describing someone who does this action

steps.m: those who reject/cover up truth (possessed by قُلُوب/hearts)
steps.n: Changes from singular (one person) to plural (many people) and shows these people belong to or are connected to hearts

steps.m: the ones who reject/cover up truth (possessed by قُلُوب/hearts)
steps.n: Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
This is a particle that negates the verb وَجَدْنَا (we found) in this ayah
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to find/discover something after searching or effort

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Adding نَا changes it from 'he' to 'we' and shows we are the ones doing the finding. This is the main verb that tells what happened in the sentence.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to mean 'more/most' by adding أَ at the start and changing vowels

steps.m: most of them (referring to the people mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِم (him) at the end to mean 'of them' - shows we're talking about most of a specific group

steps.m: for most of them (referring to the people mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added لِ at the start to mean 'for' or 'to'
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts or what it belongs to
Added a shadda (double letter mark) because it comes after another word ending in a vowel
The root letters ع-ه-د become the noun عَهْد (promise) by adding the vowel marks. Like changing 'promise' (verb) to 'a promise' (noun).
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this is any general promise/covenant. This word follows the preposition مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah.

steps.m: if/indeed (introduces a condition in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if we found' in this ayah. It can also mean 'indeed' for emphasis.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about not finding most of them keeping their covenant
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'
The alif is lengthened at the end to emphasize the 'we' sound
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Forms pattern أَفْعَل to indicate comparative/superlative meaning
The word 'most' got connected to 'them' (هُمْ) to mean 'most of them', and is being used to tell us who was found

steps.m: one who breaks away from obedience
steps.n: Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show who does the action

steps.m: those who break away from obedience (describes أَكْثَرَهُمْ - most of them)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This describes أَكْثَرَهُمْ (most of them) in the ayah.

steps.m: truly those who break away from obedience (describes أَكْثَرَهُمْ - most of them)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'

steps.m: then (connecting بَعَثْنَا/We sent with previous events)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to complete the word. This word connects this story to previous stories, showing this happened afterward.

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This is the main verb of the sentence. The نَا suffix shows that Allah is speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts from or comes from
Added kasra (ِ) under م and a small ن mark at the end. This preposition connects 'after' (بَعْدِ) to 'We sent' (بَعَثْنَا), showing when the sending happened.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to the root letters to make the word mean 'after'
Added هِم at the end to show this means 'after them'. The ِ before هِم shows this word follows مِنۢ (from) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the proper name Moses, derived from Egyptian/Hebrew meaning 'born of/child of'.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: damma (ُ) on م and fatha (َ) on س. The ىٰ at the end is the standard Quranic spelling.
Added shadda (ّ) on the م to show emphasis in pronunciation, which is common in Quranic proper names.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Root letters were combined and vowels added to make the noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle'
Made the word plural to show multiple signs
Added بِ at the beginning to show these signs were used as tools or means

steps.m: with Our signs/miracles (sent with Moses to Pharaoh)
steps.n: Added نَا to show these are Allah's signs that were given to Moses
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects Musa's sending to Pharaoh (فِرْعَوْنَ), showing who he was sent to.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
This is a borrowed title from ancient Egyptian that became used as a name, specifically referring to the ruler of ancient Egypt. Not derived from an Arabic root.
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation of this foreign word in Arabic.

steps.m: Pharaoh (as the object being addressed by موسى)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being addressed in the sentence. Moses was sent to Pharaoh.

steps.m: to gather, assemble (especially of noble people)

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he gathered) to a noun (assembly) and added kasra at the end to show it belongs to someone
Added هِۦ to show the assembly belongs to him (Pharaoh)
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. This connects it to Pharaoh mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to wrong or be unjust to someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb meaning one person did the action
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did the action
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something before it. In this ayah, it shows what they did with Allah's signs (بِـَٔايٰتِنَآ)
Added kasra to the letter ب to make it readable
Added هَا at the end to show this refers back to the signs (آيَاتِنَا) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by making it a command - added U sound at start and made the word shorter
Added فَ to show this command comes as a result of what was mentioned before. This word is connected to فَظَلَمُوا۟ (so they wronged) in the previous ayah, showing a sequence of events.
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to make the question word pronounceable
Adding the final vowel mark to complete the question word
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Adding vowels turns the root into a usable past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to make it mean 'the thing that comes after' or 'outcome'
Changed the alif to a special form (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added ُ to show this word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in the sentence

steps.m: someone who causes damage or harm
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle shows who does the action, like changing 'damage' to 'damager'

steps.m: people who cause damage or harm (possessed by عٰقِبَةُ/outcome)
steps.n: Adding ين at the end makes it plural and shows it belongs to the word عٰقِبَةُ (outcome) - like saying 'outcome of the corrupters'

steps.m: the people who cause damage or harm (possessed by عٰقِبَةُ/outcome)
steps.n: Adding ال at the start is like adding 'the' in English - it points to specific corrupters, not just any corrupters
Added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word tells us what Moses (مُوسَىٰ) did.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This shows that Moses both did something before AND said this.

steps.m: Moses (the prophet who spoke to فِرْعَوْنُ)
steps.n: This is the base form of the proper name Moses, borrowed from Hebrew/Egyptian. Since it's a borrowed name, it doesn't come from an Arabic root.

steps.m: Moses (the prophet who spoke to فِرْعَوْنُ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation: 'Mūsā'

steps.m: Moses (the prophet who spoke to فِرْعَوْنُ)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the final ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'ā' sound
This is a borrowed word from ancient Egyptian, not from an Arabic root
Added يٰ (yaa) at the start to show someone is calling out to Pharaoh, like saying 'O Pharaoh!' or 'Hey Pharaoh!'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'definitely' in English
Added ي at the end to show that Musa (Moses) is speaking about himself to Pharaoh
Changed the writing style of ي to ى at the end, but the meaning stays the same
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
The tanween (double damma) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English). This word is part of Musa's statement to Pharaoh where he identifies himself as a messenger.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from
Added shadda because this word connects strongly to the next word رَّبِّ (Lord). This happens because of a rule that makes the pronunciation flow better between words.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
Lord (one who cares for and develops everything)
steps.n: Connected the letters and added shadda to show this is someone who does this caring perfectly
Added kasra to show this Lord belongs to or is connected to something (the worlds/realms)
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: to know, to mark, to distinguish
