The final ي changes to ى, and we add tanwin (ً) to show this is a general concept of guidance. This word leads to رَحْمَةٌ (mercy) in the ayah.
Adding ة turns the root into a feminine noun meaning 'mercy'
Adds 'and' to connect this word with هُدًى (guidance) mentioned before it in the ayah
Adds double dammah to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a mercy' instead of 'the mercy')
This is the basic relative pronoun used to describe people who do something
Added the preposition لِ (for/to) at the beginning
The lam gets a shadda because it merges with another lam. This word connects to يَرْهَبُونَ (they fear) in the ayah, showing who does the fearing.

steps.m: they (referring to those who fear their Lord)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers to a group of people (masculine). In this ayah, it specifically refers to those who fear their Lord (يَرْهَبُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ). The هُ shows it's about them, and the مْ makes it plural.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose
Added هِمْ at the end to show this is their lord. This word is what they fear/have awe of in يَرْهَبُونَ (they fear/have awe)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Present tense form showing ongoing action of fearing

steps.m: they fear/are fearing deeply (for their Lord)
steps.n: The ونَ ending shows this is being done by a group of people. In this ayah, it refers to those who fear their Lord.

steps.m: to choose or select the best option

Added Form VIII pattern which shows doing something for oneself
Added alif to make it a complete past tense verb
Added و (and) at beginning and changed initial alif to connecting form

Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (what was chosen) in the phrase 'And Moses chose his people'
Added هُ with elongation mark ۥ at the end to show these are Moses's people
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic number 'seven'
Added ين to create the number 'seventy'. This word is what Moses chose (the object of اخْتَارَ - he chose)
The vowels make it into a noun meaning 'man'

steps.m: a man (object being counted in سَبْعِينَ رَجُلًا - seventy men)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon and is indefinite. Here it specifies what is being counted - seventy men.

steps.m: to set or appoint a specific time

steps.n: Added مِ at the start and ا in the middle to create a word for the time or place when something happens
Added نَا at the end to show this belongs to us
Added لِ with shadda to mean 'for/to' - the shadda shows emphasis
This is the basic particle meaning 'when' that shows something happened right after something else
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous action (Moses choosing 70 men)
Changed the final ا to آ, which is just a writing style used in the Quran - it doesn't change the meaning
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he took' in the past

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord

Adding تَ makes it 'you' (masculine singular) past tense

Adding تَ at start changes it from 'he wished' to 'you/she wish(es)'

Added conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Added first person plural object pronoun suffix نا meaning 'us'

steps.m: you (when speaking to Allah in this ayah)

Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'good'

This is a preposition with no root. The kasra (ِ) shows it's a preposition.

Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it. Here it connects to the word حَسَنَةً (good things) in the previous part.

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to عَذَابِى - my punishment)

steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of يَتَّبِعُونَ (they follow) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he followed) to present tense (he follows) by adding يَ at the start
Intensifies meaning through letter doubling
Changed from 'he follows' to 'they follow' by adding ون at the end to match with 'those who' (الَّذِينَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sending) to a noun (person who sends/delivers)
Added 'al' (the) at the start to make it specific which messenger we're talking about

steps.m: the messenger (object of يَتَّبِعُونَ - they follow)
steps.n: Added fatḥa at the end to show this word is the object being followed in the phrase 'they follow the messenger'
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Changed middle letter to ي and added ء at end to make it mean 'person who brings news'
Adding shadda makes the word stronger and more special - changes from just 'news bringer' to 'prophet'
Added ال to specify a particular prophet, and the ن gets doubled after ال

steps.m: the prophet (object of يَتَّبِعُونَ - they follow)
steps.n: Changed the ending to show this is the object being followed in the sentence

steps.m: to be like a mother, to be original/primary

steps.n: The ُ (damma) changes the word to mean a group/collection
Adding ّ (shadda) doubles the م to emphasize multiple nations
The doubled ي emphasizes this is a complete/natural state of being untaught
Adding ال makes it definite to match النَّبِىَّ (the Prophet) it describes
The َ shows this word follows النَّبِىَّ in the sentence

steps.m: which/who/that (points to one male person or thing)
steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that points to one male person or thing. In this ayah, it points to النَّبِىَّ (the Prophet)

steps.m: the one who/which/that (points to النَّبِىَّ - the Prophet)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ with a shadda makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The first root letter و drops because it's a weak letter.
Adds ون to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
Adds هُۥ to show who is being found (him)

steps.m: written down (describing the Prophet ﷺ mentioned in يَجِدُونَهُ)
steps.n: The word changed from 'write' to 'written' by adding مَ at the start and و before the last letter - like changing 'write' to 'written' in English

steps.m: written down (as something they find يَجِدُونَهُ in their scriptures)
steps.n: Adding ًا shows this describes what they find (يَجِدُونَهُ) in their scriptures
Added kasra (ِ) and fatha (َ) to make it a word showing location or presence

steps.m: with them (referring to what they find written يَجِدُونَهُۥ مَكْتُوبًا)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this is happening with or to them. This connects to the previous phrase about what 'they find written'
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ى at the end to show this is a complete preposition. This word connects to التَّوْرَىٰةِ (the Torah) to show what something is written in.
This is the basic form of the word Torah, borrowed from Hebrew. In Arabic, foreign words are adapted to fit Arabic patterns.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ت, sukun on و, and elongated alif after ى
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific (like 'the' in English). The ت gets a shadda because it comes after ٱل.
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word فِى (in/within) in the ayah
This is a proper noun borrowed from Greek (Evangelion) meaning 'good news'
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the Gospel' instead of just any gospel
The ِ at the end shows it follows the word فِى (in) in this ayah
وَ connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now. The sukun (ْ) and damma (ُ) are part of this present tense pattern.
Adding هُم shows who is being commanded (them)

steps.m: of what is known to be good/right
steps.n: The root 'to know' becomes 'what is known/good' by adding ma- at start. The -i ending shows it follows a preposition
Adding ال makes it definite - referring to specific known good conduct

steps.m: with/by what is right (object of يَأْمُرُهُم - he commands them)
steps.n: Adding the preposition ب changes it to 'with/by what is right'. This phrase describes how he (the Prophet) commands them, connecting to the verb يَأْمُرُهُم (he commands them) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, and changed the middle vowel to sukun (ْ)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being stopped
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. It has no root since it's a particle.
Added vowel marks to show how it connects to the next word المُنكَرِ (the wrong). The kasra (ِ) at the end helps connect it smoothly to the next word.

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Added مُ at the beginning to show this is the thing being rejected, not the one doing the rejecting

steps.m: the wrong deed (object of يَنْهَىٰهُمْ عَنِ)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific wrong deeds. This word is what they are being forbidden from in the phrase يَنْهَىٰهُمْ عَنِ (he forbids them from)

steps.m: from the wrong deed (object of يَنْهَىٰهُمْ عَنِ)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this comes after عَنِ (from/against). Together they mean 'from wrong deeds' in the phrase 'he forbids them from wrong deeds'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he permitted) to a noun (permissible thing) by changing middle vowel and adding shadda
Added يُ at the beginning to make it mean 'he makes' instead of just 'to make'

steps.m: and he makes permissible (connected to يَأْمُرُهُم)
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect to previous phrase and ُ at end to show it's doing the action
Starting with the basic parts: ل meaning 'for/to' and هم meaning 'them'
Adding vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced: fatha on ل and damma on ه

steps.m: for them (connected to يُحِلُّ - makes permissible)
steps.n: Adding final damma to help connect smoothly with the next word. This word is connected to يُحِلُّ (makes permissible) in this ayah.
Adding shadda intensifies the meaning
Changes from 'pure/good' to 'pure/good things' by adding -aat ending
Adding definite article to make the noun definite
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is the object of a preposition

steps.m: to forbid or make something sacred/protected

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger - like the difference between 'forbid' and 'strictly forbid'

steps.m: he declares (something) forbidden (connected to يَأْمُرُهُم - he commands them)
steps.n: Present tense form (يُ at start) of 'forbid' - matches with 'he commands them' later in verse. The dots/marks show it's happening now.

steps.m: and he declares (something) forbidden (connected to يَأْمُرُهُم - he commands them)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous actions mentioned about the Prophet ﷺ
This is a preposition showing something being on top of or over something else
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding endings

steps.m: upon them (object of يُحَرِّمُ - he forbids)
steps.n: Added هِمُ which means 'them' (for a group). This word shows who the forbidding applies to in the phrase 'he forbids upon them'
Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing something was bad
Changed from 'became bad' (past tense verb) to 'bad' (describing word)
Changed the word to plural form to talk about multiple bad things
Added ٱلْ to specify these particular bad things, and َ at the end because it's being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to put or place something down
and to put or place something down
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this action with what came before it
Added يَ to show this is happening in the present/future tense
Added ُ at the end to complete the present tense form
This is a preposition that doesn't come from a root
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (for a group). This word is receiving the action of يَضَعُ (he removes) in this ayah.
Changed the أ to إِ and added a sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'burden'

steps.m: their burden (that is being removed from them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show the burden belongs to them. This burden is mentioned as being removed from them in this ayah.

steps.m: to bind or restrain with chains

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun for a single chain or shackle
Changed from one chain to many chains by adding أ at start and making it plural (أَغْلال)
Added the definite article 'the'

steps.m: and the chains/shackles (object of يَضَعُ - he removes)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning and the fatha at the end to show this is the object being removed
This is the basic form of the feminine relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to feminine nouns
Added ال with shadda at the beginning to make it definite, and changed the ending to ى. This word refers back to the good things (الطَّيِّبٰتِ) and bad things (الْخَبٰٓئِثَ) mentioned earlier.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle weak letter و changes to ا because it's in past tense
Added ت with sukun to show the subject is feminine. This word refers back to الَّتِى (which) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that comes from the concept of being high or elevated. The root letters show the basic idea of height or rising.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting to the next part. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added هِمْ which means 'them' when referring to a group of males or a mixed group.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what they describe, like saying 'the one who' or 'that which' in English
Adding ال makes it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: so those who (connected to what comes after ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this is a result of what was mentioned before, connecting it to what follows (ءَامَنُوا۟)
Changed root 'trust' into past tense 'believed' by adding long alif at start - Form IV verb pattern
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected. It doesn't come from a root.
Added هِ which shows this connects to 'him/it'. In this ayah, it refers back to believing (ءَامَنُوا۟).
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense.
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding وا at the end

steps.m: they honored and supported him strongly (referring to الرَّسُولَ)
steps.n: هُ shows who receives the action - in this case referring back to the Messenger (الرَّسُولَ) mentioned earlier in the verse
و is the coordinating conjunction meaning 'and'
Added vowel marks to make it 'he helped' in the past tense
وا indicates third person masculine plural past tense
و connects this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and doubled ت with shadda to show stronger meaning
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and they followed closely/earnestly (النُّورَ - the light)
steps.n: Added وا at end to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to النُّورَ (the light) which comes after it in the ayah, showing what they followed.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding damma on first letter and making the و long
Word becomes definite with addition of ال

steps.m: the light (object of وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ - they followed)
steps.n: The fatha shows this word is receiving the action of 'following' in the phrase 'they followed the light'
Base word that indicates possession or attribution
Adding ال with shadda changes this into a relative pronoun that connects descriptions to nouns. Here it describes 'the light' (النُّورَ) mentioned before it.
The added mark (ٓ) shows that this word should be pronounced with a longer sound when reciting
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive form by adding أُ at start, sukun on ن, and kasra on ز. This shows the action happened to something rather than something doing the action.
This is a preposition meaning 'with'. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: with him (referring to النُّورَ - the light)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to mean 'him'. This refers back to 'the light' (النُّورَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added ۥٓ which are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are far away
Adding ئِ helps show we're pointing to something far away
Adding كَ at the end completes the word. This word points to the believers mentioned before, who are the subject of هُمُ (they are) in this ayah
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' that refers to a group of people. In this ayah, it connects to the word الْمُفْلِحُونَ (the successful ones) to say 'they are the successful ones'
Added مُ at the beginning to show who does the action (like adding '-er' in English), and added ِ after ل to complete the pattern
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific. This word comes after هُمُ (they are) in the ayah
Added ون and َ at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This is the predicate (describing word) after هُمُ (they are) in the ayah, telling us who 'they' are
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on last letter, 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the people.

steps.m: to point to or indicate something
which/what (asking about or pointing to something)
steps.n: Root letters أيي combined into أيّ with shadda to make a question word meaning 'which/what'
Added يٰٓ at beginning to make it a call for attention
Added هَا with ُ to complete the calling phrase
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic word
Changed to a noun meaning 'people' (those who are social with each other)
Added emphasis by doubling the ن
Added 'the' to make it definite
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like saying 'I am definitely...' or 'I am surely...' in English
Added ى at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This connects to the next word رَسُولُ (messenger), making the full meaning 'Indeed I am the messenger'

steps.m: to send or deliver a message

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject being talked about. In this ayah, it's followed by اللَّهِ to show this is Allah's messenger specifically.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word belongs to رَسُولُ (messenger) in the phrase 'messenger of Allah'
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. It's like pointing to where something is going.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when something is attached to it. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to you all (connected to رَسُولُ meaning I am a messenger to you all)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being spoken to. This connects to the earlier word رَسُولُ, showing Muhammad (SAW) is a messenger to all people.

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Added ي to show this describes a complete group that's gathered together, like saying 'all of them as one'

steps.m: all together (describing how something is done in the ayah إِلَيْكُمْ جَمِيعًا - to all of you together)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to make it an adverb - shows how something is done 'together as one group'
This is the base word that points to someone or something we're talking about

steps.m: the one who (describes رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ - the Messenger of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific and points to someone particular. The ل combines with ذ to make a stronger ذ sound (shown by ّ). In this ayah, it's describing the Messenger of Allah.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition ل
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who something is for. This refers to Allah based on the context.
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to own or rule over something
steps.n: Basic meaning of ownership or authority
Changed the word into a noun by adding damma (ُ) at start and sukun (ْ) in middle. This makes it mean 'kingdom' instead of 'to rule'
Added 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action (subject)

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated
steps.n: Root meaning of height or elevation
Transforms root into noun meaning sky/heaven by adding اء ending
Changed from single sky/heaven to multiple heavens, with a special plural form
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Root meaning related to earth/ground
The root letters combine with a fatha mark to form the common noun meaning 'earth' or 'land'

steps.m: the earth (affected by ownership of مُلْكُ)
steps.n: The word 'earth' gets 'the' (ال) at the start and a kasra (ِ) at the end to show it belongs to or is owned by something else

steps.m: and the earth (part of مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - dominion of the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Connects 'the earth' to 'the heavens' (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ) mentioned before it, showing Allah's dominion over both
Basic negative particle used to negate what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates the existence of any deity (إِلٰهَ).
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Changed to elongated alif (آ) to show emphasized pronunciation. This emphasis matches its important role in negating the existence of any deity except Allah.
Changed the first letter to إِ and added ا after second root letter to make it a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
The word is affected by لَآ (no/there is not) before it, which requires the َ ending. This combination لَآ إِلٰهَ means 'there is no deity'.

steps.m: except (introduces an exception to لَا إِلٰهَ - 'there is no god')
steps.n: This is a special word used to show exceptions. In this ayah, it's used to say 'there is no god EXCEPT Him'. It's made up of three parts that work together to create this meaning.
Basic form of the third person masculine singular pronoun, showing who we're talking about
Added وَ to make it a standalone pronoun that can be used by itself (referring to Allah in this ayah)
Root meaning of life/living
Root letters connected with vowels to make 'give life' verb
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense and who is doing the action
Changed spelling to match Uthmani script, but pronunciation stays the same
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in this pattern
Added يُ at start and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'causes/makes someone die' instead of just 'died'
Added ُ at end to show this is the main verb form (not a command or condition)
Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous phrase about giving life
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he trusted'. Made from the root letters that mean 'trust/security'
Changed to make the action stronger - from just trusting to fully believing in something
Adds فَ (fa) at the start to mean 'so/then/and'
Changes from past tense 'he believed' to a command 'believe!' (not plural - single person command)
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the one and only God
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done or the means by which it happens, and added ٱ (alif with wasl) for pronunciation
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed vowels and added و to show this is someone who delivers messages as their role
Added ِ to show this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Added هِ to show the messenger belongs to Him (Allah)
Added و to connect this word with what came before it
Changed root letters into a noun pattern with vowels and ي to mean 'someone who brings news/messages from God'
Added 'the' (ال) which combines with the ن to make it stronger (doubled)
Changed from regular spelling to a special spelling of 'prophet' that shows it's connected to another word (رَسُولُ) in the sentence

steps.m: to be part of a community/nation

steps.n: Doubles middle letter م and adds ُ to make the word mean 'mother'
Adds -iy ending to make an adjective meaning 'like a mother' (someone who can't read/write, as if they are like when they came from their mother)
Adds the definite article ال to specify this refers to the Prophet Muhammad specifically
This is the base demonstrative word that points to something. The ى at the end shows it's for masculine things.

steps.m: who/which/that (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding ٱلّ at the beginning changes this from pointing at something to connecting parts of a sentence. Here it connects Allah with what comes after (that He has dominion over the heavens and earth).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the one and only God
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done, and ٱ (alif with wasl) for pronunciation

steps.m: to speak or communicate with words

steps.n: Root 'speak' became 'one word' by adding ة at the end to make it singular
Changed from singular 'word' to plural 'words' by adding ات at the end. The long alif (ٰ) shows it's a special type of plural for this word.
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect this word to previous phrase
Added هِۦ to show these are His (Allah's) words. The ۦ mark after هِ is a pronunciation guide. This word follows بِ (in/with) in يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَكَلِمٰتِهِۦ (believes in Allah and His words).
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to follow'
Changed from 'he followed' to a command 'follow!' with extra ت showing strong/complete following
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and follow him! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added هُ at end to show who is being followed
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation for something to happen. Here it's expressing hope for guidance (تَهْتَدُونَ - you may be guided).

steps.m: so that you all may (be guided - تَهْتَدُونَ)
steps.n: Adds attached pronoun كُمْ to indicate the subject 'you all'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت in middle to make the meaning 'do it to yourself' - like changing 'guide' to 'guide yourself'
Prefix ت changes verb from 'he' to 'you' and from past to present tense

steps.m: you all find guidance for yourselves
steps.n: Suffix ون changes subject to masculine plural 'you all'

steps.m: from/among (of قَوْمِ مُوسَىٰٓ - the people of Moses)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing something comes from or is part of something else. Here it introduces where the people come from.

steps.m: and from/among (of قَوْمِ مُوسَىٰٓ - the people of Moses)
steps.n: The و at the start means 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before. It shows this is an additional group being mentioned.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the noun meaning 'people'
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, to Moses/مُوسَىٰٓ)

This is a proper noun (name) borrowed from Hebrew meaning 'drawn out of water'. It refers to the prophet Moses (peace be upon him). In this verse, Moses's people are being described - some of them guide with truth.

steps.m: to lead or be a source/origin
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'community' or 'nation'
Adds feminine ending to make it a noun meaning a group or community
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any community/nation, not a specific one
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The final ى changes back to ي in present tense.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to يَعْدِلُونَ (they act justly) in the ayah, showing the same group doing both actions.
Forms a noun meaning 'truth' or 'right'
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to make it stronger
Added ٱلْ to make it specific, like saying 'the truth' instead of just 'truth'

steps.m: with the truth (used with يَهْدُونَ - they guide)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show 'with/by' and ِ at the end because it follows بِ. This word explains how they guide - they guide with/by the truth.
Combines the preposition 'with' (بِ) with the pronoun 'him/it' (هِ) to show something is being done using or with something
Adds 'and' to connect this phrase to what came before it in the verse
Adds a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This word connects to يَعْدِلُونَ (they judge) showing what they judge with.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The ي prefix shows this is happening now (present tense)
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end, matching with يَهْدُونَ (they guide) to show the same group doing both actions
Adding shadda (ّ) on ط shows the action was done repeatedly or thoroughly
Added نا to show 'we' did the action, with sukun (ْ) before it for pronunciation
Added هُمْ to show the people being cut/divided

steps.m: and we cut them into pieces
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start, changed final vowel to ُ because another word follows

steps.m: to double or make into two

steps.n: Changed the root letters into the number 'two' by adding special marks
Added ٱ at start to make it a command
Added تَ at the end to change it to 'you' form

steps.m: two (feminine, in a pair with عَشْرَةَ)
steps.n: Changed ending to make it work with عَشْرَةَ to mean 'twelve'. Like saying 'two' in 'two-teen'
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a number
In Arabic, numbers 3-10 take the feminine form (with ة) when counting masculine nouns, and vice versa. Here it's feminine because it's counting عَيْنًا (springs) in the phrase 'twelve springs'

steps.m: nations (as object of قَطَّعْنٰهُمُ/We divided them into)

steps.m: to inspire or reveal divine guidance

steps.m: when (connecting to ٱسْتَسْقَىٰهُ - his people asked him for water)

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the word to give a command. In this ayah, Allah is commanding Prophet Musa to strike the rock with his staff.

steps.m: to gush forth or burst out

Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something (in this case, Moses striking the rock)
Added ت at the end because the subject (the spring/عَيْنًا) is feminine in Arabic
This is a combination of 'from' (مِنْ) and 'it' (هُ)
This is a number word that doesn't come from a root. It's the feminine form of 'two'

steps.m: of a people (belonging to كُلُّ - every/all)

Added هُمْ at the end to show this belongs to them

Added 'we' ending. This is the main verb of this part of the sentence, showing what Allah did.

Added ت with sukun at the end to show this is about a feminine subject (the town)

steps.m: being present (feminine, describes القَرْيَةِ - the town)

Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun

Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
