Changes the word to mean 'more close/low' by adding أ at the start. This describes the worldly goods (عَرَضَ) mentioned in the verse.
Adds ٰ at the end which is a common ending for words showing 'more' of something
Adding ال (the) makes it specific - now means 'the nearer/lower' instead of just 'nearer/lower'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changes from past tense (he said) to present tense (he says) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Adding ون shows that multiple men are doing the action
Adding conjunction 'and' at the start
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he forgave' to 'is forgiven' by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show someone receives the action
Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for us (in يَقُولُونَ سَيُغْفَرُ لَنَا - they say, 'It will be forgiven for us')
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this is happening to 'us'. This word connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) and سَيُغْفَرُ (will be forgiven) to show who will receive the forgiveness.

steps.m: if (introduces a condition in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else happening

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to what came before)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this condition to the previous statement
Added يَ at the beginning to show who is doing the action (he) and when (now/future)
Added هِ to show who receives the action (him/it)
Changed from 'him' to 'them' by adding م

steps.m: to show up temporarily, to present itself briefly

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels changes this from an action into a thing - specifically something temporary or fleeting that presents itself as a benefit
Adding tanween (double damma) makes this an indefinite noun - meaning 'a' temporary gain rather than 'the' temporary gain

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Root meaning 'similar' becomes a noun that compares things - like saying 'this is like that'
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing something in the sentence, like being the subject
Adding هُ at the end makes it mean 'his/its' - like saying 'similar to it'
Adding ُ (damma) makes it nominative case
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding و at the end shows multiple men are doing the action
Adding هُ shows what is being taken
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to their opposite in the past tense
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يُ at the beginning and changed vowels to show something is being done to the subject rather than the subject doing it

steps.m: it is taken (in a conditional statement)
steps.n: Changed أ to ؤ because it comes after the 'u' sound, and made the end silent (with sukun) because it's in an 'if' statement
This is a preposition that shows something is above or affecting something else
The ى changes to ي with sukun to connect with the next part

steps.m: upon them (referring to يَأْخُذُونَ - those who take)
steps.n: Adding هِم shows this affects multiple people (them). In this ayah, it refers back to those who take worldly gains mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to bind firmly or make secure

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowels and alif, making it mean the actual binding or chain used to tie something
Added مي at the start to make it mean a more serious and formal promise or agreement

steps.m: the covenant (subject of يُؤْخَذْ - was taken)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and ُ at end to show this is the subject of 'was taken' in the verse. The long alif is written as ٰ here.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'wrote' to noun meaning 'something written'
The ال at the start makes it 'the book' (specific), and the ending changes to ِ because it's being acted upon by another word

steps.m: that (introduces what يَقُولُوا۟ they should say)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects يَقُولُوا۟ (they say) to what comes before it. It shows what they shouldn't say, like how we use 'that' in English to introduce what someone says.
This is the basic negative particle that makes something 'not' happen
The shadda (double letter mark) on ل makes the word stronger, emphasizing that they must 'not' say anything except the truth about Allah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show 'he' in present tense. The middle و changed to ُ (u sound) which is common for this type of word
Added ون to show multiple male speakers
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. The small circle (۟) is just a pronunciation mark. This is in subjunctive mood because it comes after أَن (that) in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, against, or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show what they shouldn't say things about.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is the object of the preposition عَلَى (upon/about). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Starting with the conditional particle إِلْ which means 'if'

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: These are actually two different words - إِلَّا is not derived from إِلْ. إِلَّا is its own word meaning 'except' or 'only'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The verb 'he was truthful' becomes the noun 'truth' by dropping a letter and adding a shadda mark, turning an action into the idea itself
Adding ال makes it refer to absolute truth rather than just any truth
The fatha shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يَقُولُوا (they say) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Transforms singular past tense to plural past tense

steps.m: and they studied deeply (what was in الْكِتٰبِ - the Book)
steps.n: The و at the start means 'and', connecting this to what came before. In this ayah, they studied what was in the Book (Scripture).
This is a special word that connects to فِيهِ (in it) to ask about what is in something. In this ayah, it's asking about what is written in the Book.
This is a preposition that shows location or being inside something

steps.m: in it (referring to what's in الْكِتٰبِ - the Book)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show what the word 'in' refers to. Here it refers back to the Book (الْكِتٰبِ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to move in a circle/turn around
dwelling/home (from the idea of people moving around in it)
steps.n: Changed from action to place where action happens. The و becomes ا after fatha.
Added the definite article ال with shadda on د
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added و at beginning to connect to previous phrase
Added long alif after first root letter and kasra after second root letter to make it mean 'the one that comes after'. This describes الدار (the home/abode).
Added ة at the end to make it a noun specifically referring to the life that comes after this one
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject being described as better (خَيْرٌ) for those who are mindful of Allah
Changes from verb 'to be good' into the noun 'good/goodness'
The double-damma (tanween) at the end makes this word 'better' act as the main subject of the sentence
This is a special word used to connect descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who' or 'that which'
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding لِ at the start shows this is being done for or to these people
The ل at the start merged with another ل, making it stronger (shown by the shadda)
Changed the verb to show it's happening in the present time
Added ت with shadda to show the person is doing the action to protect themselves
Added ون to show many people are doing this action. In Islamic context, this often means being mindful of Allah and avoiding sin.

steps.m: question marker (asking about تَعْقِلُونَ - you reason)
steps.n: This turns the statement into a question, like adding 'Do...?' or 'Will...?' at the start of an English sentence
Added فَ to show this question follows from what was said before, like saying 'so then' or 'therefore' in English

steps.m: will they not then (asking about تَعْقِلُونَ - you reason)
steps.n: Added لَا to make the question negative, like adding 'not' in English. The whole phrase is asking 'Will they not then use reason?'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed vowels and added ت at start to show this is happening now and the doer is 'you'
Added ون to show this is addressing multiple people
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that describes people or things
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person

steps.m: and those who (who يُمَسِّكُونَ hold fast)
steps.n: Adding و at the start connects this to what came before. This introduces people who hold fast to the Book (يُمَسِّكُونَ بِٱلْكِتٰبِ)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels - makes it mean 'he grasps' instead of 'he grasped'
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to بِٱلْكِتٰبِ (the Book) which comes after it.
Present tense verb meaning 'to hold/grasp firmly' with ون showing multiple people doing the action
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the vowel pattern and added alif to make it mean the result of writing (a book)
Added ال to make it specific (the book)

steps.m: by/with the book (held onto by يُمَسِّكُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show how the book is used, and ِ at the end because it comes after بِ. This word is the object of يُمَسِّكُونَ (they hold fast to).
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in middle
Added hamza prefix to create causative meaning
Added conjunction to connect with previous phrase about those who hold fast to the Book

steps.m: and they established (referring to those who hold fast to the Book)
steps.n: Added plural marker to show this refers to the same group mentioned before (those who hold fast to the Book)
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowel marks
Changed from a verb (to pray) into a noun meaning one prayer, by adding ة at the end and changing و to ا
Added a fatha mark to show this word is in the accusative case
Added 'the' (ال) to make it refer to the formal Islamic prayer specifically
Added ending mark to show this is the object of the verb أَقَامُوا۟ (they established)
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to نُضِيعُ (we let go to waste) in the next part of the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. Here it negates نُضِيعُ (we let go to waste), making it mean 'we do not let go to waste'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to present tense 'we' form. This word is negated by لا before it, meaning 'we do not waste/lose'

steps.m: to pay back or give what is due

steps.n: Changed the action into a thing by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the actual reward instead of the act of rewarding.
Added fatha at the end to show this is what's being acted upon. In this ayah, Allah is saying He won't waste/lose (نُضِيعُ) this reward.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end because it's the object of the verb نُضِيعُ (we do not let go to waste)
This is a time word that connects to a past event. Here it introduces when Allah raised the mountain.

steps.m: and when (connecting to نَتَقْنَا - We raised)
steps.n: The و connects this event to previous events being mentioned
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: We pulled/uprooted forcefully (referring to Allah lifting the mountain)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show that Allah (We) did the action. The sukun (ْ) is added before نَا according to Arabic grammar rules.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together to form the basic word for mountain

steps.m: the mountain (that was raised above them)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the mountain') and َ at the end because it's being acted upon in the sentence - this is the mountain that was raised above the people
Basic meaning indicates spatial position above something
Adding vowel marks to make it a proper noun meaning 'above'

steps.m: above them (referring to the mountain being above the Children of Israel)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to the mountain being raised above the Children of Israel.
Starting with the basic comparison particle كَ
Adding أَنَّ to كَ creates a stronger comparison particle that means 'as if'

steps.m: as if it (referring to الْجَبَلَ - the mountain)
steps.n: Adding هُۥ shows what is being compared. Here it refers back to the mountain (الْجَبَلَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to provide shade or cover from above
steps.n: Basic meaning relates to providing shade or cover
Root ظ-ل-ل became ظُلّ by adding damma (ُ) and combining the two ل letters into one doubled letter with shaddah (ّ) to show a lot of shade

steps.m: a canopy/covering (that was raised above them)
steps.n: Added ة at the end to make it into a specific instance of shade - in this case, the mountain that was raised above them like a canopy
Added ٌ to show this is any canopy, not a specific one
Basic meaning relates to having a thought or belief
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to make the past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and they thought (about the mountain above them)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this action with what happened before. This shows their reaction to seeing the mountain raised above them.
This is a particle that introduces a new clause

steps.m: that it (referring to the mountain, object of they thought)
steps.n: Adds هُۥ at the end to show we're talking about the mountain. This connects to the verb ظَنُّوٓا۟ (they thought) to show what they thought about.
Basic meaning relates to something falling or occurring
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes verb to active participle (shows who/what is doing the action). This describes the mountain that they thought was falling on them.

steps.m: falling (describing the mountain, in a general way)
steps.n: Adds tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite. This word describes what they thought about the mountain - that it was falling on them.
Basic preposition showing how something is done or connected to something else

steps.m: with them (referring to the mountain being above them)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ to show this applies to a group of people (them). In this ayah, it refers to the mountain being above them.
Basic meaning relates to taking or grasping something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he took' to 'take!' by making it a command - dropped first letter and changed inside vowels
Added وا to make the command for a group instead of one person

steps.m: what/that which (connects to فِيهِ - in it)
steps.n: This is a basic word that means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it connects to فِيهِ (in it) to mean 'what is in it' or 'that which is in it'.

steps.m: what/that which (connects to فِيهِ - in it)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning.
Basic meaning relates to coming or bringing
Changed to Form IV pattern which makes the meaning more active - from 'come' to 'give'
Changed final ى to يْ to prepare for adding suffixes
Added نٰ to show 'we' are doing the action
Added كُم to show the action is done to 'you all'
Basic meaning relates to strength and power
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning strength
Added ة at the end to make it into a specific instance of strength
Added tanween kasra to show it's indefinite and follows a preposition
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something should be done. This connects to the command خُذُوا۟ (take/hold) in the ayah, showing how they should take it - with strength.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Command form (to one person) with helper alif at start
The وا shows we're talking to multiple people (like 'you all' in English)
Added و at beginning to connect this command with the previous one in the verse
This is a relative pronoun connecting to ءَاتَيْنٰكُم (we gave you) in this ayah. It shows the object being given.
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'. This is one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show 'it'. This pronoun refers back to ما (what) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: perhaps/maybe (expressing hope that تَتَّقُونَ will happen)
steps.n: Basic particle expressing hope or expectation. In this ayah, it connects to the next word تَتَّقُونَ (you become mindful), showing hope that the people will become mindful.

steps.m: so that you all (might become mindful - تَتَّقُونَ)
steps.n: Adds 'you all' (كُمْ) to the end of 'perhaps' (لَعَلَّ)

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm
steps.n: Basic meaning relates to protection and guarding
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added تَتّ at beginning to show 'you' doing something to yourself. The و changes to ي at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. Changed the last vowel to ُ before adding ون.
This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. Here it connects to the next word أَخَذَ (took) to show when that action happened.
The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the Quran, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: he took (subject of a past action)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This is the main verb describing what Allah did.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The middle letter ب is doubled to show strong/intensive nurturing
The word changed from رَبّ to رَبُّ by adding a ُ (damma) because it's doing the action - it's the one who 'took'
Added كَ to show possession. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb أَخَذَ (took) in this ayah.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or originates. In this ayah, it connects 'their offspring' (ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ) to 'children of Adam' (بَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ), showing the offspring came from them.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he built' verb to special plural noun 'children/descendants' - often used in 'children of Adam'
Changed the final ي to ى with elongation mark (ٓ) for proper Quranic spelling
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic verb

steps.m: from (showing that the children came from the backs of Adam's descendants)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects بَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ (children of Adam) with ظُهُورِهِمْ (their backs), showing where Allah took them from.

steps.m: to appear on the surface, emerge, become visible

steps.n: Root means 'appear/emerge' - changed to mean the back since it's the visible outer part of body
Changes the word to show multiple backs
Adds kasra to show this word follows مِن (from) in the ayah

steps.m: their backs (possessed by children of Adam)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ to show these are the backs of the children of Adam mentioned earlier in the ayah
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding ة makes it refer to a single line of descendants
Changes the ending to show it's the object of a verb
Adds the possessive pronoun 'their' to show who the descendants belong to
The هم was already there - only a sukoon mark was added at the end. The word means 'their offspring/descendants' in both cases.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes someone else do the action. Here it shows Allah making others witness.
Added و meaning 'and'
Added هُمْ meaning 'them' to show who was made to witness. This refers back to the children of Adam mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something being on top of, upon, or over something else. In this ayah, it connects with أَنفُسِهِمْ (themselves) to show what was being witnessed upon.
Added an alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show elongation in pronunciation. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun meaning one self or soul
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding أ at start and changing vowels to make it mean 'many souls'

steps.m: their selves (meaning 'themselves' in this ayah where Allah made them witness against themselves)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. This word relates to أَشْهَدَ (made witness) in the ayah, showing Allah made them witness against themselves.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed from 'he is not' to 'I am not' by removing ي and adding تُ at the end

steps.m: Am I not? (asking بِرَبِّكُمْ - your Lord)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question. In this ayah, Allah is asking humans if He is not their Lord.

steps.m: to nurture, raise, care for completely
lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does this action completely - the ultimate nurturer and caretaker
Added كُمْ which shows this refers to all of you being addressed, not just one person

steps.m: by your (all of you) lord (as an oath)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being used as an oath, like saying 'I swear by your Lord'
Added fatha (َ) after each letter to show this happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes between two fathas - this is a common change in Arabic words.

steps.m: they said (responding to أَلَسْتُ/Am I not)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together. The ُ before وا helps connect the sounds smoothly. This word is answering the question أَلَسْتُ (Am I not) in this verse.

steps.m: yes indeed! (responding to أَلَسْتُ - 'Am I not?')
steps.n: This is a special word used to strongly agree with a negative question. In this ayah, it's answering Allah's question 'Am I not your Lord?' (أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ) with strong confirmation.

steps.m: to witness or testify about what one has personally seen
he witnessed (in response to أَلَسْتُ - 'Am I not?')
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form. This is part of humanity's response to Allah's question.
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for the suffix
Added نَآ at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The alif at the end is lengthened to show emphasis in this important testimony.

steps.m: that (introduces what will be said in يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what people might say on the Day of Judgment. It works like quotation marks in English, showing the beginning of what someone says.
Added vowels to connect letters and show past tense. The middle و changes to ا in this form.
Changed to present tense with تَ prefix to show 'you' as the doer. The middle ا returns to و in present tense.

steps.m: you (all) say (so that you don't say)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are being addressed. In this ayah, it comes after أَن which makes it mean 'so that you don't say' in context.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun 'day'. The middle letter و keeps its original form.
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being used as a time expression meaning 'on' or 'during'. It connects to القِيٰمَةِ (the Resurrection) to form the phrase 'on the Day of Resurrection'.
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding 'aa' sound in middle
Changes verb 'stood' into noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Added feminine ending to specify the particular meaning of resurrection
Added definite article to specify this is THE resurrection
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added ا at the end which means 'we'. This word connects to كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah, showing who was being talked about.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Changed from past tense 'was' to command form 'be!' by changing vowels and removing alif
Adding shadda (doubling middle letter) makes the command stronger and more emphatic
Adding ا at the end changes it from a command to past tense 'we'
This is a preposition that shows what something is about or away from. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَقُولُوا (you say) to show what they would be unaware about.
Basic pointing word that shows something is near
Added ذا to make it specifically mean 'this' for something near you that you can point to
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to be unaware or neglect something
one who is unaware (describes كُنَّا - we were)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show who does the action, like changing 'neglect' to 'neglector' in English. This describes the subject of كُنَّا (we were).
Added ين at the end to show this describes many people. The ِ before ين shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence.
The َ at the end shows this word is being acted upon directly in the sentence
This is a basic connecting word (particle) that gives choices between options. It doesn't come from a root.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it - َ (fatha) on أ and ْ (sukun) on و. This word connects to تَقُولُوٓا۟ (you say) to show different options of what might be said.
Added fathas and changed و to ا due to it being a hollow verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The و returns in present tense.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people. The alif after و (ٓا) is added to show length, and the small و (۟) shows it's plural. This verb comes after أَوْ (or) which makes it subjunctive.
Added kasra under إ and shadda on ن to make the emphatic particle that means 'indeed'
Added ما to make it mean 'only/just' - like putting a limit on what comes after
Added the elongated alif (آ) at the end to show a pause in reading. This word introduces what comes next in the ayah (أَشْرَكَ - 'associated partners')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made something a partner (with Allah)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'caused partnership' or 'made something a partner'. In Islamic context, this specifically refers to associating partners with Allah (shirk).
The singular word for 'father' becomes plural by stretching the first 'a' sound and adding a long 'a' at the end
Added hamza at the end to complete the broken plural pattern
Changed ending to show this word is doing the action (like saying 'the fathers did' instead of just 'fathers')
Added نَا at the end to show these are our fathers. This word is the subject doing the action of أَشْرَكَ (associated/partnered with others).
This is a basic preposition that shows origin, source or relation
Added kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, مِن connects with قَبْلُ to mean 'from before' or 'previously'.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: The root letters got vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make a word meaning 'before' in time
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
Changed root letters into a short command meaning 'be!' by adding damma and sukun marks
Added نَّا to show 'we' did this action in the past
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added damma and shadda to create a basic noun form
Added ي at the end to make it an adjective meaning 'related to offspring/descendants'
Added ة to create a noun for the group that results from spreading/multiplying
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object of كُنَّا (we were) in the ayah
This is a primary particle that shows where something starts or comes from
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (ذُرِّيَّةً)
Added a small noon at the end to show clear pronunciation before the next word

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Root letters ب-ع-د were joined together and given vowels (َ ْ) to make the common word 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: after them (connected to ذُرِّيَّةً/offspring in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'after them'. In this ayah, 'them' refers back to the offspring mentioned earlier (ذُرِّيَّةً)

steps.m: to destroy or cause to perish

steps.n: Basic vowels added to make 'he' past tense form of 'destroy'
Changes from past tense 'destroyed' to present tense 'destroy(s)'
Added نَا at the end to show who is being destroyed (us)
Added أَ at beginning to make it a question
Added فَ to show this question follows from previous statement
Starting with the separate particles that combine to form this word

steps.m: with/by what (referring to what فَعَلَ the wrongdoers did)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show how the particles connect. This word asks about what the wrongdoers (ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ) did (فَعَلَ).

steps.m: he did (subject of what ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ did)
steps.n: Added fathas to show this is something that happened in the past. This word shows what ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ (the wrongdoers) did.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added ون to show multiple people doing this action

steps.m: the ones who make things false
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at end to show its role in the sentence
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that'
The لِ is added to give more emphasis to the pointing word
The كَ is added to show you're addressing someone when pointing to something
The كَ is added at the beginning to mean 'like' or 'similar to'
The و is added to connect this word with what came before. This word is connected to the verb نُفَصِّلُ (we explain in detail) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The shadda makes the meaning more intensive - not just to separate, but to explain thoroughly

steps.m: we explain in detail (the subject of نُفَصِّلُ الْءَايٰتِ - we explain the signs)
steps.n: Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word connects to الْءَايٰتِ (the signs) which comes after it.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Changed root into a noun meaning one clear sign
Changed from singular to plural form
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word is being described by another word
This is a basic particle that expresses hope or expectation that something will happen

steps.m: perhaps they (expressing hope they will يَرْجِعُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this hope applies to 'them'. This connects to the next word يَرْجِعُونَ (return) - expressing hope that they will return.

steps.m: and perhaps they (expressing hope they will يَرْجِعُونَ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at the start and changing the middle vowel
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to وَلَعَلَّهُمْ (and perhaps they) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed from past tense 'he recited' to a command 'recite!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or about something else. Here it connects to the word نَبَأَ (news/story) to show we're talking about the story of someone.
Added connecting letter yaa (يْ) to prepare for attaching the pronoun
Added هِمْ which means 'them' (referring to multiple people)
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
The word changes from a verb (told) to a noun (news) and gets a fatha mark to show it's the thing being recited
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English
Added ال at the beginning which merges with the word and gets a shadda. This makes it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'
Added a long vowel mark to show this word connects to the next word ءَاتَيْنٰهُ (We gave him). Together they mean 'the one whom We gave'
Changed first letter to hamza with madd (ءَا) and added alif at end (ى) to show past tense. The meaning shifts from 'come/bring' to 'give' in this form.
Added نا at the end to change from 'he gave' to 'we gave'
Added هُ to show who received the action (him)

steps.m: to show signs or become clear
steps.n: This root relates to signs, evidence, and things becoming clear
Adding ة turns the root into a noun meaning 'sign'
Adding ات makes the word plural, showing there are multiple signs
The alif at the start is written with hamza (ء) and has a fatHa. The middle alif has a small alif (ٰ) above it to show it's long.

steps.m: our signs (object of ءَاتَيْنٰهُ/we gave him)
steps.n: Adding نَا shows these signs belong to us (Allah). This word is what was given in the phrase 'We gave him Our signs.'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
The ان prefix shows the action is done to oneself - like saying 'he removed himself' instead of just 'he removed'

steps.m: so he removed himself (from مِنْهَا - from it)
steps.n: The فَ shows this action happened as a result of something before it. The word connects to مِنْهَا (from it) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Added sukun to show where the word pauses before connecting to the next part

steps.m: from it/her (referring to the signs/verses mentioned as ءَايٰتِنَا)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what is being moved away from. In this ayah, it refers back to the signs/verses (ءَايٰتِنَا) that were mentioned earlier.
Basic meaning of following or pursuing
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at start makes it causative - now means making someone follow rather than following directly

steps.m: he made him follow (الشيطان made him follow)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'him' as object of the verb. The 'him' refers to the person mentioned earlier who was given Allah's signs but turned away from them.

steps.m: so he made him follow (الشيطان made him follow)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action happened as a result of what came before - the person turning away from Allah's signs led to Satan making him follow

steps.m: to be distant from good or rebellious
steps.n: Root relates to distance and rebellion
Added ي after first letter and ا after second letter to show this describes someone who does this action very strongly
Added ٱل at start to make it specific, doubled the ش with shadda, lengthened the ا to ٰ, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action in فَأَتْبَعَهُ (then followed him)
Basic meaning of existence or being
Past tense form of 'to be' - the و in the middle becomes ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Added فَ to show this follows from the previous action in the verse where 'the devil followed him'
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْغَاوِينَ (those who went astray)

steps.m: to stray from the right path
steps.n: Basic meaning of deviation or straying
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he strayed' in the past
Changed to active participle form showing someone who does the action
Changed to plural form to show multiple people
Added definite article ال and final vowel َ because it comes after مِنَ (from/of)

steps.m: if/even if (connects to شِئْنَا - we willed)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This word introduces a conditional statement about what could have happened.

steps.m: And even if (connects to شِئْنَا - we willed)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This shows this is part of a continuing discussion.
Added fatha vowels and the weak letter alif to make the past tense form
Changed the vowel to kasra to start making it 'we' form
Added sukun to show there's no vowel here
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb
This is a past tense verb with 'we' as the subject. The 'we' is built into the verb form itself.
Added لَ to show this is part of a conditional statement (if we willed, surely we would lift...)

steps.m: surely we would lift him up
steps.n: Added هُ (him) at the end to show who was being lifted up. Like changing 'we lifted' to 'we lifted him'
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or used

steps.m: with her/it (referring to لَرَفَعْنٰهُ - We would have raised him)
steps.n: Added هَا (meaning 'it/her') to بِ (meaning 'with') to show what is being done with something. Like saying 'with it' instead of just 'with'
This is the base word that means 'but' or 'however'. It's used to show contrast with what came before.
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting idea with the previous statement
Added هُ at the end to show who this statement is about. The ۥٓ marks are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together

Added أَ at the beginning and a sukun (ْ) on the first root letter to show this is making someone do the action. In this ayah, it shows someone clinging to worldly things (the earth) instead of higher spiritual matters.
This is a basic preposition showing direction. In this ayah, it connects the verb أَخْلَدَ (he inclined) with ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth), showing he inclined towards the earth.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added 'al' to make it specific and kasra at the end because it follows the word إِلَى (to/towards)

steps.m: to follow or go after something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ا at start and doubled the ت with shadda to show doing something with extra effort or intensity

steps.m: and he followed closely (connected to أَخْلَدَ - he clung)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect with previous action (أَخْلَدَ - he clung). The hamza on the alif becomes wasl hamza after و.

steps.m: to desire strongly or follow one's whims

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun by adding fathas and writing with an alif maqsurah (ى) at the end to mean 'desire'
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to show proper pronunciation
Added هُ at the end to show the desire belongs to him. This word is being acted upon by the verb وَٱتَّبَعَ (and he followed) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to create the noun form meaning 'example'
Added the possessive pronoun هُ (his) as a suffix, with appropriate case ending (ُ) before it. This word is the subject in the comparison being made, showing what something is like.
Added the conjunction فَ (so/then) at the beginning of the word
Changed from basic root 'to compare' into noun form by adding the vowels 'a-a' to make the word for 'example'
Added كَ at beginning to mean 'like', showing comparison to what follows
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together and make it a proper noun

steps.m: the dog (being compared to in مَثَلُ)
steps.n: Added 'al' (the) at the beginning to make it specific, and changed the ending because it's being compared to something

steps.m: if (introduces condition for يَلْهَثْ - he pants)
steps.n: This is a conditional word that means 'if something happens, then something else will happen'. In this ayah, it means 'if you put a load on the dog, it pants'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this happened in the past

steps.m: you carry (in condition with يَلْهَثْ - pants)
steps.n: Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now, and added sukun at end because it's in a conditional statement with يَلْهَثْ (pants)
This is a basic preposition showing location or direction. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.

Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'. The ي with sukun (يْ) is added between the word and the pronoun to make it easier to pronounce. This word connects to يَلْهَثْ (pants) in the ayah, showing who is doing the panting.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he panted) to present tense (he pants) by adding يَ at the start
Changed the ending to sukun to show this is part of a conditional statement - 'if you burden him, he pants'
Basic connecting word that gives a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects two conditions about the dog: 'if you attack it' or 'if you leave it'.
Added the vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و. This word connects two similar descriptions of the dog's panting.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it past tense
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as subject, changed vowels to show present tense, added sukun at end for jussive mood (because it's in a conditional sentence)
Added هُ at the end to show 'him/it' as the object being left. This is part of a conditional sentence with يَلْهَثْ (he pants/pants heavily).
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he pants (in response to إِن تَحْمِلْ - if you burden)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense and changed vowels. This is part of a conditional statement - 'if you burden him, he pants'
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to things, like saying 'this' in English.
Adding ل makes the pointing stronger, like saying 'this right here'
Adding ك changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away
Added vowel marks and emphasis (shadda) to help with pronunciation
Added vowel marks to transform the root into a noun meaning 'example' or 'likeness'
Added the ُ ending to show this word is the subject being compared in the phrase 'like the example of'
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'people' or 'group' from the concept of standing together
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific group of people

steps.m: of the people (who denied Our signs)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word belongs to or follows another word. Here it's describing the people mentioned in the parable who denied Allah's signs.
The base word ذا (this) changes to ذي to create a word that connects descriptions to things or people
Adding ال makes it more specific, like adding 'the' in English

steps.m: those who (describes كَذَّبُوا۟ - they who denied)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it refer to many people. This word connects to كَذَّبُوا۟ (they who denied) in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: those who (describes كَذَّبُوا۟ - they who denied)
steps.n: Added proper pronunciation marks and changed ال to ٱلّ with shadda for proper Quranic spelling
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Doubling the middle letter ذ makes the meaning stronger, showing emphatic denial
Changed from singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding وا. This word relates to بِـَٔايٰتِنَا (our signs) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
This is the plural form showing multiple signs or miracles. The word is in accusative case because it is the object (the thing being denied/rejected) in the phrase 'those who denied Our signs.'
Added the preposition بِ to show this is done 'with/by' something
Added نَا meaning 'our' to show these are Allah's signs. This word is the object of كَذَّبُوا (they denied/rejected)

steps.m: to tell a story, to trace

steps.n: Command form made by adding 'u' sound at start and changing middle/end vowels - tells someone to narrate/tell
Added فَ to connect it to previous statement, and kasra at end to connect it to next word (ٱلْقَصَصَ)

steps.m: to tell a story by following its tracks

steps.n: The fathas show this is a noun meaning 'the act of telling stories'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - 'the story' instead of just any story
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the command 'narrate' (فَٱقْصُصِ). Also, the alif at the start is written as ٱ when it has a hamza wasl.
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation that something will happen
Adds هُمْ at the end to show this hope/expectation is about 'them'. This connects to يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (they reflect/think) in the next ayah.
Adding the short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the basic past tense verb 'he thought'
Intensifies meaning through doubling of middle root letter

steps.m: to think deeply about oneself/to ponder
steps.n: Adding تَ prefix makes the intensive verb reflexive - doing the deep thinking to/for oneself
Changes from past to present tense with يَ prefix, meaning 'he' is pondering now rather than pondered before
Adding ون at the end makes this plural - showing many people are doing the action together
Added short vowel marks (fat-ha) to make it mean 'it was bad' in the past tense
The alif after س changes it to mean 'became bad' instead of just 'was bad'
The alif is lengthened to show emphasis, changing the meaning from a statement to an exclamation. This word is describing how evil the example (مَثَلًا) is.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun that means 'example'

steps.m: an example (object of سَآءَ - how evil)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show this word is receiving the action - it's the thing being called evil

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Changed from action to noun meaning people who stand together as one group
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific group. This group is being described by سَآءَ (how evil/bad) in this ayah.
The ُ shows this word is doing the action in the sentence
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا and changing it to the relative pronoun form ذي
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Adding the final vowel marks and changing ال to ٱلّ. This word is the subject (doer) of كَذَّبُوا۟ (they denied) in this ayah.

steps.m: to deny or reject something as false

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows that the denial was strong or emphatic
Added وا to show many people did this action. This word is connected to يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong) in the ayah, showing these are the same people doing both actions.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'a sign'
Makes the word plural by adding ات
Adds نَا to show these are our signs (referring to Allah's signs)

steps.m: by/with our signs (object that was denied by كَذَّبُوا)
steps.n: Adds بِ at the beginning to show these signs were the means/object of denial. This word connects to كَذَّبُوا (they denied) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a singular noun meaning 'soul' or 'self'. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong/oppress) in this ayah.
Changes to plural form by adding أ at the start and ُ before the last letter. This is a special plural pattern in Arabic.
Adds هُمْ at the end with َ before it to show these souls/selves belong to them
وَ means 'and' - connects this to what came before
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he was' by adding vowel marks. The middle letter و becomes ا, which is common in Arabic verbs
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did this action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense with يَ prefix
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end, showing multiple people doing the action

This is a special word that introduces a conditional statement. In this ayah, it means 'whoever' and refers to any person that Allah guides. It's connected to the verb يَهْدِ (guides) that comes right after it.

steps.m: to guide or show the right path

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra (ee sound)

steps.m: he guides (after مَن in a condition)
steps.n: The ي at the end is dropped because this verb comes after مَن (whoever) in a conditional sentence
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' as a proper name. The first letter changed to match the ل sound.
Added emphasis on the ل with shadda, changed first letter to ٱ for easier pronunciation, and added ُ to show this is the doer of the action 'guides' in this ayah
Added vowel marks to show this is a complete pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'. In this ayah, it refers back to whoever Allah guides.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. It connects this part to the previous statement about who Allah guides.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show the action happening to oneself
Changed to active participle form to show someone who has this quality
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and changed final ي to ى for writing style. This word is the subject after فَهُوَ (then he is) in this ayah.
This is a conditional word that means 'whoever'. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يُضْلِلْ (leads astray) that comes after it.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this part with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes the verb mean 'to cause someone else to do the action'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he makes (someone) lose the way (in the condition 'whoever Allah...')
steps.n: The sukun at the end shows this is part of a condition (whoever Allah leads astray...)
This is the base demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those' for things that are far away
Added ئِكَ at the end to mean 'those over there' - points to something far away
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This word is the subject of هُمُ (they are) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the separate pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed gender
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of the pronoun. This pronoun is the subject of الْخٰسِرُونَ (the losers) in this ayah.
Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Added alif after first root letter to show who does the action
Added ون to make it plural (more than one loser)

steps.m: the losers (those who are the real/ultimate losers)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the beginning and final vowel mark to show this is talking about specific losers mentioned in the ayah
Added fatha to show how the word is pronounced
Added لَ which adds emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Added قَدْ which shows certainty or completion, making the statement even stronger

steps.m: to create or make something multiply and spread

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he created'
Added نَا to show that 'We' (referring to Allah) did the action. This is connected to لَقَدْ (indeed/certainly) which comes before it.
This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehenna', referring to the place of punishment in the afterlife
Changed ending mark to show Hell is being acted upon

steps.m: for Hell (showing purpose in ذَرَأْنَا/We created)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is the purpose or destination of Allah's creation mentioned in ذَرَأْنَا (We created)
Added ي between the last two root letters and vowels to show this describes a strong quality
The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of being created. In this ayah, Allah is telling us He created many jinn and humans.
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added emphasis (shadda) to show strong connection with what follows
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word الْجِنِّ (the jinn). This shows what group is being talked about.

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight

steps.n: The kasra and sukun pattern creates a noun for a type of being that is hidden from human sight
Shadda intensifies meaning through letter doubling

1. Added ال to make it specific
2. Added kasra at the end because it follows مِنَ (from/of)
Changed the first letter to إِ and added sukun and kasra to make it into a noun meaning humans/mankind
Added وَ (and) at start, ٱلْ (the) before the word, and ِ at end because it comes after مِّنَ (from)
Starting with the basic preposition that shows possession or direction
Added هُمْ to show who the preposition refers to. In this ayah, it connects to قُلُوبٌ (hearts) to show these hearts belong to them.
The root meaning of turning/transforming became associated with the heart, since it can turn emotions
Root letters got vowels to make the common word for heart - like how hearts can turn/change emotions

steps.m: hearts (that لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ بِهَا - do not understand with them)
steps.n: Changed to plural form with tanwin (ٌ) to show these are some hearts. This word connects to the next phrase which says these hearts don't understand.
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the negation. This emphasizes that they absolutely do not understand with their hearts.
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to understand'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
Starting with the basic preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/by it (feminine) (referring to أَعْيُنٌ - eyes)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun ها to show this refers to eyes (أَعْيُنٌ) which is feminine. This word connects back to eyes in the phrase 'eyes with which they do not see'
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation of the preposition
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this idea with the previous one
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is for/to them. In this ayah, it's used to show possession - that they have eyes (أَعْيُنٌ)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun for a single eye
Changes single 'eye' to plural form for small numbers (3-10 eyes)
Basic negative particle that makes actions or statements negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it negates the verb يَفْقَهُونَ (they understand), making it 'they do not understand'.
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on the ل to show strong connection with previous word. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show this is happening now. The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) are part of the present tense pattern.

steps.m: they see/understand (present) (referring to those who don't use their eyes to understand Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who have eyes but don't use them to understand Allah's signs.
Starting with the preposition ب that shows how something is done
Added ها which refers back to the hearts (قُلُوبٌ) that they don't understand with. The feminine form is used because قُلُوبٌ (hearts) is feminine in Arabic.
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this idea with the previous one

steps.m: and for them (connected to أَعْيُنٌ - eyes)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this is for 'them'. This word connects to أَعْيُنٌ (eyes) in the ayah, showing these eyes belong to them.
Changes from verb 'to hear' to the noun 'ear' by adding damma vowels
Changed from single 'ear' to plural 'ears' by adding aa at the start and lengthening the middle sound

steps.m: ears (as the subject of لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb 'they do not hear' in this ayah
Emphatic negative particle formed by adding shadda to لا
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Added kasra to show it's a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by means of'
Added هَا which refers back to the hearts (قُلُوبٌ) that don't understand in this verse
Added alif (آ) to lengthen the final vowel sound for emphasis when stopping at this word
Basic form of the demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something far away
Adding alif shows this refers to multiple things
Adding ئِ shows these things are far away
Adding كَ completes the demonstrative pronoun. In this ayah, it points to the people mentioned before who don't use their hearts, eyes, and ears properly. It's the subject of هُمُ (they are).
Changed from root letters to a simple noun by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ)
Plural form that specifically means grazing animals like cows, sheep and camels
Added the definite article ال and genitive case ending ِ

steps.m: like the cattle/livestock (being compared to those who don't use their senses)
steps.n: Added كَ to show comparison with the people mentioned earlier who don't use their hearts, eyes, and ears properly

steps.m: rather (contrasts with the previous statement about being like cattle)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects ideas by showing contrast. Here it introduces a stronger statement - they aren't just like cattle, but even worse.
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject form of 'they'
Added sukun (ْ) to show the end of the word
This root represents the basic concept of deviating from the right path or being lost
Added vowels and combined the double ل into one letter with shadda
Added أَ at beginning and changed final vowel to ُ to make it comparative, like changing 'lost' to 'more lost'
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people
Adding the damma and alif maksura shows we're pointing to something far away
Adding the hamza with kasra makes it specifically for plural

steps.m: those (far away, plural, being pointed to)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end completes the pointing action, like saying 'over there'
This is the basic form of the pronoun that means 'they' or 'them' when talking about a group of men or a mixed group
The sukun (ْ) changes to a damma (ُ) because this word connects to the next word الْغٰفِلُونَ (the heedless ones). It's like saying 'They are the heedless ones'
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show someone who does this action
Added ال (with hamza-wasl ٱ) at start to make it specific
Added ون with damma to make it plural (many people), and the alif became long (ٰ)
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added ل at beginning to show direction or purpose

steps.m: and to/for Allah (connected to what follows: ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what follows - Allah's beautiful names
Root letters combine to make the common word for 'name'
Makes the word plural - used for multiple names (3 or more)
Makes the word definite

steps.m: the names (subject of belongs to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds damma to show this word is the subject that belongs to Allah in the phrase 'to Allah belong the most beautiful names'
Added damma and sukun to create the basic noun form
The ى ending makes this the feminine form of the superlative
The word became 'the most beautiful' by adding ال (the) at the start

steps.m: to call upon, invoke, pray to

steps.n: Command form made by dropping the last letter and adding a 'u' sound (damma) at the end. Like saying 'Call!' instead of 'to call'
Adds helping alif to start word since Arabic words don't start with sukun
Adds و before هُ to connect the pronoun smoothly, then adds هُ meaning 'him' as the object being called upon

steps.m: so call upon him! (connected to using Allah's beautiful names mentioned before)
steps.n: Adds فَ meaning 'so/then' to connect this command with the previous mention of Allah's beautiful names
This is a preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with her/it (referring to الْأَسْمَاءُ - the names)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'the names' mentioned earlier in the verse. This connects this word to what was mentioned before.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is in the nominative case
Changed to command form by adding وا at the end to tell many people to leave something
Adding و with fatha to connect this command with the previous command فَٱدْعُوهُ (so call upon Him)
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'
Added final vowel marks and changed spelling slightly. This word is the subject of يُلْحِدُونَ (they deviate/corrupt) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they turn away/deviate (referring to those who)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) from the previous part of the ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or being within something. Used here to show being 'in/within His names'
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show it's a preposition
Added elongation mark (ٓ) over ى to show extended pronunciation
Root letters س-م-و changed to make the common noun 'name' (اِسْم)
Changes from singular 'name' to plural by adding letters to make 'names'
Changes to genitive case because it comes after في (in/concerning)
Adds the possessive pronoun meaning 'his'

steps.m: to give what is due (reward or punishment)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

steps.m: he is given what is due
steps.n: Added يُ at start to make it present tense, and changed vowels to make it passive (something being done to someone instead of someone doing it)

steps.m: they are given what is due
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people receiving the action

steps.m: they will be given what is due
steps.n: Added سَ to show this will happen in the future

steps.m: what (refers to يَعْمَلُونَ - they do/used to do)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb after it. Here it refers to the actions that are mentioned in يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/used to do).

steps.m: what (refers to يَعْمَلُونَ - they do/used to do)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of verb with a weak middle letter
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in the next ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
Starting with the base relative pronoun مَن which refers to people
Added مِن (from) at the beginning. The ن of مِن combines with the م of مَن, shown by the ّ mark
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Basic root letters خ-ل-ق became a complete past tense verb خَلَقَ by adding the short 'a' vowels (fatha marks)
Changed the last vowel to a sukun (stop mark)
Added نا with long alif to show 'we' did the creating
Added damma and doubled the م to show the basic noun form meaning source or origin
Adding ة changes it from 'source/origin' to mean a group or community of people
The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is 'a' community - like saying 'a nation' instead of 'the nation'
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last letter gets a kasra (ِ).
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing the guiding. This word is connected to يَعْدِلُونَ (they act justly) in the ayah, showing the same group of people doing both actions.
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to make it stronger
Added ٱلْ at the start to mean 'the'
Added بِ at the start to mean 'with'

steps.m: with the truth (guides يَهْدُونَ with truth)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the word بِ. This word explains how the guiding (يَهْدُونَ) is done - with truth.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with'
Added هِ at the end to show what/who is being referred to
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. The small ۦ at the end is just a pronunciation mark that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adds يَ at the start to make it 'he' in present tense instead of past tense
The ون shows that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
This is a special word that helps point to specific people or things. We start with its basic form.
Added 'al' (the) at the beginning to make it more specific - like pointing to someone
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about many people, not just one
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with what came before
The doubled middle letter shows they did this many times. This word is connected to سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم (We will lead them gradually) in the ayah, showing what they did to deserve this.
Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they' (a group of people) in the past tense. This shows it was multiple people who denied Allah's signs.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Root letters changed to make a noun meaning 'sign'. The two ي letters combined with ا to make the special آ sound at the start.
Made the word plural to show multiple signs or miracles

steps.m: our signs or miracles (object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: Added 'our' to show these are Allah's signs. This is what is being denied in the phrase 'they denied our signs'

steps.m: with/by our signs (object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how they denied - specifically with or by means of the signs

steps.m: to move step by step or gradually

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show purposeful, gradual leading or drawing someone in
Changed to present tense with 'we' as the subject
Added سَ at beginning to show future tense
Added هُم at end to show who is being led
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The م gets doubled (with a shadda) because it connects smoothly with the word before it when reading
This word is used to talk about a place or location

Added vowel marks to create the word meaning 'from where'. In this ayah, it's connected to لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (they do not know), describing how they don't know where the gradual punishment is coming from.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) into 'they do not know'. It's one of the most common words in Arabic for saying 'no' or 'not'.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to show this is happening now or generally

steps.m: they know/understand deeply (referring to those who deny Our signs)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knows' to 'they know' by adding ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

Added أُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'cause to have time'

steps.m: and I give time (to لَهُمْ - them)
steps.n: Added conjunction و to connect to previous statement. This verb connects to لَهُمْ (to them) in the following words.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition

steps.m: to/for them (connected to أُمْلِى - I give respite)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' (referring to multiple people). This word connects to أُمْلِى (I give respite) to show who is being given respite.
This is the basic form of the word that shows a condition might happen
Adding the shadda changes this from 'if' to a word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. Here it emphasizes that Allah's plan (كَيْدِى) is truly firm/strong (مَتِينٌ).

steps.m: to plan secretly, to plot against someone

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'plot/scheme'. The word is being used after إِنَّ (indeed) in this ayah.
Added ى at the end to show the plot belongs to the speaker (Allah in this case)
Added vowels and ي to make it into a describing word showing a strong quality
Added tanween to show this is describing something in a general way
This particle turns the statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English. It's asking about the verb يَتَفَكَّرُوا (they reflect/think)
Added و with fatha to connect this question with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added لَ to add emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: and have they not? (connected to يَتَفَكَّرُوا - reflected/thought)
steps.n: Added مْ with sukun to make the question negative, like adding 'not' in English. The whole phrase is asking 'Have they not reflected?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show deep personal reflection
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Changed to plural form to show multiple people reflecting

steps.m: to accompany or be close to someone

steps.n: Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show someone who accompanies others
Added kasra at the end because something will be added to show who owns this companion
Added هِم at the end to show the companion belongs to them (like saying 'their' in English)
Added بِ at the start to show this companion is being used with something (like saying 'with' in English)
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of

steps.m: from/of (showing where something comes from, connected to جِنَّةٍ)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م in speech, causing the sounds to blend together. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning. Here it connects to جِنَّةٍ (madness/insanity) to mean 'from madness'.

steps.m: to cover or conceal from sight

steps.n: The shadda makes the meaning stronger and more specific
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific jinn, or can mean madness/possession

steps.m: any madness (in مَا بِصَاحِبِهِم مِّن جِنَّةٍ - there is no madness in their companion)

steps.m: if/whether (connecting to هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a possibility or condition. Here it connects to the following phrase 'he is but a clear warner', making the meaning 'if/whether he is but a clear warner'
This is a pronoun that refers to Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah. It connects to نَذِيرٌ (warner) to say 'he is only a clear warner'
This is the base form of the exception particle. It shows that what comes after it is different from what came before.

The shadda and alif are added to strengthen the meaning and make it flow better. In this ayah, it creates a contrast with مَا that came before, emphasizing that the Prophet ﷺ is nothing but a clear warner.

steps.m: to warn or alert others about danger
someone who warns others (describing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ي between ذ and ر and vowel marks to show this describes someone who warns others. In this ayah, it's specifically talking about Prophet Muhammad ﷺ being a warner.
The tanween (ٌ) at the end shows this is 'a' warner rather than 'the' warner. This word tells us what هُوَ (he) is in the phrase 'he is but a warner'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'the one who' and changed inside vowels to show it's describing something that makes things clear
Added two damma marks at the end to show it's indefinite (not specific)
The م has a shaddah (doubled letter) to make it stronger, and the tanween ending shows it describes نَذِيرٌ (warner) in the ayah
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding 'is it that...?' at the start of an English sentence
Adds 'and' to connect this question with what came before it

steps.m: and have they not...? (connects to يَنظُرُوا۟)
steps.n: Adds لَمْ which shows that something hasn't happened yet. This connects to the next word يَنظُرُوا۟ (they look), making the full meaning 'and have they not looked?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows it's happening in present/future
Adding وا makes it plural - shows many people are looking. This verb follows أَوَلَمْ (and did they not) in the ayah, making it a subjunctive verb.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects to the word مَلَكُوتِ (dominion) to show location within it.
The ي at the end changes to ى when writing. This doesn't change the meaning, it's just a writing style.

steps.m: to rule or have authority over something

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he ruled'
Added وت to create a special form showing complete or total dominion

steps.m: complete dominion/supreme authority (of السَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'the heavens' that comes after it, showing it means 'dominion of the heavens'

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added ء at the end to create a noun meaning the place above us
Added وات to make it plural, showing there are multiple heavens
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific
Added ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in/within). The alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make them pronounceable
Added 'the' at the beginning and a kasra at the end to show it's connected to something else in the sentence
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ) mentioned before it in the ayah
This is a question word asking about what Allah created. It connects to the verb خَلَقَ (created) that comes after it in the ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the ayah. This connects the question about creation to the previous mention of looking at the heavens and earth.

steps.m: to create or shape something from nothing
he created (subject of what اللَّهُ created)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word shows what Allah did - He created something.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, and the doubled ل shows the specialness of this name. The ُ shows this is the doer of the action in the sentence.
Basic preposition that connects words by showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects 'created' (خَلَقَ) with 'thing' (شَىْءٍ) to mean 'created of/from things'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form. The ي becomes ى when it's between two vowels.
The two dots under (ٍ) show this word follows the word مِن (from). When a word comes after مِن, it gets these two dots under it.

steps.m: that (introduces what they did not look at)
steps.n: Basic particle that introduces what comes after it. Here it connects to يَنظُرُوا (they looked) to show what they didn't look at.

steps.m: and that (connects to previous things they did not look at)
steps.n: Added و to connect this to the previous things mentioned that they didn't look at (the dominion of the heavens and earth)
Adding vowel marks to make it readable
The word gets a small alif (ٰ) and a long mark (ٓ) on top to show it means 'hopefully' or 'maybe' something will happen soon
This is a particle that connects two ideas. Here it introduces the possibility that 'their appointed time might be near.' It works with عَسَىٰٓ (might be) to express this possibility.
Added damma and sukun to make the basic noun form
Added يَ at the start to make it 'he' in present tense, and ُ at the end to show it's complete
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to show it's in a special grammar position
Basic form of the emphasis particle that shows something definitely happened or is definitely true
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it connects to the next word اقْتَرَبَ (has approached). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added ٱ at start and ت in middle to show doing something to/for oneself (like getting closer by one's own effort)

steps.m: to set a time limit or deadline

steps.n: Adding the basic vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb

steps.m: appointed time/deadline (subject of اقْتَرَبَ - has drawn near)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb اقْتَرَبَ (has drawn near)

steps.m: their appointed time (subject of اقْتَرَبَ - has drawn near)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'their' when referring to a group of people
Adding the basic vowel mark to help pronounce the word
Added kasra to show this word is being acted upon by the preposition before it
Added بِ at beginning to show this is asking about how or by what means
Added فَ to connect this question to what came before, showing consequence. This word connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) later in the ayah.
