Added kasra tanween at the end to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English) and is being described by the word يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic preposition meaning 'after'

steps.m: after it (refers to أَجَلُهُمْ/their appointed time)
steps.n: Added هُۥ (him/it) at the end to show what comes after. Here it refers back to أَجَلُهُمْ (their appointed time) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure with

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Changed from basic 'trust' to religious 'belief' by adding long alif at start - like changing from 'I trust you' to 'I have faith'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: whoever (subject of يُضْلِلِ - Allah leads astray)

Changed to Form IV pattern which shows making someone do something
Added a small i-sound (kasra) at the end because this word connects to Allah's name after it

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show it's the subject
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. In this ayah, it negates the word هَادِىَ meaning there is no guide.

steps.m: to guide or show the right path

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels. The final ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word.
Changed to the pattern that shows who does the action. The alif is added after the first letter, and kasra is added under the second letter to show this is someone who does the guiding.
Added fatha at the end because this word comes after لَا (no) in the sentence. The ي is written as ى when it has a vowel mark.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for him (object of يُضْلِلِ - leads astray)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ meaning 'him'. This shows who is being led astray in the phrase 'whoever Allah leads astray'

steps.m: for him (object of يُضْلِلِ - leads astray)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to leave something alone or abandon it

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Adds و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and he leaves them (object of يَذَرُ)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ to show who is being left/abandoned
Basic particle showing location or state. In this ayah, it shows they are deep within their state of transgression.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show the intense state of going beyond limits
Added kasra at the end to show it belongs to/is connected to something

steps.m: their extreme transgression (in which يَعْمَهُونَ - they wander blindly)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this belongs to them. This word connects to يَعْمَهُونَ (they wander blindly) showing how they are lost in their transgression.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and adjusting vowels

steps.m: they are wandering blindly (subject of يَذَرُهُمْ - he leaves them)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word describes what happens to the people mentioned in يَذَرُهُمْ (he leaves them).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this happens in the present/future
Adding ون shows a group of people (at least 3 men or mixed group) are doing the asking

steps.m: they ask you (about ٱلسَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows you are being asked. In this ayah, they are specifically asking about the Hour (the Day of Judgment)
This is a preposition that shows what يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ (they ask you) is about. Here it connects to ٱلسَّاعَةِ (the Hour)
The sukun (ْ) changes to kasra (ِ) because the next word starts with ال. This makes it easier to pronounce.
The root letters form a noun by adding an ا in the middle and ة at the end to mean a unit of time

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific Hour - the Day of Judgment. The س gets a shadda to help pronunciation.
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word عَنِ (about). The alif at the start changes to a waṣla (ٱ) for smoother pronunciation when connected to the previous word.
This is the base interrogative word meaning 'which'

steps.m: when (asking about time) (asking about السَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Adding ان at the end changes this from a general 'which' question word to specifically ask about time. In this ayah, it's asking about when the Final Hour will come.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added مُ at start to show place/time, and changed ending to ى because it's a place noun from a root ending in و
Added alif above ى to show it's a long vowel

steps.m: its appointed time (referring to the Hour)
steps.n: Added هَا to show possession. In this ayah, it refers to the appointed time of the Hour (Day of Judgment) that was mentioned earlier.
Added damma and sukun to make basic command form
The middle weak letter waw is dropped to make it easier to say
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Adding مَا changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, showing that something is limited or exclusive. Here it shows that the knowledge belongs only to Allah.
Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added a u-sound (damma) at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence
Added ها at the end to show this knowledge belongs to her/it (referring to the Hour)
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Adding vowels changes this into a word meaning 'at' or 'with', like in 'the book is with me'
Added vowel marks and combined the doubled ب with shadda to show intensity in nurturing/sustaining
Changed to 'my Lord' by adding the kasra sound at the end
Added ى at the end to show it belongs to me (first person possession)
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. Here it makes 'reveals it' into 'does not reveal it'
This root carries the basic meaning of making something clear or visible
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more intensive
Added يُ at start to show 'he' doing it now (present tense), and added ي in the middle to show extra strength/intensity
Added ها to show the feminine object (referring to الساعة - the Hour) that is being made clear
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning a specific time or moment
Added ها to show the time belongs to something feminine (referring to الساعة - the Hour)
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or direction (referring to when the Hour will occur)
Changed the ending to آ to add emphasis, showing the importance of the time of the Hour

steps.m: except (what comes after بَغْتَةً from لَا تَأْتِيكُمْ)
steps.n: This is a special word that shows an exception. In this ayah, it shows that the Hour will only come suddenly (بَغْتَةً), with no other way of coming.
This is a third person masculine singular pronoun. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah (رَبِّى) who is the only one who reveals the knowledge of the Hour.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is describing something becoming heavy in the past

steps.m: it (feminine) became heavy (describing الساعة - the Hour)
steps.n: Added feminine marker to match with الساعة (the Hour) which is feminine in Arabic
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows that something is heavy/difficult in the heavens.
Forms feminine plural noun 'heavens'
Makes the word plural by adding ات ending
Adds alif to show vastness of the heavens
Added 'the' at the beginning. The س gets doubled (shadda) because it's a sun letter
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in). The alifs are written as long alifs (ٰ) in this style
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun
The word becomes definite with 'al' and takes the genitive case because it follows the preposition 'fi'
Added وَ (and) to connect this word to السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it
This is a negative word that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it changes 'reveals it' to 'does not reveal it'. It's one of the first words Arabic students learn.
This is a basic movement verb with a weak letter ي as the last root letter
Connected the root letters together to form the basic verb
Added ت at beginning to make it present tense, and the ي at end shows it's 'she/it' doing the action
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added كُمْ to show the action affects multiple people
This is a special word that means 'except'. In this ayah, it's used to say that only Allah knows when the Hour will come - no one else.
This root carries the idea of something happening without warning
Added vowels to the root letters to make a noun meaning 'sudden' or 'unexpected'
Added ة to show one specific instance of suddenness

steps.m: suddenly (describes how لَا تَأْتِيكُمْ/it will not come to you)
steps.n: Added -an ending to make it describe HOW something happens (like adding -ly in English to make 'sudden' into 'suddenly')
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Adds present tense prefix for 3rd person masculine singular
Adding ون shows it's plural (more than one person asking)

steps.m: they ask you (about ٱلسَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Adding ك shows you are being asked. In this ayah, they are specifically asking about the Hour (the Day of Judgment)
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added أَنَّ which combines with كَ to create a stronger meaning of comparison

steps.m: as if you (asking about the Hour)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is talking about 'you' (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
This root carries the basic meaning of showing deep care or concern
Added short vowels (fat-ha, kasra, fat-ha) to make it mean 'he cared deeply' in the past
Changed و to ي and added shadda and dammatan to make an intensive adjective. In this ayah, it describes how deeply concerned people are about the Hour (the Day of Judgment).
This is a preposition that shows what something is about

steps.m: about it (referring to الساعة - the Hour)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to الساعة (the Hour) mentioned earlier in the ayah. Since الساعة is feminine, we use the feminine pronoun ها.
Changed the root 'say' into a command by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ (like saying 'Say!')
The و is removed because it's between two vowel marks (ُ and ْ)
This is an emphasis particle that adds certainty to what follows
Adding ما changes the meaning from emphasis to restriction. In this ayah, it restricts the knowledge (عِلْمُهَا) to being only with Allah.
Root meaning 'know' becomes the noun 'knowledge' by adding i-sound at start and making middle letter silent
Added damma to show this word is the subject after إِنَّمَا (indeed/verily)

steps.m: its knowledge (referring to السَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Added ها to show this knowledge belongs to the Hour (السَّاعَةِ) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is the basic form of the word that expresses the concept of being present with or near something
Added vowel marks to show this is being used as a preposition. In this ayah, it shows where knowledge (علمها) is located.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل. This word is possessed by عِندَ (with/at) in this ayah.
This is a particle that shows contrast between ideas. It's like saying 'People think X, but actually Y'.

steps.m: and however (connecting to 'They ask you as if you are well-informed about it')
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this idea with the previous statement. It shows this is part of a longer thought.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it increased/became many'
Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'more/most' instead of just 'many'. Like turning 'many' into 'most of the people'.

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: The hamza at the beginning is dropped because it's not needed for pronunciation
Added ِ to show this word belongs to or follows another word (أَكْثَرَ meaning 'most of')
The shadda adds emphasis to the word. This is a common form for this word in the Quran.

steps.m: of the people (owned by أَكْثَرَ - most)
steps.n: Added ال to show we're talking about specific people. This word is possessed by أَكْثَرَ (most), forming the phrase 'most of the people'
This is a negative word that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it negates the verb يُجَلِّيهَا (reveals it), making it mean 'does not reveal it'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with prefix يَ

steps.m: they know (referring to most people in لٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knows' to 'they know' by adding ون at the end to match with 'most people' mentioned before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Dropping the final vowel. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
This is a basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'. It's used to make something negative, like changing 'I can' to 'I cannot'.
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after another ل in قُل, making the two ل sounds combine into one stronger sound.
The آ (alif with madd) shows that we should stretch the 'aa' sound when reading. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added hamza at start for first person present tense, sukun on first letter, and damma at end to show subject is 'I'
Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'self'
Added لِ at beginning to show direction/purpose, and kasra at end to show it's affected by another word

steps.m: for myself (object of أَمْلِكُ - I possess/control)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show it means 'my'. This word is what's being possessed/controlled in the phrase 'I do not possess/control for myself'
Changed the root letters into a noun pattern that shows what the action produces (like changing 'help' to 'a help'). This word is what's being controlled in the phrase 'I do not control'.
Added ًا at the end to show this is something being controlled or affected by the action. Like saying 'I don't control (any) benefit'.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word negates ضَرًّا (harm) in the ayah.
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show the harm is more intense
Adding tanween alif shows this word is receiving the action of the verb أَمْلِكُ (I possess/control). The speaker is saying they don't control/possess harm.

steps.m: except (what follows is excluded from قُل لَّآ أَمْلِكُ)
steps.n: This is an exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. In this ayah, it excludes مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ (what Allah wills) from the previous statement about not having control over benefit or harm.
This is a relative pronoun that means 'whatever' when followed by a verb. Here it introduces what Allah wills, making the phrase mean 'whatever Allah wills'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
Changed to a hollow verb form by adding long alif between first and second root letters
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'god' in general
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject/doer
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations

steps.m: and even if (connects to أَمْلِكُ - I possess)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This connects to the verb أَمْلِكُ (I possess) from earlier in the ayah.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و drops out and changes the vowels around it - this is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)

steps.m: I was (in لَوْ كُنتُ أَعْلَمُ - if I knew)
steps.n: The suffix تُ shows this is first person (I) in the past tense. This word is part of a conditional statement with لَوْ (if) followed by أَعْلَمُ (I knew)
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed to comparative form (like 'bigger' or 'more' in English) by adding hamza at start and changing vowels

steps.m: more knowing (as the subject of كُنتُ - I was)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) in the sentence. It comes after كُنتُ (I was) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be absent or hidden from sight

steps.n: Changes from describing being hidden to naming a hidden thing. This word is what is being known in the phrase 'if I knew the unseen'.

steps.m: the unseen (what is being known in أَعْلَمُ - I know)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific unseen realm that only Allah knows. The َ at the end shows this is what is being known.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb
Added است to show seeking or wanting more of something. The ْ (sukun) shows where syllables break.

steps.m: I sought abundance (of الْخَيْرِ - good things)
steps.n: The تُ at the end shows that 'I' did the action. This connects to الْخَيْرِ (good things) mentioned later in the ayah.

steps.m: I would have sought abundance (of الْخَيْرِ - good things)
steps.n: The لَ at the start combines with وَلَوْ earlier in the ayah to mean 'and if...then I would have' - showing what would happen in an imaginary situation
This is the basic form of the particle that shows something comes from somewhere else
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْخَيْرِ (the good). This shows what the word 'from' is referring to.
Changes from root letters to a noun meaning 'good'
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the good thing' instead of just 'good thing')
Added ِ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the phrase 'I would have acquired much of the good'

This is a basic particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's being used with مَسَّنِىَ (touched me) to say 'would not have touched me'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This و means 'and' and connects this phrase to the previous statement about knowing the unseen.
Added vowels and doubled the middle س (seen) with shadda to make it mean 'he touched' in the past
Added نِ at the end to show the meaning 'me'
Added ى and َ at the end to show this is being affected by something else in the sentence. In this ayah, it's showing what hasn't touched the speaker.
Changed the action into a thing by adding ُ at the start. Like how 'harm' is the thing that comes from 'to harm'
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter
Added ٱل to point to specific harm, and ُ to show it's doing the action. The ٓ (long vowel mark) shows the vowel is stretched in reading.
This is a negative particle that negates the word أَنَا۠ (I am) that comes after it. In this context, it means 'I am not' rather than its other possible meaning of 'if'.
This is the basic form of the personal pronoun meaning 'I'
Added an 'a' sound at the end to make the complete 'I' word

steps.m: I (in أَنَا۠ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ - I am only a warner)
steps.n: Added a small mark (۠) above the final alif. This is a pronunciation mark that shows this is the end of a word, especially when followed by another word starting with alif.

This is a compound particle formed from combining إن (indeed) + لا (no) that evolved into a single word meaning 'except'. In this ayah, it introduces an exception to the previous statement about not having control over benefit or harm.

steps.m: to warn or alert others of danger
one who warns others (describing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ي between ذ and ر and vowel marks to show this describes someone who warns others. In this ayah, it refers to Prophet Muhammad's ﷺ role as a warner.

steps.m: a warner (describing Prophet Muhammad's ﷺ role)
steps.n: Added ٌ at the end to show this is describing a role or job, like saying "a teacher" instead of "the teacher"

steps.m: bearer of good news (paired with نَذِيرٌ)
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'one who gives good news'. This word is paired with نَذِيرٌ (warner) in this ayah to show the Prophet's dual role.
The ٌ shows this is any bearer of good news, not a specific one

steps.m: and a bearer of good news (connected to نَذِيرٌ)
steps.n: The وَ connects this to نَذِيرٌ (warner) earlier in the ayah, showing these are two roles together

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/gathering together becomes a word for a group of people who gather as one
Adds لِّ (with emphasis) meaning 'for/to'

steps.m: for/to a people (owned by warner and bearer of good news)
steps.n: Adding the tanween (double dots under) makes it 'any people' rather than a specific group, and shows it's connected to 'for'

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza gets a waaw carrier due to the damma.
Added ون to show multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
This is a pronoun that refers to Allah in this ayah. It's the subject (doer) of the verb خَلَقَكُم (created you). Pronouns like this one don't come from roots - they're basic words in Arabic.
This is the basic form of the word that connects a description to what's being described, like saying 'the person who runs' or 'the book that I read'

steps.m: the one who (subject of خَلَقَكُم - created you)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning with a shadda to make it specific. This word points to Allah (هُوَ) who is doing the action of creating (خَلَقَكُم)
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This is Allah doing the creating.

steps.m: He created you all (object of خَلَقَ)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show who was created - in this case it refers to all people being addressed. The ُ (damma) on ك shows this is addressing multiple people.
This is a helper word that shows something comes from somewhere else. In this ayah, it's used to show humans came from one soul.
The م gets a shadda (double mark) to help connect it smoothly to the next word نَّفْسٍ (soul). This is just for easier pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, be a living being

steps.n: Root letters were connected with vowels to make the noun 'soul/self' instead of the verb 'to breathe'
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object being created in the phrase 'created you from a single soul'
Root letters connected with vowels to make a word meaning 'single'
Added alif to show who has this quality
Added ة to make it feminine, since it describes نَفْسٍ (soul) which is feminine
Added kasratain to show it belongs to something, and changed alif to alif with bar for writing style

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Adding fathas (َ) after each letter shows this happened in the past and was done by 'he'

steps.m: and he made (refers to Allah making Eve from Adam)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this action to what came before it in the ayah (Allah creating you from one soul)
Added sukun to show where the word stops. This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'.
Added ها at the end which means 'her/it'. In this ayah, it refers back to نَّفْسٍ (soul) which was mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to pair or join together as mates

steps.n: Root letters joined with vowels to make the noun meaning 'spouse' or 'mate'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلَ (made/created)
Added هَا at the end to show this is her spouse. This word is what Allah made/created (the object of جَعَلَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb

steps.m: so that he may become still/calm/at rest (with her)
steps.n: The لِ here shows purpose - Adam was created with Eve so that he could find rest and tranquility with her
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added ي and sukun (ْ) to prepare for attaching the pronoun

steps.m: to her (referring to the soul/نَّفْسٍ mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها which means 'her' to show who the action is directed towards. This connects to the verb لِيَسْكُنَ (so that he might dwell) showing where the dwelling is directed.
This is a conditional particle that means 'when' or 'after'. The ا at the end makes it work with past tense verbs.

steps.m: and when (connects to تَغَشَّىٰهَا - he covered her)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happens right after something else. Here it connects to what happens next in the story (when he covered her).
The ت at start shows the person is doing the action to themselves
The alif maqsurah (ى with two dots changed to ٰ) shows this verb ends with a weak letter و that changed to ى

steps.m: he covered her (referring to Adam covering Eve)
steps.n: Added هَا which shows the action was done to a feminine object (her). In this ayah, it refers to Adam covering Eve.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: she carried/bore (referring to Eve carrying a child)
steps.n: Changed from 'he carried' to 'she carried' by adding ت (taa) at the end to show a female did the action
Changes verb 'to carry' into the noun 'a carrying/pregnancy'

steps.m: a pregnancy (object of حَمَلَتْ - she became pregnant)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word receives the action of the verb حَمَلَتْ (she became pregnant)
Added short vowels (fatha and kasra) to make it mean 'became light' in the past
Added ي to make it an adjective describing the quality of being light
Added -an ending to make it describe how something happened (it became an adverb meaning 'lightly')
Added fatha on first letter and shadda on second letter to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: and so she passed (after حَمَلَتْ - she conceived)
steps.n: Adds 'fa' meaning 'and so/then' to show what happened next
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or what it's done with

steps.m: with it (referring to حَمْلًا - the pregnancy)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'it' to show what is being carried. The ۦ mark after هِ is just to show proper pronunciation.

steps.m: when (connects to تَغَشَّىٰهَا - he covered her)
steps.n: This is a time particle that introduces a sequence of events. In this ayah, it connects to when the husband covered his wife.

steps.m: then when (connects to تَغَشَّىٰهَا - he covered her)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this event follows from what happened before

steps.m: then when (connects to تَغَشَّىٰهَا - he covered her)
steps.n: Added long alif at the end for emphasis and to show end of phrase
This is the basic concept of heaviness
Shows something becoming heavy over time
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing something to happen. Changed vowels to match this pattern.

steps.m: she became heavy (referring to the pregnancy mentioned in حَمَلَتْ)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show a female subject. In this ayah, it refers back to the woman mentioned earlier who became pregnant (حَمَلَتْ).
Connected the root letters and added fathas. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end after fatha.
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This often happens after certain particles like فَلَمَّا in the ayah.
Adding وا makes it refer to two people (in this case, Adam and Eve)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled. This is a unique word - there is only one Allah.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being called upon in دَّعَوَا)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being called upon in the phrase 'they both called upon Allah'
This root carries the basic meaning of nurturing and caring for something to help it grow and develop
The doubled middle letter (shadda) with fatha vowels creates a noun meaning someone who does this action perfectly
Adding fatha at the end prepares the word to receive the possession suffix
Adding هُمَا shows the lord belongs to two people
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces what the people will say if Allah gives them a righteous child.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely' before 'if'. This shows how serious their promise is to be grateful if Allah gives them a righteous child.
The hamza and alif at the start make this a causative form - changing 'come' to 'give'
Adding ءَا at the start changes 'come' to 'give/bring' (making something come to someone)
Adding sukun (ْ) on the last letter makes it a complete past tense verb
Added ت to show 'you' (male) did this action in the past
Added نَا at the end to show who received the action (us)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from 'he made good' to 'good/righteous one' by adding an alif after first letter and changing middle vowel - this is how we turn a verb into a word describing someone with that quality

steps.m: a righteous [child] (object of ءَاتَيْتَنَا - You give us)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this is describing what they're asking to be given - a righteous child. It's connected to the verb ءَاتَيْتَنَا (You give us) in their prayer.
Added نَ at the beginning to mean 'we', and changed the root letters to make it mean 'we be/become'
Added لَ at start to emphasize 'surely/definitely'
Added shadda on ل for extra emphasis
Added نَّ at the end for maximum emphasis
This is a basic preposition that shows something is part of or coming from a group. Here it connects to الشَّاكِرِينَ (the grateful ones) to show being part of that group.
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الشَّاكِرِينَ (the grateful ones). This is a common change when مِن comes before words starting with ال.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he thanked' to 'one who thanks' by adding an alif after first letter and kasra under second letter - this makes it describe the person doing the action
Adds ين to show multiple thankful people. This word comes after مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah.
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, like changing from 'thankful ones' to 'the thankful ones'
This is the basic temporal particle meaning 'when' that introduces a past event followed by another event
Added فَ at the beginning to show this event follows something before it
The alif at the end is written with a long آ to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning.
The root letters were changed to make 'he gave' - added a hamza with a long aa sound at the start, and changed the ending to make it past tense
Added هُ at the end to show who received the giving - means 'him' or 'it'
Added هُمَا at the end to show the dual recipients (them both)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show who or what has this quality
Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: a righteous one (object of جَعَلَا - they made)
steps.n: The alif in صَالِحًا is written as a long alif (صٰ) but pronounced the same way - it's still 'righteous one' as the object of 'they made'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb

steps.m: they both made (referring to Adam and Eve in this ayah)
steps.n: The ا at the end shows this is about two people (Adam and Eve) doing the action together. This relates to the previous part of the ayah where it mentions آتَىٰهُمَا (He gave them both)
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to شُرَكَآءَ (partners) in the ayah, showing these partners were attributed to Him.

steps.m: to share or join something with something else
someone who shares or joins with others
steps.n: Added vowels and ي to make it mean 'someone who shares'. This pattern is used to describe someone who does the action.
Changes the word to mean multiple partners by using the broken plural pattern

steps.m: partners (as the object of جَعَلَا - they both made)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object (thing being acted upon) in the sentence. It's what 'they both made' (جَعَلَا).
Basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added ما which means 'what' to create the compound meaning 'in what'
The final alif is elongated to alif madd (آ) when connected to help with pronunciation
Changed from basic 'come' to 'gave' by adding ءَا at the start - this is a common way to make words mean 'giving'. The ىٰ shows it's past tense 'he gave'.
Added هُمَا at the end to show the giving was done to two people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added تَ at start and ى at end to make it mean 'He is high above' (showing greatness and perfection)

steps.m: so he is exalted (above what they associate with اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to connect this to the previous statement, showing this is a result or consequence. The alif is written as ٰ when it comes after a tall letter.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a preposition with no root - we start from the word itself
Added the question word ما (what) after the preposition عَنْ
When عَنْ and مَا combine, the ن merges with the م creating a doubled م (shown by the ّ mark). This is a common change in Arabic when these letters come together.

steps.m: to make something a partner/equal with something else

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he became a partner'
Changed to present tense and causative form - making something happen rather than just being in a state
The ون ending shows this is being done by multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who associate partners with Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding ون changes the subject from singular 'he' to plural 'they'

steps.m: do they associate partners (with Allah)? (followed by مَا لَا يَخْلُقُ - that which does not create)
steps.n: The أَ prefix turns this into a question meaning 'do they...?', expressing shock at how they could associate partners with Allah who cannot create
This is a question word used to ask about things or connect parts of sentences
Added the fatha mark to show how to pronounce it. In this ayah, this word is asking about what things they are associating as partners with Allah.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَخْلُقُ (creates/makes) that comes after it. It's a basic word that doesn't come from a root.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added vowels to the root letters to make the common noun meaning 'thing'
Added the accusative case ending to show this is the object being created in the phrase 'does not create a thing'
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being acted upon
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed group). In this ayah, it's the subject of يُخْلَقُونَ (they are created).
Adding و at the start connects this phrase to what came before it. It shows these ideas are related: they associate partners with Allah (أَيُشْرِكُونَ) AND they themselves are created (يُخْلَقُونَ).
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he created' to 'being created' by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to show something is receiving the action
Added ون to show multiple people are being created
This is a negative particle that negates the ability mentioned in يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able/capable)
The و (wa) at the beginning means 'and', connecting this negation to the previous statement. Together with يَسْتَطِيعُونَ it means 'and they are not able'
Past tense verb meaning 'he was able'. Root means 'to be able'
Added letters at beginning to show something developing or becoming possible
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing internal vowels
Added ون to show multiple people. This word connects to يَنصُرُونَ (they help) in the ayah, showing the same group can't help.
Added fatha to show this word connects to the verb يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able), showing who cannot help.
Added هُمْ to show this refers to a group (them). This connects to the previous verb يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able), showing who cannot help.
Changed from basic root meaning 'help' into the noun 'help/support' by adding vowel marks (fatha-sukun pattern)

steps.m: any help/support (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object receiving the action in the sentence. This word is being used with يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able) to say what they cannot do.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb that follows it (يَسْتَطِيعُونَ - they are able)

steps.m: not at all (negates يَسْتَطِيعُونَ - they are able)
steps.n: Adding the elongated alif (آ) makes the negation stronger, like saying 'not at all' instead of just 'not'

steps.m: and not at all (negates يَسْتَطِيعُونَ - they are able)
steps.n: Adding وَ (and) at the beginning connects this phrase to the previous statement
Changes from root meaning to concrete noun meaning
Changes singular 'soul' to plural form by adding hamza at start
Added هُمْ at the end to show possession and added fatha before it since this word is the object of the verb يَنصُرُونَ (they help)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is connected to يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able) in the ayah.
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)
Added vowels to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a special type of word.
The ت at the beginning shows 'you' are doing it, and the vowels change to show it's happening now rather than in the past

steps.m: you call them (referring to the false gods mentioned earlier in the surah)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ at the end shows who is being called. In this ayah, it refers back to the false gods that were mentioned before.
This is a basic preposition showing direction. In this ayah, it connects 'you call them' (تَدْعُوهُمْ) with 'the guidance' (ٱلْهُدَىٰ), showing what they are being called to.
Added damma and fatha to convert the root into a noun meaning 'guidance'
Added alif maqsurah (ى) at the end to make it a feminine noun form

steps.m: the guidance (that is being called to in تَدْعُوهُمْ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific guidance. This word refers back to what is being called to in the phrase تَدْعُوهُمْ (you call them to)
Added small alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's pronounced long. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يَتَّبِعُوكُمْ) mean the opposite - showing they will not follow you. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing action
Added emphasis by doubling the ت with a shadda and changing vowel to kasra
Added و and ا at the end to make it plural 'they'
Added كُمْ to show who is being followed (you all)
Adding fatha marks makes it a past tense verb meaning 'to make equal'
Changed from a verb (doing something) to a noun meaning equal/same by adding alif and hamza at the end
Added double damma marks to show this is an indefinite noun in the subject position
Starting with the preposition على which shows something being on top of or upon something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and changed و to ا because it's at the end
Added و to show multiple people did the action
Added تُ to show 'I' did the action
Added هُمْ to show who received the action ('them')

steps.m: did you all call out to them?
steps.n: Added أَ to make it a question + changed 'I' (تُ) to 'you all' (تُمُو)

steps.m: or (connecting two equal choices in a question)
steps.n: This is a special word that connects two equal choices in a question, like asking 'Did you call them OR are you staying silent?' It's different from the regular 'or' (أَوْ) because it's specifically used in questions where both options are equally possible.
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when talking to someone
Adding م with sukun at the end makes it refer to multiple people being addressed
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to describe someone who has this quality

steps.m: ones who are quiet (plural for men)
steps.n: Added ون to make it plural for a group of men or mixed group. This word describes أنتم (you all) in the ayah.

steps.m: ones who are quiet (plural for men)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) to make the word grammatically correct in this position
This word adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding an exclamation mark in English. In this ayah, it makes the statement about those you call upon (ٱلَّذِينَ) stronger.
This is the basic form of the word that points to or describes someone/something
Adding ال with shadda makes it point to a specific person or thing
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word describes the people who are doing the calling (تَدْعُونَ) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added تَ at start to show 'you' are doing it. Changed middle vowel to sukun and last vowel to damma to show it's happening now. The final و shows it's a present tense weak verb.
Added نَ at the end to show multiple people are being addressed. This word appears in a question about false deities that people pray to besides Allah.
This is a helper word that shows separation or distance from something. In this ayah, it works together with دُونِ to mean 'besides/other than'.
Adding damma (ُ) to make it a complete word
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it
Added shadda to emphasize the L sound
Changed first letter to hamzat al-wasl and added kasra at end to show possession

steps.m: to serve, worship, or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes from singular 'servant' to plural form meaning 'servants'
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (not specific servants). This word tells us what those called besides Allah are - they are just servants like you.

steps.m: to be like or resemble something

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'example' by adding the vowels a-a
Forms broken plural showing multiple examples or similar things
Adds nominative case ending showing this is the subject
Adds suffix showing these examples/likes belong to multiple people being addressed. This word describes عِبَادٌ (servants) in the ayah.
Changed to basic command form by adding damma and sukun. The final و drops because it's a weak letter in command form.
Added helper alif with damma at start to make pronunciation easier
Added و at end to make it a proper command form
Added هُمْ to show who is being called (them)
Added فَ at start to connect with previous statement, and the اُ changes to ٱ after فَ
Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form
Added است at beginning to show seeking/requesting
Changed from past to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels
Added plural marker for 'they'
Added ل to make it a command/request
Added فَ to show this is a result or sequence
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added كُمْ which shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ (so let them respond) in this ayah, showing who should respond.

This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it means 'if you are truthful' as a condition for the previous statement about calling upon false gods. The word connects to فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ (then let them respond) which shows what should happen if the condition is true.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Changed from 'he was' to a command 'be!' by dropping the middle letter و and changing vowels
Added تُ at the end to change from a command ('be!') to past tense 'I was' (not 'you were')
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to make it plural 'you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changes from describing the action to describing someone who does the action regularly
Makes the word plural and shows it's being affected by كُنتُم (you were). In Arabic, after كُنتُم, we use this special ending to show what someone/something was.
The َ at the end shows this word is being affected by another word in the sentence that makes it accusative case
This is a basic word showing possession or having something
Added هُمْ (hum) which means 'they/them' to show who owns or possesses something
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question. This is asking about the false gods' abilities in the ayah.

steps.m: to walk, to move with legs

steps.n: Changed the action into the body part used for that action
Changed from singular to plural form by adding أَ at start and changing vowels
Added the -un ending (two damma marks) to show this is the subject doing the action
Added يَ at beginning to show it's happening now, and added vowel marks to connect the letters

steps.m: they walk (referring to false gods' idols)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show many are doing the action. This is talking about idols that pagans worship, showing they can't even walk.
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: with it (referring to يَد "hand" in يَبْطِشُونَ بِهَآ)
steps.n: Added ها to show what is being acted upon. In this ayah, it refers back to the hand (يَد) that they use to grasp with.
Changed the final alif to an extended alif (آ). This is just a writing style used at the end of words and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: or (connecting questions about false idols' abilities)
steps.n: This is a special kind of 'or' used to connect questions that expect 'no' as an answer. Here it connects questions about whether false gods have hands, eyes, and ears - with the understanding that they don't have any of these abilities.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation

steps.m: for them/they have (connected to أَرْجُلٌ - feet)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هم (them) to show who the preposition affects. In this ayah, it's asking if these false gods have feet to walk with.
Forms the basic singular noun for 'hand'
Changes from singular 'hand' (يَد) to broken plural 'hands' (أَيْد) by adding alif at start

steps.m: hands (that can grasp with) (object of لَهُمْ - do they have)
steps.n: The word gets a special ending mark to show it's the object being asked about - 'Do they have hands?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he did) to present tense (he does) by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they seize/attack violently (referring to false gods/idols)
steps.n: The ون ending shows multiple subjects are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods/idols who cannot seize or attack.
Combined the preposition ب (with) with the pronoun ها (it) to show how something is used. In this ayah, it's referring to the feet (أَرْجُلٌ) that they walk with.
The alif at the end is elongated when it comes at the end of a word in the Quran. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: or (connecting questions about لَهُمْ 'do they have')
steps.n: This is a question word that connects multiple questions about what these false gods have or don't have. In this ayah, it connects questions like 'Do they have feet?' and 'Do they have hands?'
Added fatha to the preposition ل to show its basic meaning of 'for' or 'to'
Added the basic singular masculine pronoun ه with damma to show possession

steps.m: for/to them (referring to the false gods' supposed feet, hands, eyes, and ears)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change the singular pronoun to plural, showing it refers to multiple false gods mentioned in the verse
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a singular noun
This is a special plural form for body parts that come in pairs or small groups

steps.m: eyes (that do the seeing in يُبْصِرُونَ)
steps.n: The tanwin (double damma) shows this is indefinite (any eyes). These eyes are what do the seeing in the verb يُبْصِرُونَ (they see)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added ون to show many are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods/idols that cannot see.
Starting with the basic feminine pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before

steps.m: with it (referring to the feet they walk with)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ to show how something is done. The آ at the end shows a pause in reading.

steps.m: or (connecting questions about لَهُمْ 'do they have')
steps.n: This is a special word that connects multiple questions together. In this ayah, it's connecting questions about what false gods have or don't have (feet, hands, eyes, etc.)
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: for them (referring to the false gods/idols)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which shows this is happening to multiple male things/people. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods/idols being asked about.
Basic root meaning
Adding damma vowels creates a noun for a body part
Changed to plural form to show multiple ears
Added tanween to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adds يَ at start to change from past (he heard) to present/ongoing (he hears)

steps.m: they hear/are hearing (referring to false gods who cannot hear)
steps.n: Plural suffix shows multiple subjects. In this ayah, it refers to false gods who are being criticized for their inability to hear.
Basic preposition showing how something is done or used

steps.m: with it (feminine) (referring to the body parts mentioned in يَمْشُونَ بِهَا)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun ها to show what is being used. In this ayah, it refers back to the body parts (feet, hands, eyes, ears) that are being discussed.
Basic root meaning
Changed to a command by adding 'oo' sound (damma) at start and making middle letter silent (sukun)
The weak letter و is dropped in command form, and kasra is added at the end because this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: to call out to someone or invite them
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Dropped last letter and added marks to make it a command - like saying 'you call!'
Added helper alif with damma at start to make pronunciation easier
Added وا at the end to make it a command for multiple people

steps.m: to join or associate with others
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Takes the root meaning 'join/associate' and makes it into a plural word for 'partners/associates'
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) but keeps the same meaning
Adds كُمْ at the end to show these partners belong to the people being spoken to
This is a connecting word (particle) that shows one thing happens after another. The shadda (ّ) on م makes the meaning stronger, showing a clear separation in time between events.

steps.m: to plan harm against someone secretly
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Changes from verb (to plot) into noun (a plot)
Adds damma to show this is the subject or predicate
Adds و to make it plural - like saying 'they plot' instead of 'he plots'

steps.m: plot/scheme against me (command to a group)
steps.n: Adds the object pronoun 'me' to show who is being plotted against
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence. This word negates the verb تُنظِرُونِ (you give respite) that comes after it.
Basic root meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra) - means 'he gives' instead of 'he gave'
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يُ with تُ
Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all'
Adds 'me' as the object receiving the action
This is the basic form of the word that shows emphasis
The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show extra emphasis. The kasra (ِ) helps with pronunciation. This word makes وَلِۦِّىَ (my protector) change its ending to show it's being emphasized.

steps.m: to protect, be close to, or take care of

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the last letter to create a noun meaning 'one who protects'
Added ي and fatha to show possession (my) and its role as the subject of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'deity' became the name 'Allah' by adding ال (the) and merging it specially. The ُ at the end shows it's doing the action in the sentence.
This is the base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to things
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it specific and definite. In this ayah, it's describing Allah (اللَّهُ) who sent down the Book.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down repeatedly (subject of the relative clause ٱلَّذِى)
steps.n: The shadda on ز shows this was done multiple times or with special care. This word describes Allah (ٱللَّهُ) through the relative pronoun ٱلَّذِى (who).
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. In this ayah, the word كِتَاب is in accusative case because it is the object (the thing being sent down) of the verb نَزَّلَ (to send down).

steps.m: the book (object of نَزَّلَ - sent down)
steps.n: Added definiteness with ٱلْ to show this is 'the book' rather than just any book
Added damma (ُ) and fatha (َ) to show this is a complete pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The ي changed to a doubled ل (shown by shadda) and ended with ى to make the action stronger, meaning 'took complete control'
Added تَ at start to show doing action to oneself

steps.m: he takes charge of (subject of نَزَّلَ)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show this is happening in the present/future. This word describes what Allah does, who was mentioned as the one who sent down (نَزَّلَ) the book.

steps.m: one who is righteous/good (doer of righteousness)
steps.n: This form shows someone who has this quality as a characteristic. The word is the object of يتولى (he takes care of/protects).
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple righteous people
Adds 'the' to specify these particular righteous people. The ص gets doubled (shadda) because it combines with the ل from ال.

steps.m: the righteous/good people (who are being protected by يَتَوَلَّى)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is receiving the action of يَتَوَلَّى (he protects/takes care of)
Starting with the demonstrative base that points to something
Adding ال and ي changes it from pointing at something to describing someone or something
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: and those who (who تَدْعُونَ/you call upon)
steps.n: Adding و at the start connects this to what came before. The word describes people who are being called upon or worshipped, as shown by تَدْعُونَ (you call upon) in the ayah.

steps.m: to call out to, invite, or pray to
steps.n: Changed to past tense plural form meaning 'they called/were called'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ at start. The و at the end shows it's present tense. The middle letter got quiet (sukun).

steps.m: you all call/pray (to those besides اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added نَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This word refers to calling upon false deities, as shown by 'min doonihi' (besides Him) in the ayah.
Basic form of the preposition that shows starting point, source, or separation
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Added damma and sukun to connect the letters and make it a usable word
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition مِن
Added هِ with an elongation mark (ۦ) to show possession - 'his/its'
This is the basic form of the negative particle that makes things 'not' true
Adding the fatha (َ) makes the word stronger and clearer to hear
Adding alif (ا) makes the word stronger and extends its sound. In this ayah, it's saying they are NOT able to help you.
This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to make it mean 'trying to be able to' or 'seeking ability' - like asking if you can do something
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is negated by لا in the verse.

steps.m: to help, support, or give victory
steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: helping you all (object of يَسْتَطِيعُونَ - they are not able)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this help/support is directed to 'you all'
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added elongation mark (آ) to show extended pronunciation
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
The meaning changed to 'self' because it's the precious, breathing part of a person
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a word meaning one self or soul
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon
Added هُمْ at end to show it means 'their selves'. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَنصُرُونَ (they help/support)
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: if (introduces a condition like 'if you do this...')
steps.n: This is one of the most common conditional words in Arabic. It introduces the first part of an if-then statement, like saying 'If I study...' before completing with '...then I will learn'

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to what came before)
steps.n: The و connects this condition to the previous statement. In this ayah, it connects to the previous discussion about false gods, adding another point about their inability to help.
Added basic past tense form with fatha vowels. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense 'you' form. The ا changes back to و because it's present tense.
Added هُمْ to show who is being called (them)
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْهُدَىٰ (guidance) to show 'towards guidance'

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding damma and tanween. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific guidance. The tanween is removed because ٱلْ makes it definite.
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَسْمَعُوا (they hear), making it mean 'they do not hear'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb comes after لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not hear'.
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وَ (and) at start, and هُمْ (them) at end to show who is being seen
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows this is happening now (present tense) and 'he' is doing it. The ُ (damma) marks show it's ongoing action.
The ونَ shows this is for a group of males. In this ayah, they are looking at the Prophet (peace be upon him).
Basic preposition meaning 'to'
Added ي with sukun (ْ) to connect the preposition to the coming pronoun
Added كَ meaning 'you' (masculine singular). This word connects to يَنظُرُونَ (they look) in the ayah, showing who they are looking at.
This is a pronoun for a group of males. It's the subject of the verb لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (they do not see)
The وَ adds 'and' to connect this clause with the previous statement about them not hearing guidance
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَسْمَعُوا۟ (they hear) negative, changing it to 'they do not hear'. It's one of the most common words used to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic.
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce the word correctly as 'laa'

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of seeing with your eyes
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they see (referring to the disbelievers who look at the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah who are looking at the Prophet ﷺ.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he took' to 'take!' by making it a command - dropped first letter and changed vowels
Added kasra at the end to help connect with the next word (الْعَفْوَ)
Changed into a noun meaning the act of pardoning. This is what is being 'taken' in the command 'take pardoning'.
Added 'the' at the beginning and the َ at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the command 'take'
Changed to command form to match the previous command خُذِ (take/hold). The sukun on أ and ر show it's a command, while the damma on م is the basic command vowel.
Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the previous command خُذِ (take/hold)

steps.m: to know/recognize what is good and right
good custom/practice (what people recognize as right)
steps.n: Changed the verb 'recognize what is good' into a noun meaning 'what is recognized as good'
Added 'the' to make it specific. This word is what is being commanded (object) in أْمُرْ (command)

steps.m: with the good custom/practice (object of أْمُرْ)
steps.n: Added 'with' at beginning and kasra at end to show how the commanding should be done - with good customs
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he turned away' to a command 'turn away!' by adding أ at the start and changing the vowels

steps.m: and turn away! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this command with the previous commands in the verse (take what is offered and enjoin what is right)
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows separation, distance, or turning away from something
Added kasra at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word الْجٰهِلِينَ (the ignorant ones). In this ayah, it means to turn away from the ignorant ones.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who lacks knowledge. This comes after the word عن (from/about) in the ayah.
Added ين to show we're talking about many people who lack knowledge

steps.m: the people who lack knowledge (after عن)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (the) at the beginning to make it specific and َ at the end because it comes after عن
This is a conditional word that introduces a possibility. The ما makes it more general, like saying 'whenever' instead of just 'if'
The و at the beginning connects this condition with what came before it

steps.m: to whisper evil thoughts, to provoke mischief

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start shows this is happening in the present time
Adding نَّ with a shadda makes it stronger, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'

steps.m: he surely whispers evil thoughts to you
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows this evil whispering is being done to 'you'. This comes from the Shaytan (الشَّيْطٰنِ) who is mentioned right after this word.
This is a preposition that shows something coming from or originating from something else. Here it connects to 'the devil/Satan' (ٱلشَّيْطٰنِ)
Added َ (fatha) at the end because the next word starts with ال. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to be rebellious or distant from good

steps.n: Adding ي after ش turns the action (being rebellious) into a noun meaning someone who is very rebellious
Adding ا after ط makes it into a noun meaning 'devil' - one who completely embodies being rebellious against good
Adding ٱل makes it specific and doubles the ش letter
The ِ at the end shows this comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah

steps.m: to whisper evil thoughts or poke/prod someone towards doing bad things
he whispered evil thoughts (subject of يَنزَغَنَّكَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb. This is the base form of the word that appears in يَنزَغَنَّكَ (he whispers evil to you) in this ayah.

steps.m: an evil whisper/prod (referring to الشَّيْطٰنِ/Satan's action)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that means one instance of the action. The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is indefinite - like saying 'an' evil whisper rather than 'the' evil whisper. This word describes what might come from الشَّيْطٰنِ (Satan) mentioned in the ayah.
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he sought protection'
Added است at the start to show doing something for yourself - like asking for protection
Changed from 'he sought protection' to 'you seek protection!' by changing the middle vowel from 'a' to 'i' to make it a command

steps.m: then seek protection! (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect it with the previous statement about Satan's evil suggestions
This is the base word - Allah's name. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added i-sound (kasra) at the end to show it belongs to or is connected to something

steps.m: in/by/with Allah (object of فَٱسْتَعِذْ - seek refuge)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show how we're connecting with Allah. This word is what we're seeking refuge in when the verb فَٱسْتَعِذْ (seek refuge) is used.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: indeed he/it (referring to Allah being سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ - All-Hearing, All-Knowing)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end which means 'he/it'. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is described as All-Hearing and All-Knowing in the words that follow.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
This form shows someone who hears perfectly and completely - when used as one of Allah's names, it means He hears everything
The tanween (double dammah) at the end shows this is describing Allah as All-Hearing
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Changed to the intensive form to show complete and perfect knowledge. This word describes Allah in this ayah, showing His perfect and complete knowledge.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English.
Adding the kasra under إ and shadda on ن makes the emphasis even stronger. This word connects to ٱلَّذِينَ (those who) in this ayah to emphasize that they are the ones being talked about.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who' or 'which' when talking about one person or thing
Added ن at the end to make it refer to many people instead of just one
Added ال at the beginning with a shadda on the following letter. This points to specific people - in this case, those who are mindful of Allah (ٱتَّقَوْا۟)

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to make it mean 'to protect oneself' instead of 'to protect others'
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Form VIII pattern showing someone doing action to/for themselves
This word is used to talk about something that happens at a specific time, like saying 'at the moment when'
Adding ا at the end makes this word connect what happens at one time to what happens right after. In this ayah, it connects when something touches them to what they do next.
When two same letters come together, they combine with a shadda

steps.m: touched them (referring to those who are God-conscious when touched by evil thoughts)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was touched. This refers back to 'those who are God-conscious' (ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to go around or circle something

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'circle around' to noun meaning 'circling' by adding vowel marks fatHa and sukoon
Changed the middle و to ا and added ِ to show who does the action. This describes something that comes from Satan (الشَّيْطٰنِ) in this verse.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any wanderer, not a specific one

steps.m: a whispering/evil thought (that goes around in one's mind)
steps.n: The alif was lengthened (ٰ) to emphasize the meaning of something that circles/goes around in one's mind. The ٌ (tanween) shows it's any general whisper, not specific.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or starts
The shadda (ّ) makes the 'م' sound stronger to emphasize the meaning
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this connects to the next word الشَّيْطٰنِ (the devil/Satan), making it mean 'from the devil'

steps.m: one who is rebellious and distant
steps.n: Added ي and ا to the root letters to make it mean 'the one who rebels/acts distant' - like turning 'rebel' into 'rebel-er'
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific devil, and doubles the ش because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it follows مِّنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show doing the action to oneself
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
This is a conditional word that shows something happens after something else, like saying 'when' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. Here it connects to تَذَكَّرُوا۟ (they remember) to show what happens after something.
This is the basic form of the pronoun that refers to a single male person

steps.m: they/them (referring to those who are mindful/fear Allah)
steps.n: Adding م changes the pronoun from singular (one person) to plural (many people). In this ayah, it refers back to 'those who are mindful/fear Allah' mentioned earlier.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Added shadda for emphasis
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding vowel marks
Changed from a verb about being brotherly to a noun meaning brotherhood by changing the vowels
Added ان to make it plural, showing multiple brothers
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in يَمُدُّونَهُمْ (they extend/aid them)
Added هُمْ to show possession
Added وَ to connect this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with shadda to show intensity
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they extend them (in evil) (connected to فِى ٱلْغَىِّ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who receives the action. This connects to 'in evil' (فِى ٱلْغَىِّ) in the ayah, showing they extend/help them in evil ways.
This is a basic particle that shows location or position. It doesn't come from a root.
The ى at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it shows being 'in misguidance (الْغَىِّ)'.

steps.m: to stray from the right path

steps.n: Root letters were connected with short vowels (fatha) to make past tense 'he strayed'
The و changes to ي and gets a shadda to show this is the concept of straying itself
Added ال to make it specific
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after the word في (fee) which means 'in'

steps.m: then (connects to يَمُدُّونَهُمْ - they extend them)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. The shadda (ّ) on م makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'and then' instead of just 'then'. In this ayah, it connects what happens after the extending/pulling mentioned in يَمُدُّونَهُمْ.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يُقْصِرُونَ) mean the opposite. It changes 'they cease' to 'they do not cease'.
Adding fathas to create basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ prefix and changing internal vowels
Adding ون to show multiple people doing the action
The ون ending shows multiple people (they) are doing the action of stopping
This is a basic particle that shows when something happens. It usually introduces a condition or time.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb تَأْتِهِم (come to them) from present to past negative, making it mean 'did not come to them'
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and vowel marks to show present tense
Added هِم at the end to show who receives the action (them)
The root letters combine with the first two letters merging into a long آ sound
The ة shows this is one specific instance of a sign
The بِ shows this sign is being used as a tool or means

steps.m: with any sign/miracle (object of تَأْتِهِم - bring to them)
steps.n: The ٍ shows this is any general sign (not a specific one) and is being acted upon by the verb تَأْتِهِم (bring to them)
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to make it 'they said' instead of 'he said'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: why not (questioning why they didn't bring a sign)
steps.n: Added لا (not) to create a compound particle that expresses questioning or criticism. In this ayah, it questions why a sign wasn't brought.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Form VIII pattern indicates doing the action reflexively/for oneself. The ا prefix and ت infix are characteristic of this form.
Adds feminine object pronoun to indicate what was chosen
The weak letter و is dropped in the command form. This word appears at the start of divine instructions to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in the Quran.
When we add ما to إِنَّ, it changes from just meaning 'indeed' to meaning 'only'. This helps limit or restrict what comes after it in the sentence.
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added ا and ت to show the person did the action for themselves
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels from -a- to -i-
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by replacing يَ with أَ
This is the basic form of the word that refers to non-specific things or actions

steps.m: that which (connects to يُوحَىٰٓ - is revealed)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it connects what comes before (I only follow) with what comes after (is revealed to me).

steps.m: to inspire or reveal divine guidance

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he revealed' to present tense 'is being revealed' by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show something is receiving the revelation
Changed to passive form to show revelation is being received (by رَسُول), not sent
The ُ (damma) shows this is passive - something being received rather than done
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the basic preposition
Added ىَّ (pronounced as yya) at the end to show the meaning 'me' with emphasis
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its origin. Here it shows these insights/clear proofs come from your Lord.

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who does this caring completely

steps.m: lord (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: Added kasra to prepare for connecting to the next part
Added ي at the end to show it belongs to me
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon
This is the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Added ها at the start makes it mean 'this' specifically (for something close by), like pointing at something
Added the alif mark (ٰ) over ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone/something with this quality
Changed from a word meaning 'insightful' to its plural form 'insights' by adding آئِ in the middle - like changing 'wise' to 'wisdoms'
Added damma to show this word is the subject
