This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects رَّبِّى (my Lord) to what came before it, showing that the revelation comes from Allah.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
steps.n: The root letters show the basic idea of caring for something and helping it grow
Adding shadda makes it mean someone who does a lot of caring/nurturing

steps.m: O my Lord! (calling upon one's Lord)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end makes it into a way of calling out to your Lord
Added كُمْ (kum) at the end to change from 'my Lord' to 'your Lord' (when talking to many people)

steps.m: to guide or show the right way
steps.n: These three letters together represent the basic idea of guiding or showing direction
The root letters h-d-y became a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding the special ending ى with double marks ً
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to the word before it, like saying 'and'
Adding ة changes this from a verb into a feminine noun meaning 'mercy'
Adding tanween (double damma) makes this an indefinite noun - 'a mercy' instead of 'the mercy'
وَ means 'and' - it connects this word to what came before it

steps.m: to stand together, to rise up as one
steps.n: The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
The root meaning 'to stand' becomes a noun meaning 'people who stand together'. The word shows who receives mercy in this ayah.
Adds the preposition لِ to indicate 'for/to'
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and tanwin at the end to show indefiniteness. This word connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in the next ayah, describing which people are being referred to.
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The alif changes to hamza (ؤ) because it's in the middle of the word now.
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing the action
This is a conditional/temporal particle that introduces a time condition. In this ayah, it sets up when something should happen - specifically when the Quran is recited.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it

steps.m: to read or recite something out loud

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by changing the vowels. This shows the Quran (ٱلْقُرْءَانُ) is being read, rather than someone reading it.

steps.m: recitation (the act or result of reciting)
steps.n: Changed the word structure to show the result of reciting. The hamza (ء) helps connect the parts smoothly.

steps.m: the Quran (the divine recitation being read in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific to THE Quran. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject of what's happening in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
This form shows actively seeking to hear something - like trying to listen carefully

steps.m: so listen attentively (connected to قُرِئَ - was recited)
steps.n: Adds فَ to show this is a response to when the Quran is recited

steps.m: so all of you listen attentively
steps.n: Changes from command to one person into command to many people by adding وا at the end
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added هُ which is a pronoun meaning 'him' or 'it' (referring to القرءان - the Quran)
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper for the هُ when connecting to the next word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from 'he was silent' to 'be silent!' by adding أَ at start and changing middle vowel to 'i' sound - this makes it a command
Adds وا to make the command apply to a group instead of one person
Adds و at beginning to connect this command with the previous one (فَٱسْتَمِعُوا۟)

steps.m: A word that expresses hope or expectation, like saying 'perhaps' or 'maybe'
steps.n: This is a particle that doesn't come from a root. It's one of the special words in Arabic that has its own meaning.

steps.m: Perhaps you all (connected to تُرْحَمُونَ - you will receive mercy)
steps.n: The كم at the end shows we're talking to multiple people. This word connects to تُرْحَمُونَ in the next ayah, expressing hope that the listeners will receive Allah's mercy.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he showed mercy' to 'mercy is shown to you' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show someone else is doing the action

steps.m: you (all) are shown mercy (so that you may receive mercy)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show it applies to multiple people. This verb comes after لَعَلَّكُمْ (so that you may) in the ayah.
Added sukun on first letter and damma on second letter to make it a command form
Added alif with damma at start to strengthen the command

steps.m: and remember! (command to رَّبَّكَ your Lord)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'. The alif changes to hamzat-wasl (ٱ) because it comes after و. This command connects to رَّبَّكَ (your Lord) which comes next in the ayah.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps grow (master/lord)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does a lot of caring and helping others grow

steps.m: your Lord (the one being remembered in وَٱذْكُر)
steps.n: Added كَ to show it's your Lord. This Lord is being remembered because the word before (وَٱذْكُر) means 'and remember'

steps.m: your Lord (the one being remembered in وَٱذْكُر)
steps.n: Added shadda on first letter because it comes after a word ending in a sukun (وَٱذْكُر). This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'remember' with 'yourself' to show where the remembrance should happen.
Added ى at the end which is standard for this preposition. This shows the complete form used to connect words showing location or containment.
Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added كَ to show this means 'your self'. This word follows the preposition فِى (in)
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself intensely
Changed vowel to ُ to show receiving the action
Added tanween to make it indefinite
Changed the vowel mark to show it's describing how to do something (with humility)
The root meaning 'to fear' becomes a noun meaning 'fear' by changing the middle letter و to ي and adding a kasra (short i sound) before it
Added ة to show one specific instance of fear

steps.m: and a single instance of fear
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to connect with previous word تَضَرُّعًا (in humility)

steps.m: and in fear (describing how to remember Allah)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this describes how the remembrance should be done, matching with تَضَرُّعًا (in humility)

steps.m: to be beneath or lower than something

steps.n: Added damma and fatha to make it into a word meaning 'below' or 'beneath'. This word describes the position relative to الْجَهْرِ (loudness) in this ayah.

steps.m: and below/beneath (describes position relative to الْجَهْرِ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it in the ayah
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning the quality of being loud
Added 'the' (ال) at the start and a kasra sound (ِ) at the end because it comes after the word دُونَ (less than)
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of something
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْقَوْلِ (the speech/saying). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when reading.

steps.m: speech (the act or content of speaking)
steps.n: Changed from a basic root meaning 'speak' into a noun meaning 'speech' by adding the vowels 'a' and 'sukoon'
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by الجَهْرِ (loudness) in the phrase 'loudness of speech'
Added damma vowels and changed the weak letter و to a shadda to create the noun form
Added the definite article ال
Added the preposition ب to indicate 'in/with/by'
The ي makes it into a noun describing the time of day
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding alif after first letter and alif before last letter
Added the definite article to specify these particular evenings
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows بِٱلْغُدُوِّ (in the morning) in the ayah
Added و to connect this word with the previous phrase about mornings
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'do not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This connects to the verb تَكُن (be) that comes after it in the ayah.
Added fatha on lam to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you be (in a command or conditional statement)
steps.n: The word was shortened by dropping the final ون when it comes after لا, changing from 'you are' to 'you be not'
Added emphasis (shadda) to show strong connection to what follows
Added fatha at the end to connect this word with the next word الْقَوْلِ (speech/words) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the action
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added ين at the end to show multiple people. The word comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah.

This is a basic word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It makes the sentence stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English. Here it emphasizes the description of those who are with your Lord.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The shadda (ّ) appears because ال combines with the first letter.

steps.m: those who (referring to angels in عِندَ رَبِّكَ)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple beings (in this case, the angels mentioned as being with your Lord)
This root conveys the idea of being present or near something
Adding kasra (ِ) under the ع changes this from an action into a word that shows location
Adding fatha (َ) at the end shows this word connects to the word after it (رَبِّكَ)
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter with shadda to create a noun meaning 'the one who does this action'
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by what follows it
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to 'you'. The word عِندَ (with/at) before it requires this ownership form.
This is a negative particle that makes what follows it negative. In this ayah, it negates يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (they are arrogant), making it mean 'they are not arrogant'.
This root relates to size and greatness
Added vowels (fatha, damma, fatha) to make the root into 'he became big/great' in the past tense
Changed from 'became big' (كَبُرَ) to 'felt proud' (كَبِرَ) by changing middle vowel - shows inner feeling instead of physical size
Added 'ist' at start to show doing the action in an excessive/arrogant way
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowel
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

This is a helper word that shows either physical distance (away from) or what topic is being discussed (about). Here it connects to the word عِبَادَتِهِۦ (His worship) to show what they are not being arrogant about.
This root relates to serving or worshipping
Changes from singular to broken plural form, showing multiple worshippers
Added ة to change from 'people who worship' to 'the act of worshipping'
Added kasra to indicate genitive case
Adds attached possessive pronoun 'his'. The word is the object of عَنْ (from/about) in يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ (they are arrogant about)
This root originally meant to swim, but came to mean praising God by declaring His perfection
The root letters got vowels and the middle letter doubled to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he glorified'
Changed from past tense to present tense, showing ongoing glorification
Changed from singular 'he' to plural 'they'
Added 'him' to show who is being glorified
Added 'and' to connect this action with previous ones
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to make it readable
Added هُ at the end to show this means 'to him'

steps.m: and to Him (connected to يَسْجُدُونَ - they prostrate)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes before it. The word is connected to يَسْجُدُونَ (they prostrate), showing who they prostrate to.
This root relates to bowing down in worship
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
